The latter was extremely powerful, and he was the original wielder of the Rune Weapon Chart. The Flame Emperor was at least of the same standing as Yu the Great in the legends, and he should have been able to curb the monsters. 

Zu An harbored many doubts in his mind. He had stumbled upon the remnants of different dynasties in the Cultivation World, and these dynasties had all wielded incredible powers, unlike the mundane dynasties in his previous world. 

But where did those incredible powerhouses go? It would only take one of them to suppress the monsters. While this Cultivation World from several thousand years back is much stronger than it is in the future, it's still no match for the Monster World.

The person in the cave replied, “It’s all fated.”

Zu An was speechless. He expected to hear more, but that was all. All his talk about fate makes him sound like a con artist. 

Knowing there was no point in persisting with this topic, he decided to ask about another, more pressing concern. “I've heard the Firecloud Cave is proficient in medicine. May I ask if there is a way to save someone afflicted with the innate Cruel Poison Constitution?”

The man in the cave was surprised by that question. “I thought the Cruel Poison Constitution was just a thing of legend. That constitution grants one high affinity toward all kinds of poisons, but if it acts up, it can only be suppressed by a more potent poison. That poison can be used in combat, but it only worsens the condition. It’s only a matter of time before the poison becomes insuppressible and destroys the host and the world along with it.”

The Peacock Princess looked at Zu An with worried eyes. Why is big brother suddenly asking about that? Does he have the Cruel Poison Constitution?

The Mermaid Queen knew the truth. Is his little sister’s illness that terrifying? No wonder he hasn’t found a cure for her to date.

The person in the cave continued, “I do know a way to resolve the Cruel Poison Constitution, however. With certain cultivation techniques and rare materials, you can condense the poison into an inner core and achieve a balance. It won’t solve the problem at its root, but it can at least spare the host from an untimely death.”

Zu An found those words weirdly familiar.

“The stronger the cultivation technique used, the stronger the condensed poison core will be. You’ll have to look for a cultivation technique that complements the Cruel Poison Constitution by yourself. It’ll depend on the luck of both you and the host of the Cruel Poison Constitution.

“I am, however, familiar with the rare materials required—Defying Spirit Tears, a Brilliant Pinflower, and an Aurora Poison Dragon’s inner core. Since you are here to treat the poisoned Ocean races, I presume you already have the Aurora Poison Dragon’s inner core,” the man in the cave said. 

Zu An was baffled. For a moment there, he wondered if the person in the cave was Salamay. However, he knew that was impossible. How did Salamay learn about this method?

It was unlikely for Salamay to have been to the enigmatic Firecloud Cave, and there was no reason for her to have sought a solution to the Cruel Poison Constitution. If so, there could only be one explanation. This world’s Salamay had to have learned about it from him, the Mermaid Queen, or the Peacock Princess. 

After some thought, he thought it was more likely for Salamay to have heard about it from him. He was reminded of what Mi Li had previously told him about spacetime. Is this considered closing the loop too?

For a moment there, he even wondered about the eternal paradox—did the chicken or the egg come first?

He quickly reined in his thoughts and continued asking, “Do you know where I can find Defying Spirit Tears and a Brilliant Pinflower?”

“You can find Brilliant Pinflowers in this world, but finding such rare materials is highly luck-dependent. If it’s not fated, it won’t make a difference even if I provide you with a location. Conversely, if it’s fated, you'll find it even if I don’t say a word,” the man in the cave replied. 

Zu An was speechless. Salamay had also previously mentioned that this world could have Brilliant Pinflowers, so he'd thought he could gather more information from this fellow.

“As for Defying Spirit Tears…” The man in the cave suddenly paused as if he was contemplating something. “They don’t exist in the world right now, but you might have a chance to obtain them in the future.”

Zu An was confused.

He tried asking for more details, but the man in the cave gave cryptic replies, such as, “The time isn’t ripe yet. You’ll know when the time comes.” 

Ugh, there’s nothing more frustrating than riddlers! I don’t even know what Defying Spirit Tears are, let alone how to obtain them!

Just then, four pills flew out from the cave. “Place these four pills in each of the four seas. They will dissolve in water and neutralize the poison from the plague formation.”

The Mermaid Queen quickly stowed away the four pills. “Elder, thank you for your medicine. The Ocean races will remember this favor.”

“It’s all decided by fate,” the man in the cave replied calmly.

The trio had long gotten used to this answer.

“Our fate is up. Water Boy, send them out.”

The boy nodded. He walked over and gestured for them to leave with him. 

Zu An suddenly remembered another matter and asked, “One last question. I heard Shennong was the first Human Emperor. May I know where the Human Path’s power insignia is currently?”

The boy was annoyed. “You don’t know your manners. My master is asking you to leave. You shouldn’t be asking any more questions.”

A chuckle echoed from the cave. 

Zu An frowned. He sensed a deeper intent to the laugh, but he couldn’t tell what it was about.

“Why are you searching for the Human Path’s power insignia?” the man in the cave asked. 

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. Since he’s entertaining my question, there should be a chance. Thus, he told the man in the cave about the Six Paths of Reincarnation. He reckoned that considering his stature, the latter would know something about it. However, he withheld key information concerning Houtu as a precaution. 

“Completing the Six Paths of Reincarnation would be a huge merit, but it won’t be easy,” the man in the cave said. 

“I still have to give it a try. If I collect the power insignias one at a time, there will come a day when all six power insignias are gathered.” Zu An didn’t reveal that he already had some of the power insignias, because the other party was simply too mysterious.

“That’s a good mindset.” The man in the cave expressed his approval. “All right. You can find the Human Path’s power insignia with the Fiend races.”

“The Fiend races?” Zu An was perplexed. Why is the Human Path’s power insignia not with the humans, but with the Fiend races?

Sensing his doubt, the man in the cave laughed. “Why do you think the Fiend races don’t belong to the Human Path? This world itself is the Human Path. Since the Fiend races are the rulers of this world, it should go without saying that the Human Path’s power insignia is in their hands.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2482: Bitterness
chapters.body:
Chapter 2482: Bitterness

Zu An was stunned. He suddenly realized that he had unknowingly narrowed the scope of his thoughts too far. He'd instinctively assumed the Human Path’s power insignia was with the humans, and that the humans were the rulers of this world. It was only upon the other party’s reminder that it dawned on him that the Human Path had more than just the human race.

In the future, it was proven that the Cultivation World was the Human Path; it was just that the Fiend races were the stronger ones now. That sparked a thought in his mind, but the thought drifted away before he could clutch onto it. 

Nevertheless, it was a fruitful journey. He earnestly said out of gratitude, “Thank you for the enlightenment, elder.”

The man in the cave calmly replied, “If you are truly grateful to me, fulfill my request.”

As if fearing Zu An would ask more questions, the space around them distorted. Before they knew it, the three of them had already returned to the Crystal Boat. They quickly scanned their surroundings, but the island and the Firecloud Cave were nowhere to be seen. 

“Was I dreaming earlier?” The Peacock Princess rubbed her eyes in confusion.

“No, it wasn’t a dream,” the Mermaid Queenreplied with a quivering voice as she looked at the pills in her hands. 

Zu An looked at the vast ocean and remarked, “What a profound formation.”

All that was left in front of them was the ocean, meaning that someone had moved the island elsewhere. Zu An was awed by the Firecloud Cave’s methods. Speaking of which, is he truly the successor of the Flame Emperor…

The Mermaid Queen was glad that her people could be saved, but the realization that she would have to part ways with Zu An caused her smile to stiffen up. 

The Peacock Princess just had to poke the hornet’s nest then. “Big sis queen, shall we first accompany you back to the Dragon Palace?”

The Mermaid Queen could see through her intention, but she shook her head and said, “No, you still have other matters to attend to. I shall not hold you back.” She then turned to Zu An and asked, “May I have a private conversation with you?”

Upon seeing the plea in her eyes, Zu An’s heart softened. He nodded and followed her to the other end of the deck. 

The Mermaid Queen had many things she wanted to tell him, but now that their moment of parting had come, she suddenly found herself at a loss for words. They stood in silence for a long time. 

It was Zu An who broke the silence in the end. “Stay safe. You should take the Crystal Boat back.”

The Mermaid Queen shook her head. “The Crystal Boat is the Dragon King’s gift to you. I can’t take it. Besides, there’s still some distance before you reach land. You’ll need it.” 

The corners of her lips curled up. Without this Crystal Boat, big brother Zu might have to carry the Peacock Princess for the rest of the journey. This shall be my little vengeance against her. 

“But it’s a long distance for you…” Zu An was worried.

The Mermaid Queen smiled. “Have you forgotten my race? The ocean is my home turf.”

Zu An thought those words made sense. In the water, mermaids are fearsome adversaries. 

Silence descended between them once more. 

The Mermaid Queen realized it was about time for her to leave and asked, “Can I have a hug before we part ways?”

Zu An was taken aback. He hadn’t expected such a request from her. He wondered whether to turn down her request, especially since he wasn’t from this era and didn’t want to influence her.

Sensing his rejection, the Mermaid Queen laughed miserably. “This might be our final parting. After all the time we spent together, do I not at least deserve a friendly hug?”

Tears trickled down her cheeks and landed on the deck, producing crisp knocking sounds. Mermaid tears turned into pearls upon striking the ground, after all. 

Zu An knew this could be their final farewell too. His determination wavered. He was about to open his arms to embrace the Mermaid Queen when a trembling body suddenly held onto him tightly.

Zu An fell into a daze. At that moment, he experienced the emotion called bitterness.

He had never believed in monogamy. Under normal circumstances, he wouldn’t have turned the Mermaid Queen down. But the difference in their eras evoked his helplessness. He knew there was no future between the two of them. 

Instead of hurting each other, it was better for them to sever ties for good. 

It was no wonder that in his previous life’s literary works, love had so many hurdles. He had always scoffed at those tropes, but now, he understood that not all hurdles could be overcome with effort. 

Zu An let out another sigh. He was about to speak when he saw the Mermaid Queen’s face approaching him, and a pair of soft lips pressed against his. His eyes widened in astonishment. 

It was a kiss most men in the world would have been envious of, but he was in no mood to enjoy it. Instead, his mind was in disarray. 

All of a sudden, he felt a sharp pain. He instinctively touched his lips and saw a trace of blood.

The Mermaid Queen had already let go of him and retreated to the corner of the boat. She looked at him with a mischievous smile, saying, “I don’t know if we’ll ever meet again, so I want you to remember that sensation forever. Farewell, my lover.”

She turned around and leaped into the ocean, turning into a beautiful mermaid as she swam into the distance. She didn’t turn around, fearing that she wouldn’t be able to leave anymore if she did. 

The setting sun cast glistening specks of light on the ocean's surface. Zu An couldn’t tell if those were just light reflections or her tears. 

The Peacock Princess walked up to Zu An and sighed sadly, “Big sis queen has left.”

She regretted playing such a trick on the Mermaid Queen earlier. Big brother doesn’t belong to this era anyway, so what’s the point of competing for him? She thus said, “Big brother, once you’re done with your business in the Fiend races' side, you can return to the Ocean races' side.”

Zu An shook his head. There were too many things he had to do, and the world was always throwing new tasks at him. Who knew if he would have a chance to return to the Ocean races' side?

The two of them continued their journey to the Fiend races’ imperial court. The Dragon King’s Crystal Boat could soar through the sky as well, though it was much slower than traveling on the ocean. Still, it was much faster than the airplanes in Zu An's previous life. 

Along the way, the Peacock Princess asked Zu An about the descendant he had gotten close to, whereas Zu An asked her about the Fiend races. 

Soon, they arrived at the Fiend races’ temporary imperial court. 

The Fiend race experts guarding the perimeter were already expecting their arrival. “Princess, this must be the great hero our Saintess mentioned. The Saintess has been awaiting your arrival!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2483: Saintess
chapters.body:
Chapter 2483: Saintess

The Xuan-Su Guards were from the Peacock race and had the ability to fly. They had returned in advance to inform the Fiend races about what had happened in the Ocean races' territory.

The Saintess had quickly gotten to work and built Zu An up as a peerless hero who single-handedly defeated the Incestuous King’s army. 

The Fiend races had been oppressed by the monsters for a long time. Even though the Saintess occasionally scored some victories, all that had done was stop the Fiend races from declining further. They had never experienced such a huge victory over the monsters before.

Some had been overjoyed to hear the Saintess’ words, but most took a skeptical attitude. They had fought the monsters for a long time and knew how formidable the Incestuous King was; it was hard to believe a person could single-handedly defeat the Incestuous King and his army.

Despite their skepticism, however, they were tactful enough not to question it in public. The Fiend races were in urgent need of a major victory to boost their morale, and they lauded the Saintess for her wise move. A few did believe that Zu An was a hero, but most were feigning excitement while secretly investigating his background. 

Now that they were looking at Zu An in person, they frowned. They knew not to judge a book by its cover, yet he was so dashing that they wondered if he was a moocher; but at the same time, there was an inexplicable aura shrouding him. 

Damn, he’s so handsome that even the Peacock Princess is staring holes into him.

Years of warring with the monsters had resulted in the death of the older fiends, and the ones carrying the flag now were the younger generations. Most still had a youthful temperament. 

The Peacock Princess was a renowned beauty in the Fiend races, and unlike the Saintess, she didn’t feel out of reach. Countless Fiend race men adored her for that, which was why it irked them to see the Peacock Princess standing so close to Zu An. 

Still, even though they were young, they knew their priorities. No one was so foolish as to jump out and antagonize Zu An at a time like this. 

A dashing man dressed in golden armor walked over and asked, “Little sister Qingwu, is this the great hero who defeated the Incestuous King? Why don’t you introduce him to us?”

Another man wearing a golden cape voiced his agreement. 

These two men appeared to be the leaders of these youths. 

Despite their courteous attitudes, Zu An still keenly sensed a faint hostility from the crowd. He noticed the gazes they directed at the Peacock Princess, and the truth became apparent to him. A beautiful lady is bound to have many suitors. That’s perfectly normal. It’d be excessive to make a fuss over this. 

But the Peacock Princess frowned in dissatisfaction. “The Saintess is waiting for big brother.”

The words 'big brother’ further fanned the crowd’s jealousy. 

The golden-caped man said, “We’re still a fair distance away from the Saintess’ residence. Why don’t we head there together, and you can introduce him to us along the way? We have nothing but admiration for the great hero who single-handedly destroyed the Incestuous King’s army.”

Zu An calmly watched the spectacle. Judging from the other party’s enunciations, he could tell that the latter didn’t believe he could defeat Mojard’s army, but the current Zu An had weathered too many storms to be fazed by something like this. It’s just a bunch of hot-blooded youths. 

The Peacock Princess had witnessed Zu An’s prowess in person, and she mistakenly thought others knew about his strength too. That led to the golden-caped man’s sarcasm flying over her head. “This is big brother Necropolis. He might be young, but he is extremely powerful. Even the Incestuous King isn’t his match, let alone you lot.”

Black streaks appeared on Zu An’s face. This young lass speaks her mind. Her words are so candid that it’s drawing hatred for me. But he wasn’t bothered by it. Rather, he was curious to know whether these young fellows would challenge him. 

The expressions of the Fiend race youths darkened. “Big brother Necropolis is indeed incredible to have defeated the Incestuous King. May we ask about the details of the battle?”

Zu An looked too young, and he didn’t give off an air of intimidation either. It was hard to think of him as a powerful expert, so it was no wonder they didn’t believe the Peacock Princess’ words. 

The Peacock Princess finally sensed the skepticism in the air. “Shoo; my big brother has no time to talk about these things with you.” Fearing that some fools might actually challenge Zu An, she quickly added, “That golden-armored guy who looks like a nouveau riche is the Fiend races’ third prince. That guy with a yellow cloth wrapped around his body is the Golden Peng race’s crown prince…”

She only introduced the important figures around her, and even so, her introduction was extremely brief, as she didn’t think that someone as important as her big brother had time to waste on mere brats. 

The Third Prince and the Golden Peng Crown Prince had awful-looking expressions. All of them had grown up together, and the Peacock Princess often talked to them like that. However, they were in the presence of an outsider. It felt as if they were being slighted. 

Zu An lamented his ill fate with the Golden Crow race and Golden Peng race. Even now that he had returned to the past, they still viewed each other with hostility. 

Fortunately, with the encroachment of the monster army, the only ones left among the Fiend races were the true elites. Despite feeling a little humiliated, they were able to rein themselves in and not cause a fuss. 

The crowd escorted Zu An to a palace, though it felt like a stretch to call it a palace, as it only looked marginally better than the houses he saw along the way.  The Fiend races had suffered a series of losses, losing even their imperial court. This was the best building they could construct on the spot, since the bulk of their resources would be devoted to fending against the monsters. 

A guard at the doorway stopped the crowd. “The Saintess wishes to meet the great hero alone. Please wait outside.”

The crowd was taken aback, but they didn’t dare to defy the highly-esteemed Saintess. 

“The Saintess should have personally welcomed you upon your arrival, but for some reason, she called you over instead. Big brother, I hope you don’t mind it. The Saintess must have her reasons for doing so,” the Peacock Princess explained telepathically. 

Zu An smiled. “Don’t worry, I’m not that narrow-minded.”

He stepped into the palace. A maid brought him to a serene courtyard and said, “The Saintess is waiting for you inside.”

The maid bowed and took her leave.

Zu An was surprised. The Saintess should have met him in a public space, such as her study, but this courtyard appeared to be her personal residence. For a moment, he felt vigilant. Could the Saintess be scheming something to blackmail me?

On second thought, he remembered that the Yumen Beiqing he'd met at the Sealed Land wasn’t an underhanded person. So, he entered the courtyard, though he continued to keep his guard up lest he fall for the other party’s trap.

“You’re finally here,” a voice echoed from the room, sending shudders through Zu An’s body.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2484: The Most Familiar Stranger
chapters.body:
Chapter 2484: The Most Familiar Stranger

A dignified lady stood in the courtyard, dressed in a snowy-white shirt and a loose red skirt. Her clothes showcased no feminine curves, but if the world were a portrait, her ravishing visage would be what made the painting the most beautiful thing in the world. When contrasted with her clothes, her fair skin looked even fairer. Her beauty was slightly beneath that of the Mermaid Queen, and she was also nowhere as vibrant as the Peacock Princess. However, she was graceful and dignified. She emanated a natural air of inviolability just by standing there. 

For a moment, Zu An thought he was looking at Kikyo from Inuyasha. 

No wonder the Fiend races’ men all compliment the Saintess’ beauty, but her suitors are nowhere as many as the Peacock Princess. She’s a mortal, but somehow she feels divine. 

Yumen Beiqing had been crafting talismans when she sensed Zu An’s entrance and lifted her head.

Zu An felt an uncanny sense of familiarity from her, possibly because he had once met her in the Sealed Land. He said with a smile, “Paying respect to the Saintess."

He felt the joy of reuniting with an acquaintance, though the latter wouldn’t know that they had once met each other. 

Little did he expect the Saintess to suddenly shoot to her feet and quickly walk up to him. Zu An could tell that the body beneath those loose clothes was trembling. She seemed to be exerting a great deal of willpower to hold herself back. That made him frown. What’s with Yumen Beiqing’s reaction?

Both the Ocean races and the Peacock Princess had said that the Saintess was someone who could remain perfectly composed even when in dire straits. It was bizarre for her to display such a blatant breach of etiquette. Is she nervous or afraid because she’s planning something?

Zu An scanned the surroundings with his divine sense, worried that there could be an ambush, but there was no one else in this courtyard other than the two of them. This is weird…

As the other party drew closer, Zu An cleared the air with a cough and called out, “Saintess?”

As she looked at him, Yumen Beiqing bit her lower lip. Her eyes glistened with tears, and she looked as if there were countless words she wanted to tell him. 

Zu An’s heart quivered at the sight of such pitiful eyes. Why is she acting as if she's known me for a long time? Does she remember our meeting in the Sealed Land? But that’s supposed to happen in the future!

While he was perplexed, the Saintess suddenly leaped into his chest and embraced him tightly. He was dumbfounded.

He had thought of countless possibilities when the two of them met, but he hadn’t expected the legendary Saintess to leap into his arms. Could this truly be a scheme to blackmail me? Does the Saintess intend to use her beauty to recruit me as a fighter for the Fiend races?

But the genuine emotions in the Saintess’ eyes and her trembling body didn’t seem to be a lie. This didn’t feel like a honey trap. 

The Saintess couldn’t have fallen in love with me at first sight, right? Zu An quickly snuffed out that notion as soon as it surfaced in his mind. That was impossible.

Who could have known a pair of lips would press against his the next second?

Zu An was bewildered. His body stiffened up. What in the world is going on?

Feeling the need to get to the bottom of this, he was just about to push the Saintess away when the latter suddenly said, “Big brother Zu, you are finally here to see me.”

Goosebumps rose all over Zu An’s body. The only one who knew his name was the Mermaid Queen. Even the Peacock Princess only knew him as ‘Necropolis’. How had the Saintess learned his name?

Now that he thought about it, the Saintess had also acted as if she'd known him for a long time when they met in the Sealed Land. Did I, at some point in the future, return even further back in time and become acquainted with her?

While he was completely confused, the Saintess realized something and hurriedly explained with a reddened face, “Big brother Zu, I am Ling’er!”

Zu An was shocked. “Little sister Ling’er?”

He stared at the Saintess in disbelief. She is Xie Daoyun?

While there was an uncanny sense of familiarity about her, she looked nothing like Xie Daoyun, though it wouldn’t be right to say there were no similarities at all. Her air of serenity and grace did resemble Xie Daoyun’s. 

“In the Monster World’s underground city, I came into contact with a mysterious piece of jade and was transported to this world. I thought I would never see you again!” The Saintess dropped her usual strong front and sobbed like a child. 

Those words dissipated all of Zu An’s doubts. He tightly hugged her too. “Little sister Ling’er!”

Back when Xie Daoyun first disappeared, he was horrified, though he had an inexplicable feeling that they would meet again soon. He hadn’t expected them to reunite under such circumstances.

The two of them embraced each other tightly. Before long, their lips were locked together as if these acts of intimacy were the only ways they could let the other party know their feelings. 

The Saintess placed her hands on Zu An’s shoulders. Her slender fingers were like ivory tusks, and her forehead accessories gleamed under the sunlight, just like her mesmerizing eyes. 

… 

The Peacock Princess nervously paced to and fro outside the courtyard. She had no idea why the Saintess had requested to meet Zu An in private. The Saintess had always been transparent, choosing to open everything up for discussion. This was the first time she had requested to meet someone in private. 

She wasn't worried about Zu An being harmed by the Saintess; the former could protect himself, whereas the latter wasn't one to do something that foolish. What worried her more was the Saintess falling in love with the perfect Zu An. 

However, she quickly shrugged off that thought as soon as it surfaced. The Saintess was called as such because she aligned with what the Fiend races imagined a Saintess to be. She was beautiful yet inviolable, and she displayed incredible charisma as she led the Fiend races in the battle for their survival. She treated everyone with warmth, yet she showed no interest in secular love. Everyone in the Fiend races acknowledged her because they knew she wouldn’t be biased toward any of them. 

The discussions of the Fiend youths around her showed as much. They were more worried about Zu An doing something disrespectful to the Saintess. 

That angered the Peacock Princess. Big brother isn’t that kind of person!

Most of them quickly realized that their worry was unnecessary. Even if they couldn’t trust Zu An, surely they could trust the Saintess. The discussion thus moved on to speculations about what the two were talking about. 

“They are probably discussing a partnership.”

“Regardless of whether his feats are exaggerated or not, it is a fact that he helped the Ocean races defeat the monster army. Securing his help will be greatly beneficial to us.”

Just then, someone meekly asked, “You’re all talking about the Saintess winning him over to our side. Have you considered the possibility of him taking our Saintess away instead? I mean, he is quite good-looking.”

The next moment, silence descended. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2485: He Is My Man
chapters.body:
Chapter 2485: He Is My Man

After a brief silence, laughter erupted. 

“How is that possible?”

“Indeed. Our Saintess wouldn’t be shaken by secular love.”

“So many outstanding youths have courted her in recent years, but she didn’t even spare them a glance. That’s why everyone was eventually forced to give up.”

… 

The Fiend youths laughed as if what they had just heard was the biggest joke ever. 

The Peacock Princess was delighted but frustrated. She was glad she didn’t have to worry about the Saintess going after Zu An, but she didn’t like how those words made it seem as if her feelings for him were crude.

Our romance transcends spacetime, alright?!

But the reminder that Zu An didn’t belong to this era dampened her mood. 

“My princess, can you tell us about the great hero’s battle with the Incestuous King? Did someone as young as him really defeat the Incestuous King?” someone asked curiously.

The others voiced their agreement too. The news delivered by the Xuan-Su Guards was so inconceivable that they wondered if they had exaggerated the details because their cultivation was too low to discern the battle’s details. The Saintess could have only accepted their statements to raise their people’s morale. It would be more reliable to hear the account of an expert like the Peacock Princess.

The eyes of the Peacock Princess lit up. Even three days wouldn’t be enough for her to speak about Zu An’s exploits. She began sharing the details. 

Her mention of how the abrupt appearance of the monsters had driven the Ocean races into despair invited nods from the crowd—they had witnessed the monster army’s prowess in person before. 

Then, Zu An had appeared. He'd dove into the monster army and wrecked them like a berserker, causing the monsters to scream and blood to flow like a river. 

There was no discrepancy between reality and her account, but the Peacock Princess, due to her rose-tinted glasses, thought that her version was much more awe-inspiring.

Many people voiced their doubts.

“How can an individual rival an army? What about the monster formation masters? Did they not do a thing at all?”

The Peacock Princess slowly replied, “Big brother has an ultimate move—he conjured countless weapons out of thin air to decimate the formation masters.”

“What ultimate move can be that formidable?” The crowd found it inconceivable. Formation masters were the most protected group in an army. They weren’t just skilled in long-ranged offense, but defensive formations too. Even the Fiend Emperor couldn’t destroy the formation masters in an army single-handedly. 

The Peacock Princess scoffed at their doubts. “The Ocean races’ experts were all present, including the Mermaid Queen whom you all dream about. You can corroborate the details with them if you don’t believe me.”

Those words won her some credibility.

“What about the Incestuous King? Someone as strong as him wouldn’t just watch as the great hero decimated his army,” someone pointed out. 

“He made his move. He released his beast form and his body grew to hundreds of meters tall,” the Peacock Princess said, then recounted what happened then.

The faces of the Fiend youths darkened. They had witnessed the prowess of the Incestuous King’s beast form in the battle where they suffered the greatest loss. They remembered the immense pressure they'd felt, and just the thought of it evoked a hint of despair in them.  The Incestuous King had charged into their army and massacred them as if they were mere ants. Their army formation had collapsed, and it had eventually become a one-sided slaughter. 

The Peacock Princess’ eyes gleamed with excitement as she continued, “But big brother is even more formidable…” As if she had become a roadside storyteller, she vividly shared the situation with the crowd, occasionally taking some creative liberties to make Zu An seem even greater. 

The Fiend youths were flabbergasted. They had thought the previous story was an exaggeration, but who could have thought the truth would be even more exaggerated than that? 

The Golden Crow Prince and Golden Peng Crown Prince exchanged fearful looks. It’s a relief we didn’t provoke that guy in the heat of the moment, or else we would have become clowns.

The crowd was overjoyed to hear that the Ocean races and the mysterious races beneath the Eye of the Ocean were willing to ally with the Fiend races. 

“With the Saintess’ leadership and an expert of the great hero’s caliber, we can surely defeat those vile monsters!”

The crowd’s faces beamed with excitement as they dreamed about defeating the monsters and returning to their homeland.

But the Golden Peng Crown Prince poured cold water over the enthused crowd. “It’s too early to celebrate. There’s no guarantee an expert of that caliber will help us. We might not be able to afford his help.” 

The crowd’s hearts sank. 

There’s no reason for an expert of that caliber to help us. Someone that strong would be celebrated as an esteemed guest no matter where he went, even in the Monster World. He might even be able to usurp the Incestuous King’s position.

There’s nothing he can gain from helping us, especially now that the Fiend races are in their weakest state ever. 

The crowd turned to the Peacock Princess.

The Peacock Princess’ face reddened. “Why are you looking at me?”

“Princess, how far have you progressed with the great hero?” a curious youth asked.

Those words piqued the anger of those who adored the Peacock Princess.

The usually sharp-tongued Peacock Princess suddenly lost her usual eloquence. “I-it’s not like what you think!”

“Princess, why don’t you sacrifice yourself for the sake of our Fiend races’ future? An expert of his caliber should be worthy of you,” another youth from a major race said. He favored the Saintess more than the Peacock Princess, allowing him to objectively decide the course of action that maximized the Fiend races’ interest. 

The Peacock Princess sighed. “It’s not that I’m reluctant, but…” She briefly paused before continuing, “Even the Mermaid Queen tried courting him, but to no avail.”

“What?!” The crowd was stunned. 

If the Peacock Princess was only famous in their circle, the Mermaid Queen was a world-famous beauty! It wouldn’t be a stretch to call her the number one beauty in the world, given how she had turned down even the Fiend Emperor. To think that legendary woman would court the man from earlier and fail at that! That would have been a dream for any other man, but he turned her down! Is that fellow still a man?!

Just then, the courtyard doors opened. 

“The Saintess is out!”

The crowd looked over, only to freeze in place. Their usually placid Saintess was now resting against Zu An’s chest with flushed cheeks. 

The Saintess was embarrassed to have so many gazes on her, but she still gently spoke up. “Let me introduce him to you. He is my man.” 

The crowd was speechless.

The Peacock Princess was bewildered.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2486: The Domineering Saintess Is In Love With Me
chapters.body:
Chapter 2486: The Domineering Saintess Is In Love With Me

Zu An and the Saintess shared such a passionate kiss that they naturally landed on the bed. The last time they met, they had just promised themselves to each other before being abruptly torn apart. Now that they had reunited, they were like newlyweds. Passionate activities naturally ensued. 

When their bodies and souls became one, the two of them shed tears of joy… though a large part of the Saintess’ tears were out of pain. 

Zu An was taken aback to see that. “Why are you still…”

Xie Daoyun shyly explained, “In this world, I am Yumen Beiqing, the Fiend races’ Saintess.”

Zu An was stunned. “What about your original body?”

Xie Daoyun looked wistful. “I don’t know either. I don’t know how much time passed after I took that jade. I felt as if I was floating in chaos before abruptly waking up in this body. I don’t know if I can ever return to my original body.”

Zu An quickly consoled her, saying, “Don’t worry, Yumen Beiqing should just be your identity in this world. You should revert to your original body once you return to your original era.”

In truth, he wasn’t confident about that. What if Xie Daoyun’s return to the future caused the Saintess’ body to disappear, and her original body didn’t return? Wouldn’t that cause her soul to dissipate?

But I should have regained my position as the underworld’s master by then. Even if she’s only left with her soul, I should be able to find a way to save her. 

Xie Daoyun meekly said, “Big brother Zu, let’s not talk about unhappy things. I’m already very happy to be able to see you again.”

It was only upon seeing her reddened face that Zu An was reminded of what they were in the midst of. Still, looking at her familiar yet foreign face felt rather weird to him. “Somehow, it feels as if I'm getting intimate with someone else.”

Xie Daoyun’s face reddened. “But it’s still me. Besides… After realizing that, you seem even more excited.” She was so embarrassed that she averted her eyes. Saying such words was already a lot for her. 

Zu An felt awkward. They were in such an intimate position that there was no way to disguise his physical reactions. Looking at her adorable actions and her ethereal aura as a Saintess, he couldn’t resist the urge to kiss her once more.

Xie Daoyun moaned as she tightly hugged her lover’s neck, channeling her yearning to alleviate her pain. Slowly, the pain dissipated into tender sweetness. 

… 

Some time later, Xie Daoyun crawled up from Zu An’s chest. She looked flustered. “Ah, I forgot there are Fiend race officials waiting outside. This won’t do. I need to talk to them, or else they might do something out of recklessness.”

Her unique disposition, which was an amalgamation of purity and seductiveness, made Zu An reluctant to let her go, but he knew that they had spent too much time in the room. Thus, he got up and dressed himself. “Shall we pretend not to know each other? Otherwise it will be hard to…”

A finger pressed against his lips before he could finish speaking. “No, I don’t want you to suffer any grievances. I want everyone to know about our relationship.”

Xie Daoyun's face reflected decisiveness and courage like never before. This was the leadership she had honed over the years as the Saintess, leading the Fiend races against the monsters. 

Zu An chuckled. Am I in a ‘Domineering CEO dotes on her little husband’ script?

He found it interesting, and he wasn’t averse to it either. He followed her out and heard her blissfully introduce him as her man. 

A brief, unnerving silence ensued before an uproar exploded. Many people looked desperate. 

“Please think thrice, Saintess!”

“Saintess, did he coerce you into this?”

“Just give us a nod, and we’ll slaughter him!”

… 

Countless people were caught off guard. 

The beautiful Saintess commanded incomparable prestige in the Fiend races. She was beautiful, gentle, strong, and a good leader. There was no one in the Fiend races who didn’t like her. Outstanding youths from distinguished backgrounds had confessed to her but were all rejected. They'd been told that her mind was so devoted to fending against the monsters, that she didn’t have the energy to think about romance. 

That further elevated the crowd’s respect for her. From that moment on, they had seen the Saintess with an air of divinity. The Saintess had rejected many princes and young masters from the Fiend races’ upper echelons, but no one saw a problem because they were all rejected equally. If anything, that made them respect her even more. Since then, anyone who dared court the Saintess had become a public enemy. Even though the Saintess’ popularity had shot up, more people courted the Peacock Princess instead. 

That was why they were horrified to see the Saintess hugging another man and saying those words. Their first thought was that the Saintess had been coerced into doing the other party’s bidding. 

Looking at the hundreds of thousands of Rage points surging in, Zu An was awed by the Saintess’ incredible popularity. 

The Saintess was startled to see the crowd up in arms. She anxiously explained, “I am not being coerced. He truly is my lover!”

When she confirmed her stance, the sound of breaking hearts was almost audible. 

Many noticed the Saintess’ flushed face and recalled how intimate they had been when they walked out of the courtyard. Their minds couldn’t help but imagine what happened inside the room. 

Our sacred, flawless Saintess has just been violated by a man. Crap, an image is forming in my head. No, shut it down! Shut it dooooown!

Everyone, including the Peacock Princess, looked as if they were on the verge of a mental breakdown. 

She recalled how she had been telling the crowd about how Zu An had dove into the monster army and wrecked them like a berserker. 

Little did I know he was diving into the Saintess and wrecking her in the meantime. 

The thought of that nearly drove her mad. 

Isn’t big brother from the future? Why is he getting together with the Saintess? He didn’t even sway when big sis queen courted him! 

Even though the Saintess boasted incredible popularity among the Fiend races, the only one whose femininity the Peacock Princess admired was the Mermaid Queen. The Saintess felt more like a pillar of support. She was gorgeous, but her air of inviolability made one forget that she was a woman. 

Why would big brother get together with the Saintess on their first meeting? Was he lying to us? Was he actually not from the future?

Many people began questioning the Saintess about what was going on.

The Saintess gestured for the crowd to calm down before gently explaining, “We are already engaged; it is just that we were separated due to various reasons. I’m glad the world has brought us back together.” She looked at Zu An with gentle eyes. 

Zu An held her hand. “Indeed, we are a couple. I’m thankful to fate for our reunion.”

Looking at the lovey-dovey couple, the Peacock Princess suddenly felt a surge of anger. Saintess, you’re being unfair!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2487: I’Ll Quit Being A Saintess
chapters.body:
Chapter 2487: I’Ll Quit Being A Saintess

Unable to take it anymore, the Peacock Princess protested, “Big brother, how can the two of you be lovers? Aren’t you…”

Thankfully, she realized it would be unwise to reveal the secret that he was from the future, causing her words to trail off right there. Her face slowly turned bright red. 

The others thought that she was just agitated because she had a crush on Zu An. But she wasn’t the only one. Nearly all of the Fiend youths gathered here were agitated. They'd thought a strong ally had arrived to help them defeat the monsters, but the first thing he'd done upon arriving was to snatch away their respected Saintess!

They had only been able to come to terms with the Saintess’ rejection because she did the same for everyone. But now that she was cuddling against the chest of another man, they suddenly felt jealous like never before. 

To think that we were still so deeply in love with the Saintess!

The looks of hostility coming from the surroundings left Zu An with a headache. He decided to start with the Peacock Princess’ question. “Princess, we are indeed a couple. It is a long story. I’ll share it with you in private next time.” 

The Peacock Princess pouted. She still felt terrible, but Zu An’s consideration of her feelings alleviated her anger a little. 

The other Fiend race youths sneered. Is he trying to two-time now? Is snatching our Saintess away not enough for you, and you want the Peacock Princess too?

The Golden Peng Crown Prince reached the limit of his patience and sneered, “I don’t know how you duped our Saintess, but I shall expose you today!”

The Saintess was acting out of character today. It was completely unlike her usual pure, inviolable image. It felt as if she had suddenly plummeted to the secular world and was ravaged.

Did he use an aphrodisiac on the Saintess to take advantage of her? He just came from the Ocean races's side, and I've heard that the North Sea sea monster’s tentacle fluid is the world’s most potent aphrodisiac.

Just the thought of that made him feel like a knife had been plunged into his heart. 

I must rip this despicable man to shreds!

Many shared the same thought, as the disparity between the Saintess before and after meeting Zu An was too great! They suspected that Zu An had cast some kind of sorcery on the Saintess to bewitch her. 

A crowd followed the Golden Peng Crown Prince when he rushed over. This wasn't a duel. This matter concerned the safety of the Saintess and the Fiend races. Pride and honor were secondary concerns. 

A large pair of golden wings emerged from the Golden Peng Crown Prince’s back. He flapped his wings, and the feathers flew toward Zu An like sharp swords. 

I was nearly intimidated just now, but the rumors about the great hero were likely the Saintess and the Peacock Princess exaggerating to bulk up their lover’s reputation. How can a human be that formidable? 

Today, I shall rip apart that con artist’s mask!

He even paid careful heed to the angle to avoid harming the Saintess. 

Other Fiend experts also attacked Zu An from different angles. They clearly had the same thoughts in mind too. 

Just then, a massive golden bell silhouette appeared out of nowhere, shrouding both Zu An and the Saintess. 

Dong!

The attacks on the bell produced a loud reverberation. The rebound caused the crowd’s heads to spin and their blood flow to go into disarray. 

The Golden Peng Crown Prince had it the worst. The feathers he had unleashed toward Zu An shattered upon contact with the bell, causing him to bleed. Then, the ensuing reverberation sent his blood flow into disarray, causing him to plummet from the sky. However, his companion from the Golden Peng race rushed forward to catch him, sparing him from further injuries. 

Zu An sneered at the toppled crowd. “I held back on the Saintess’ account. You won’t be as lucky next time.”

Those planning to attack Zu An immediately stopped what they were doing. His words weren’t intimidating, but the cold look in his eyes sent chills down their spines. For a moment there, they felt like they were facing a deity from heaven. 

The ones gathered here were all in the Fiend races’ upper echelons. Those who could reach such a high stature were anything but fools. Judging from the brief ‘clash’, it was apparent to them that Zu An was indeed unfathomably powerful.

The Third Prince was relieved. He had considered attacking Zu An too, but fortunately, the Golden Peng Crown Prince beat him to it, sparing him from humiliation. 

The Saintess finally snapped out of her daze and roared in anger, “What are you doing?!”

She pulled out a uniquely-designed talisman that crackled with lightning. Her usually gentle eyes were blazing with rage. 

Zu An recognized that as Xie Daoyun’s High Firmament Talisman and was heartened. She was gradually gaining control over these divine talismans thanks to the growth in her cultivation. 

The crowd was shocked to see the Saintess bringing out her strongest talismans. They anxiously tried to placate her. “Saintess, there’s no need to go this far!”

The Saintess had lost her temper. She looked at them with frosty eyes. “I kindly introduced my lover to you so that you could share the joys of our reunion, yet you seek to kill him?”

The Third Prince quickly stepped forward to mediate, saying, “Saintess, it’s a misunderstanding! We have never heard about your lover, so we thought he might have used underhanded methods to take advantage of you. It’s all a misunderstanding!”

“Do I look as if I was taken advantage of?” The Saintess regretted her actions a little. She was so happy to be reunited with Zu An that she'd let her feelings get ahead of herself. It had been a long time since anyone confessed to her, so she'd forgotten about that. 

Ah, it’s my fault. I wanted to introduce them to big brother Zu, but I ended up making a mess of things. 

“Of course not, of course not…” the Third Prince sheepishly replied.

The Saintess had rarely brought out these divine talismans, even in the battles against the monsters. It was unlikely that she would bring them out even if she was under someone else’s control. Besides, she looked earnestly angry. 

The Saintess coldly eyed the crowd. “After the years we have spent together, I thought we were a family, but I did not expect you to react to my reunion with my lover with such antagonism. I don’t need this Saintess position anymore.” She reached for the wreath representing her Saintess identity, looking as if she was going to toss it away. 

“You mustn’t, Saintess!” This time, the crowd was truly flustered. They tried to dissuade her. 

The Peacock Princess rushed over and grabbed her hand, saying, “Please calm down, big sister Saintess. They only acted recklessly out of concern. Now that you have proven that you are lovers, there's no way they would oppose your relationship with big brother.”

As she said those words, she looked at Zu An with eyes so aggrieved that it looked as if tears would fall from them. 

The others nodded in agreement. If they lost their Saintess, the Fiend races would be doomed. 

So be it if she has a man. She can have ten or a hundred men if she wants. We won’t say a thing about it anymore.

The Saintess looked at Zu An and the Peacock Princess with a perplexed frown. She sent a telepathic message to Zu An, asking, “Big brother Zu, don’t you think she resembles Kong Nanwu?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2488: A Shot At Victory
chapters.body:
Chapter 2488: A Shot At Victory

Zu An took a look at the Peacock Princess. If not for her young age, she would have looked as if she was carved from the same mold as Kong Nanwu.

The Saintess asked with a peculiar voice, “You couldn’t have stolen her heart within such a short time, could you?”

Zu An rolled his eyes. She’s still in the mood to joke even at a time like this. 

More people who hadn’t made a move earlier stepped forward to mediate as well. The Saintess heaved a sigh, wiped away the Peacock Princess’ tears, and said, “Little sister Qingwu, I’ll stay.”

The Peacock Princess’ face lit up in joy, though there was still a shadow on her face when she thought about Zu An and the Saintess’ relationship. 

After this farce, everyone’s complexions looked terrible. 

Soon, some members of the Fiend races’ upper echelons came out to scatter the crowd before respectfully inviting the Saintess and Zu An to their conference room for a discussion. This was an implicit acknowledgment of their relationship.

The Saintess led Zu An to the conference hall. She was still angry due to the earlier incident, so she dragged Zu An in and settled him down beside her. The Fiend races’ upper echelons exchanged looks, but in the end, they could only accept it with bitter smiles. 

A youth from the Tiger race stepped forward and bowed to Zu An. “I apologize for the misunderstandings we had. From the looks of it, you indeed defeated the Incestuous King and his army. We were ignorant.” 

The other race leaders also stepped forward to apologize. 

Their courtesy convinced Zu An to let the matter go. “We're all here to deal with the monsters. Given the high stakes involved, it’s important to be prudent.”

The Tiger youth laughed. “Thank you for your magnanimity.”

That significantly alleviated the tension in the air. 

Soon, the topic turned to the Ocean races. The Saintess turned to Zu An and asked, “Based on the intelligence we received, you were the one who convinced the Ocean races to abandon the monsters and ally with us, right?”

“The monsters harbor malicious intent, and there's no lack of wise people among the Ocean races. They understand the importance of huddling in the face of winter,” Zu An replied with a smile. “The Ocean races are currently dealing with a poisoning incident. Once they're ready, they should send an envoy over for further talks about an alliance.”

“I heard the poison was widespread,” the Saintess said.

Worry painted the other faces in the room. They would have less manpower to oppose the monsters if their future ally suffered a heavy loss here. 

“Rest assured, big brother has found the antidote, and the Mermaid Queen has transported it back to the Ocean races. They will overcome this crisis.” The Peacock Princess thought about the time they'd spent in search of the antidote, and a smile formed on her lips.

The others cheered in delight as they looked at Zu An with gratitude. 

The Saintess shot a contemplative look at the Peacock Princess. Her face lights up whenever she talks about big brother Zu. She's already fallen for him. Haa, it would be hard for a young lady like her to resist big brother Zu’s charms. It had been the same for her too. Thinking about the past made her feel wistful. 

The Third Prince spoke up. “Still, the Ocean races suffered a huge blow. It will take some time before they regain their fighting prowess. Our circumstances still aren’t optimistic.”

The Peacock Princess pointed out, “Big brother recently severely injured the Incestuous King and decimated a monster army.”

The Saintess narrowed her eyes at the mention of the Incestuous King. She had met Mojard in the future, after all. 

The Third Prince calmly explained, “Mojard is the third most powerful person in the Monster World, but the one calling the shots is the second-in-command, the Monster Lord. Mojard and the Monster Lord have been on bad terms, and there is significant infighting between the two of them. Their rivalrous relationship has benefited us over the years, securing us some victories.”

The crowd looked to be in low spirits. The Fiend races were usually proud of themselves, but years of war with the monsters had shattered their pride. They had to admit that the victories they'd scored weren't clean, and they understood what the Third Prince was driving at. 

The fall of the Incestuous King only made the monsters a greater threat, as the Monster Lord could now devote his effort to dealing with them. The crowd even felt angry at Zu An for shattering the fragile balance, causing their circumstances to worsen.

The Saintess could tell that the Third Prince was trying to lead the crowd on, and her face turned cold. “Third Prince, I disagree with your statements. Big brother Zu has eliminated an elite monster army. As numerous as the monsters are, this is still a huge blow to them. Also, you only see the cons but not the pros behind this matter.”

“If I may ask, Saintess, what benefits are there to this?” the Third Prince sneered. 

This woman has let her emotions get to her head. It’ll damage her reputation if she can’t justify herself here. This is a good opportunity for me to regain the authority the imperial family has lost and become the new Fiend Emperor. 

Due to his respect for the Saintess, he had never harbored such thoughts before today, but seeing the goddess he revered hugging another man with a flushed face had angered and hurt him deeply. 

Since the Saintess doesn’t know what it means to have self-respect, I shall take her place then!

He'd liked the Saintess because of the latter’s inviolable halo, but now that she had become another man’s spoil of war, his interest waned. He wasn’t interested in getting together with someone else’s wife. In fact, he was extremely hygienic about that. He only liked the purest woman in the world.

Zu An sent a telepathic message to the Saintess with a frown. “Little sister Ling’er, that fellow harbors bad intentions. Shall I just get rid of him?”

The Saintess shook her head and replied telepathically, “The Third Prince represents the imperial family. If you make a move against him, the harmony within the Fiend races will collapse. At a time like this, the Fiend races can’t afford such a loss. Don’t worry, big brother Zu. I know what I’m doing.”

She cleared her throat and addressed the race leaders. “The Monster Lord has always been ambitious. He dared not run rampant due to the Incestuous King keeping him in check, but now that the Incestuous King has fallen, those shackles have loosened. What do you think his next move would be?”

Zu An smiled. He could guess where she was driving at. It hasn’t been long since we parted ways, but little sister Ling’er has grown a lot. 

“Of course it’s to rally his army to eradicate…” The Third Prince suddenly froze halfway through his words as a thought surfaced in his mind. 

Some of the smarter ones in the conference hall also fell into contemplation. 

The Saintess slowly added, “Think about it. The monsters have occupied our most prosperous lands. Would they still be interested in these barren lands? To the Monster Lord, even though we have launched a couple of successful retaliatory attacks, the war is already over. We can’t turn the tables around anymore. He can destroy us whenever he wants. But there is a much more pressing problem than us.”

“What is it?” someone anxiously asked.

“The Lord of Slaughter!” the Saintess slowly enunciated a name.

As they remembered the horrors associated with that name, the conference hall fell silent. 

The Saintess finally continued, “While the Lord of Slaughter was away, the Monster Lord moved to seize authority within the monster ranks. The one who can curb him, the Incestuous King, is out of the picture. The only obstacle left in his way of becoming the true Monster Lord is the Lord of Slaughter.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2489: Clear My Mind
chapters.body:
Chapter 2489: Clear My Mind

Upon hearing the Saintess’ words, the quiet conference hall suddenly buzzed into life. Sorrow vanished from their faces, replaced with excitement. 

“That’s true. Why didn’t I think of that?”

“As expected of the Saintess. You are able to see through everything!”

“Under the Saintess’ leadership, it is only a matter of time before we chase the monsters out of our world!”

… 

No one thought that the Saintess was under Zu An’s control anymore. She was still as wise and astute as ever, seeing things that others overlooked.

The Third Prince was taken aback too, but he pressed on. “Still, there is a possibility that the Monster Lord might first target us to further build his reputation among the monsters. That would give him more leverage against the Lord of Slaughter.”

The crowd frowned. It was evident that the Third Prince was trying to undermine the Saintess, but they couldn’t deny the possibility of what he had just mentioned.

Zu An finally spoke up. He couldn’t let Xie Daoyun shoulder the pressure alone. “There is such a possibility, so I’ll convince the Monster Lord to go for the other option.”

The Third Prince harrumphed. “The Monster Lord is formidable and skilled at psychological warfare. He wouldn’t change his mind over you.”

“It’s impossible for you, but it’s not too hard for me,” Zu An calmly said.

“You!!!” The Third Prince was infuriated. “Do you dare to issue a military ultimatum?”

You have trolled the Third Prince for +444… +444… +444…

The Saintess frowned. “He’s a guest who came to help us. Third Prince, aren’t you going too far by demanding a military ultimatum from him?”

The Third Prince snorted, “Since he said that it’s not difficult for him, why don’t we strike a deal? If he pulls it off, we’ll let him marry our Saintess. Otherwise, our Fiend races would probably find it hard to accept this marriage.”

The Golden Peng Crown Prince quickly voiced his agreement. “The Third Prince is right.”

Anger flared in the Saintess’ eyes. Zu An also spoke up, saying, “Third Prince, you've gotten something wrong. The Saintess and I are mutually in love, and our relationship doesn’t need anyone’s recognition. Your agreement means nothing to me, not to mention you aren’t qualified to accept my betrothal gifts either.”

The Third Prince sneered, “I must question how much you love the Saintess if you can’t even do this much for her.”

“Indeed!” Almost half of the people in the conference hall voiced their support for the Third Prince.

The Saintess’ face darkened. “Third Prince, you have no business in my private life, much less the right to order my man around.”

The Third Prince sighed. “Saintess, don’t let your emotions get the better of you. You are our Fiend races’ Saintess. Your business is our business.”

“Looks like I haven’t made myself clear.” The Saintess stood up. “If that’s the case, you can be the Saintess instead.”

Those words alarmed the other race leaders. They quickly stood up to placate the Saintess. Some said that the Third Prince had gone too far, whereas others said that the Third Prince was speaking from a good place and it wasn’t a bad thing to have Zu An prove himself. 

That further frustrated the Saintess. She was about to speak when Zu An said, “Sure, I can go to the Monster Lord and convince him to go against the Lord of Slaughter.”

The Third Prince and the Golden Peng Crown Prince exchanged looks and saw the smirks on each other’s faces.

The Golden Peng Crown Prince was impressed by the Third Prince’s methods. 

The Third Prince played him like a fiddle. The Monster Lord is not to be trifled with. No matter how strong that fellow is, it’s unlikely that he’ll survive the journey. 

And if he succeeds, it will benefit the Fiend races. Either way, there's nothing to lose. 

I am enlightened. No matter how strong a person is, if he doesn’t have a good head, he is nothing more than a tool. I mustn’t be that reckless in the future. I should learn more from the Third Prince. 

While the two of them were feeling gleeful, Zu An continued, “But I’m feeling very irked right now. I need to clear my head first before doing this.”

Someone instinctively asked, “How do you intend to clear your head…”

There was a sudden blur before that sentence could be finished. Zu An seemed to have moved, yet he was where he was when everyone turned to look at him. 

Zu An wiped off the blood on his fingers as he replied, “My mind is clear now.”

“You…” The Third Prince pointed at him in horror. Blood seeped from his mouth, preventing him from speaking more. Just like that, he collapsed to the ground. 

Someone nearby quickly checked on him, only to see that he had fainted. His body was limp, and there wasn't the slightest trace of ki to be felt from him. All his meridians had been severed. 

An uproar broke out in the conference hall. This fellow crippled the Third Prince just like that?

The Saintess was taken aback too, but she quickly pulled herself together and calmly declared, “The Third Prince has been attempting to drive a wedge here, weakening the forces we have against the monsters. We need to have a stable foundation before we can fight the monsters. This is also my will.”

Seeing her take responsibility for this matter evoked a laugh from Zu An. Xie Daoyun had clearly warned him earlier, but he'd still gone ahead to eliminate the Third Prince. He knew that this could result in dire consequences, but the Third Prince had been looking at him with resentful eyes, and it looked as if he'd harbored thoughts about the Saintess too. Rather than leaving a ticking time bomb, Zu An preferred to make a decisive move and contain the threat.

“What do you mean by this, Saintess?” Horrified, those who were close with the Third Prince shot to their feet and surrounded her. 

The Saintess calmly explained, “At this juncture, infighting mustn’t break out in the Fiend races. I’d rather deal with him on the spot than to let him fracture us.”

“You crippled the Third Prince just because he questioned you? Are you trying to become a dictator?” The Golden Peng Crown Prince was infuriated too. The person he was just admiring a second ago had fallen just like that. 

The Saintess had a headache too, but she didn’t blame Zu An for it. The Third Prince had indeed been a threat. Eliminating him was a solution too, not to mention the two of them didn’t have too much time to squander here. 

Zu An stepped forward and said, “Crown Prince, since you're on such close terms with the Third Prince, would you like to follow in his footsteps?” 

The Golden Peng Crown Prince hurriedly retreated in fear while waving his hands. “No no no, it’s a misunderstanding…”

Upon seeing the Golden Peng Crown Prince’s behavior, the agitated crowd slowly calmed down. 

The Third Prince was an expert too, yet he had been crippled before anyone could register what was going on. This showed just how powerful Zu An was. Only a fool would criticize Zu An now. 

After a long silence, an older person stood up and said, “The Third Prince indeed erred, but the punishment seems overboard…”

The crowd exchanged looks. They noticed his words were now much more subdued. 

Zu An calmly replied, “He offended me time and time again. It’s only on the Saintess and the Peacock Princess’ account that I spared his life.”

The crowd belatedly remembered that this was a world where the strong got their way. It was only normal for an expert who could destroy the Incestuous King and his monster army to be offended when challenged by the likes of the Third Prince. If he had been this arrogant in front of the Incestuous King, the Third Prince would have died a worse death, let alone someone who defeated the Incestuous King. He only had himself to blame for foolishly denying the rumors and underestimating the man he was dealing with.

It was a pragmatic world. No one would oppose a powerful expert for a crippled prince, especially not when the prince had it coming. On top of that, the Third Prince’s enemies and the Saintess’ supporters stepped forward to criticize the Third Prince and affirm the Saintess’ decision. With that, the remaining people didn’t dare to say anything. 

Zu An had gone through enough to know how the crowd would react. “Don’t worry. Now that I've cleared my mind, I'll help you deal with the Monster Lord on the Saintess’ account.”

The crowd was delighted to hear that. “We’ll be counting on you. Thank you, Saintess.”

Sacrificing a Third Prince to resolve an imminent threat sounded like an excellent deal. 

The Saintess began to deal with the aftermath. The Third Prince had been crippled, but his supporters remained. These supporters needed to be controlled right away, lest they rally together to cause trouble. 

Zu An nodded in approval. Xie Daoyun was previously a sheltered young miss, but her methods were now on par with the Empress and Bi Linglong. We might have only parted for a short time, but it looks like you've spent a long time here and grown a lot. 

He was waiting for the Saintess to be done so that he could ask her about the Fiend races’ power insignia, when the Peacock Princess went up to him and asked with reddened eyes, “Big brother, were you lying to me?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2490: Whereabouts Of The Power Insignia
chapters.body:
Chapter 2490: Whereabouts Of The Power Insignia

Zu An shook his head. “I didn’t lie to you.”

“If you didn’t lie to me, why would the Saintess say that you’re her lover? Aren’t you from the future?” The Peacock Princess’ tears began falling. He’s still lying to me even at a time like this.

A few people noticed their interactions and frowned. 

Look at how sorrowful the Peacock Princess is. She must have truly liked him. 

That vile man! To think that the Saintess isn’t enough for him; he still wants the Peacock Princess on top of that!

Even so, after what had just happened, the crowd was intimidated by Zu An. No one dared to stand up for the Peacock Princess. 

Zu An was distressed. It was hard to explain the details to the Peacock Princess. His silence only made her cry even more sorrowfully. 

Just then, a gentle voice spoke up. “Qingwu, he didn’t lie to you. I am also from the future.”

The Saintess was already beside the two of them, and she'd constructed a noise isolation barrier around them before speaking. 

“What?” The Peacock Princess was so startled that she stopped crying. She stared at the beautiful Saintess with shocked eyes. However, she soon shook her head and said, “That’s impossible. You’re Yumen Beiqing, the Yumen’s big miss! How could you be from the future?”

“I don’t know the details myself, and I have no way of explaining it.” The Saintess grabbed the Peacock Princess’ hand. “But please trust us. We have no reason to deceive you or everyone else.”

She regretted her reckless decision to flaunt her lover, as she hadn’t expected such a huge mess. She was so happy to be reunited with Zu An that she hadn’t thought things through. 

The Peacock Princess thought about everything that had happened in recent years. 

The Saintess did seem to have appeared out of nowhere and achieved miraculous feats one after another. That’s very similar to big brother’s appearance. No wonder she’s so outstanding. She’s from the same place as big brother.

She looked at Zu An with a wretched smile. “Big brother, is the Saintess the little sister you want to save?”

“Little sister?” The Saintess pricked up her ears.

Zu An shook his head. “It’s someone else.” He turned to the Saintess and explained, “Xiaoxi has the innate Cruel Poison Constitution. The poison just flared up, and I’m looking for a solution for her.”

“Ah? Is Xiaoxi fine?” The Saintess was taken aback. Anyone who had spent time with Ji Xiaoxi would naturally become fond of that adorable girl.

“She’s fine for the time being, and I have some clues as to how I can solve her problem. You don’t need to be too worried.” Upon seeing how worried she was, Zu An consoled her. 

After hearing their conversation, the Peacock Princess had no choice but to concede that the two of them were from the future. She turned to the Saintess and asked, “If you’re from the future, why did you treat me differently all these years?”

She'd initially thought that the Saintess treated her exceptionally well because they got along well. Due to that, they'd become close friends who shared everything with each other. It was only now that she realized things weren’t that simple. Why would someone from the future care so much about her?

The Saintess hesitated before replying, “In the future, I came to know a know a big sister who greatly resembles you, which is why I felt a sense of intimacy with you…”

The Peacock Princess recalled what Zu An told her when they first met, and realization dawned on her. “All this while, I was just someone else’s substitute. No wonder you all treat me so well. It was all my one-sided feelings…” She fled the scene in a fluster, looking forlorn.

The Saintess wanted to chase after her, but Zu An grabbed her and advised, “Give her time to cool down.”

The Saintess bit her lips and chose to give the Peacock Princess some space. She knew there wasn’t much she could say even if she caught up with the latter. She could only pray that time would heal everything. However, after everything that had just happened, she didn’t want to be in the open with Zu An anymore, so she brought Zu An back to her room. 

“Big brother Zu, I haven’t had time to ask you why you are here. Is it to save Xiaoxi?”

“That’s one of the reasons…” Zu An told her everything that had happened since he entered this spacetime fragment. “I’m overjoyed to be able to meet you here.”

“There’s a saying that destiny will eventually bring two fated people together, no matter how far they are,” the Saintess replied with a furious blush. Her lover traveling through time and finding her felt like fate to her. 

Zu An grabbed her hand, and the two of them smiled. Suddenly, he remembered his reason for coming here and asked, “Speaking of which, do you know what the Fiend races’ power insignia is?”

“The Fiend races’ power insignia? Why do you ask that?” The Saintess was confused, as she struggled to understand what a ‘power insignia’ was.

Zu An summarized the chain of events that had transpired in the underworld and his hunt for the Six Paths’ power insignias. 

“I see.” The Saintess nodded in realization. “It should be the Fiend Emperor’s scepter. That's the Fiend races’ symbol of power.”

“Scepter…” Zu An fell into deep thought.

The Saintess added, “The Fiend races had a legendary relic known as the Sun Slaying Bow, but it disappeared a long time ago. No one knows where it went, so they forged a scepter and used it as a power insignia instead.” At this point, she suddenly smiled. “If the Fiend races learn that you have the Sun Slaying Bow, they might just serve you as the chosen one.”

Zu An laughed. “I have no interest in staying with this era’s Fiend races.”

The Saintess’ eyebrows shot up. “Of course, I’m not surprised you miss the future Fiend races more. You have the Medusa Queen, the Empress, the Elf Princess, and so on. Actually, this world also has empresses, medusas, and elves too. Shall I help you find them?”

“Have mercy on me. I already have enough on my hands,” Zu An replied with a bitter smile.

“What can we do when big brother Zu is so charming? You stole the Peacock Princess’ heart away so quickly,” the Saintess said with a sigh.

Zu An was speechless. It was such an awkward topic that he quickly changed the subject. “Speaking of which, do you know where the Celestial World is?”

“The Celestial World?” The Saintess frowned. “Why do you think I’d know that, big brother Zu?”

“The current Fiend races are the descendants of the Celestial Court’s Fiend races,” Zu An said, then told her what he knew. 

The Saintess had previously heard the True Demon race claim that their ancestors were the Celestial Court’s Fiend races. “So it turns out those True Demons are just the Celestial Court’s Fiend races’ servants and pets. We were nearly duped by them, though I reserved my doubts toward their claims after what I saw in recent years.”

“Ah, I get it now!” The Saintess’ eyes lit up. “I was wondering why we suddenly fared so well with the monsters, and why the Lord of Slaughter suddenly disappeared from the battlefield. The Lord of Slaughter must have been studying the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb! If there is a way to reach the Celestial World, it has to be the Imperial Tomb! The Lord of Slaughter must be searching for the Celestial Court!”

Zu An nodded. That was what he thought too. As the Fiend races’ Saintess who boasted a deeper understanding of this era’s Fiend races and monsters, if Xie Daoyun had reached the same conclusion too, it would give greater credence to his conjecture. 

“Looks like I’ll have to make a trip to the Imperial Tomb…” Zu An said. “Do you know where the Fiend Emperor’s scepter is?”

The Saintess shook her head. “After his death, the scepter disappeared along with the Fiend Emperor. I don’t know where it is, but it has to be with the monsters.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2491: Disappearance
chapters.body:
Chapter 2491: Disappearance

“What makes you so certain?” Zu An was intrigued. 

“The Fiend Emperor always brought the scepter with him. The day he died, he self-detonated in the monsters’ camp to bring down as many monsters as he could. The scepter should have fallen into the monsters’ hands then,” the Saintess explained. “The Fiend races can sense the scepter. We would have known if it was with a fiend, but the scepter remains missing even after so much time has passed. So, it's most likely with the monsters. However, I don’t know whether it’s with the Monster Lord, the Lord of Slaughter, or the other monsters.”

Zu An nodded. It was fine as long as the scepter really was with the monsters. He was going to visit the Monster Lord anyway, so he could take a look around while he was there. 

The Saintess suddenly asked, “Big brother Zu, you previously mentioned that you came to this spacetime fragment with a few other big sisters. Where are they?”

“I was about to ask you.” Zu An looked grim. “Have you not seen Big Manman?”

“Big sister Pei?” The Saintess was taken aback. “I haven’t. What makes you ask that?”

Zu An’s heart sank. “We split up to deal with different matters. Jiang Luofu and Yun Yuqing took Xiaoxi to search for the Brilliant Pinflower. Suolun Shi stayed behind to guard the future Mojard, Salamay, and the others. Big Manman’s mission was to contact the Fiend races’ surviving army. Back then, she mentioned that she intended to reach out to you, the Saintess.”  He realized that Xie Daoyun would have recognized Pei Mianman if they met and would have told him about it by now. 

The Saintess was stunned. She asked him about the time and location of where they'd parted. Doing a rough calculation, Pei Mianman should have long since arrived here. She wondered, “Did something happen to big sister Pei?”

Zu An shook his head. “Manman is quite strong now, and she’s carrying many protection artifacts. She should have no trouble protecting herself. She might have been tied down by other matters along the way.” Despite saying so, he was still worried.

“Don’t worry, big brother Zu. I’ll send my men to look into it.” The Saintess called her subordinates in and ordered, “Send some men to search the vicinity. I’m looking for a human woman with the family name of ‘Pei’. Miss Pei is extremely beautiful, and…” She almost blurted out that Pei Mianman was big, but that would have been distasteful, so she said, “She leaves a strong impression. Anyone who has seen her would remember her.”

“Yes, Saintess!” The subordinate rushed over to relay her order.

The Saintess turned to Zu An and gently consoled him, “Don’t worry, big brother Zu. I’m sure big sister Pei is fine.”

Zu An nodded, though he remained nervous. He wasn't in the mood to set off and visit the Monster Lord anymore. 

Fortunately, the subordinate swiftly returned with updates. “Saintess, there are sightings of a woman who fits your description in a small town 150 kilometers southwest. She was looking into you.”

“Where is she?” Zu An hurriedly asked.

The subordinate shook his head. “I’m afraid I don’t know. She should have been heading in our direction, but no one saw her afterward.”

Zu An frowned. While 150 kilometers would be a long distance for mortals, Pei Mianman could cover it within a short time. What could be preventing her from coming here?

“Don’t worry, big brother Zu. I’ll send someone to investigate it. There’s no way a living human could disappear into thin air. It’s only a matter of time before we find her whereabouts,” the Saintess consoled him.

Zu An shook his head. “It’s fine. I’ll investigate it myself.” He asked about the town’s location before rushing in that direction. 

The Saintess rushed out as well. “Big brother Zu, I’ll go with you.”

Zu An noticed the fiends following below them and said, “You’re currently an important person in the Fiend races. Your abrupt departure could have ripple effects.”
