“The town isn’t too far away. It should be fine for me to take a small trip there. Still, I should give them a heads up. Big brother Zu, can you wait for me?” the Saintess asked apologetically.

Zu An nodded. As anxious as he was, he could afford to wait a bit. He had clashed with the Fiend races earlier in the day, so they could misunderstand if he suddenly left with the Saintess without giving them a heads up. If the unity among the Fiend races unraveled at this juncture, it would become exceedingly hard to deal with the monsters. 

The Saintess quickly descended to explain the situation to the panicking crowd. Shortly after, she returned to Zu An’s side and said, “Let’s go, big brother Zu.”

“Thank you, little sister Ling’er,” Zu An said.

“Big brother Zu, you’re being distant. I'm worried about big sister Pei too, and she disappeared in my territory. It’s only right for me to do my part,” the Saintess said. 

Looking at ethereal moonlight reflecting off the Saintess’ face, Zu An could understand why the Fiend races revered her. There is something divine about her.

Zu An grabbed her hand and rushed toward the town. With his traveling speed, it didn’t take long before they reached their destination.

The town was small but prosperous, possibly because it was en route to the Fiend races’ main camp. It was already nightfall, but there was still a number of people on the streets. They came from different races, and their occupations ranged from merchants to cultivators, adventurers, and treasure hunters. 

Zu An frowned. This place feels chaotic. It won’t be easy to investigate. 

They made their way to the town’s largest inn. Zu An headed straight for the manager and asked about Pei Mianman’s whereabouts.

The manager shook his head. “How could I remember every guest when we have so many people coming in each day?”

Zu An detected dishonesty in his words, and his face darkened. He was about to exert his methods when the Saintess took off her veil and gently said, “Manager, please give it some thought. Miss Pei is very important to us.”

When she took off her veil, the dimly-lit hall visibly brightened up. Her beautiful and pure face surprised the manager, prompting him to point a trembling finger at her. “Y-You are…”

The other patrons recognized her and exclaimed in delight, “It’s the Saintess! It’s the Saintess!”

A huge commotion followed. Everyone rushed over and surrounded Zu An and the Saintess, but at the same time, they kept a meter away from her, as if they were worried about sullying her. It wasn't Zu An’s first time witnessing this, but he was still awed by the Saintess’ high standing in the Fiend races. 

The Saintess nodded. “I came here because a friend of mine went missing. Have any of you seen her?” She proceeded to describe Pei Mianman’s traits. 

Zu An was worried about not being able to find clues, but to his surprise, many people raised their hands and exclaimed, “I’ve seen her! I’ve seen her!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2492: Overlooked
chapters.body:
Chapter 2492: Overlooked

Zu An and the Saintess exchanged looks. The Saintess pointed to a person and asked, “May I know where you met her?”

“I met her outside town. She was about to enter the town when she encountered a foreign adventurer party,” the person said, feeling honored to be selected by the Saintess. “That adventurer party was mesmerized by her beauty and stepped forward to tease her, but that woman made quick work of them.” Fear flickered across his eyes as he recounted the chain of events.

Zu An asked about the details of the battle. The person replied with a pale face, “She unleashed a black flame. It was terrifying. The adventurers came into contact with it and couldn’t put it out. They were burned to ashes.” 

Looks like it really is Manman. That’s her unique black flame. Those adventurers must have deserved death for her to go that far. 

“What happened next?”

“I fled because I dared not watch any further. I don’t know where she went next,” that person replied sheepishly. “Given how powerful she is, she should be fine.”

“I know where she went next. She entered the town and bought a stick of candied hawthorn,” someone else added.

Zu An smiled. Despite Manman’s mature appearance, she has a childish side too. 

“She went to the bridge and asked about the Saintess, but we didn’t tell her anything because we were wary about her unknown background.”

“That afternoon, she came to our inn and ordered a few dishes.”

“I saw her south of town…”

The sudden influx of clues felt suspicious, so Zu An asked, “Why do so many of you remember her?” 

Usually, one would have been lucky to stumble upon one or two of such clues in their investigation, but a huge bunch of them suddenly sprouted out here. Something felt amiss. 

Someone meekly replied, “That lady is so beautiful and… And…”

The Saintess’ face turned cold. “And what?”

Intimidated by the Saintess, the person quickly finished his sentence, “And big! No one could possibly forget about her!”

The Saintess and Zu An were speechless.

Considering Pei Mianman’s curvaceous figure, those words made perfect sense.

Through further questions, Zu An was able to map out Pei Mianman’s movements after entering the town.  By the same afternoon, she had already figured out where the Saintess and the Fiend races’ resistance army were. She'd left town and was about to head there when she suddenly received a letter and reversed course, traveling in the opposite direction instead. 

Zu An frowned. What could have been in the letter to make her change her route? More importantly, who sent that letter to Big Manman? She doesn’t have acquaintances in this timeline, so it doesn’t make sense for her to receive a letter. 

The Saintess suddenly spoke up. “Big brother Zu, what do you think big sister Pei saw that made her leave right away? Could someone have told her you were in danger?”

Zu An shook his head. “No, that wouldn’t work.”

He and Pei Mianman knew each other very well, and they had codes to verify each other’s identity too. It was unlikely for anyone to be able to deceive her about him. Besides, she knew how powerful he was. The odds of him encountering danger so quickly were slim. 

“I can only think of one possibility—she must have left because she found clues about the Brilliant Pinflower, Defying Spirit Tears, or the Aurora Poison Dragon,” Zu An said. Only when it came to the matter of rescuing Ji Xiaoxi would Pei Mianman stray from the plan.

“Ah!” the Saintess suddenly exclaimed. She noticed Zu An looking at her with perplexed eyes, so she explained, “I don’t know about the other two, but there are Brilliant Pinflowers growing near the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb.”

“The Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb?” Zu An was taken aback. “I was there in the future, but I saw no such flower.”

The Saintess gave the matter some thought before replying, “There are Brilliant Pinflowers in this world, but there are very few of them. Each generation’s Fiend Emperor cultivates them to offer them to heaven. If they're no longer there in the future, they must have gone extinct at some point. The existence of Brilliant Pinflowers is a secret, but there are fiends who know about it. Someone might have told big sister Pei about it while she was gathering information.”

Zu An nodded. That was the most plausible explanation. He said, “I’ll make a quick trip to the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb.”

He was worried about Pei Mianman. The Lord of Slaughter was at the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb; Pei Mianman could be in danger if she headed there recklessly. Besides, Ji Xiaoxi and the others were also looking into the Brilliant Pinflowers’ whereabouts. They could be on their way to the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb too. If so, they could reunite there.

The Saintess exclaimed, “I’ll go with you!”

Zu An advised her otherwise. “Little sister Ling’er, as the Fiend races’ Saintess, you have many responsibilities on your shoulders. The resistance army needs you. Chaos will break out if you arbitrarily leave your post.”

“But…” The Saintess knew he was right, but she couldn’t bear to part ways with him. 

Just then, a female elf scout descended from the sky and said, “Saintess, there’s an urgent report from the frontlines. We have detected anomalous movements from the monster army. Everyone is worried that the monsters are preparing for an attack, so they sent me here to invite you back to discuss this matter.” 

The Saintess was stunned. She looked at Zu An with a conflicted expression. “Big brother Zu…”

Zu An patted her shoulder. “Little sister Ling’er, you should return. I can settle things on my end. Besides, you'll be indirectly helping me by drawing the monster army’s attention.”

The Saintess nodded. “All right, I’ll return for now to make arrangements. I’ll find you as soon as I’m done. You must take care of yourself.”

Zu An was startled. “You mustn’t go to the Imperial Tomb alone. You’re probably at the top of the monster army’s bounty list now, and the Imperial Tomb is under the monsters’ control. You would be delivering yourself into the enemy’s clutches.” 

The Saintess revealed a sweet smile. “Rest assured, big brother Zu. I'm not a reckless person. The monsters won’t be able to capture me that easily.”

Zu An belatedly remembered that the Saintess had already fought with the monsters for many years now. His worry was unfounded. Thus, he stopped the nagging and instead stepped forth to embrace her. “Little sister Ling’er, I’ll take you back to our original time period once we settle everything in this spacetime fragment.”

The Saintess nodded. “I believe that you’ll be able to do it, big brother Zu.”

The two of them hugged for a long time.

“Little sister Qingwu will likely ask about your whereabouts when I return. Should I tell her?” the Saintess suddenly asked as they separated from each other’s arms.

Zu An shook his head. There’s no point in prolonging the pain. I should use this opportunity to make the Peacock Princess forget about me.

The Saintess nodded, knowing any relationship between those two couldn't bear fruit.

With that, they finally bade each other farewell and left. 

While leaving, the elf scout couldn’t help but glance at Zu An. She was curious about the man to whom the Saintess had given her heart. 

Zu An wondered if this female elf scout was related to Xue’er. Considering how elves had a long lifespan, she could be Qiao Xueying’s elder from three to four generations ago. However, he quickly reined in his thoughts. His priority at hand was to find Pei Mianman. 

A bone-chilling wind blew against him. He had a feeling he had overlooked something, but he couldn’t figure out what it was. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2493: Pit Them Against Each Other
chapters.body:
Chapter 2493: Pit Them Against Each Other

Something seemed to have surfaced in Zu An’s mind, but it slipped past his clutches, and no matter how he racked his brains, he simply couldn’t remember what it was anymore. Still, he knew there was no point panicking at a time like this; he could only wait for the epiphany to arrive once more later on. 

He headed off in the same direction as Pei Mianman when she left the town. Along the way, he tried gathering information about her, but it was to no avail. Pei Mianman’s appearance and distinctive physique were bound to leave a deep impression on anyone who saw her, similar to what had happened in the town. However, the monster invasion had left most areas desolate and barren. He didn’t meet a single person along the way, so there was no way to gather information. 

Quite some time had passed since Pei Mianman left the town, and there was no guarantee that she would drop by the other towns along the road to the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb. With that in mind, Zu An thought it would be best to first head to the imperial tomb to take a look.The intelligence he had received suggested that Pei Mianman had likely changed course because she received information about the Brilliant Pinflower. 

Earlier, he'd received intel from the Saintess that the monsters could be broadly divided into four groups. 

The first group was stationed in the imperial tomb with the Lord of Slaughter; it was likely the Lord of Slaughter’s army. The second group was under the Incestuous King; most of its troops were veterans, but the bulk of them had been obliterated from the face of the world. The third group consisted of powerful and influential military commanders who waged war and conquered new lands. The last group was the Monster Lord’s army; it was the strongest of the four groups, and it was tasked with the mission of conquering the Cultivation World. It had the power to mobilize other armies, except for the Lord of Slaughter’s army stationed at the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb. 

The Monster Lord’s army was stationed 100,000 kilometers away from the Fiend races’ resistance army, sandwiched between the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb and the resistance army. That would have been a long distance in Zu An’s previous world, but in this world full of cultivators, it was no big deal. 

Zu An decided to first pay a visit to the Monster Lord.

Even though he had once defeated the Monster Lord, it was largely because the other party’s cultivation had been suppressed by the world’s barrier, and Zu An had communed with the world using the World Law Beacon. In this ki-rich world, the monsters’ cultivation wouldn’t be suppressed, and he didn’t have the World Law Beacon here. Facing off against the Monster Lord would be dangerous to him. 

However, the strongest entity in this era was still the Lord of Slaughter, or else the Monster Lord would have long since revolted. Even in the future, despite the Lord of Slaughter having been suppressed for millennia, they were only able to vanquish him when Qiu Honglei carried out a Dance Offering to the Universe to summon an unfathomable being. She'd had to pay a heavy price for that. 

The Monster Lord wouldn’t be a match for the Lord of Slaughter now that the latter was at his peak. Zu An had to find a way to turn them against each other. 

The high concentration of ki in this world and his unique cultivation technique allowed Zu An to maintain his peak condition even when traveling without rest. Along the way, he occasionally entered the pendant’s cave paradise to visit Qiu Honglei and the Jing Teng sisters and tell them what was happening outside. 

Qiu Honglei was amazed when he told her that he had gone back thousands of years in time, but once she learned that he could have to face off against the terrifying Lord of Slaughter again, she quickly became nervous. 

Zu An embraced her tightly. “Don’t worry, Honglei. Things are different now. I won’t put myself in a desperate situation, and I won’t endanger anyone around me either.”

Qiu Honglei lay on his chest. “You've been through so much in recent days. With your capabilities, I’m sure you’ll be fine.”

While exchanging sweet nothings, Qiu Honglei slowly drifted off to sleep. Her complexion looked much better than before, though she remained weak from having just recovered. Even a short conversation could invoke her drowsiness. Zu An knew that it would be unwise to rush things; they would have to slowly condition her body with spirit medicine over time.

With Qiu Honglei and the Jing Teng sisters’ company, the journey wasn’t boring. Time passed in a flash, and Zu An soon arrived at the monster army’s camp. He calmly surveyed the barracks that stretched for thousands of kilometers. 

There are indeed a lot of monsters. The Fiend races’ troop count was one of the reasons they were so difficult to deal with in the future, and the monsters outnumber even that. On top of that, I can sense the auras of countless experts even from a distance away. I’ll be dead meat if they discover me.

Now that he didn’t have the World Law Beacon, Zu An didn’t dare to let his guard down. He first constructed a formation at the barracks’ outer perimeter before making his way in. He continued to construct dozens more teleportation formations to ensure his safety along the way.  

“Hold it right there. Who are you?” Two patrolling monster soldiers discovered him. 

Zu An brought out Daji, who immediately took control of the two monster soldiers with her Charming Voice. He eliminated the two monster soldiers, put on a set of their attire, and continued advancing into the barracks. As he walked, he began transforming into a monster soldier. 

Most military barracks were protected by defensive formations and sentry posts, making it difficult for a cultivator, no matter how powerful, to sneak in. However, Zu An, with his Myriad Transformations, could turn into a monster while concealing his aura. He kept changing his appearance along the way, making it nigh impossible for anyone to see through him. 

In a majestic tent deep in the barracks, the Monster Lord sat on a tall throne made out of white bones. A bizarre three-headed bird lay at his feet as a plump servant made a report to him.

“Master, the Ocean races have wiped out the Incestuous King and his army. No one can hold you back anymore. We should march eastward to destroy the Fiend races and make that Saintess or something your maid!” 

If Zu An were here, he would recognize the servant as the underground city’s Manager Pi. 

“Mojard has truly disappointed me! How could he and his elites be destroyed by a nobody? Others might think I’m of the same caliber as him!” the Monster Lord spat through gritted teeth.

“Master, how could Mojard be worthy of being your rival? He was only able to tie you down because of the one above,” another servant said. 

If Zu An were there, he would recognize this servant to be the War Priest. He was highly respected among the monsters, and he participated in most large-scale wars. That was why his downfall in the Cultivation World in the future was a huge shock to the Monster Lord, prompting the latter to send blood pool clones to invade the Cultivation World. 

The Monster Lord stopped the War Priest with a raised hand. “It’s not over yet. We should practice utmost prudence. Hmph! Now that Mojard is finally gone, I can go all out. Conquering the Fiend races and capturing their Saintess should grant me enough prestige to do it.” 

Just then, a voice echoed outside. “I disagree. Now is the best time to do it.”

“Who is it?!” The monsters in the tent were horrified. It could bring them endless trouble if outsiders were to learn about what they had said. 

The Monster Lord thrust his palm forth, unleashing a formidable force that decimated a section of the tent. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2494: Betrayal
chapters.body:
Chapter 2494: Betrayal

“There’s no need to be so angry, Monster Lord. I come in peace.” A figure slowly emerged from the hole in the tent.

Manager Pi and the War Priest narrowed their eyes. Even they would have struggled to deal with the Monster Lord’s earlier attack, but this fellow overcame it with such ease. This told them how powerful he was. 

Hm, he’s wearing our uniform. Whose subordinate is this? Why don’t we know about him if he possesses such strength?

They scanned the intruder from head to toe. The latter was astonishingly emaciated for a monster, and he wore a mask that covered his appearance. It was also impossible to figure out his background from his aura and cultivation technique. 

Needless to say, the intruder was Zu An. He was shocked when he saw the people inside the tent. He hadn’t expected them to be familiar faces. Manager Pi was one thing, but he hadn’t known that the War Priest served the Monster Lord as well. 

“I find it hard to believe that someone who refuses to reveal his identity bears no ill will.” The Monster Lord was alarmed, gazing sharply at Zu An. He hadn’t put his full strength into the earlier attack, such that a fair number of top-notch experts could have dealt with it, but he couldn’t think of anyone who could neutralize it so quietly. 

“Skin is superficial. I didn’t mean to deceive you, and I seek your understanding. I came here today to discuss your future.” Zu An had put on a mask because the Monster Lord didn’t recognize him in the future, so he figured he had to not reveal his true face now.

“Our master doesn’t need the likes of you to discuss his future. Men, capture this scoundrel!” Manager Pi roared. 

Monster guards rushed up and surrounded Zu An.

“Step down!” the Monster Lord suddenly roared. That surprised the guards, but they quickly bowed and backed off. 

Manager Pi didn’t look awkward despite having his order superseded. Instead, he exclaimed with a fawning smile, “Our master is wise and farsighted.” He then glared at Zu An. “Our master has given you a chance to talk! You better cherish it!”

The War Priest rolled his eyes. This bootlicker.

The Monster Lord, however, was satisfied with Manager Pi’s bootlicking. He turned to Zu An and said, “Tell me what your earlier words meant.”

Zu An first cast a glance at the crowd before saying, “What I’m about to say might be improper. Would it be fine to speak openly in front of them too?”

The Monster Lord could roughly guess what Zu An was going to say. “It’s fine. Go on.” He wasn't a patient man, but he thought that the intruder was strong enough to be worth listening to his words. 

Zu An calmly spoke, “The Incestuous King’s army has been destroyed, and the Lord of Slaughter has lost his most loyal subordinates. This is a good opportunity for you to take his position.”

The War Priest, Manager Pi, and the others turned to look at the Monster Lord. They were ready to kill the intruder on the spot as soon as the Monster Lord gave the signal. 

The Monster Lord coldly sneered, “What a crude way of sowing dissent. Did you think it would work?”

Zu An smiled. The Monster Lord’s decision to not kill him right away spoke volumes. “You misunderstand. It doesn’t matter whether my motive is to sow dissent or not. What's more important here is what is aligned with your interests.”

“I can guess your background,” the Monster Lord sneered. “We were just discussing the decimation of the Fiend races’ resistance army and capturing the Saintess when you suddenly appeared and made such a suggestion. You must be the Fiend races’ ally, though it baffles me why I have never met you before.”

Zu An didn’t deny it. “Yes, I have ties with the Fiend races’ Saintess. As a token of my sincerity, I can tell you that I was the one who defeated Incestuous King Mojard and his army.”

The War Priest, Manager Pi, and the Monster Lord straightened up in shock. “It’s you!”

“Indeed. That’s why we have a common enemy,” Zu An explained. “The Incestuous King is the Lord of Slaughter’s aide. If he learns about this matter, the Lord of Slaughter will not spare me. That’s why I wish to forge a partnership with you.” 

The Monster Lord sneered, “You’re mistaken. I am loyal to my master. Men…”

Zu An sighed. “The Lord of Slaughter has secluded himself in the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb to decipher its secrets, so he doesn’t know that you have usurped his power. If he hears about the Incestuous King’s death, he will investigate the matter and learn that you have removed his men from important positions and replaced them with your aides. How would he respond to that?”

The Monster Lord was startled. “My master is wise. He can distinguish between a normal reshuffling of manpower and an attempt to usurp power. I have never done anything disloyal. I shall take you down right now to avenge the Incestuous King!”

Terrifying pressure suddenly crashed down on the tent. The War Priest and Manager Pi, having received the Monster Lord’s signal, blocked Zu An’s path of retreat.

The Monster Lord rushed up to Zu An at an incredible speed and thrust his palm down on the latter's head, causing Zu An’s figure to shatter as if it was an illusion. 

“A teleportation formation?” the War Priest exclaimed in shock.

The Monster Lord’s complexion darkened. “It’s no ordinary teleportation formation. It bears some resemblance to our world’s technology.”

“Is he from the Monster World too?” the War Priest asked, but he couldn’t think of anyone who had those unique traits. 

Manager Pi stepped forward and volunteered himself. “Master, I’ll lead our men to capture him!”

The Monster Lord shook his head. “Don’t bother. Given his cultivation and aptitude in teleportation formations, you won’t be able to capture him.”

“Are we going to let him off just like that?” Manager Pi was indignant. 

The Monster Lord fell silent. 

The War Priest suddenly remarked, “To be honest, his proposal is an appealing one. Should we accept it first?”

The Monster Lord sneered, “You don’t even know his background. The Lord of Slaughter could have sent him here to test us. We can’t give ourselves away that easily. But he delivers a timely reminder. It looks like we’ll have to bring our plans forward.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2495: Girl
chapters.body:
Chapter 2495: Girl

Manager Pi was taken aback. “Master, you don’t intend to attack the Fiend races’ resistance army anymore?”

The Monster Lord shook his head. “His words make sense. The Fiend races barely have a breath left in them. We can clean them up afterward. That person poses a greater threat to us.”

Manager Pi had a fair complexion, but the Monster Lord’s words left him even paler. He found the Lord of Slaughter much more terrifying than the Fiend races’ remnant army. 

The War Priest looked worried. “Master, how confident are you in defeating that person?”

There was a prolonged silence before the Monster Lord replied, “If it’s a 1-on-1, my odds should be 40:60.” His true evaluation of his odds was closer to 30:70, but he inflated the number to inspire confidence in his aides.

The War Priest and Manager Pi were tactful enough not to ask who was 40 and who was 60. “Should we ally with that man and borrow his strength? That may further skew the odds in our favor.”

That mysterious man had left a deep impression on them. He'd easily neutralized the Monster Lord’s attack and safely escaped from their encirclement. His strength and wits were top-notch.

The Monster Lord shook his head. “We don’t know his motive. He could turn against us at a critical moment. We can’t use him.” 

“What should we do? Once that person learns about Mojard’s defeat and begins investigating, he will quickly learn that we have replaced all his old subordinates.” The War Priest was fearful. If this matter came to light, the cruel Lord of Slaughter would bring terrible fates upon them. 

The Monster Lord chuckled enigmatically. “Don’t worry. Sometimes, strength is not everything.”

… 

Light suddenly distorted in a remote area of the barracks, and Zu An appeared out of thin air. Unlike what the Monster Lord thought, he didn’t escape right away. Having previously interacted with the Monster Lord, he knew how cunning the latter could be. He knew that the other party wouldn’t trust him easily. Still, it was paramount to take that trip to bury a seed in the other party’s heart. When the time came, that seed would sprout.

He made his way to another tent. Judging from its prime location, flags, and elegant decor, the tent hinted at its master’s extraordinary position. Zu An hesitated at the tent’s entrance, but he still entered it in the end.  

The tent was guarded, though the security wasn't as tight as in the Monster Lord’s tent. There were still lapses in the defenses that Zu An could exploit. With his Myriad Transformations, he easily slipped into the tent and entered a courtyard. 

A long sigh came from within. A girl sat on a swing, basking in the orange glow of the setting sun. She looked adorable despite the bits of scales on her body, though it was bizarre to hear a young child letting out an aged-sounding sigh. 

The sight of that young but familiar face left Zu An in a daze. The girl was Salamay as a child. He had known beforehand that she was already born in this era, though she was still young. The first thing he'd done upon arriving at this barracks was to locate little Salamay before paying a visit to the Monster Lord. 

Zu An wondered what would happen if the future Salamay saw her, though that was impossible. The future Salamay had been restrained and was currently under Princess Suolun’s watch.

“Who are you?” The girl noticed his presence and eyed him with curiosity.

“Aren’t you afraid?” Zu An asked in surprise. He'd been prepared to subdue her in case she panicked and called for her guards, but the latter merely looked at him with blinking eyes.

“Why should I be afraid?” The girl couldn’t understand the question. “Are you here to play with me? I’m bored.”

Zu An belatedly recalled that she was the Lord of Slaughter’s daughter, an exalted entity in the monster army. Given that the Monster Lord had yet to rebel, he had to treat her with the greatest respect. Salamay had never needed to fear anyone. On the contrary, it was others who had to fear her.

“Are you very bored, first miss?” Zu An noticed her frown and asked. 

“Yup. My daddy went to some imperial tomb a few years back and I haven’t seen him since. Uncle Mojard has been missing since two years ago, and Uncle Monster Lord is too polite and busy to play with me.” The girl pouted. “Other people are too afraid of me. You are one of the few people who aren’t scared of me.” 

Zu An sighed. 

Salamay had a tough life. She never enjoyed happiness despite being the monsters’ first miss. Later, the Monster Lord usurped her father’s position, forcing her to go into hiding with his father’s old subordinates while plotting vengeance.  No wonder her heart became so warped.

As much as Zu An pitied her, he wasn’t going to let it hinder him from doing what he had to do. “I’m not here to play with you, first miss. I’m a friend of the Incestuous King, and I came here to deliver news to you.”

“Uncle Mo?” The girl’s eyes lit up. She leaped off the swing and ran over. “Did you bring me a present from Uncle Mo?”

Zu An sighed before speaking with a heavy tone, “The Incestuous King and his army were ambushed in the East Sea Dragon Palace and were defeated.”

“What? That’s impossible! Aren't the Ocean races planning to surrender to us? How could they have the strength to defeat Uncle Mo and his army?” The girl was young, but she was no fool. 

Zu An was surprised. It won’t be that easy to fool her. Thus, he explained, “It could have been a feint, and the Ocean races weren't planning to surrender in the first place. On top of that, I heard a mysterious expert has joined forces with the Ocean races.”

Little Salamay panicked. “Where is Uncle Mo now?”

“We don’t know whether he is alive or not, but the odds aren’t in his favor. That’s why I risked my life to come here to inform you of the matter, first miss,” Zu An said with sorrowful eyes. 

“Risk your life?” Little Salamay stared at him in confusion, not understanding why it was dangerous for him to report this matter to her. 

Zu An sighed. “The Monster Lord should have already heard about the Incestuous King’s defeat, but has he shared the news with first miss?”

Little Salamay’s eyes widened. Indeed, no one had informed her about this matter. Her earlier innocence vanished without a trace, replaced with grimness. “What you have just mentioned is too huge. I’ll have to verify it.”

“First miss, you mustn’t confront the Monster Lord!” Zu An exclaimed. “I came under the Incestuous King’s request. He told me to warn you about the Monster Lord if anything happened to him.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2496: The Imperial Tomb’S Secret
chapters.body:
Chapter 2496: The Imperial Tomb’S Secret

Little Salamay narrowed her eyes. “Are you telling me that the Monster Lord had a hand in Uncle Mo’s mishap?”

Zu An shook his head. “I don’t know. I am just relaying words from the Incestuous King.”

Telling a person something outright was more likely to pique their suspicion. Instead, it was more effective to lead their train of thought toward the truth. They would be more inclined to believe it if they arrived at the conclusion themselves.

Unsurprisingly to Zu An, Little Salamay soon frowned as a thought surfaced in her mind. 

“There’s another matter I need to ask first miss. Did our people recently capture an extremely beautiful woman with a very… unique figure?” Zu An decided not to reveal Pei Mianman’s name in case Salamay remembered her name. That could have ripple effects on the future. 

Little Salamay shook her head. “I haven’t heard of anyone like that. What’s your relationship with that woman?”

“We met by coincidence,” Zu An answered. There was no reason to stay here if Pei Mianman wasn’t here. “I have fulfilled the mission entrusted to me, so I shan’t disturb your rest anymore, first miss.”

“Hey…” Little Salamay tried to stop him, but Zu An’s figure blurred before vanishing into thin air. 

“Hm?” Despite her young age, Little Salamay could tell that Zu An had incredible abilities. “Does Uncle Mo have such a formidable friend?”

After some thought, she decided to verify the matter. As she made her way out of her tent, her guards and servants asked her where she was heading. 

“I’m going to look for Uncle Hulu,” Little Salamay said innocently. 

“Lord Hulu isn’t in the barracks. He was recently dispatched to the frontlines to deal with the Beast races,” a guard replied.

“Then I’ll look for Aunt Jyan,” Little Salamay replied. 

“Lord Jyan has gone to the South Sea’s shores to curb the rebellion there,” someone replied. 

“Ah!” Doubt was starting to fester in Little Salamay’s mind, but she didn’t let it show. “I’ll go play with Uncle Ajue then!”

“Lord Ajue has been imprisoned for committing some wrongdoings.” The guards began to look nervous, wondering if they had inadvertently revealed the secret. 

“What a bummer! I’ll play by myself then.” Little Salamay harrumphed before hopping back into her courtyard like an easily distracted child.

The guards wiped off their sweat. Thank goodness she didn’t probe any further, or else the Monster Lord might punish us for this. I guess she’s just a child, after all.

Little Salamay returned to her courtyard and frolicked with a ball. Some time later, she stretched her back as if she was tired, before returning to her room. She murmured under her breath, “I’m tired. Time to sleep.”

Several maids followed her to prepare her for bedtime. Shortly after Little Salamay lay on her bed, her breathing became long and deep.

The maids looked envious. Children fall asleep so easily. They tucked her into her blanket, lit a fragrant incense stick, and quietly retreated from the room. 

As soon as the door closed, little Salamay’s eyes shot open. She carefully got up from her bed and took out an artifact. The artifact lit up and formed a small sound-isolation barrier around her. She made sure to confirm that the formation was working. Next, she reached into her storage bag and took out a mirror. She stuffed several ki stones into the mirror, and its surface rippled like water. 

Soon, a figure appeared in the mirror. His bloodthirsty aura could be felt even through it. He was none other than the Lord of Slaughter! He initially looked worried, but when he saw little Salamay, he heaved a sigh of relief. “My good daughter, I thought something happened to you.”

“Daddy, when are you coming back?” Little Salamay asked with a pout, sounding a little tearful. 

That sent the Lord of Slaughter into a panic. He felt an urge to wipe off her tears even across the screen. “Don’t cry, my good daughter. Who bullied you? I’ll rip him into shreds!”

“No one bullied me.” Little Salamay shook her head. “I miss you.”

“My good daughter, daddy is too busy to leave right now. Once I’m done with things here, I’ll return right away to spend time with you,” the Lord of Slaughter said gently. He took some time to coax his daughter before asking, “I told you not to contact me unless something urgent cropped up. Did something happen?”

His daughter was young but sensible. It was unlikely for her to contact him for no reason. 

“I don’t know if it’s urgent or not.” Little Salamay took a moment to gather her thoughts before saying, “I think Uncle Mo’s army was recently decimated, and we don’t know if he is dead or alive.”

“What?!” The Lord of Slaughter was enraged to hear that. “How can Mojard be that unreliable? How did he lose his army when we have already eradicated the Fiend races’ main army? I knew the Monster Lord was more reliable!”

Little Salamay looked hesitant to speak further.

“What’s wrong, my good daughter?” The Lord of Slaughter noticed his daughter’s unusual behavior.

Little Salamay hesitantly spoke up. “The Monster Lord didn’t tell me about this.”

“Then where did you hear the news from?” The Lord of Slaughter frowned, as it suddenly occurred to him that no one had reported this matter to him. 

“It was a mysterious man. He says he was sent by Uncle Mo…” Little Salamay proceeded to tell the Lord of Slaughter about her encounter with Zu An. 

The Lord of Slaughter was bewildered. “Does Mojard have such a friend?”

“That’s not all. I was thinking of verifying the news with some of the uncles and aunts, but I learned that they were either demoted or imprisoned. There is no one here I can talk to,” Little Salamay said. 

The Lord of Slaughter’s face warped in anger. He had ruled long enough to immediately understand the implications of what he had just heard. He told Little Salamay, “Don’t tell anyone about this, especially not the Monster Lord. Keep everything to yourself and wait for my return!”

Little Salamay was overjoyed to hear that. “That’s wonderful! I can meet daddy again.”

The Lord of Slaughter tried to smile, but he couldn’t lift the corners of his lips, as if there were weights attached to them. How dare the Monster Lord betray my trust and pull tricks behind my back? 

… 

The Monster Lord was busy scheming with his aides when his eyes suddenly widened. He shot a glance at his aides before taking out a recording mirror. The Lord of Slaughter soon appeared in the mirror.

“Paying respects to master!” The Monster Lord quickly bowed.

“You may dispense with the ceremony. Get up,” the Lord of Slaughter said amicably.

“Thank you, master. Do you have any command for us?” the Monster Lord asked respectfully. 

The Lord of Slaughter looked to be buzzing with excitement. “After years of research, I have finally deciphered the secret of the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb. The gates are about to open. Come here right now to witness history with me. I have found the legendary Celestial Court!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2497: Caught
chapters.body:
Chapter 2497: Caught

“Celestial Court?” The Monster Lord’s face beamed with joy. “Congratulations, master! You have finally found the legendary Celestial Court!”

The Lord of Slaughter laughed heartily. “You don’t need to hold back with me. I wish to share this joy with you. I have heard about the prowess of the elusive Celestial Court, so I will need your help too.”

“Thank you, master. I’ll make some arrangements before rushing over,” the Monster Lord said with a beaming face. 

“Yes, you should. The Fiend races and the Ocean races still have one last breath in them, so we mustn’t give them the chance to make a comeback. Quickly make arrangements and come within the next few days,” the Lord of Slaughter instructed. 

“Yes, master!” The Monster Lord bowed respectfully. 

As soon as the recording mirror was switched off, Manager Pi stepped forward with a fawning smile and exclaimed, “Congratulations, master.”

“Why are you congratulating me?” The Monster Lord’s smile disappeared. 

Manager Pi was shocked by the Monster Lord’s sudden change in attitude. “It’s naturally because the Lord of Slaughter trusts you so much that he’s willing to share the secret he has deciphered with you.”

The Monster Lord sneered, “You’re wrong. He is planning to kill me.”

Both Manager Pi and the War Priest were stunned.

The Monster Lord explained, “That old thing spent years in the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb, but to no avail. What a huge coincidence it is for him to find the Celestial Court the moment something has happened to Mojard!”

“Master, is that person sounding you out?” The War Priest was taken aback.

“Isn’t it obvious?” the Monster Lord replied grimly. “He wouldn’t have neglected to mention Mojard otherwise. His ties with Mojard might have become strained in recent years, but he wouldn’t leave that fellow out for something this important. He must have learned about Mojard’s defeat and the power shifts in the army. He’s fearful of me, which is why he is summoning me. It’d be easier for him to take me down once I’m isolated from my troops and aides.”

The War Priest was shocked. “That old thing is a sly one! The slightest carelessness would have resulted in us falling for his trap!”

Manager Pi took this chance to fawn over the Monster Lord. “No matter how sly that old thing is, our master still saw through him right away. Try as he may, but he will never be able to pull one over us!”

The group burst into laughter. 

The Monster Lord’s eyes gleamed. “I shall prepare a huge surprise for him then.”

… 

Zu An spent some time wandering around the barracks and subduing almost a hundred monsters of different ranks, but none of them had news about Pei Mianman. Pei Mianman’s traits were so conspicuous that the monsters would have remembered her if they had seen her. 

Did she avoid the monster barracks to head straight for the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb? Or could there be another possibility?

Unfortunately, Zu An was lacking in intel, so his best bet was to pay a visit to the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb. 

He traveled day and night without rest and eventually arrived in the Imperial Tomb’s vicinity. It felt nostalgic to return to the same place. The last time he was here, he'd been accompanied by Yan Xuehen, Yun Jianyue, and Yu Yanluo, but this time, he was here alone to look for Pei Mianman. 

The Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb looked vastly different from how it was in the future. The buildings outside were much more majestic and densely packed. Many of the buildings had likely eroded and disappeared over time.

Zu An quietly stared at the many monsters stationed around the Imperial Tomb. 

What would Big Manman do upon arriving here?

She’s not a reckless person. Knowing that the Lord of Slaughter is here, she wouldn’t risk her life to obtain the Brilliant Pinflower. She would instead find a way to inform me, so we could come here together to obtain it.

Could she be on her way to the Ocean races, but we missed each other? If she meets the Mermaid Queen, she should be able to find out what happened. 

That quelled some of Zu An’s worries. To be safe, he still decided to infiltrate the monster army. He spent some time walking around before entering a monster commander’s room. He suppressed the latter and asked, “Did you capture a woman…”

He briefly shared Pei Mianman’s traits, though there was no need for too many details, as those who met her would remember her easily. 

“N-No, I haven’t met her.” Fearing that Zu An wouldn’t believe him, the monster commander anxiously added, “Who would infiltrate this place when our master is here…” His words suddenly trailed off as he realized the man before him was one such intruder. 

Zu An thought those words made sense. 

The monster commander continued, “I advise you to free me. Our master is invincible. You won’t be able to survive his wrath even if you have nine lives. If you leave on your own accord, I’ll pretend this never happened.”

In truth, the monster commander was scared out of his wits. He'd been caught off guard, but the intruder was strong enough to subdue him before he could react. That meant the intruder was at least on par with the War Priest. 

As soon as I scare this fellow off, I’ll alert my comrades and have him captured. Who knows? I might even be rewarded for this! 

Zu An could tell what the monster commander was planning from his gaze, but he didn’t expose the latter. Instead, he asked, “Where’s the Lord of Slaughter?” 

“How dare you call our master’s name?!” The monster commander paled in horror.

“Answer my question.” Zu An grabbed the monster commander’s shoulder and exerted a powerful suction force. 

The monster commander was horrified. He sensed that the intruder had the power to reduce him into a withered corpse at will. Left with no choice, he replied, “Our master is in the Imperial Tomb.” 

“What is he looking for?"

“I don’t know. I only know he recently found a treasure and was extremely happy. He hasn’t been this happy for a few years now. I suspect that the treasure contributes greatly to what he’s looking for.”

Zu An was surprised. Did the Lord of Slaughter find a way to enter the Unknown Region?

Just then, he raised his head.

Footsteps echoed outside, and someone shouted, “Lord Aku, our master has summoned everyone who is of commander rank and above to the Imperial Tomb!” 

The monster commander was overjoyed. He tried to call for help, only to be horrified to learn that he couldn’t speak at all. On top of that, his surroundings suddenly distorted, isolating the space around him from light and sound.  Then, he felt his ki gushing out, causing his body to quickly weaken. 

“Pl… ease…” He tried to speak, but hardly a sound came out before his consciousness faded. 

Zu An lay his carcass down before replying in his voice, “Give me a moment. I’ll be right there.”

This monster commander named Aku carries a strong stench of blood. He must have many lives on his hands. He deserved to die. 

To Zu An’s dismay, the monster camp was in an uproar over something. If he barged into the Imperial Tomb at this juncture, the odds were that he would be discovered quickly. So, he had no choice but to take on Aku’s appearance, wear his clothes, and enter the Imperial Tomb first to observe the situation. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2498: The Lakeside Assault
chapters.body:
Chapter 2498: The Lakeside Assault

After putting on Aku’s clothes, Zu An produced a spark of flame and burned the corpse into ashes. He left the room and saw several monster guards waiting outside. They were all Aku’s subordinates.

“Sir, the other commanders have already headed over,” the guards said worriedly. They were worried that his tardiness in responding to the Lord of Slaughter’s summon could implicate them. That would put them in a bad position.

Zu An looked up and saw countless figures rushing toward the imperial tomb. The Monster World is indeed at its peak. None of these commanders are weak, and there are even a few who are exceptionally powerful. 

Knowing that he would draw unwanted attention if he tried to escape right now, he decided to head over and observe the situation first. He made his way to the imperial tomb together with the monsters. The gates to the tomb were wide open, and many sculptures of the Fiend races’ ancestors had been destroyed. Only the most majestic Fiend God sculpture remained intact. 

Now that he was back here, Zu An couldn’t help but notice a peculiar aura shrouding the Fiend God’s sculpture that made it seem solemn and powerful. Beneath the sculpture was a giant stone bench that had bloodthirst lingering on it, likely left by people who had sat on it. 

Zu An took a look around; there were almost a hundred monsters gathered here. Aku was one of the weaker ones. The conspicuously stronger monsters in front had all surpassed earth immortal rank. 

Soon, a brawny figure walked in. His eyebrows stood at forty-five degrees, looking as if they would pierce through the sky. He looked at the monsters before him with sharp eyes. 

“Paying respect to master!” The monsters bowed.

Zu An secretly sized up the Lord of Slaughter. The latter was indeed stronger than the one who had been suppressed for millennia in the future. His bloodthirst was so overpowering that it almost felt palpable. Those beside him trembled uncontrollably before the Lord of Slaughter’s presence. 

“He’s too powerful.” Zu An didn’t dare to check the Lord of Slaughter’s cultivation with his divine sense, lest the latter detect it. He could only fathom a guess. The latter was undoubtedly stronger than him and the Monster Lord. 

I wonder how the Monster Lord suppressed him in history. 

“Master, may I know why you have summoned us urgently?” one of the monsters in front asked.

“Something is happening among us,” the Lord of Slaughter said. 

The commanders were perplexed. Some whispered among themselves as they tried to figure out what those words meant.

“Incestuous King Mojard and his army were recently wiped out by the Ocean races,” the Lord of Slaughter said. 

“What?!” 

The commanders were taken aback, but there were also a few who exchanged glances with each other. 

“I was shocked when I first learned this news. But what shocked me more was…” The Lord of Slaughter stared at the commanders with eyes as frigid as winter, causing them to shudder in fear. “Why has no one reported this matter to me?” His voice sounded like the roar of a primal beast. 

Pah! Pah! Pah!

One after another, several monsters exploded into bloody mist. A powerful monster in front cried miserably as he tried to get up and flee, but he was unable to suppress the destructive energy rampaging inside him and exploded. 

Blood and flesh splattered on the other monsters, causing them to shudder even more fearfully. The survivors were frightened and confused, and they dared not move lest the Lord of Slaughter turn his wrath upon them.

Goosebumps rose all over Zu An’s body. He had only come here to weather the crisis and escape as soon as everything calmed down. He hadn’t expected to stumble upon the Lord of Slaughter massacring his subordinates. It was a relief he wasn’t one of the targets. 

The ‘fireworks’ killed a third of the monsters gathered here. The remaining ones were trembling, looking as if they wanted to fight back yet dared not. 

If we’re going to die anyway, we might as well try fighting back.

The Lord of Slaughter is too strong. I’ll surely die if I attack first. I should wait for others to make a move.”

What’s going on? Has our master lost his mind?

Different thoughts floated in the monsters’ minds. Fortunately, the Lord of Slaughter spoke up. “Calm down, I was only purging the traitors. I know that the rest of you are loyal to me, and I don’t mistreat those who are loyal to me.”

“Traitors?”

Most of the monsters were stunned. The Monster World was at its prime, and it was already on the verge of conquering the Cultivation World. Who were these traitors defecting to?

A few smart ones had already figured out what was going on, though.

The Lord of Slaughter harrumphed. “The Monster Lord even dares to bribe my subordinates.”

A huge commotion broke out.

The Lord of Slaughter had gone into seclusion to uncover the secrets of the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb, and he'd entrusted all military affairs to the Monster Lord. The Monster Lord had always enjoyed great prestige because of his strength and commanding prowess, and years of monopolizing power had elevated his standing to be almost on par with the Lord of Slaughter. However, the two of them still fell out in the end. 

“We vow to serve master to our deaths!” the remaining monsters roared. As the Lord of Slaughter’s aides, it was in their best interest to continue serving him. Even if they tried to defect to the Monster Lord’s side, it was unlikely that the latter would trust them. 

Zu An’s eyebrows shot up. Looks like my visit to little Salamay paid off. She does have a way to communicate with the Lord of Slaughter. 

The Lord of Slaughter continued, “The Monster Lord removed my aides and put his people in power. I now see that my trust in him was misplaced. That ambitious bastard dreams of overthrowing me! It’s about time for an internal purge.” 

One of the commanders anxiously spoke up, “Our army is now under the Monster Lord’s control, and the Incestuous Lord has fallen in defeat. The other troops loyal to our master are stationed in the distant borders and won’t be able to make it back in time. Master, I beseech you to calm down and think things through!”

The Lord of Slaughter laughed. “Relax, I am not a reckless fool. I played a little trick. The Monster Lord will be coming to the imperial tomb within the next few days. We’ll take him down while he is on our turf.”

The monsters were overjoyed to hear that. “We will fight him to our dying breath!” It would be advantageous to them if they were fighting on their home turf, especially since the Lord of Slaughter’s cultivation was higher. 

Zu An was awed. The Lord of Slaughter was indeed a formidable individual, to concoct a scheme against the Monster Lord so quickly. I wonder what he used as bait to lure the Monster Lord over. Is it the imperial tomb’s secret? He looked contemplatively at the imperial tomb.

The Lord of Slaughter proceeded to craft a detailed plan to deal with the Monster Lord.

Zu An nodded in approval. It would be hard for the Monster Lord to deal with such arrangements. I wonder how he turned the tables on the Lord of Slaughter in history.

To prevent news from getting out, none of the monsters were allowed to leave the imperial tomb until this matter was settled. The monsters spent the next few days constructing all kinds of formations in the imperial tomb. If they were tired, they would simply find a place to lie down and rest. There was plenty of space inside the imperial tomb anyway. 

Zu An thus found a chance to sneak into the imperial tomb. He remembered that this place was structured as a Yin Yang Tomb. The Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb was merely a distraction to conceal the Unknown Region hidden below.

He made his way to a deep lake. Back then, the entrance to the Unknown Region had been located at the bottom of the lake, but he had no idea whether the gates were currently opened or not. He was thinking of taking a look when he suddenly heard a sharp whiz.

A black shadow flew toward his neck. At the same time, a small formation appeared underneath his feet, causing the surrounding air to become viscous. It was a slowing formation, hindering him from dodging the attack.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2499: Reunion
chapters.body:
Chapter 2499: Reunion

Zu An frowned. He sidestepped with the Steps of Rising Lotus. Given his deep understanding of space, very few formations could hope to trap him now. 

“Hm.” The black figure was surprised to see how easily Zu An struggled free of her formation. 

Hearing that gentle voice and looking at the figure's delicate frame, Zu An quickly stopped his attack and called out, “Little sister Ling’er?”

“Hm?” The black figure was about to attack with another talisman when a shudder ran through her body. She stared at him in disbelief. “B-Big brother Zu?”

Looking at her joyful yet skeptical expression, Zu An suddenly remembered he was still disguised as a monster, so he quickly reverted to his original appearance with a smile. 

Upon seeing his face, the black figure also took off her veil to reveal a gentle and pure face. Who else could it be other than Xie Daoyun? Or perhaps it was more apt to call her this world’s Saintess Yumen Beiqing. 

Zu An was delighted too. “What brings you here?”

The Saintess’ lips crept up. “After parting ways, I returned to the Fiend races and made arrangements. As soon as I was done, I rushed here to look for you. I couldn’t gather any information about you, so I disguised myself and snuck in. I didn’t expect us to reunite under such circumstances. This must be fate.”

Zu An was worried. “You’re the Fiend races’ Saintess, the most wanted person by the monsters. It’s too dangerous for you to be here.”

The Saintess quietly looked at him. “But I was worried about you, and we have been separated for so long that I don’t want to be away from you anymore.”

Her pitiful appearance squeezed Zu An’s heart. He pulled her into his arms and said, “We’ll never be separated from each other again.”

To him, he had only been away from Xie Daoyun for a few months, but to her, they had been separated for many years now. Like newlyweds who were forced to part for years right after their marriage, what woman could control her yearning for her lover?

They embraced each other tightly for a long time. By the time they let go, the Saintess’ cheeks were already flushed. Big brother Zu is still as naughty as before. 

“What are you doing here, big brother Zu?”

“I came here to see if the Unknown Region has opened.”

The Saintess was taken aback. “Big brother Zu, you know the secret of the imperial tomb?”

Zu An laughed. “Have you forgotten? In the future, I once entered the Fiend race’s dungeon. The Unknown Region was hidden in the imperial tomb. I heard rumors that the Unknown Region opens once every few millennia, and we have returned a few millennia in time. It might be opening soon.”

The Saintess was awed. “Big brother Zu is indeed overflowing with luck. Most people wouldn’t see an Unknown Region in their entire lifetime. Even the Lord of Slaughter couldn’t find a clue despite having investigated this place for many years. Yet, you stumbled upon the opening of the same Unknown Region twice!”

Zu An embraced her slender waist and chuckled. “I have always been lucky. How else did I meet you?”

The Saintess’ cheeks were completely red. She rested her head on his shoulder and said, “Big brother Zu, I feel so blissful now.”

Zu An wrapped his hand around her. “Let’s return to the future once we settle things here. This bliss shall continue till the end of time.”

The conversation soon turned toward their current plight.

“It doesn’t look like the Unknown Region is open yet.” The Saintess frowned. 

“Let’s head down to take a look.” Zu An grabbed her hand and brought her toward the lake. 

The Saintess was about to take out a water repulsion talisman when the lake suddenly split open, revealing a path for them to pass through. Big brother Zu is incredible.

They flew around the lake twice.

Zu An was confused. “Hm? The gate isn’t there despite the powerful ki pulsations in the vicinity.”

The Saintess pondered the situation, wondering, “Is the teleportation gate not open yet?”

Zu An nodded. “From the current looks of it, that’s the most likely explanation.”

“Earlier, you mentioned that the Lord of Slaughter claimed that he had uncovered the Imperial Tomb’s secret. Do you think he’s telling the truth or lying?” the Saintess asked.

“It should be true.” Zu An thought about the Lord of Slaughter’s earlier behavior. “He's been obsessed with the Imperial Tomb’s secret for years now, yet he was calm for the last few days. He must have discovered something.”

“A pity that it doesn’t matter anymore,” the Saintess said. “He’s probably summoning the Monster Lord here to get rid of him, but based on the trajectory of history, he’s doomed to fail and be replaced by the Monster Lord.”

“Indeed. It’s going to be an intense battle.” Zu An fell into deep thought. 

Speaking of which, where’s the Time Scribe? Shouldn’t he be with the Monster Lord or the Lord of Slaughter? Why didn’t I see him? I don’t think he’d miss such an important historical event. 

They didn’t linger around the lake for fear of being discovered. Zu An quickly brought her back to his accommodation. 

Fortunately, the imperial tomb was massive, and only monsters of commander rank and above had been summoned, so everyone had chosen a room or cavern of their own. Aku, whom Zu An was disguised as, was one of the weakest monsters; so he could only pick the most remote, smallest location. However, it was also the perfect place to hide the Saintess, as it was unlikely that other monsters would find her. 

“Who should we side with when the time comes?” The room was simple, with a piece of rock serving as a makeshift bed. The Saintess’ face reddened with embarrassment at being alone with Zu An, and she scrambled for a topic to discuss. 

“We’ll go with the flow. Whatever we do should align with the progression of history.” Zu An had initially thought that he would stay out of it, but in their history, the Lord of Slaughter was the loser. If the Monster Lord faltered, he could end up having to lend the latter a helping hand. Of course, he would have to be prudent about the extent of help he offered. Otherwise, the Monster Lord could just turn around and destroy him. That would be the worst-case scenario. 

The two of them lay on the makeshift bed as they shared their experiences after parting ways. They hadn’t had much private time in the Fiend races' camp so there were many things they hadn’t shared yet. 

Zu An wanted to ask the Saintess about how she had fared in the last few years, but the latter was more interested in his experiences. He didn’t hide anything from her, though he wasn't so foolish as to divulge his relationship with the Mermaid Queen and so on of his own accord. 

At some point, however, Zu An’s hands began to wander. 

The Saintess quickly became angry and grabbed his hand. “Big brother Zu, now that we’re surrounded by enemies, we should be warier than ever.” 

Zu An looked at her with a smile. “Little sister Ling’er, I feel like we've drifted apart since our reunion.”

Fluster flickered across the Saintess’ eyes, and her cheeks soon became flushed. “Nonsense! There’s no such thing~”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2500: The Showdown
chapters.body:
Chapter 2500: The Showdown

The Saintess’ usually gentle and elegant voice carried a hint of coyness, and that stirred something in Zu An’s heart. He kissed her cheeks as he said, “I didn’t see any signs of the Brilliant Pinflowers in the imperial tomb. Are they really here?”

He had been searching the imperial tomb, but there were no signs of Brilliant Pinflowers.

“I remember there being some here, but I don’t know the exact location.” The Saintess looked apologetic. “I was in a rush to look for you, so I forgot to look up more information.”

“I probably just haven’t found them yet,” Zu An murmured.

He didn’t dare to search openly under the Lord of Slaughter’s watch. Given how big the imperial tomb was, it would take more time for him to cover more ground. It was also possible that the Brilliant Pinflower wasn't inside the imperial tomb but rather outside; it was just that he currently couldn’t head outside to take a look. 

Just then, a pair of soft lips suddenly pressed against his. The desire in the Saintess' eyes lit up Zu An’s lust. The two of them embraced each other and tossed everything else to the back of their mind. The only thing they were interested in now was exploring each other’s bodies. 

Zu An constructed a voice isolation formation around them, lest the Lord of Slaughter or other monster commanders hear them while their feelings were at their climax. 

His action caused the Saintess’ face to redden further. She bit his shoulder and scoffed, “It’s all your fault.”

Zu An chuckled. “Who knew that there was such a side to the pure and dignified Saintess?”

“You’re so bad~” the Saintess moaned. She wrapped her hands around his neck, and the two of them soon melted in each other’s warmth. All kinds of unspeakable pleasures followed…  

The following day, Zu An’s eyes suddenly shot open, and he sat upright. “That’s not right!”

“What’s wrong?” The Saintess leaned against him, revealing her impeccable body. 

“Something is amiss about the situation outside.” Zu An quickly put on his clothes.

“What’s amiss?” the Saintess asked as she put on her clothes too. 

Zu An removed the voice isolation formation and spent a moment listening before he replied, “It’s too quiet.” 

There were powerful monster armies stationed outside the imperial tomb. They paled in comparison to the Monster Lord’s main force, but they were still a force to be reckoned with. 

Just then, a terrifying howl echoed, “There’s a change in situation. Hurry up and gather!”

“It’s the Lord of Slaughter,” Zu An told the Saintess. “Find somewhere to hide. Don’t move around in the meantime.”

The terrifying roar caused the Saintess’ reddened face to turn pale. “Be careful.”

Zu An nodded. He quickly put on his clothes before rushing outside. As he headed off, his appearance slowly transformed into Aku's. The Saintess was awed by his transformation. 

By the time Zu An arrived, most monsters were gathered and were staring at the Lord of Slaughter in confusion. The Lord of Slaughter looked infuriated, but no one knew what was going on. 

“Master, what happened?” a monster standing at the forefront asked. 

Zu An had learned his identity over the last few days. He was the Warped Monster, one of the Eighteen Monster Generals of the Monster World. In the Monster World, the Eighteen Monster Generals’ influence and strength were only beneath that of the three behemoths—the Lord of Slaughter, the Monster Lord, and the Incestuous King.

“The Monster Lord is already here,” the Lord of Slaughter said grimly. 

“What?” The Warped Monster was taken aback before his face lit up in delight. “It’s good that he arrived early. We can use our home turf advantage to suppress him with our army outside!”

The Lord of Slaughter’s complexion looked awful. “Our army outside is gone.”

The commanders were all flabbergasted. How could so many troops disappear overnight without them noticing?

Zu An was awed. As expected of the Lord of Slaughter. His senses are keener than mine. I merely noticed that it was abnormally quiet outside, but he already confirmed that his troops were gone.

But how did the Monster Lord do it?

The troops stationed outside were a force to be reckoned with—they were the Lord of Slaughter’s elite army. How could they be massacred in a single night without any noise? 

The commanders voiced their doubts too. They couldn’t believe that their subordinates were killed so easily. 

The Lord of Slaughter sneered coldly, “It looks like the Plague Monarch has betrayed us.”

Zu An’s eyebrows shot up. The Plague Monarch?

He had clashed with the Plague Monarch in the future. The other party’s fighting prowess was nothing impressive, but his ability to spread plague was a potent weapon in large-scale battles. Even those with higher cultivation would struggle to match his destructive prowess. 

Many monsters gasped upon hearing the Plague Monarch’s name. The latter was capable of rendering an entire army powerless overnight. To make things worse, the commanders were trapped inside the imperial tomb, leading to a collapse in the command chain outside. This hindered the troops outside from rallying together and putting up a fight. Of course, none dared to point that out, as it was the Lord of Slaughter’s idea to keep them here. The Lord of Slaughter was a proud man who didn’t take criticisms well. 

Just then, a voice echoed outside. “Master, I request an audience with you.” Those words were uttered softly, but they echoed to every nook and cranny of the imperial tomb, sending chills down the commanders’ spines.

The Monster Lord was here. 

The Lord of Slaughter narrowed his eyes. He glanced at the commanders in the imperial tomb, telling them to follow the plan. The commanders quickly flitted off to their respective hiding spots to lie in ambush. 

The Lord of Slaughter turned to the gates and said, “Come in.”

The gates opened. Sunlight spilled into the imperial tomb, bringing it some life.

The first to enter was the Monster Lord, followed by a large group of monsters. 

Zu An recognized the monsters gathered around the Monster Lord. On his right was the War Priest, and on his left was the Shadow Monster King. The Shadow Monster King’s incredible prowess had left a lasting impression on him in the Sealed Land. The War Priest and the Shadow Monster King were likely the Monster Lord’s strongest subordinates here. 

There were also others whom Zu An had clashed with in the future, such as the Demonic Motherbug, Manipulator of Death, Iron Fist Rock, Scorpion King, Thousand-Footed Elder, Shadowhorror Devil, Plague Monarch, Doomsday Spirit… He had crossed blows with them in the future, but he hadn't thought that they were all the Monster Lord’s subordinates in this era. 

The Lord of Slaughter turned grim. “I didn’t expect half of the Eighteen Monster Generals to have defected to your side.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2501: Making An Example To Intimidate
chapters.body:
Chapter 2501: Making An Example To Intimidate

Having spent days with the monster commanders, Zu An knew that the Eighteen Monster Generals were eighteen renowned experts in the Monster World. Not only were they strong, they also each commanded great power and ruled over a territory. It could be said that anyone supported by these eighteen powerhouses was the true ruler of the Monster World. 

The Monster Lord had ten of the Eighteen Monster Generals on his side, whereas the Lord of Slaughter only had the Warped Monster. There was a huge disparity in their fighting prowess. To make things worse, the latter's loyal subordinate, the Incestuous King, could potentially be dead. Things didn’t look optimistic for him. 

The other commanders also had awful looks on their faces. After seeing the other side’s lineup, their earlier confidence had shattered. 

While Zu An had fought these experts before, that was when they were weakened by the world barrier, whereas they could all exert their full strength now. He had previously been thinking of supporting the Monster Lord if the latter was at a disadvantage due to the ambush, but now, his priority shifted to how he could escape from this ordeal. 

The Monster Lord’s lips crept upward as he spoke with his uniquely feminine voice. “You’re pulling our legs, master. They are only here so that we can serve you better.”

The Lord of Slaughter felt infuriated. That fellow is still putting on an act even at this point!

In truth, he had enjoyed such bootlicking in the past, or else he wouldn’t have trusted the Monster Lord so much as to even neglect his old subordinates like Mojard. And yet, in just a few years, the Monster Lord had already revealed his claws. 

He suppressed his anger and put on an amicable face. “You came at a good time. I have just comprehended the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb’s secret and need more manpower to aid me. With all of you here, even the Celestial Court won’t be a threat.”

The Monster Lord maintained a respectful tone. “May I know where the Celestial Court is?”

“This concerns the biggest secret of our Monster World. I can’t divulge it in public.” The Lord of Slaughter spent a moment in thought before beckoning him over. “Follow me. We’ll discuss this further in the secret chamber.”

The Monster Lord was moved. He was tempted by the secret of the Celestial Court as well. However, in the end, his rationality got the better of him, and he stood his ground. “Master, you don’t need to fool me. You have laid a trap in the Imperial Tomb. I will be dead if I head over.”

The Lord of Slaughter’s face darkened. “Brother, what do you mean?”

The Monster Lord laughed. “Master, those are tricks I have used for a long time. Don’t you think you’re underestimating me too much by using them against me?”

The Lord of Slaughter’s cheeks twitched. He had always been an arrogant man. This was the one time he'd lowered his pride to lure his opponent into a trap, only to have the latter mock him for it. It made his cheeks sting. 

However, he let out a hearty laugh. “Yes, I am beneath you in terms of schemes and ploys, but do you think you have won? Only the strongest is worthy of taking this seat!”

His laughter reverberated deafeningly in the hall. The weaker ones among those present had to clutch their hearts to pull themselves together, but even the stronger ones felt their blood flow going into disarray. 

Zu An had to concede that the Lord of Slaughter was terrifyingly powerful, but it didn’t change the fact that the odds were stacked against him. 

The Lord of Slaughter understood there was no turning back once the battle began, so he held back the full strength of his voice. He wasn’t as decisive as when he'd reduced a third of his commanders into a blood fog. 

The Monster Lord and his aides were startled by the terrifying pressure coming from the Lord of Slaughter’s laughter. However, the Monster Lord let out a harrumph, neutralizing the laughter’s bloodthirst with his feminine voice. That greatly reduced the pressure caused by the laugh. 

“Times have changed, master. Individual prowess can only go so far. I am now in control of most of the Monster World’s army, while the soldiers loyal to you are all gone. You are alone, and I am surrounded by talents. Do you still not understand where you have gone wrong?” The Monster Lord sighed. “Let me offer you words I have learned from the new world—those who win the hearts of the masses win the world!”

“Pui!” The Lord of Slaughter spat in anger. “You’re nothing more than a dog I reared. You desperately tried to climb up the ranks while I was investigating the imperial tomb’s secret.”

He turned to the Monster Lord’s aides and said, “Upon my word, I have never mistreated you. If you side with me now, I won’t pursue this matter and will even share the Monster Lord’s territory and wealth with you, as well as the Celestial Court’s secret. You should know how important the Celestial Court is to our Monster World.”

That left many of the experts wondering if the Lord of Slaughter had truly deciphered the Celestial Court’s secret. In the Monster World, there was a legend that their origin was tied to the Celestial Court, and their founding ancestor was the master of the Celestial Court. There were rumors about how powerful the Celestial Court was, and that finding the Celestial Court would allow them to fully awaken their bloodline powers and become many times stronger than they currently were. 

Sensing that his aides were beginning to waver, the Monster Lord interjected, “Master, you are still pulling our legs. If you have found the Celestial Court, you would have long since awakened your bloodline’s powers. All of us combined wouldn’t have been a match for you. There would have been no need for you to negotiate with us.”

The monsters widened their eyes in realization. That’s true. We were almost duped by that old thing!

The Plague Monarch snickered, “Others might consider surrendering to you, but I wouldn’t dare do so after having just poisoned your personal army outside. I wouldn’t dare believe your words even if you promised me the world.” 

The Lord of Slaughter’s face distorted in anger and he roared, “So you were the culprit!” He wouldn’t have been in this predicament if not for his personal army falling for the Plague Monarch’s poison.

The War Priest stepped in front of the Plague Monarch and said, “Rest assured, Plague Monarch. We are on the same side. We all know that old thing is arrogant and hot-tempered. If we continue working under him, it’s only a matter of time before he kills us in a fit of anger.” 

The other experts nodded. The Lord of Slaughter was known for his cruelty. It was unlikely for things to end well for them if they defected to his side. 

Seeing that his plan had failed, the Lord of Slaughter was infuriated. “Bastard! I’ll kill you!”

He dashed over to the War Priest and swiped his claws at the latter’s head. He was so fast that the other experts had no time to react at all. 

He intended to first eliminate the War Priest to intimidate the others!

The War Priest was prepared for his attack. He quickly whipped out a pitch-black Soul Summoning Banner and unleashed a surge of black aura at the Lord of Slaughter. 

However, the aura that could repel countless beings failed to stop the Lord of Slaughter. The latter’s claws gripped the War Priest's face like the death god’s scythe. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2502: Lord Against Lord
chapters.body:
Chapter 2502: Lord Against Lord

The stronger a cultivator was, the tougher their body would be. Someone of the War Priest’s cultivation would have a head tougher than metal. And yet, the Lord of Slaughter’s claws sliced through it as easily as tofu before crushing it. 

The surrounding monsters were horrified. The War Priest was ranked second among the Eighteen Monster Generals, making him one of the top experts of the Monster World. And yet, the Lord of Slaughter had killed him in an instant. Quite a few monsters regretted their decision. They had underestimated the Lord of Slaughter’s strength, and now felt it was unwise to have made such a risky move. 

Only Zu An remained calm. Having fought the War Priest in the future, he knew that the latter wouldn’t die that easily. 

As expected, the Lord of Slaughter was frowning, but he didn’t stop there. With a casual strike, he smashed the monster commanders beside the War Priest into bloody mist.

The Eighteen Monster Generals reacted fast and quickly escaped while the War Priest was being attacked. 

The Lord of Slaughter charged at the Plague Monarch, perhaps due to the latter’s slower speed or the fact that he harbored great hatred toward the latter for poisoning his personal army.

The Plague Monarch could only barely keep the attacks from striking his vitals. A punch struck his shoulder, and he spurted a mouthful of fresh blood. His body crashed into a stone sculpture like a tattered sandbag, causing the sculpture to crumble. He was so weakened that he couldn’t return to his feet despite several attempts to do so. He had temporarily lost his ability to fight. 

However, a grayish mist had enveloped the Lord of Slaughter’s hand and was relentlessly creeping upward. The Plague Monarch was one of the Eighteen Monster Generals, after all. His poisons weren't to be scoffed at. 

Iron Fist Rock’s eyes lit up at the opportunity. 

The Lord of Slaughter had just killed the War Priest and severely wounded the Plague Monarch. Those attacks had to have severely depleted his ki. Furthermore, the Plague Monarch had managed to inflict his poison on him. This was a rare opening.

So, Iron Fist Rock hurled his renowned iron fist forth to strike the Lord of Slaughter’s back. He released a punch so fast that it produced a sonic boom and caused the surroundings to light up with iridescent colors; the light was caused by the intense friction between ki and air.

The Lord of Slaughter harrumphed. With a flick of his fist, he expelled the grayish poison aura out of his body before confronting Iron Fist Rock’s punch head-on.

Boom!

A miserable cry of agony emerged, accompanied by the sound of fracturing bones. Iron Fist Rock anxiously retreated while clutching his right arm. Cold sweat was dripping down his face. His fist was now hanging limply. It looked as if the bones inside had all shattered. 

The monsters were horrified. Iron Fist Rock was known throughout many worlds for his iron fist. His punch could even shatter a city into ruins, or break apart a mountain. And yet, he suffered such a tragic loss against the Lord of Slaughter! Those thinking of exploiting the situation dropped those thoughts and determinedly retreated. 

This greatly boosted the morale of the Lord of Slaughter’s group, including the Warped Monster. They charged out to battle with the Monster Lord’s subordinates. 

While the Lord of Slaughter's group had fewer top experts, these commanders were all elites among the elites. They were far stronger than ordinary monsters. With the formations they had prepared beforehand, they were surprisingly able to match the ten Monster Generals’ army.
