The Lord of Slaughter didn’t continue his onslaught, instead eyeing the Monster Lord. He sensed the latter trying to make a move several times, only to hold back because he couldn’t find a good opening to exploit. 

“Master, your strength is indeed unparalleled. You killed the War Priest in a single blow and severely wounded the Plague Monarch and Iron Fist Rock,” the Monster Lord said flatteringly, but his eyes reflected fear.

“Hmph, the War Priest wouldn’t die that easily. Are you hiding there in hopes of finding a chance to assault me?” the Lord of Slaughter sneered.

The light at the corner of the hall distorted, and the War Priest slowly reappeared. “I see that it’s impossible to deceive your sharp eyes, master.” 

The Lord of Slaughter said in praise, “You survived my all-out attack. You do live up to your reputation as the number two of the Eighteen Monster Generals.”

He could only unleash his full force in his first surprise attack. When he was dealing with the Plague Monarch and Iron Fist Rock afterward, he'd had to keep an eye out for the Monster Lord, lest the latter backstab him. Due to that, the pressure faced by the three Monster Generals was vastly different. 

The other Monster Generals shot awed looks at the War Priest. He’s stronger than I expected. 

Even the first-rank Shadow Monster King felt wary. This fellow is a threat to me. 

“It’s my honor to receive master’s recognition.” The War Priest relished in the other Monster Generals’ admiring eyes, although he felt pained by his loss.

He'd had to use his precious war puppet to survive the ordeal. The puppet was extremely valuable, having strength on par with the main body. Not only was it his second life, it was also a powerful offensive tool. If he brought it out in battle, his enemy would have to simultaneously deal with two of him. Even if the Shadow Monster King was stronger than him, could he be two times stronger?

It was the War Priest's greatest trump card, and he would never have revealed it unless cornered. He hadn’t expected the Lord of Slaughter to destroy it right away. His heart was bleeding, but he had to feign composure as if it was nothing. I’ll have to find another trump card. 

Not long ago, he'd found a secret dungeon rumored to contain the legendary Oblivion River Water. He had refrained from entering due to the risks, but it looked as if he had no choice now. Only by refining the Oblivion River Water would he stand a chance against the Shadow Monster King.

The Lord of Slaughter noticed the fear in the War Priest’s eyes and harrumphed. He turned his attention back to the Monster Lord. “Scheme all you want, but you can’t scheme your way out of a fight with me. Ultimately, you still have to speak with your strength.”

The Monster Lord knew that the Lord of Slaughter’s sudden assault had intimidated the ten Monster Generals, so they wouldn’t charge ahead for him anymore. However, he laughed. “You’re too arrogant. Do you think that you’re invincible?”

He suddenly charged at the Lord of Slaughter with speed that was on par with the latter’s earlier surprise attack. Very few people in the hall could see his movements. Flickering red marks revolved in his eyes. With a black aura in his right hand and a green mist in his left, he unleashed his attack upon the empty space right beside the Lord of Slaughter.

Both the Shadow Monster King and the War Priest were startled. Why was he attacking the air? Isn’t he exposing his opening to the Lord of Slaughter?

Only Zu An understood what was going on. 

The Lord of Slaughter suddenly vanished in the next moment, only to reappear where the Monster Lord was attacking. It almost looked as if the Lord of Slaughter was running into the latter’s attack!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2503: Tricked
chapters.body:
Chapter 2503: Tricked

The monsters were all shocked, be it those allied with the Lord of Slaughter or the Monster Lord.

Those on the Lord of Slaughter’s side were appalled to see their master in danger, whereas those on the Monster Lord’s side hadn’t expected him to have become so much more powerful as to anticipate the Lord of Slaughter’s reaction. 

Only Zu An understood, having previously crossed blows with the Monster Lord. The latter possessed special eyes that could see his opponent’s openings. Often, he only had to strike the opening, and most enemies would crumble and fall into deep fear. Having one’s every move perfectly read and countered was enough to drive most cultivators into a mental breakdown. Only those with tremendous willpower could retain their sanity. 

Needless to say, the Lord of Slaughter was no ordinary person either. He was stunned for a moment, but he quickly snapped out of it and sliced through space with his sharp claws. 

The surrounding space collapsed, and the opening vanished. The Monster Lord had no choice but to change his move. They traded dozens of blows in the blink of an eye. Their movements were so fast that most monsters in the hall couldn’t keep up. 

“I hadn’t expected you to acquire that legendary eye technique, but did you think you can defeat me just because you can see through my openings?” the Lord of Slaughter said mockingly. 

“Master’s cultivation is formidable. Not only do you reveal few openings, but you move so fast that your openings vanish in an instant. Naturally, you wouldn’t be afraid of my eye technique,” the Monster Lord replied, but he didn’t sound disappointed at all. 

The Lord of Slaughter harrumphed. He also knew it wouldn’t be that easy to deal with the Monster Lord’s eye technique, though the earlier clash gave him some confidence. Despite the latter's swift growth, the Monster Lord was still lacking compared to him. Even at the expense of incurring severe injuries, he was determined to end the Monster Lord right here. Once the ringleader was dead, his subordinates would have no choice but to return to his side. 

Sensing their intensifying auras, Zu An knew that both sides were about to get serious. 

All of a sudden, the Monster Lord said, “Master, I have prepared a gift for you to thank you for the years you have spent nurturing me.”

The Lord of Slaughter’s heart quivered. He thought the other party had acquired a formidable artifact or potent poison. 

Little did he expect the gate to suddenly open, upon which a girl exclaimed, “Daddy~”

“Salamay!” The Lord of Slaughter flew into a rage.

It turned out that the Monster Lord’s subordinates were holding his daughter hostage at the entrance. In truth, he had already guessed as much when the Monster Lord arrived with his subordinates, but he'd still harbored hope. After all, Salamay was still young and didn’t pose a threat to the Monster Lord. Furthermore, in the Monster World, she was a well-known princess.

He'd figured that if the Monster Lord didn’t want everyone to know about his traitorous behavior, he wouldn’t lay his hands on Salamay. But the Monster Lord was more despicable than he'd expected. 

He had always doted on his daughter, so he couldn’t turn a blind eye to this.

“Let her go!” 

The Lord of Slaughter’s roar caused all the monsters in the hall to tremble; it was as if they were confronted with an enraged primal beast. The terrifying sonic boom sent the commanders holding Salamay hostage into shock, causing them to lose control over their bodies. If not for the Lord of Slaughter holding back for fear of hurting his daughter, they would have been reduced to bloody mist by now. He made use of the opportunity to fly over to rescue his daughter. 

But the Monster Lord wouldn’t give him a chance to do that. He persistently stuck to the Lord of Slaughter as if he was the latter's shadow.

Even though the Lord of Slaughter was stronger than the Monster Lord, the gap wasn’t significant enough for him to quickly suppress the latter. With the Monster Lord persistently hounding him, he wasn’t free to save Salamay. 

“Master, why are you in such a rush? Aren’t you snubbing me too much?” the Monster Lord sneered. “Master, why don’t you surrender? I promise to spare you and the little princess. Otherwise, I can’t guarantee the little princess’ safety.”

The Lord of Slaughter was both alarmed and furious. If he ignored the Monster Lord to save his daughter, the chances were that the latter would deal him a heavy blow. That would be the end of him. But if he didn’t head there now, the opportunity he had created with great difficulty would be squandered. The other party wouldn’t give him such a chance again. 

All of a sudden, another figure charged at Salamay. 

“Warped Monster!” The nearby Scorpion King and Thousand-Footed Elder noticed the other party and rushed forward to block his path. 

However, the Warped Monster squeezed through the gap between the two of them like a slippery snake, charging straight toward Salamay. The commanders holding her hostage were just starting to recover from their shock when he snapped their necks.

“Princess!” The Warped Monster picked up Salamay and freed her from her restraints. 

“Uncle Warped!” Salamay was moved too. 

The Shadow Monster King sneered, “You’re courting death!” His eyes glowed as two rays of light shot toward the Warped Monster, who froze on the spot.

It was the Shadow Monster King’s ultimate skill—Paralysis Ray!

A massive corpse ball rolled up to the Warped Monster, and the countless skeletons stuck to the ball gnawed at him. It was the Manipulator of Death!

Even though the Warped Monster was paralyzed by the Shadow Monster King, as one of the Eighteen Monster Generals, he had his own survival methods. His body warped once again, causing the attacks to miss his vitals. 

However, he was unable to dodge the attacks coming from his left. The massive corpse ball managed to latch onto him, and a miserable cry followed. The entire left side of his body vanished into thin air, and his exposed innards wriggled. A closer look at his wounds revealed bite marks, as if they had been chomped off by countless mouths.

The Lord of Slaughter was infuriated. He shook off the Monster Lord and hurled a punch at the Manipulator of Death. The latter's countless mouths shrieked in agony as the sheer force of the punch exploded his countless corpses. 

However, the Lord of Slaughter’s face still looked grim. He knew that the Manipulator of Death was made out of countless corpses, and he had incredible regenerative abilities. Despite having received a heavy blow, it wouldn’t be long before he recovered.

The Lord of Slaughter thus quickly cast a skill on the Warped Monster, allowing his ruined left body to regrow flesh and blood. “Take Salamay away!” he shouted as he stood in front of the Warped Monster to block the onslaught of the Monster Lord, Shadow Monster King, War Priest, and the other experts. 

“Master, you should run! I’ll cover your escape. Rally your army to eliminate these traitors,” the Warped Monster said anxiously. 

That was indeed the Lord of Slaughter’s initial plan, but he laughed. “You don’t have the means to cover my retreat. Let me do it…”

He was about to say that he could easily find an opening to escape when his face suddenly darkened. Without any hesitation, he thrust a palm strike behind himself. 

The Warped Monster spurted blood as he was sent flying, but there was a smirk on his face as he gleefully waved a pitch-black spike. 

The Lord of Slaughter felt a numbing sensation spreading from the back of his waist to the rest of his body. He turned his sharp eyes toward the Scorpion King. “Your stinger!”

The Scorpion King snickered. “Master, there are few in the Myriad Worlds who can withstand my stinger’s poison. How does it feel?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2504: Someone Who Shouldn’T Be There
chapters.body:
Chapter 2504: Someone Who Shouldn’T Be There

Zu An was surprised. He had also collected the Scorpion King’s stinger after killing him back then. He had known that the stinger contained a lethal poison, but he hadn’t expected it to be so potent as to even get the better of the Lord of Slaughter.

“Very well, very well!” The Lord of Slaughter’s complexion looked awful. “You have planned this out meticulously. Not only did the Scorpion King hand his greatest weapon to someone else, but you even put on a perfect show to deceive me. I am impressed.”

The Monster Lord smiled. “Master, you have always been mistrustful. We had to go this far to convince you.”

He thus surrounded the Lord of Slaughter with his subordinates while waiting for the poison to spread through the latter’s body. He opted for a defensive posture lest the Lord of Slaughter dragged one of them down with him in his desperate counterattack.

The Lord of Slaughter turned to the Warped Monster and asked, “What did that fellow promise you to turn against me?”

The Warped Monster had not only lost half of his body, but also faced simultaneous attacks from the Shadow Monster King and the Manipulator of Death. Had the Lord of Slaughter not intervened, he would have been dead. Even so, after losing half his body and receiving such a heavy blow, he would be weakened for many years.

Thus, the Lord of Slaughter couldn’t understand what the Warped Monster was after.

The Warped Monster wiped off his blood and chuckled. “Master, you are cruel and despotic. You are nowhere as virtuous as the Monster Lord. When so many of your old subordinates have turned against you, shouldn’t you reflect on yourself…”

Before he could finish speaking, the Lord of Slaughter rushed up to him and smashed a palm down on his head.

The Warped Monster was horrified. He anxiously lifted Little Salamay up as a shield while warping his body to dodge the attack. However, due to his severe injuries, his reaction was much slower than usual. There was no way he could dodge the Lord of Slaughter’s vengeful attack.

Pah pah pah!

The Warped Monster was shredded apart before exploding into a bloody mist. The Lord of Slaughter took this opportunity to snatch Little Salamay back from his hands. It happened so fast that no one had time to react.

The Monster Lord was stunned. “Master’s cultivation is unrivaled in this world. You remain as valiant as ever despite being inflicted with lethal poison. But channeling your bloodline power will only cause the poison to spread faster. Is there a need for that?”

“Traitors have to die!” the Lord of Slaughter spat with gritted teeth. Due to the effects of the poison, however, his breathing sounded more ragged. 

The Monster Generals surrounded him while keeping their distance. Despite knowing that the Lord of Slaughter was severely poisoned, none of them dared to confront him. The Monster Lord had won this battle, but anyone who made a reckless move at this juncture could end suffering the same fate as the Warped Monster. 

“Daddy, are you okay?” Little Salamay asked tearfully as she anxiously grabbed the Lord of Slaughter’s robe. 

Zu An, who had been watching from the shadows, was perplexed. At this point, the Lord of Slaughter’s defeat was guaranteed, but in history, the Fiend races had suppressed him under the Sealed Land’s snow mountain. Also, according to history, Salamay was supposed to have escaped. 

Furthermore, when he first came into contact with her, she'd been deceived by the Monster Lord, and it was only later that she learned about her father. But now that she had witnessed everything, how could she still be deceived by the Monster Lord even if the latter spared her life?

Were her memories altered by some method?

Zu An was worried that the alteration in history could affect those around him. 

“Rest assured, I’m fine,” the Lord of Slaughter replied with a strained smile as he gently patted his daughter’s hand. Then, he turned to his enemies. 

How can I escape? I'm at a disadvantage here, but I can put up one last fight. I can sacrifice my commanders to stall the enemies while I escape from here. If I gather my old subordinates, there's still a chance I can turn the tables around…

While he was thinking of a way to survive this ordeal, his body suddenly shuddered. He looked down at his chest. A bloodied little hand had pierced through it to grab his heart.

All the Monster Generals were stunned. They couldn’t understand what was going on.

The Lord of Slaughter arduously turned his head around, only to see little Salamay looking at him with an eerie smile. 

Zu An was speechless. This time, even he was confused as well. The future Salamay had always been saying that she would exact vengeance for her father, but it turned out that she was the culprit instead?

However, he quickly rejected that thought. He had crossed blows with the Lord of Slaughter in the Sealed Land, so there was no way the latter would die here. 

“Why?” the Lord of Slaughter asked as blood gushed out from his mouth, accompanied by bits of his innards. His eyes reflected his shock and sorrow. 

Instead of answering the question, little Salamay ripped out his heart and retreated from him. Her expression began to change. “In the end, the renowned Lord of Slaughter only amounts to this much.” Her physique began to enlarge as she transformed into a blue-skinned man.

Realization dawned upon the Lord of Slaughter. “The Lord of Myriad Transformations!” He turned to the Monster Lord and exclaimed, “So this is your true hand!”

The Monster Lord chuckled. “Master, you are indeed smart, but you realized it too late.”

The Lord of Slaughter sighed in desolation. “I hadn’t expected you could win over someone as elusive as the Lord of Myriad Transformations. I deserve my loss.”

Zu An was taken aback too. The Warped Monster’s betrayal was ingenious, but to think that even that was a feint! The true killing blow was the Lord of Myriad Transformations disguised as little Salamay!

But how would the Lord of Slaughter escape this?

“The heart of an expert. Ahh, I can’t wait!” The Lord of Myriad Transformations was so excited that he swallowed the heart on the spot. 

The other Monster Generals looked at him in envy, knowing that he would grow much stronger assimilating such a powerful heart. 

The Lord of Myriad Transformations initially had a look of absolute bliss, but a hint of confusion soon surfaced in his eyes. “Hm?”

“What’s wrong?” The Monster Lord frowned. He instinctively sensed that a variable beyond his control had emerged, and he would hate it if anything went wrong at this point.

“I can’t put my finger on it.” The Lord of Myriad Transformations was perplexed. “I should have eaten his heart, but it doesn’t feel like it. It somehow feels… empty.”

The Lord of Slaughter harrumphed. A peculiar light suddenly shone from his body as a heart reappeared in his previously empty chest, and then his wounds began healing at a visible pace. 

“Quick, let’s attack him together! He might have a way to retrieve his heart, but he should now be in his weakest ever state!” The Monster Lord lost his composure. He didn’t dare to hold back in case something went wrong.

Just then, a woman’s voice echoed from the entrance. “Bastard Zu, save my father right now if you don’t want anything to happen to your women!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2505: Threat
chapters.body:
Chapter 2505: Threat

Zu An was stunned. He had been leisurely watching the show while wondering how the Lord of Slaughter would escape from this deathtrap. He hadn’t expected to be dragged into the conflict. 

The monsters in the hall were taken aback. They turned toward the source of the voice and saw a man and a woman standing at the entrance. 

The Monster Lord was taken aback to see the man. It’s Mojard?

Many years back, Mojard had been an expert who stood on the same pedestal as him. Even though the Monster Lord’s cultivation had advanced significantly in recent years, he was still wary of Mojard. 

I thought he died against the Ocean races? Why is he here? He feels… different. He doesn’t have his usual confidence, and he looks much older. 

Who is the woman next to him? Why is she looking at me with eyes filled with hatred? She looks familiar, but I can’t put my finger on it.

…

The other monsters were confused too.

“Brother Mo!” The Lord of Slaughter was delighted to see Mojard, but at the same time, he felt regret. Many years ago, Mojard had warned him about the Monster Lord’s swelling ambitions, but he'd thought that Mojard was merely trying to put down his rival and ignored him. It was only now that he understood who was truly loyal to him.

After hearing about the latter's tragic defeat to the Ocean races, he'd thought that Mojard was dead, so he was heartened to see the other party safe and sound. 

“Incestuous King.”  When he saw Mojard, the Monster Lord initially thought everything had gone wrong, but with a quick scan using his divine sense, he realized that the two of them had come here alone. On top of that, Mojard’s aura was extremely withered. That put his mind at ease. “I was worried sick when I heard about your defeat against the Ocean races. It’s good to see you safe and sound.”

“You scum. Drop your hypocritical act!” Mojard’s complexion looked terrible. 

“Yes, you’re right. There’s no point continuing the act. I've been sick of it for a while.” The Monster Lord turned to the woman next to him and asked, “Who is this lady? She looks familiar.”

“Of course she looks familiar, for she is…” Mojard was about to reveal her identity when Salamay stopped him. Her coming from the future was a useful card, and she didn’t intend to squander it just like that. 

Salamay looked at the Lord of Slaughter with a rapidly beating heart. After so many years, she was finally able to see her father once more. However, she suppressed her excitement and instead looked around while shouting, “I know you’re here. Let’s all die together if you refuse to come out.”

Zu An reverted to his human form and slowly emerged from the shadows. He made sure to wear a mask to avoid any complications in the future.

The Monster Generals were startled. They hadn’t noticed a human hiding there. 

The Monster Lord was unsurprised. “I thought that master had an expert hidden in the shadows, but it looks like my worry was unfounded.”

He had been guarded against Zu An, worried that the latter would spring a surprise attack on him. That was why he hadn’t gone all out against the Lord of Slaughter. He was aware that this was the same person who had approached him for an alliance against the Lord of Slaughter.

Similarly, the Lord of Slaughter had thought that Zu An was the Monster Lord’s subordinate.

Due to their reservations, the two top experts had chosen not to reveal his existence despite both having noticed him.

Zu An ignored the two monsters and instead addressed Salamay with an awful look on his face. “How did you escape? Where’s the princess?”

Her presence here suggested that something had happened to Suolun Shi. To avoid affecting history, he took care not to reveal Suolun Shi’s name. 

“That foolish woman is in my hands,” Salamay replied with a gleeful smile. “And why are you only worried about her? You should also be worried about the others. You haven’t been able to contact them, right?” 

Zu An was taken aback. He hadn't received any messages from Pei Mianman, Ji Xiaoxi, and the others for quite a while. Ji Xiaoxi has Jiang Luofu and Yun Yuqing with her, so they should have no trouble protecting themselves. 

That thought allowed him to calm down a little. “You’re trying to trick me. I won’t fall for it.”

“You can believe whatever you want to. Just don’t regret it when the time comes.” Salamay was in no hurry to explain herself. She seemed to be confident. 

Zu An frowned. Could she have something to do with Manman’s disappearance?

“Give me more details, and I’ll decide whether to help or not.”

Salamay’s expression turned cold. “You don’t have a choice, whether it’s for your lovers or to ensure that everything… corresponds with the future.”

Zu An understood what she was driving at. In their world’s history, the Lord of Slaughter didn’t die in the Monster Lord’s hands. Conversely, that meant he had to ensure that the Lord of Slaughter survived this ordeal to prevent history from affecting the future. And the only one who had the power to pull that off now was him. 

He was dazed for a moment. It was often said that history shaped the future, but why did it look as if history was determined by the future too?

“Who are you?” The Monster Lord’s face quivered. He had never been ignored like that before. 

Zu An smiled. “We're merely passers-by.”

“Passers-by or not, you aren’t getting away scot-free!” The Monster Lord shot a glance at his subordinates, and the Demonic Motherbug and Thousand-Footed Elder, who were nearest to Zu An, lunged at him from his flanks.

The Thousand-Footed Elder had a head on each of his his feet, and these heads simultaneously opened their mouths and spewed a poisonous black fog at Zu An from all directions, shrouding him in it in an instant. 

The other Monster Generals breathed a sigh of relief. The Thousand-Footed Elder was a giant centipede, and his black fog contained lethal poison. Even the other Monster Generals would be reduced to pus when enveloped in the black fog. 

However, their smiles quickly stiffened.

The thick black fog suddenly began dissipating. An instant later, the mysterious man dashed out of the black fog. There was a cold gleam, and dozens of the Thousand-Footed Elder’s feet were suddenly severed. The heads on the severed feet screamed in misery as they dropped to the ground before swiftly withering into bones and falling silent. 

The Demonic Motherbug launched her attack too. She was extremely fast, morphing into four silhouettes to attack Zu An from different directions at the same time. It looked as if red flashes of lightning were tearing through space. Despite having witnessed the setback faced by the Thousand-Footed Elder, she wasn’t worried in the slightest. Her talent put her in an increasingly advantageous position the longer the battle dragged out. As she generated more clones, she would eventually become undefeatable. The only way to defeat her was to simultaneously destroy all her clones. 

The Lord of Slaughter and the Monster Lord might be able to pull that off, but they don’t know that the key is to destroy all my clones at once. This fellow doesn’t feel strong. I doubt that he can end me instantaneously.

As soon as those thoughts surfaced in her mind, she suddenly found herself faced with a massive black fog. That was the Thousand-Footed Elder’s poison!

Her surroundings were immediately enveloped by the poison. She couldn’t avoid it entirely no matter how fast she moved. As a result, all four of her clones plummeted from the sky and dropped dead. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2506: Chaotic Battle
chapters.body:
Chapter 2506: Chaotic Battle

All the monsters in the hall couldn’t believe what they were seeing. Both the Thousand-Footed Elder and the Demonic Motherbug were top experts of the Monster World, so how could they be defeated in an instant?

In particular, the Demonic Motherbug was so strong that she was close to coming on par with the War Priest and the Shadow Monster King. She was well-known to be a difficult opponent. The longer the battle dragged out, the stronger she became. And yet, she was defeated right away. That was a feat only the Lord of Slaughter and the Monster Lord were only supposed to be capable of.

The Shadowhorror Devil was excited. He could sense the fear lingering in the air, and some of it came from experts of the same caliber as him. Fear from experts was the greatest tonic to him in the world. He felt better than ever. 

In contrast, the Monster Lord was frowning. Sensing his subordinates’ low morale, he roared, “That fellow has an evil art that allows him to absorb energy. Be careful!”

Those words snapped the Monster Generals out of their daze. Now that they thought about it, the other party had merely absorbed the Thousand-Footed Elder’s poison fog and used it against the Demonic Motherbug. The Demonic Motherbug had been caught off guard and fallen for it. 

The Demonic Motherbug must have been vulnerable to area-of-effect attacks right from the start. Now we know how to deal with her in the future.

The monsters smiled in glee. They were on the same side now, but they were still rivals. That was why they avoided revealing their trump cards and weaknesses, lest the others take advantage of them. It was a good thing for them to learn about a renowned expert’s weakness.

Salamay looked at Zu An in awe. He’s a capable one. It’s a pity I can’t use him… 

The Lord of Slaughter was delighted by the twist in the situation. He'd thought that he was doomed, but several helpers had suddenly appeared out of nowhere. 

Where did Mojard find this helper from? Who is that woman?

Most important of all, who is that mysterious expert? His strength appears to be be on the same level as the Eighteen Monster Generals, yet he’s able to curb them with ease. It’s almost as if he knew about their weaknesses beforehand. 

The Monster Lord noticed the same thing too. He turned to his remaining subordinates and warned them with a frown, “Something is off about that fellow. Don’t let your guard down. Attack him together and bring him down as soon as possible.”

He had to watch the Lord of Slaughter to stop the latter from escaping. Even though the Lord of Slaughter had sustained severe injuries from the earlier surprise attack, the Monster Generals still weren’t a match for him. Thus, he couldn’t make a reckless move. 

Quite a few top experts had been taken out, leaving just the Shadow Monster King, War Priest, Shadowhorror Devil, Doomsday Spirit, and a few others. After seeing how easily he'd defeated the Thousand-Footed Elder and the Demonic Motherbug, they were hesitant to fight Zu An one-on-one. More than that, they were worried that they would be forced to reveal their trump cards. That would be a huge loss to them.

The monsters exchanged looks and soon arrived at a consensus. They would attack Zu An together and settle this as quickly as possible.

The Shadow Monster King was the first one to make a move. He floated into the air and began emitting light like a miniature sun, but unlike a sun, his body at the very center was pitch black. 

The black sun suddenly shot out two beams of light. Many in the hall recognized it to be the Shadow Monster King’s ultimate skill—Paralysis Ray! Those struck by it would find their bodies stiffening up, rendering them completely helpless. 

Wary of the bizarre movement skill Zu An displayed earlier, the Shadow Monster King utilized soulspeak on top of that. “This move will hit its target.” He wasn’t afraid of letting others know about his soulspeak, as some here already knew about it, and his soulspeak was inferior to Mojard’s anyway. 

Zu An’s movement immediately halted, allowing the two Paralysis Rays to strike him squarely. 

Salamay was appalled. Why is that fellow so useless? He fell for the Shadow Monster King’s tricks so easily! Isn’t he a tenacious bastard who even killed the Monster Lord?

Zu An had clashed with the Shadow Monster King in the future, so he knew about the other party’s tricks. He could have used ‘Whatcha lookin’ at’ to interrupt the other party’s soulspeak, but he decided against that. He was concerned that the Shadow Monster King and the Monster Lord would recognize him right away as soon as he used the skill against them in the future. He was weaker back when he faced them, and their heightened vigilance could make things dangerous for him. Thus, he'd decided to take on the other party’s control skill instead. 

The Monster Lord’s group was delighted to see that. The Shadowhorror Devil and the Doomsday Spirit charged at Zu An. It was the perfect opportunity for them to snatch the kill while the latter was paralyzed by the Shadow Monster King. This would give them more leverage later when discussing contributions and rewards. 

The Shadowhorror Devil briefly paused halfway through his charge, as he couldn’t sense any fear from Zu An. This left him confused. He still isn’t panicking yet. Does this mean he has other tricks up his sleeves?

Out of vigilance, he slowed down and allowed the Doomsday Spirit to overtake him. He was thinking of using the latter as fodder to probe the other party’s hand for him. 

The Doomsday Spirit did notice the Shadowhorror Devil’s little movement. He’s still as cowardly as ever, even at this point. Looks like I’ll be getting this head. 

All of a sudden, however, he heard a bell, and it induced inexplicable fear in his heart. 

Isn’t that the Shadowhorror Devil’s renowned artifact—the Horror Bell? Why is he making a move against me? 

Ah! He must be a spy from the Lord of Slaughter. That’s why he’s turning against me at this critical moment! No wonder he intentionally slowed down. He was planning to get behind me so he could cooperate with that mysterious man to launch a pincer attack on me!

That raised the Doomsday Spirit’s fear. Without any warning, he turned around and unleashed his attack on the Shadowhorror Devil instead.

The abrupt attack startled the Shadowhorror Devil. He anxiously put up his defense while exclaiming, “What are you doing? Are you out of your mind?”

“I’m out of my mind?” The Doomsday Spirit was infuriated too. “You traitor who defected to the Lord of Slaughter! How dare you turn against us right now?!”

The Shadowhorror Devil was stumped. “What nonsense are you spouting? Why would I defect to the Lord of Slaughter’s side?”

Even the Lord of Slaughter was dumbstruck. Huh, is the Shadowhorror Devil on my side? Why don’t I know that?

The Monster Lord frowned. “Do you have evidence, Doomsday Spirit?”

“Why don’t you ask him where his Horror Bell is?” the Doomsday Spirit roared. 

“Of course it’s with…” The Shadowhorror Devil was about to take out his artifact when he suddenly froze in place. Eh? Where’s my Horror Bell?

That evoked the Doomsday Spirit’s sneer, prompting him to attack with greater fervor.

Zu An smirked. He dared to take the Shadow Monster King’s Paralysis Ray head-on because he had made preparations beforehand. He had killed the future Shadowhorror Devil and obtained the Horror Bell from him. He usually didn’t have much use for the Horror Bell, but it was the perfect tool to divide the Monster Generals in this tense moment.

However, he was surprised to see the Shadowhorror Devil unable to produce his own Horror Bell. Could it be that duplicates of an item can't exist at the same point in spacetime, and the moment one is taken out, the others will disappear?

While Zu An was wondering about that, a desolate war horn echoed. Countless cavalrymen appeared out of nowhere and charged at him. 

The shocked monsters in the hall hurriedly leaned against the walls to dodge the cavalrymen’s charge. Not even the Lord of Slaughter and the Monster Lord would confront a charging army of such scale. 

With the cover of his comrade, the War Priest had unleashed his ultimate skill—Invincible Army! 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2507: Closing The Spacetime Loop
chapters.body:
Chapter 2507: Closing The Spacetime Loop

Confronting thousands of charging cavalrymen was a truly terrifying prospect. Even the Lord of Slaughter’s surviving commanders despaired at the situation. 

The War Priest required a long channeling time to summon his army, but if he succeeded, he could easily crush any enemy. The only one who could potentially be able to escape from his army was the Lord of Slaughter. The others wouldn’t stand a chance.

The Monster Lord felt chills. The War Priest is too powerful once he unleashes his full prowess. I must never let him summon his army if I ever fight with him in the future.

By the time the cavalrymen charged over, however, Zu An had already regained his mobility. He brought out his Human Emperor Seal. The golden seal revolved as it floated into the air, and then suddenly enlarged until it was the size of a mountain and crashed down on the cavalrymen. 

Boom!

A loud explosion ensued. The cavalrymen at the forefront were crushed into mincemeat. 

“Human aura?” The Monster Lord frowned. He sensed emperor ki from the seal, and it made him wonder if the humans of this world had a Human Emperor or something. 

However, he quickly rejected that guess. The emperor ki was weak, which showed that its connection with the humans of this world was faint. It hadn’t received the recognition of the humans. Nevertheless, it still showed that this mysterious man had connections with the human race. 

“Don’t worry, his seal doesn’t contain enough faith. Its prowess is lacking,” the Monster Lord said upon seeing the War Priest lurching in shock.

The War Priest snapped out of his shock and chuckled. “I was wondering why that thing felt off. Turns out it only looks formidable on the surface.”

He waved a flag, and the formation masters in his army unleashed their skills on the massive Human Emperor Seal. A black aura enveloped the Human Emperor Seal, and the ground under it turned into a black bog. It was as if the area had turned into a marsh. The Human Emperor Seal began sinking into it.

Zu An sighed. The Human Emperor Seal lacks legitimacy here, so it’s unable to unleash its true prowess. 

The corruption of the Human Emperor Seal was eroding his connection with it, so he quickly took it back without any hesitation. 

The War Priest lamented Zu An’s swift reaction. Just a bit more, and I could have erased the seal’s divine sense imprint and taken it for myself. He could tell that the seal was growing, and once it matured, it could potentially exert prowess comparable to a deity-grade artifact. 

At this point, he decided to stop holding back. He waved his flag once more, and his army encircled Zu An. Even though his vanguard had suffered heavy losses due to the bizarre seal, he could easily conjure more soldiers as long as his army formation wasn’t breached. Even ants could devour an elephant in sufficient quantity, let alone his strong soldiers.

Seeing Zu An in a predicament, the Lord of Slaughter advised him, “You have to defeat the War Priest himself, or else he can resummon his army.”

He couldn’t let his powerful helper die just like that. Unfortunately, he was severely injured by the Warped Devil and the Lord of Myriad Transformations, and the Monster Lord was keeping a close eye on him now. He couldn’t intervene in Zu An’s battle. 

Hearing his father’s concern for that fellow, Salamay’s eyes drooped. Father doesn’t know how powerful he is. He’s strong enough to slay the Monster Lord, so how could the War Priest stand in his way?

In the entire hall, it was probably her who had the most confidence in Zu An. The only worry was that this world didn’t have world laws that imposed restrictions on intruders, so the Monster Lord and his subordinates were far stronger than they were in the future. This could lead to variables.

In the center of attention, Zu An calmly faced the army charging toward him. With a flick of his hand, a turbid yellow water droplet appeared on his palm. There was a hint of blood red hidden in the droplet. 

“Go!”

With a wave of Zu An’s hand, the Oblivion River Water flew out and quickly expanded into a stream, then a river, and finally a massive tsunami. 

The charging army didn’t have time to dodge, although it wasn't as if they planned to. Why would they dodge when they were strong enough to disperse even a rapid river?

Only the War Priest felt uneasy. Looking at the turbid yellow water tinged with bloody red, he was suddenly reminded of the water from the legends. He anxiously waved his flag to order his army to avoid the tsunami, but it was already too late.

The Oblivion River Water washed through the hall, devouring the War Priest’s army. Some of his soldiers weren’t afraid of the tsunami at first because they were adept in water skills. However, when they came into contact with the Oblivion River Water, their bodies jolted and they forgot all their skills. Their memories had faded into oblivion. 

The War Priest’s army was devastated.

Some of the Lord of Slaughter’s commanders and the Monster Lord’s subordinates failed to dodge in time and were touched by the Oblivion River Water, causing them to lose their wits and become floating corpses.

“It’s the Oblivion River!” The Monster Lord recognized the origin of the water. Even with his strength, he had no choice but to dodge the tsunami.

The Monster Generals fled in a fluster, having heard about the Oblivion River Water’s legend. 

Salamay and Mojard seized the opening to cover the Lord of Slaughter and escort him to Zu An’s side. 

The Monster Lord’s face darkened. The only silver lining was that he had sealed off the entrance, so the Lord of Slaughter wouldn’t be able to escape just yet. However, if the Oblivion River continued rampaging, more variables could emerge. 

Thus, the Monster Lord tossed a copper coin toward the raging Oblivion River Water. It was none other than the Treasure Sinking Coin. 

Zu An had been curious to know what would happen if he took out the Treasure Sinking Coin before the Monster Lord, but out of consideration for the future, he decided against it. 

However, the Monster Lord seemed to be lacking understanding of the Treasure Sinking Coin. This artifact could only nullify created artifacts; it was completely useless against intrinsic items. The Oblivion River Water was an intrinsic item. 

Zu An suddenly recalled Mi Li’s talk about spacetime. This was a good opportunity, so he pretended that the Treasure Sinking Coin had worked on the Oblivion River Water and severed his connection with it. Without his control, the rampaging Oblivion River Water reverted to its initial droplet, and by some stroke of fate, it landed in the War Priest’s vicinity.

Without any hesitation, the War Priest rolled up his sleeves and stowed away the droplet. He nearly burst into laughter, gladdened by the windfall. This mysterious fellow must have beaten me to that dangerous dungeon, but his gains only ended up benefiting me. 

The other Monster Generals looked at the War Priest in envy. They had suffered heavy losses, whereas the War Priest had gained something valuable instead. 

Even the Monster Lord looked at the War Priest with envious eyes. He was interested in the Oblivion River Water, and he hadn’t expected it to fall into the War Priest’s hands. It was a pity he still needed the Monster Generals, so he couldn’t snatch it in public, lest he lose their support. 

Salamay scoffed at Zu An. How can he be this careless as to lose such a valuable treasure?

The Lord of Slaughter consoled Zu An, saying, “Don’t worry, my friend. As long as you escort me to safety, I’ll find an even more formidable artifact for you in the future.”

Zu An smiled. He had done all that merely to ‘return the item to its master’. Only by entrusting the Oblivion River Water to the War Priest now could he snatch it away from the War Priest in the future. Through this, he was closing the spacetime loop.

“Let’s get him now that he doesn’t have the Oblivion River Water anymore. We mustn’t give them any chance to make a comeback!” the Monster Lord roared as he charged at the Lord of Slaughter. 

The other Monster Generals charged at Zu An, hoping to get something on par with the Oblivion River Water from him. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2508: Losing The Catch And The Bait
chapters.body:
Chapter 2508: Losing The Catch And The Bait

Both sides’ subordinates had been mostly wiped out by the Oblivion River Water. The only ones alive were experts of the Eighteen Monster Generals’ caliber. 

But despite the fewer monsters present, the battle only grew more intense. There had been too many distractions earlier, and some of the experts had been using the cover of the commotion to slack off. But now that the distractions were eliminated and both sides were done probing each other’s capabilities, it was time for them to get serious.

The Monster Lord was no longer holding back; every attack he unleashed was a killing blow. He was determined to kill the Lord of Slaughter for good. When everything had been under his control, he'd prioritized safety and opted for a war of attrition to wear down the latter. However, the appearance of this mysterious but powerful expert left him with a feeling of danger. 

If he manages to rescue the Lord of Slaughter, it will be the worst-case scenario.

The Lord of Slaughter had ruled for so long that he had many old subordinates loyal to him. Furthermore, those in the middle and lowest echelons, oblivious to his true nature, revered him. It had taken the Monster Lord years of scheming to dispatch the Lord of Slaughter’s subordinates to rural places, thus temporarily isolating the latter. But if the Lord of Slaughter escaped and gathered his old subordinates, the tables could turn. Considering what was at stake, the Monster Lord didn’t dare to hold back anymore.

The Lord of Slaughter charged at the Monster Lord with a furious roar. His hatred for the Monster Lord ran so deep that he wanted nothing more than to rip the latter to shreds. Unfortunately, he had suffered two consecutive blows, such that he struggled to even protect himself from the Monster Lord. It would be hard for him to take down the Monster Lord here. 

Seeing the two bosses going all out, the Monster Generals felt a tremendous boost in morale. They unleashed their strongest skills, and even those who were severely injured climbed to their feet to help out. Experts of their caliber, as long as they weren’t killed on the spot, boasted incredible regenerative prowess. By now, those who had been injured were ready to fight once more. 

Of course, the Shadowhorror Devil and the Doomsday Spirit were still fighting with each other and didn’t have the time to join the battle.

The Monster Generals wouldn’t dare to intervene in the battle between the Monster Lord and the Lord of Slaughter. Now that the Lord of Slaughter was a beast trapped in a cage, there was no telling what he could do out of desperation. It was unwise to provoke him right now.

Thus, they turned their attention to Salamay. They didn’t know who she was, though she had mentioned ‘Father’ when she first entered. It was perplexing, as they knew that the Lord of Slaughter didn’t have a daughter of that age better than anyone else. 

The stronger a cultivator was, the lower the leakage of their spirit would be, thus making it tougher for them to have a child. The Lord of Slaughter only had an extremely young daughter, and the Monster Lord had stationed her tens of thousands of kilometers away from here. So, who was this woman?

In any case, her weakness made her the perfect target.

Mojard tried his best to protect Salamay, but with his current strength, he was no match for so many Monster Generals. Soon, he started receiving injuries.

“Hm? When did you become so weak, Incestuous King?”

“Where’s the soulspeak you’re so proud of?”

“Is he feigning weakness?”

… 

The Monster Generals were perplexed, but they weren’t too worried. They had the absolute advantage here. It wouldn’t change a thing even if Mojard was feigning weakness. 

“Save me!” Salamay was no weakling, but this was a battle involving the top experts of the Monster World. She was severely outclassed here.

The Lord of Slaughter didn't know her identity, but he could tell she was on her side. Furthermore, he felt an inexplicable sense of intimacy toward her, so he howled in her direction.

A terrifying sonic boom jolted the Monster Generals’ bodies, causing their blood flow to go into disarray. That gave Mojard and Salamay a chance to regain their footing. 

The Monster Lord harrumphed. “I’m your opponent!”

He summoned a three-headed bird to attack the Lord of Slaughter. 

The Lord of Slaughter’s eyes reddened. He revealed his true body, which resembled an enlarged T-rex, and both of them fought each other even more intensely. Wherever they passed by, the sculptures in the Imperial Tomb shattered into dust. 

Surprisingly, the Fiend God sculpture and the Imperial Tomb’s overall structure remained untouched despite the powerful shockwaves emanated by the two bosses. The Monster Lord and the Lord of Slaughter couldn’t resist looking at the towering Fiend God sculpture, wondering if there was a secret hidden in it. However, this wasn’t the time for their minds to be wandering. They had to prioritize eliminating the enemy before them first.

Without the Lord of Slaughter’s help, Mojard and Salamay soon fell into a disadvantageous position once more. Salamay could only shout for Zu An to help her.

Zu An dodged the Shadow Monster King’s attacks while replying telepathically, “Tell me how they're faring, and I’ll save you.”

Salamay understood that she couldn’t reveal her relationship with the Lord of Slaughter, or else the enemy would use her as a hostage to threaten her father. She gritted her teeth in anger, but this brief moment of hesitation caused another attack to land on her. Left with no choice, she replied telepathically, “Don’t worry, Suolun Shi is still alive. I merely temporarily imprisoned her elsewhere. If you don’t save me, she'll go down with me.”

“What about the others?” Zu An remained unmoved.

“I don’t know their whereabouts. I was lying,” Salamay replied helplessly.

Zu An had figured as much, and he breathed a sigh of relief. That being said, he couldn’t turn a blind eye to Suolun Shi’s plight, so he performed an instantaneous movement to appear at Salamay’s side and dragged her away from the Scorpion King’s incoming giant pincers. 

Before the two of them could exchange words, a flash of light appeared beside Zu An. “Your opponent is me!”

The light and shadow surrounding the Shadow Monster King began to distort. He was confident that he was within the top three of the Monster World. With the downfall of the Incestuous King, he even thought about having a showdown with the Monster Lord. His confidence was rooted in his ability to slow down the time around him. The space around him was folded to such an extent that his opponent couldn't attack his main body. That made him undefeatable. On top of that, he had another trump card up his sleeves. 

He'd noticed that when the War Priest acquired his droplet of Oblivion River Water, Zu An didn’t look regretful at all. That suggested the latter had more valuable artifacts with him. He was determined to acquire them while the Monster Lord was distracted, or else he wouldn’t get this chance again. 

As the Shadow Monster King pressed closer, Zu An remained on his guard, knowing that he was a fearsome adversary. Fortunately, there was an information disparity between both sides. Zu An knew the tricks the former had up his sleeves. 

Breaching the Shadow Monster King’s distorted space was easy enough for him—the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra did the job perfectly. That was how he'd pull it off in the future. But to avoid digging holes for himself in the future, he had to give up on that easy solution.

It was a good thing that Zu An's grasp of spatial transformations had greatly deepened. He unleashed the Star Shattering Imprint, using his tenfold attack power to penetrate the folded space around the Shadow Monster King to strike the latter’s main body. 

The Shadow Monster King was horrified. He couldn’t understand how Zu An’s attack had reached him. But there was no time for him to think further. He could already feel death encroaching on him. Without any hesitation, he activated his survival skill—Time Severing—to stop this deadly attack. Then, he took this opportunity to quickly retreat. Fear lingered on his face, and he hesitated to confront Zu An once more. 

What’s with that fellow? How is he this formidable? More importantly, it feels as if he knows my abilities.

It’s a huge loss this time. Not only did I fail to acquire any of his artifacts, but I ended up using Time Severing once. I can now only use it once more. 

He felt so regretful that he thought his intestines would turn green. 

Zu An was startled. When he'd first fought the Shadow Monster King in the Sealed Land, the other party had revealed that he had once encountered a powerful enemy and was forced to use Time Severing, resulting in him only having one use left. Could that powerful enemy be me?

A blue light suddenly glowed from the hall, and a massive formation emerged from beneath the crowd’s feet. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2509: Gate Of World Crossing
chapters.body:
Chapter 2509: Gate Of World Crossing

“What’s going on?” The Monster Generals were stunned. 

Did the Lord of Slaughter prepare a trap beforehand? But we already defeated his commanders and unraveled his formations. 

The blue light enveloped Zu An, the Lord of Slaughter, Salamay, and Mojard. 

“This isn’t good. It’s a teleportation formation!” The Monster Lord recognized the nature of the light right away and thrust his palm on the ground. A destructive force rippled forth, causing the blue formation to flicker. 

Mojard anxiously pushed Salamay toward the Lord of Slaughter. “Master, you leave. I’ll cover your back!”

“Brother Mo!”

“Uncle Mo!”

The Lord of Slaughter and Salamay were horrified. 

Mojard looked at them with a smile. “Otherwise, none of us will be able to get away. To be able to help master and first miss in my final moments, I have no regrets.”

He lunged forward to tightly embrace the Monster Lord. He used a secret art that intensified his aura severalfold, such that even the Monster Lord struggled to shake him off. 

“Let me go!” The Monster Lord was infuriated. One of his hands turned completely green and the other turned completely black as he smashed them ceaselessly into Mojard’s body. 

The areas struck by the green aura instantly decomposed until only bloodied white bones were left, whereas the areas struck by the black aura turned into puddles of pus. 

Normal enemies would have been destroyed by that, but Mojard had once an expert of the Monster Lord’s caliber. Even though he was severely weakened, the prowess he could exert out of desperation was still not to be underestimated. Lights surfaced from his body, swiftly healing the injured areas. 

Unfortunately, the Monster Lord attacked so quickly that the lights slowly dimmed. Mojard's rate of recovery was unable to catch up with the rate he was receiving damage. It wouldn’t be long before his body was reduced to a bloody mist. 

Tears streamed down Salamay’s eyes. The Lord of Slaughter’s eyes reddened as well. He felt bitter regret. Had he listened to the Incestuous King years ago, he would never have ended up in this state. 

Zu An was impressed. Mojard was truly loyal to the father and daughter, though Zu An wondered how much the latter's twisted love contributed toward that.

In the blink of an eye, all of them vanished into thin air. Before they knew it, they were already beside the lake in the Imperial Tomb. 

“Are you all right?” the Saintess asked anxiously. She only breathed a sigh of relief upon confirming that Zu An was safe and sound. 

“I’m fine. Were you the one who teleported us here?” Zu An quickly scanned his surroundings. There was no one on their tail yet. 

“Mmhm. I saw the Monster Lord bringing so many of his subordinates along and realized that I wouldn’t be able to make a difference in the battle. So, I took the time to construct a formation here instead.” The Saintess patted her chest in relief. “I’m glad you managed to hold on.” 

“You’re the Fiend races’ Saintess?” The Lord of Slaughter was astonished to see Xie Daoyun. He had naturally met the legendary Saintess before, considering how they had been adversaries for so many years. He'd never thought that the day when he was saved by her would come. 

The Saintess nodded, to which the Lord of Slaughter quickly replied, “I’ll remember this favor, Saintess. If I curb the rebellion, I’ll make peace with the Fiend races.”

Zu An and the Saintess didn’t think much of it. That old thing is just making empty promises. In any case, he was strongly suggesting for them to help him, since his promise could only be fulfilled if he emerged victorious. 

“We aren’t out of danger yet. We’re still inside the Imperial Tomb. It won’t be long before our enemies find us, and that would be the end of us,” the Saintess said. 

The Lord of Slaughter knew she was right. He had received severe injuries from the earlier two betrayals, and the subsequent battle with the Monster Lord only worsened his injuries. 

While the mysterious man was formidable, he was only marginally stronger than the Monster Generals. He was only able to get the better of the Monster Generals due to his special methods and the Monster Generals’ reluctance to reveal their aces. If it came down to a desperate fight, two or three Monster Generals could take him down.

Suddenly, there was a loud explosion in the distance, causing the Imperial Tomb to tremble. 

Tears trickled down Salamay’s eyes. “It’s Uncle Mo. He must have self-destructed.”

The Lord of Slaughter’s complexion looked awful. He was about to console her when a white light suddenly shone from her waist pouch. 

A bead flew out from the pouch. Mojard emerged from it and fell to the ground. His face was ghastly pale.

“Flesh Puppet?” The Lord of Slaughter quickly made sense of the situation. 

Mojard revealed a bitter smile. “Master is knowledgeable. Yes, this is my means of survival.” He then bowed apologetically to Salamay and said, “I seek first miss’ forgiveness for placing my life bead with you.”

Salamay was overjoyed. She quickly supported him to his feet. “There’s no need to seek my forgiveness. I’m more than happy to see you alive.”

The two of them had weathered storms together for many years. Mojard had continued to stay by her side even after she had fallen from grace. As a sentimental being, how could Salamay not have any feelings for him? She'd long been aware of Mojard’s feelings for her, but she'd feigned ignorance and instead used it to control him. However, his decision to sacrifice himself to buy time for their escape had moved her profoundly. She realized that she wasn’t as heartless as she thought.

“You call her first miss?” The Lord of Slaughter was perplexed.

“She is First Miss Salamay, albeit from the future. We are all from the future,” Mojard knew they were in a predicament, so he didn’t hide anything and quickly explained the situation. 

“Hahaha! No wonder I feel a sense of closeness to her! No wonder she called me father!” The Lord of Slaughter was overjoyed. He pulled Salamay into his embrace. “My good daughter, you have grown so big!”

“Father!” Salamay was moved too. She had been searching for a way to save her father for millennia now, and it was only now that they were finally reunited. 

The Saintess interjected with a cough. “I don’t want to interrupt your reunion, but our priority should be to escape from here. The Monster Lord will find this place very soon.”

The Imperial Tomb was filled with the Monster Lord’s subordinates. There was nowhere for them to escape to.

The Lord of Slaughter laughed. “I was hesitant, but since Brother Mo has returned alive and my good daughter has reunited with me, I shall take you to the legendary Celestial Court!”

Zu An was surprised. “You found a way into the Celestial Court?”

“Of course,” the Lord of Slaughter replied proudly. “The Monster Lord only got the better of me because I spent years devoted to investigating the secret of the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb. My effort wasn’t in vain.”

He turned to Zu An and the Saintess and said. “Even though you have just helped me, the Celestial Court is of grave importance. I can only take you with me if you promise to help me defeat the Monster Lord in the future.” 

Zu An and the Saintess exchanged looks. The Saintess told him telepathically, “Big brother, since we couldn’t find the Brilliant Pinflower in the Imperial Tomb, it might be in the Celestial Court instead.” 

Zu An had been planning to make another trip into the dungeon anyway, so he nodded. “All right.”

The Saintess also spoke up. “His will is my will.”

The Lord of Slaughter looked at Zu An in surprise. This human wields such influence over the Fiend races’ Saintess.

Salamay looked at Zu An in disdain. This fellow flirts with women no matter where he goes. 

Suddenly, the Monster Lord’s roar echoed. He was already not too far away from them.

Without any hesitation, the Lord of Slaughter took out a transparent box. Zu An and Salamay’s breathing quickened. They had seen this item before—it was the Nature Jade Case!

As he opened the box, their surrounding space rippled. The lake before them began revolving swiftly until a glowing door manifested. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2510: Anomaly
chapters.body:
Chapter 2510: Anomaly

“Master, we haven’t determined the victor in our battle. Why did you flee like a coward? If news of this gets out, you’ll become the world’s laughingstock.” The Monster Lord suddenly appeared in the distance, taunting the Lord of Slaughter as he swiftly approached. 

“Bastard!” The Lord of Slaughter was infuriated. He wanted to step forward to confront the Monster Lord. He had been a conqueror for a good half of his life; it had been a long time since he last suffered such a grievance. 

Salamay and Mojard quickly held him down. “Father (Master), you mustn’t fall for his provocation! You can always make a comeback in the future!”

The Lord of Slaughter took a deep breath. “Forget it. You went through so much trouble to find me. I can’t let your sacrifice be in vain.” He beckoned Zu An and the Saintess over. “Hurry up and enter the gate. It’ll close very soon.”

He grabbed his daughter’s hand and Mojard’s shoulder before charging into the light gate. 

Zu An grabbed the Saintess’ hand and said, “Hug me tightly.”

The Saintess was confused, but she still did as she was told with a reddened face. Ah Zu, this is too much. Do we need to get affectionate in public?

Just as they entered the gate, the massive energy ball hurled by the Monster Lord arrived and blasted the lake. Unfortunately, the gate had already disappeared by then.

The Shadow Monster King and the other Monster Generals arrived a second too late. Upon learning what had just happened, their faces darkened. They asked, “Master, what should we do?”

They were beginning to regret their decision. Despite having prepared such an elaborate trap, the Lord of Slaughter had still escaped from their encirclement. If the latter recovered and made a comeback, they would be in grave danger. They hadn’t forgotten how brutal the Lord of Slaughter had been over the years. 

The only one who was in a celebratory mood was the War Priest. He had made a killing by acquiring a droplet of the legendary Oblivion River Water. 

The Monster Lord could guess what the Monster Generals were thinking. “What are you panicking for? We’ll start by encircling the Imperial Tomb, then we'll take this time to purge that old thing’s loyal subordinates. Even if he escapes, what can he do alone?” 

“But that’s the Celestial Court! What if that old thing awakens his bloodline power inside? By then, we might not be a match for him even if we join forces,” the Shadowhorror Devil said, gulping in fear. 

The others shared the same worry. The Celestial Court was the Monster World’s most elusive legend. It was the dream of every monster, but it was a pity they'd missed this golden opportunity.

“How can a person grow stronger that easily? Don’t worry, everything is still within my grasp,” the Monster Lord said with a confident sneer. 

The Monster Generals exchanged looks. They initially thought that the Monster Lord was saying those words to console them, but his confident expression made them wonder if he had a trump card that they didn’t know about.

… 

Right after Zu An and the Saintess passed through the light gate, it vanished. The Monster Lord’s howl was vaguely audible behind them. 

The Saintess released Zu An’s waist, only to realize they were in the middle of a large body of water. There was nowhere for her to step on, causing her to plummet. She was horrified. She had never felt so helpless before. 

However, a warm hand grabbed her in time, keeping her from plummeting deeper. The water around her parted, creating a bubble-like space. 

Zu An chuckled. “I told you to hold me because there’s something weird about the water here.”

He'd remembered that what was on the other side of the lake was the legendary Weak Water, which was water that offered so little resistance that not even a feather would float in it. It was fortunate that the powerful Fiend Emperor had been with them, or else he, Yan Xuehen, Yun Jianyue, and Yu Yanluo would have drowned. Recalling the past brought a smile to his face. How I wish to return and see them again…

Just then, he sensed something was off. He lowered his head and saw the Saintess’ face was pale, and she was shivering a lot. He asked anxiously, “What’s wrong?”

“I’m afraid of water,” the Saintess replied with a strained smile. 

Zu An embraced her tightly. He patted her shoulder while consoling her. “Don’t worry, I have the power to control water now. I won’t let any water near you.”

The Saintess buried her head in his chest. It might have been his words or his warm embrace, but her shivering reduced. 

Zu An quickly ascended through the water together with her. In the past, he had been completely helpless against the Weak Water, but the growth in his cultivation and the water control ability he'd acquired from fusing the Crown of the Ocean Deity and Hundredwarble allowed him to deal with the Weak Water with ease. 

When he reached shore, he was surprised to find that the Lord of Slaughter and the others weren’t there. He searched the shore, but they were nowhere to be seen. He eventually turned his gaze back to the Weak Water. Did they drown in there? 

He quickly rejected that thought. The Lord of Slaughter would no doubt be strong enough to overcome the Weak Water, even if he was weakened. Besides, he had Mojard and Salamay to help him. 

“We might have been teleported to different places,” the Saintess said. Now that she was out of water, her complexion recovered. She sat on a stone next to the shore. The cold wind tugged at her clothes and left her hair unkempt, making her look a little pitiful. 

Now that he had grasped the secrets of spatial transformations, Zu An knew her words made sense. Given that they had entered the light gate at different times, it wouldn’t be surprising for them to have been teleported to different locations. The last time he came here, he and the others had been inside the Fiend Emperor’s sleeves, which was why they were teleported to the same destination. 

“What a pity. I wanted to get more information from Salamay.” Zu An sighed. He was curious how Salamay had escaped, and how Suolun Shi had been reduced from a warden to a prisoner.

“Don’t worry. Now that we're allies, she won’t hurt the princess. They still need our help to deal with the Monster Lord,” the Saintess consoled him.

“Let’s hope that’s the case.” Seeing that the Lord of Slaughter wasn’t here, Zu An grabbed the Saintess’ hand and led her deeper in. 

Along the way, he passed by familiar sights, such as the giant stone tortoise sculpture and the towering Feather Mountain. What surprised him, however, was the absence of the stone tablet carved with the words ‘Feather Abyss’.

He initially didn’t think much of it, but as he ventured deeper into Feather Mountain, his expression slowly turned grim. Why isn’t there resentment that's so intense it's almost corporeal?

Back when the Fiend Emperor forced him, Yan Xuehen, and the others to lead the way, they had begun sprouting black scales under the effects of the resentment. If they hadn’t taken turns holding onto Yu Yanluo’s Lord Sui’s Pearl, they would have become a part of the resentment too. 

“What’s wrong?” The Saintess noticed something amiss with Zu An’s expression.

Zu An didn’t reply. He grabbed her hand and began rushing up to the mountain peak. With his current cultivation, the aerial monsters no longer posed a threat to him. Soon, they arrived at the peak. 

Zu An was stunned. Where’s the Feather Mountain Spring Water?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2511: Appearance
chapters.body:
Chapter 2511: Appearance

Zu An had a deep impression of the Feather Mountain Spring Water. While it was incapable of truly reviving a dead person, its effects were very close to that. He had already consumed all the spring water he obtained the first time, and he often regretted not procuring more.

Now that he was here again, he'd wanted to gather more spring water. He was surprised to not find it here. 

“What are you looking for?” the Saintess asked.

Zu An told her about the Feather Mountain Spring Water. “I've noticed that there are many differences from the last time I entered this place. I’m even starting to wonder if it’s the same place.”

The Saintess chuckled. “Other than the points you have mentioned, the rest is the same, right?”

Zu An thought about it. “Yes, most things remain the same except for those details.”

“The divergence could be caused by the difference in spacetime.” By now, the Saintess had already recovered from the shock of falling in the water. “The last time you entered this dungeon was millennia in the future. Much could have changed in the meantime.”

Zu An thought that was a sloppy explanation, but he couldn’t find any explanations for the anomaly.

“Speaking of which, when talking about this dungeon, the Monster Lord and the Lord of Slaughter mentioned the Celestial Court. Where is the Celestial Court?” he wondered as he looked upward. He remembered seeing a brilliant light in the sky back then, and then the Celestial Emperor had gifted him the Sun Slaying Bow. If there was a Celestial Court here, that was the only sign of it he'd seen during that trip. 

Unfortunately, he'd seen neither the Celestial Emperor’s face, nor the Celestial Court. He only remembered that the sky had been covered in colorful, auspicious clouds. 

The Saintess was perplexed. “I don’t know why the monsters thought there was a Celestial Court here, but they must be disappointed now.”

Tens of thousands of kilometers away from where Zu An and the Saintess were, the Lord of Slaughter looked at the vast world before him in a daze. “Where’s the Celestial Court? Where’s the Celestial Court?!”

He had no idea what the Celestial Court looked like, but he didn’t think it would be like this. The world around him was completely barren. How could this be the Celestial Court?

Salamay also knew about the legends concerning the Celestial Court. “This world’s ki is richer than our original world. The Celestial Court might be hidden here somewhere.”

The Lord of Slaughter’s eyes lit up. “Yes, you’re right. I lost my nerve for a moment there. I should have thought about that. Let’s take a good look around. We should be able to find the Celestial Court.”

Salamay nodded. From time to time, her eyes would scan the surroundings.

“Are you looking for the Fiend races’ Saintess and that mysterious man?” the Lord of Slaughter asked.

Salamay nodded. “Mmhm. Uncle Mo is missing too.”

The Lord of Slaughter spent a moment in thought before replying, “It could be because we entered the light gate at different times, resulting in us being transported to different places.” As the leader of the Monster World, he had also grasped the secrets of space. It wasn’t difficult for him to deduce what was going on. 

“I see…” Salamay looked both regretful and relieved at the same time. It would be troublesome if Zu An interrogated me while my father is injured. Once my father recovers from his injuries, I won’t have to fear anyone anymore. 

“Earlier, you mentioned that you came from the future. Tell me more about it,” the Lord of Slaughter said. 

He had already secretly brought up matters that only he and his daughter knew about to test her, and he could also feel his bloodline aura in her. That convinced him that she was his daughter as an adult. But this matter still sounded inconceivable to him. He was also curious to know who that powerful, mysterious man was. There were too many questions in his heart. 

Salamay knew that for them to work together, she had to share her information with her father, so she told him about everything that had happened. 

Despite everything the Lord of Slaughter had been through, he was still shocked to hear Salamay’s story. His face darkened. “You’re saying that the Monster Lord successfully replaces me in the future?”

Salamay assessed her father’s expression and carefully replied, “But the Monster Lord didn’t meet with a good end either. He eventually died to that fellow named Zu An.”

The Lord of Slaughter frowned. “That mysterious fellow is strong, but he’s still lacking compared to the Monster Lord. How did he defeat the Monster Lord?”

“If I’m not mistaken, the Monster Lord was weakened by the world’s law…” Salamay explained the situation then. In truth, however, she was also unsure how Zu An had defeated the Monster Lord. 

“It must be the compounded effect of the world barrier and that Zu An’s possession of the World Law Beacon. By weakening the enemy and strengthening himself, he gained an advantage over the Monster Lord and killed him,” the Lord of Slaughter sneered. “I didn’t expect the Monster Lord’s real body to be his mount. Even I was fooled. If I didn't know that, I would be at a huge disadvantage if I fought a life-and-death battle with him.”

Salamay looked hesitant to speak. 

The Lord of Slaughter laughed. “You may speak frankly. Your father is not that weak-hearted.”

Salamay thought it was best for him to know about how he'd ended up so he could guard against it, so she said, “Father, you’ll be sealed inside a formation in a place called the Sealed Land in the Cultivation World. The culprit seems to be the Fiend races’ Saintess.”

“Her?” The Lord of Slaughter frowned. “How did she have the power to seal me?”

Salamay added, “But now that you know, as long as you tread carefully, that woman won’t be able to get her way. We should kill them after we’re done using them, lest they become a threat.”

The Lord of Slaughter nodded, but he still felt uneasy. 

Meanwhile, Zu An was discussing their next destination with the Saintess. He was thinking of heading somewhere he could obtain useful information.

“Why don’t we go to Kunlun Mountain?” the Saintess proposed. 

“Kunlun Mountain?” Zu An was surprised. That wasn't one of his options. “Why Kunlun Mountain?”

“Kunlun Mountain is said to be the ancestor of all mountains. There are legends about Kunlun Mountain in ancient fiend records. Such renowned places tend to have rare herbs. If the Brilliant Pinflower exists in this world, the odds are that it’s at Kunlun Mountain,” the Saintess explained. 

Zu An nodded. Even if not for the Brilliant Pinflower, he would still decide to stop by Kunlun Mountain to gather some clues about the Celestial Court. After all, in the legends, Kunlun Mountain had intricate ties with the Celestial Court. 

“I’m feeling uneasy. Where did the Time Scribe go?” Zu An frowned. Such an elusive expert had gone completely silent. He was worried that the Time Scribe was scheming something. 

“He might have infiltrated the Monster Lord’s faction. After all, he is the true master of the Monster World millennia in the future,” the Saintess said. 

“Let’s hope that's the case.” Zu An was still feeling uneasy, however. 

Soon, sunset arrived. The many ferocious beasts in this world made traveling at night very dangerous, so the two of them found a cave to rest in. 

There were too many things to worry about, so Zu An wasn't in a mood to fool around. Instead, he spent his time in thought. 

Just then, the Saintess shyly whispered something into Zu An’s ears.

Zu An burst into laughter. “Don’t go too far away. Call me if anything happens. I’ll rush over.”

“All right. You mustn’t sneak up on me!” the Saintess said coyly. 

“I’m not so perverted as to pounce on someone relieving themselves.” Zu An laughed. 

The Saintess glared at him before rushing out with a reddened face.

A moment later, Zu An shook his head. She sure is shy to run so far away. Oh well, she’s no longer a sheltered miss, but the renowned Fiend races’ Saintess. She can protect herself now, and I can rush over if anything happens too.

Thus, he continued racking his brain to remember the details of what he'd seen when he came here millennia in the future. He felt as if he had overlooked something. 

Meanwhile, the Saintess glanced back at the cave coyly, as if she was afraid that her lover would follow her. But as she walked further away, the coy expression on her face slowly disappeared. Suddenly, she sped up and began traveling rapidly toward the mountain. Some time later, she halted her footsteps and coldly called out, “Isn’t it about time for you to reveal yourself? You have followed us for so long.” 

Applause echoed from behind. If Zu An were here, he would have been surprised to learn that Mojard had been following them.

Mojard looked intrigued. “I’m curious. Even Zu An didn’t notice me. How did you know?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2512: Only The Dead Can Keep Secrets
chapters.body:
Chapter 2512: Only The Dead Can Keep Secrets

The Saintess eyed Mojard and said. “You don’t look anywhere near as injured as you were before.”

Mojard had barely escaped from the Monster Lord, but invoking his Flesh Puppet came at a steep cost. His earlier condition said it all. Otherwise, the Lord of Slaughter wouldn’t have believed him so easily. And yet, the Mojard here looked perfectly fine. 

Mojard chuckled. “I used to be the Incestuous King. I’m bound to have hidden aces.”

“Is that so?” The Saintess chuckled, though her usually gentle smile looked cold. “Zu An was still wondering where the Time Scribe had gone, but I see that he has accompanied us here.” 

Mojard’s eyebrows shot up. “Saintess, are you suspecting me to be that Time Scribe or something? I am Mojard! Surely the first miss and my master can tell the difference?”

“The one who risked his life to stall the Monster Lord should have been the same Mojard, but…” The Saintess intentionally paused to scrutinize Mojard with her gaze. “The same can’t be said about the one who followed us in here.” 

Mojard laughed awkwardly. “My master should be powerful enough to tell the difference.”

“The Lord of Slaughter doesn’t know about the Time Scribe, but even if he knew, both he and Salamay were hoping that Mojard can escape alive. You used their subconscious against them and crafted some story about a Flesh Puppet to convince them. With that, you could infiltrate our midst openly using Mojard’s identity.” The Saintess eyed him coldly. “I was initially fooled by you too. It was only when Zu An mentioned the Time Scribe that I realized there was something off about you.”

Mojard sighed. His body began to distort until he reverted to his original appearance. He was now wrapped in a huge black cloak. His face was hidden by the shadows of the cloak, making it impossible to discern his true appearance. “Our disguises collapse once someone suspects the plausibility of our identities. Yesterday, I was in such a rush that I forgot to make proper arrangements.”

The Saintess understood what he meant. 

The Time Scribe had been in a rush because the Lord of Slaughter was about to take them through the light gate, and the only one the Scribe could impersonate was the recently deceased Mojard.
