None of them had witnessed Mojard’s death, so there was room for him to maneuver. His story about the Flesh Puppet sounded plausible, so no one had doubted him. But no matter how plausible his story sounded, there were bound to be gaps when scrutinized. 

“Weren’t you recording events beside the Lord of Slaughter? What are you doing here?” the Saintess asked.

Zu An had told her that the Time Scribe would only follow ‘the protagonist of the world’. Both the Lord of Slaughter and the Monster Lord had a far greater impact on the world than anyone else. That was why the Time Scribe had left Zu An’s side to follow those two and record the major events surrounding them. The Time Scribe could have impersonated Mojard because he wanted to record the legendary Celestial Court, but it was extremely suspicious how he had abandoned the Lord of Slaughter to stalk her and Zu An. 

“I discovered that the one who has the greatest luck and influence in this world is not the Monster Lord, nor the Lord of Slaughter, and not even Zu An.” The Time Scribe looked at the Saintess with heated eyes. “It’s you!”

The Saintess’ gaze turned even colder. 

The Time Scribe took out a book and quickly flipped through it. “According to history, you’re the Yumen clan’s first miss, and the Yumen clan is merely a minor clan in the Fiend races. How did they groom an outstanding talent like you?

“History states that you suddenly rose to prominence just as the Fiend races were about to meet their end and performed an incredible comeback. In the end, you even sealed the Lord of Slaughter. I can’t imagine how you were able to perform all these feats.”

“I don’t know about any such history,” the Saintess scoffed. “I’m merely doing my best to lead the Fiend races against the monsters. Perhaps big brother Zu might have helped me to accomplish many of those feats.”

“Are you trying to say that you’re Miss Xie who transmigrated into this era?” The Time Scribe chuckled. “Yes, I initially believed that story. I thought Zu An worked together with you and the Monster Lord to seal off the Lord of Slaughter. But after I met you and interacted with you close up, I realized how ridiculously wrong I was.” 

The Saintess suddenly smiled. “Oh? How were you ridiculously wrong?”

“You have far greater weight in this timeline than anyone else. That Miss Xie doesn’t have such capabilities.” The Time Scribe closed his book. “I found no records of you in history before you became the Saintess. So… Who are you?”

“Did you follow us just to speak utter nonsense?” The Saintess looked calm, as if none of this was related to her. 

“I didn’t want to reveal myself either. We Time Scribes should remain quietly in the background, recording every major event that happens in the world. But you insisted on calling me out.” The Time Scribe smiled. “Since that’s the case, I thought I would ask you in person.”

“Aren’t you afraid of being silenced?” The Saintess’ eyebrows shot up.

The Time Scribe shook his head. “Don’t worry, Saintess. I don’t bear any ill will. We Time Scribes merely record history. We don't intervene in secular affairs, so you need not worry about your identity being leaked.”

“Is that so? But I recall you intervening unnecessarily when I was in the Monster World’s underground city.” The Saintess eyed him coldly, unconvinced by his words. 

“Hm? You inherited that lady’s memories? That’s weird…” The Time Scribe picked up his brush and scratched his head. “Is it possession? But it doesn’t look like it. Ah, it could be…”

The Saintess raised her hand. “Only the dead can keep secrets.”

The Time Scribe revealed a mocking smile. “I see you’re about to do something useless, so let me remind you that we Time Scribes, unlike low-tier entities, have transcended physical bodies so we can traverse spacetime and the world. We are indestructible. You can’t kill me.” 

“Is that so…” A light suddenly shone from the Saintess’ hand. It was so blindingly bright that it outshone even the sun. This omnipresent light instantaneously engulfed the Time Scribe, causing his body to dissipate.

The Time Scribe’s horrified voice could be heard from the light. “How’s this possible? You are able to hurt me! This power… I know who you are! You are…”

He was reduced to ashes before he could finish speaking. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2513: Tolerating The Humiliation For Greater Things
chapters.body:
Chapter 2513: Tolerating The Humiliation For Greater Things

The Saintess watched everything with calm eyes as if she had done something insignificant. Under the brilliant light, her face looked exceptionally cold. It felt as if there was a light halo around her. Unlike the Shadow Monster King, who was dark at his core, she felt like a divine entity emanating auspicious light. If the Fiend races were to see her, they would once again kneel and kowtow to her, thinking that she was the Saintess chosen by heaven. 

Only when the Time Scribe was completely eradicated did the Saintess turn around to leave. Her movements appeared leisurely and slow, but it only took a step for her to leave the forest. She soon returned to the cave’s vicinity. She rubbed her face to erase its coldness, replacing it with her usual gentle smile. 

Zu An happened to rush out then, and he looked visibly relieved to see her. He rushed forward to give her a big embrace. “That scared me. I sensed something happening outside and rushed out to take a look, but I didn’t see you. I thought something happened to you.”

The Saintess' body stiffened up at his embrace, her lifted hand freezing in midair. Hearing the other party’s concern, her expression mellowed, and she naturally hugged him back too. She gently replied, “Don’t worry, I’m fine. I was surprised by the light too.”

Zu An looked in the distance and said, “Wait here for me. I’ll check it out.”

“Don’t go. It could be dangerous.” The Saintess held his hand. 

Zu An smiled. “Don’t worry, I'm strong enough to stay safe.”

The Saintess insistently shook her head. “We could be in the Celestial World. There are too many powerful entities that we don’t know about here. We can’t be too cautious.”

Zu An frowned. “Danger is no reason for us to stay still. We're already lacking intelligence about this world. If we don’t gather as much information as we can, we could die without even knowing why.”

The Saintess stared at him, amazed by his mental fortitude. “I’ll go with you then. I can at least cover you.”

Zu An thought it would be worse to leave her here and risk being separated in this foreign world, so he replied, “Alright.” He wrapped his arm around her slender waist and flew into the air.

The Saintess glanced at the hand on her waist and felt the warmth coming from it. It made her cheeks redden. Zu An had so many things on his mind that he didn’t notice anything off about her, though. 

The two of them soon arrived at where the Saintess had previously met Mojard. Zu An put the Saintess down and scanned the surroundings. 

“Did you find anything?” the Saintess carefully asked. 

Zu An squatted down in front of a hole and pinched the soil. “There were two people here just now. Both of them were very powerful.”

“Is that so?” The Saintess looked at him from behind with sharp eyes. 

“I sense traces of fire and light element ki lingering in the air. Those two people must have fought with each other.” Zu An’s face turned grim. “How weird. I only sensed an instantaneous burst of power. Was the victor determined in a single move?”

The Saintess walked up to him. “Who do you think they were? Were they humans or beasts?”

“There’s too little information to say for sure.” Zu An shook his head. “I don’t think they were beasts, as there are no traces of wildlife activities here or within a fifty kilometer radius. It’s as if the beasts dare not approach this place because they're afraid of something.”

Zu An had tried to use the jade badge’s communication ability to gather information from nearby animals, only to find that there was not a single animal within fifty kilometers. 

Upon hearing his answer, the Saintess retracted her hand and said, “If we can’t find anything, we should leave quickly to avoid complications. I feel uneasy. This place is dangerous.”

Zu An nodded. “I feel uneasy too.” He considered talking about the inexplicable unease he had felt in recent days, but he decided against that to avoid worrying her. Seeing that there were no other clues around, he brought the Saintess back to the cave. 

While he was pondering with a frown, he suddenly felt a pair of fingertips gently massaging his temples, and a voice saying, “I think you have been too nervous recently. You should relax a little.”

Zu An looked at her with a helpless smile. “I don’t want to, but we’re treading on thin ice. The slightest mistake could cost my life and implicate the rest of you.” 

Suoluo Shi had been imprisoned by Salamay. Ji Xiaoxi and the others were unreachable. On top of that, something seemed to have happened to Pei Mianman. 

“Are you worried about big sister Pei?” the Saintess asked while massaging him. 

“Mmhm. I didn’t find any traces of her outside the Imperial Tomb,” Zu An said. “I’m beginning to doubt whether she was really heading to the Imperial Tomb.”

The Saintess’ hands paused. “Once we’re out of here, let’s return to that town to gather more information. Perhaps there are other clues there.”

“I guess that’s all we can do now.” 

The Saintess continued the massage. “You don’t need to be too worried. Sometimes no news is the best news.”

Zu An grabbed her hands and pulled her into his embrace. “Little sister Ling’er, you’re so understanding. After hearing your words of consolation, I now feel much calmer.”

The Saintess was embarrassed. “Big brother Zu, you’re making fun of me.”

Her coy demeanor stoked Zu An’s desire. He couldn’t resist the urge to lower her head and kiss her. 

The Saintess was taken aback. She instinctively tried to look away, but at the same time, she was worried that the other party would notice something. Her hesitation resulted in her lips being captured. Shock flashed across her eyes, but she quickly controlled her emotions to show only embarrassment and confusion.

It was only when Zu An’s hands began reaching into her robes that she regained her clarity. She coyly grabbed his hand and grumbled, “You said that this place is filled with the unknown. How can we do something like this here?”

“But you just said that we should take things in stride and not worry too much.”

“I was consoling you… Wu…” The Saintess wanted to protest, only to find her lips sealed.

Anger flickered across her eyes. She placed her fingertip behind his head. It would only take a simple jab for her to claim this fellow’s life. But she quickly remembered her ploy and gave up on it. Forget it. It’s not our first time anyway. 

In her moment of hesitation, she suddenly felt Zu An squeezing into her with unstoppable momentum. Her face reddened, and she bit down hard on his shoulder. It’s all for that… 

“Be gentler!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2514: Conditions
chapters.body:
Chapter 2514: Conditions

The following day was sunny. 

Zu An and the Saintess traveled westward to Kunlun Mountain. It wasn't hard to find—it had a high mountain peak that rose into the clouds, making it visible from all parts of the world.

Zu An was astounded. How high must that mountain be to be visible even from this far away?

This world’s Kunlun Mountain was vastly different from the Kunlun Mountain he was familiar with in his previous world, both in terms of height and size. He even wondered if this world’s Kunlun Mountain was connected to heaven, linked to the Celestial Court. Or maybe the Celestial Court is on Kunlun Mountain?

Meanwhile, the Saintess secretly glanced at his side profile, and a red tinge occasionally appeared on her cheeks. This bastard has too much stamina! 

She'd initially endured it, thinking of it as a necessary sacrifice. However, it had eventually reached a point where her head blanked out, and she succumbed to the relentless waves of attacks. Just how many loads did that fellow shoot in me last night?!

The Saintess had been both embarrassed and angry in the moment, but she had to suppress her emotions because Zu An seemed to have the ability to detect someone’s anger. And yet, when she thought back about it now, she somehow no longer felt anger. The process had been sweet… and for some reason, her cultivation had advanced a fair bit. It was as if his load was a rare tonic in the world. I guess it isn’t a loss for me. Maybe I can find him again later for more…

She quickly shook her head to rid herself of inappropriate thoughts. 

“What’s wrong?” Zu An asked, noticing her actions.

“It’s nothing.” The Saintess’ face was burning. 

Zu An looked at her side profile under the sunlight. Her hair and skin were coated in golden radiance, highlighting her fair complexion and beautiful features. He gently hugged her and said, “Little sister Ling’er, you’re beautiful.”

The Saintess sensed that she no longer stiffened up when embraced by him, as if she had grown accustomed to it. “Am I more beautiful now, or was I more beautiful before?”

Zu An’s smile froze. Why do women like to ask such deadly questions?

“As long as it's you, you're always beautiful.”

“Tsk. What an insincere answer.” The Saintess rolled her eyes, but she didn’t ask any further. Instead, she was appalled that she'd asked such a meaningless question.

The two of them chatted while flying and avoiding aerial beasts. All of a sudden, however, a beam of light came from the surface.

“Be careful!” Zu An grabbed the Saintess and dodged the attack. 

But that wasn't the end of it. Several bursts of bizarre, mysterious energy flew at them right after. The two of them dodged the attacks while descending on a nearby mountain. It was only then that they realized that the aggressors were six old men dressed in bizarre clothes. 

“Who are you? Why did you attack us without reason?” the Saintess asked with a cold gaze. She had already brought out her High Firmament Talisman, and lightning crackled around her. 

The old men wore colorful clothes made out of different bird feathers and beast skins stitched together. Their faces were obscured by bizarre golden masks, and giant necklaces made up of assorted bones hung around their necks. They didn’t look like as if would be easy to talk to. 

“You can control lightning? Are you related to that person?” The bizarrely-dressed old men were astonished.

“Calm down, everyone. We know each other.” Zu An was delighted. He recognized the old men as some of the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans. He had met them the last time he came here, and they had even helped him. 

“You know them?” The Saintess was taken aback.

Before Zu An could answer the question, a hot-tempered old man scoffed, “Who the hell are you? What do you mean we know each other?” 

Zu An’s smile stiffened. He only now recalled that he had met them in the future, meaning they weren’t acquainted yet. He could only switch to another angle. “Are you the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans? I've heard a lot about you.”

The old men exchanged looks, but their faces remained cold. “You’re mistaken. We aren’t the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans.”

Zu An was startled. It was only after he took a closer look at the old men that he noticed some discrepancies. He smiled sheepishly and said, “Ah, I apologize for the misunderstanding, but you must be Wu Peng, and this must be Wu Di. I don’t think I've met the other four before.”

Astounded, one of the old men said, “We are indeed Wu Peng and Wu Di, but we don’t remember ever meeting you.”

Zu An was at a loss. He didn’t know how to explain to them that they were friends in the future, so he spent a moment in thought before replying, “I received a dream from an immortal about the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans…” He shared some details about the Spirit Mountain he saw in the future with them.

Wu Peng and Wu Di were dumbstruck. “There’s no way to lie about those details. To think there could be such a mystical dream in the world!”

The shamans lowered their hostility toward the two, and the Saintess stored away her High Firmament Talisman too. 

Zu An took this chance to ask, “Why did you suddenly attack us earlier?”

The six shamans looked a little awkward. Instead of answering the question, they asked, “Why did you come here?” 

Zu An suddenly remembered that the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans were skilled in medicine and had a broad knowledge of herbs. They might know where the Brilliant Pinflower is. “My little sister has a constitution that threatens her life, and we’re looking for a Brilliant Pinflower to save her. Do you by any chance know where I can find it?” 

The six shamans exchanged glances as if communicating with one another. Some time later, Wu Peng said, “I know where the Brilliant Pinflower is.”

Zu An was overjoyed. “I’ll be grateful if you can tell me where it is.”

There was finally a lead after searching for so long!

Wu Peng grimly replied, “Don’t be too quick to thank us. The Brilliant Pinflower is a valuable, rare herb. We can’t give it to you for free. You’ll have to do us a favor first.”

Zu An wasn’t surprised. Even though he was friends with the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans in the future, in this time, they had only just met. He had dispelled their doubt, but that didn’t mean they would be willing to share such a valuable herb with him. Thus, he asked, “May I know what you need my help with?”

Instead of answering the question, Wu Peng said, “You should think it through. You won’t be able to turn down our request once you hear it. We can’t risk our secret getting out.”

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. He knew too little about this world to guess what they needed his help with. He didn’t dare to accept their request recklessly in case it was too convoluted or dangerous. It would be terrible if he got embroiled in a fight between major powers. 

The Saintess gently spoke up. “Big brother Zu, it’s worth taking this risk to save little sister Xiaoxi.”

Zu An thought that made sense. If they failed to find the Brilliant Pinflower, Xiaoxi would be a goner. He let out a sigh as he pointed out, “It’s just that I’ll be putting you in danger too.” 

The Saintess chuckled. “What are you saying, big brother Zu? We have been through so much together. I’ll be with you no matter what happens.”

Zu An was moved. He grabbed her hand before turning to Wu Peng. “All right, we agree.”

Wu Peng nodded. “I’ll need you to draw away some guards for us. There’ll be some danger, but with the skills you showedearlier, you should be fine as long as you’re careful.”

Zu An and the Saintess exchanged looks and saw the worry in each other’s eyes. “May I know which guards you are referring to?”

Wu Peng pointed to a nearby mountain. His expression looked tense. “They're from Shushu Mountain!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2515: Celestials
chapters.body:
Chapter 2515: Celestials

“Shushu Mountain?” The Saintess’ eyes narrowed, but she quickly lowered her head to conceal her reaction. However, her hands remained firmly clenched. I still arrived late in the end…

Zu An found that name familiar, but he couldn’t remember where he'd heard it. So, he asked, “What kind of place is that? Who are the guards? How strong are they?”

Wu Peng pointed to a nearby mountain, “The mountain five kilometers away from us is Shushu Mountain. Most of the guards are snakes. They aren’t too powerful, so you should be able to handle them. The other guards, however, are more powerful. There is a bat, a fire boar, and a swallow lady. The swallow lady is the strongest, so you must be careful of her…” 

The more Zu An listened to their intelligence, the stranger he felt it to be. “It sounds no different from delivering my head to them on a silver platter.”

Wu Di replied, “We’re not asking you to fight them. You just have to cause a commotion to draw their attention while staying safe. You can run if things get dangerous.”

The Saintess spoke up, asking, “What will you do while we distract them?”

Wu Peng shook his head. “We can’t tell you. However, you can be assured that we are not planning to do anything evil.”

The Saintess sneered, “Look, we are in a cooperative relationship now. Your refusal to share intelligence with us puts us at a disadvantage. We’ll still do our part even if you tell us nothing, but we will have to tread more carefully to compensate for it. We might end up not attracting enough attention to help you with what you seek to achieve.”

Zu An was impressed. Little sister Ling’er has grown from the sheltered young miss she used to be. This must be a skill she's honed in the years she spent as the Fiend races’ Saintess. 

Wu Peng and Wu Di were taken aback. They were worried that Zu An and the Saintess wouldn’t pull their weight. In the end, they decided to speak up. “All right, we’ll tell you the truth. We’re hoping to take advantage of the chaos to steal a corpse from Shushu Mountain. Shushu Mountain’s master is extremely powerful. It’s unlikely that you’ll be able to draw his attention, but you should take this with you in case you catch his notice. You’ll at least still have a chance to survive.”

They tossed over a massive tortoise shell carved with mysterious marks. 

Zu An was taken aback. “Is Shushu Mountain’s master someone who can take our lives with just a gaze?”

He had encountered such entities before. That kind of gap in strength was impossible to bridge. If that were the case, he would rather break his promise and fight with these six shamans than march to his death. 

“Not to that extent,” Wu Peng explained. “Shushu Mountain’s master is Er Fu, a celestial with a human head and snake body. While he is powerful, he is incapable of taking someone’s life with a gaze. With the tortoise shell’s protection, you should be fine. Also, the odds of you catching his attention are extremely low.”

“A celestial!” Zu An shuddered. This could be his chance to meet a deity.

It finally dawned on him why the name ‘Shushu Mountain’ sounded so familiar. He had heard it from the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans the last time he was here. Back then, he'd encountered a monster named Yayu in the Weak Water. It turned out that Yayu had once been a celestial and was Er Fu’s brother, but Er Fu was jealous of his prestige. To make things worse, Er Fu’s subordinate had sown seeds of discord between the two. In the end, Er Fu killed Yayu. 

Later, the Celestial Emperor had tasked someone to deliver an immortal medicine to revive Yayu. The medicine had brought Yayu back to life, but it hadn't brought back his soul. As a result, Yayu devolved into a mindless beast. Later, he'd fallen into the Weak Water and turned into a dragon-headed monster who feasted on mortals. These shamans were likely planning to steal Yayu’s corpse. 

When he first heard about it, Zu An had found it lamentable, but he hadn’t expected to one day be involved in it. To think that he would be dragged into such a huge scheme! He was beginning to regret accepting the offer. 

The Saintess read his mind and said, “This matter concerns a celestial; it is far beyond our means to deal with it. We won’t do it.”

The hot-tempered shaman roared, “Now that you know our secret, you have to go whether you like it or not. The only other option is for you to die!”

Before addressing Zu An and the Saintess, Wu Peng stopped the hot-tempered shaman with a raise of his hand. “You are mortals, right? I understand your fear of celestials, but you don’t have to put them on a pedestal. They are merely stronger entities. Given the strength you have displayed, you aren’t powerless against them. Still, we won’t make things difficult for you if you are truly intimidated. All we ask is that you keep this matter a secret.”

Zu An and the Saintess exchanged doubtful looks. The other party was being suspiciously nice. 

Just then, Wu Peng added, “Ah, I forgot to tell you that I am the only one in the world who knows where the Brilliant Pinflower is. You might never find it if you miss this opportunity.”

Zu An was speechless. I bet this fellow’s heart is black through and through.

The Saintess sent a telepathic message to Zu An, “Big brother Zu, why don’t we give it a try? This might be little sister Xiaoxi’s only hope.”

Zu An nodded. He was thinking of giving it a try too, not just to obtain the Brilliant Pinflower, but also to meet the celestial named Er Fu. He also wanted to understand the difference between a mortal and a deity. Through that, he could figure out how mortals could transcend their limits to become deities. 

“Alright. Does the celestial named Er Fu have any skills I should take note of?” Zu An decided not to tell the shamans that he knew about the story between Er Fu and Yayu. He could tell that these shamans weren’t kind, innocent beings. It would be wise to be careful around them. 

Wu Peng was delighted to see Zu An accepting their request. “You need to be wary of his incredible speed. He is known to be the fastest creature in the world.”

Zu An was speechless. Fuck. How can I escape from him if he’s the fastest in the world?

Having guessed Zu An’s thought, Wu Peng consoled him. “That tortoise shell can block an attack from Er Fu. You can use that opportunity to escape. The moment we steal the corpse, he will sense it and pursue us instead. That will free you from danger. I’m not saying that there is no risk, but it’s up to you whether you think it is worth taking it on or not.”

“Very well, we’ll accept your request,” Zu An said. “But how can I trust you to tell us the Brilliant Pinflower’s whereabouts once we accomplish our mission?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2516: Palace
chapters.body:
Chapter 2516: Palace

Wu Peng puffed up his chest and proudly declared, “We, the Kaiming Six Shamans, have at least this bit of credibility. You should know that from the dream you received from the immortal.” 

Zu An thought about how they had kept their promise in the future, so he decided to trust them. But what are the Kaiming Six Shamans? Aren’t they the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans? So, he took this opportunity to ask about it.

Wu Peng and Wu Di exchanged looks before explaining, “Someone once put our names with eight other great shamans and called us the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans, but that title is merely an acknowledgment of our standing. Instead, we are closer to these friends. We share the same interests and values, and we often gather in the east of Kaiming. Over time, the world began to call us the Kaiming Six Shamans.”

He introduced the other four shamans—they were Wu Yang, Wu Lu, Wu Fan, and Wu Xiang. The hot-tempered one was Wu Yang.

Zu An widened his eyes in realization. I guess it’s similar to North Qiao Feng South Murong. Those two don’t hang out even though their names are mentioned together. Looks like Wu Peng and Wu Di are only friends with these four.

They quickly settled the details before Zu An and the Saintess made their way to the Shushu Mountain ahead of the Kaiming Six Shamans. 

As expected of the ki-rich ancient era. Even the mountains here are far taller than in the future, Zu An thought as he looked at the massive mountain before him.

“Big brother Zu, is there a chance the shamans are lying to us?” the Saintess asked softly. 

Zu An shook his head. “I met them when I came to this world in the future. On the whole, they are still trustworthy people. I heard about this incident too.” He told the Saintess about how Yayu was killed by Er Fu and turned into a monster. 

“Isn’t it meaningless if he eventually turns into a monster?” The Saintess was confused. 

Zu An smiled. “We’re just doing our best to ensure that history corresponds to the future to minimize its influence on us. Besides, it's a chance for us to find the Brilliant Pinflower and meet a deity. All these things have meaning.”

“Big brother Zu, will everything unfold as it did in the future, no matter what we do?” the Saintess asked with a pale face. 

Zu An didn’t think deeply about that question. The same thought had occurred to him before, and it made him feel pessimistic and powerless. “It’s impossible to change major events, but it’s possible to change the details of how things unfold. For instance, there's plenty of room to maneuver unrecorded history…” He shared what he had previously heard from Mi Li with her.

The Saintess’ eyes glowed with spirit. “Big brother Zu, your interpretation has opened my mind. Thank you.”

“What’s there to thank me for?” Zu An thought she was making a big deal out of nothing. Given their relationship, there was no need for thanks between them.

They began secretly scaling the mountain. To their surprise, the place wasn’t tightly guarded. There were very few sentries and patrols. They only encountered some snake monsters now and then, although the latter more closely resembled lizard-people. It didn’t feel as if they were guarding the mountain; rather, they felt native to the mountain. As long as they were avoided, there would be no danger in scaling the mountain. 

Zu An and the Saintess soon arrived at the mountain peak, where they saw a majestic palace. Along the path leading to it were sculptures and pillars with bizarre snakes carved into them. It looked eerie.

If Yu Yanluo were here, could she play the kin card with Er Fu?

In terms of height and area, this palace was far larger than the Cultivation World’s palaces. Only the structures Zu An had seen in the Monster World’s underground city could compete with it. 

Its master must have a huge physique to live in such a huge palace. 

In most worlds, there was a correlation between strength and size.

Zu An’s expression turned grim. He could already sense faint pressure despite not having entered the palace yet. So, he constructed several teleportation formations out of precaution. He wasn’t about to entrust everything to the tortoise shell. 

The security around the palace was much tighter than on the outskirts of the mountain. It looked as if even the reptilians feared this place, as there wasn't a single one within a three kilometer radius. Eight brawny guards stood at the palace entrance. From time to time, armed snake guards patrolled the square in front of the palace gates. It seemed impossible to approach the gates in such an open space, let alone to infiltrate the area. 

“Should we cause a commotion here and retreat?” the Saintess proposed. That was their agreement with the Kaiming Six Shamans. 

Zu An shook his head. He didn’t want to miss this good opportunity to see a deity in action. He didn’t want the Saintess to risk her life with him, so he said, “Stay here to cover me. I’ll go take a look.”

But the Saintess rejected his proposal. “No, I’m coming with you. It’s too dangerous for you to go alone. I’m not going to part ways with you here.”

Upon hearing her words, Zu An hesitated. He thought about the many teleportation formations they had prepared beforehand and eventually agreed to take her along. He summoned Daji and had her play her pipa not too far away from the square. 

The Saintess' eyes widened in shock. She hadn’t thought that Zu An could summon a person out of thin air, not to mention one so beautiful. 

Daji followed Zu An’s instructions, and soon, a melodious tune drifted into the square. Zu An wasn’t planning to have her control the guards with her ‘Voice of the Devil’, however. These guards were stronger than Daji; she couldn't control any of them.

Nevertheless, the guards patrolling the square were drawn to the melodious tune and marched over to take a look. Even the guards at the entrance glanced over as well. They were mesmerized by Daji at first sight.

Beauty transcended race. Zu An noticed that even among the Fiend races, the Ocean races, or the monsters, they had a tendency to morph into humans, and they found the human physique beautiful. The guards couldn’t resist someone as beautiful as Daji. 

While the guards were distracted, Zu An grabbed the Saintess and dashed through the palace gates with Grandgale. With a few instantaneous movements, he breached the palace defense and entered the premises. 

The Saintess stepped away from his embrace to tidy up her hair, using this chance to conceal her furious blush. “Won’t the lady outside be in danger?”

“It isn’t a problem,” Zu An replied with a smile. He recalled Daji back into his mysterious space. 

The guards outside were on the verge of catching Daji when she suddenly vanished into thin air. That caused a huge commotion.

The Saintess’ astonishment deepened. He’s not particularly strong, but he has all kinds of rare abilities. 

Zu An scouted the path while making a beeline toward the palace depths. To avoid getting noticed, he used the Unshakable Daoist Manual’s ki concealment ability and plastered several concealment talismans on both of them. 

Soon, they arrived at a hall and saw a massive silhouette swimming inside.

Indeed, the being within was swimming. His incomparably thick snake body moved between tall pillars, but his head was human. In the center of his forehead, he had a yellowish-brown mark that resembled an eye. He gave off a dignified yet solemn air. 

He was staring at a stone platform at the center of the hall, where another snake body lay. However, the latter was completely stiff—it was a corpse. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2517: Divine Halo
chapters.body:
Chapter 2517: Divine Halo

Zu An figured that they had to be Er Fu and the deceased Yayu. But why did Er Fu leave his brother’s corpse here after killing him?

He gently grabbed the Saintess’ hand, worried the latter would be intimidated by a massive snake body paired with a human head. It was a sight that could swiftly erode one’s sanity. But to his surprise, the Saintess’ hand was warm. She wasn’t afraid in the slightest.

The snake body coiled around the pillars moved slowly as a human head bent down from the ceiling to examine the corpse lying on the stone platform. There was an irrepressible smile beaming on his face. He murmured obscure, ancient-sounding words. Fortunately, Zu An had been to this world, so he could comprehend 70% to 80% of the words.

“Yes, you’re so noble and incredible. Our father likes you. The other celestials like you. The shamans and the people like you. Everyone likes you. What about it? Aren’t you still a cold corpse now?” 

Frenzied laughter followed. Afterward, however, Er Fu’s face suddenly darkened. “Don’t blame me. You were asking for it. Why did you have to compete with me over everything? Our parents’ love when we were younger, and later other people’s love too.

“They could compliment you, but why did they have to compare us? The kinder and more virtuous you were, the more despicable and detestable I looked! My dumb little brother, didn’t you know those compliments would come at a price? It was those compliments that led you to your death!”

… 

Zu An frowned. He'd wanted to experience a deity's divinity close up, but what he got instead was a mentally warped monster. He was greatly disappointed.

Just then, a woman’s voice echoed, “Congratulations, master. You have finally gotten rid of your greatest threat.”

Only then did Zu An notice a skinny woman standing by the corner. Er Fu’s massive body was so visually striking that he'd failed to notice the much smaller woman right away. Zu An looked over and was flabbergasted.

The woman wore a white inner garment with a black robe reminiscent of a dress over it. The black-and-white coordination accentuated her curves. If it had been placed on the internet in Zu An's previous world, the netizens would comment that with such a beautiful figure, it would be fine even if they didn’t look at the face. 

But her face was indeed a problem, as she didn’t have a human head. She was literally a birdbrain!

No wonder the monsters believe they're the descendants of the Celestial race. I can see the resemblance. Given her swallow-like head, I bet she must be the legendary Wei Yueyan.

In the future, when the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans told him about Yayu’s death, they'd mentioned something about Er Fu being manipulated by his subordinate, Wei. This birdbrained woman seemed to be that same Wei. 

Er Fu’s cold voice boomed in the hall. “My brother is dead. What is there to congratulate?”

The birdbrain woman’s expression stiffened. What shamelessness! He wants to pretend to be a paragon of virtue despite having killed his brother?

Of course, she didn’t dare to express those thoughts aloud. She mustered her courage and replied, “Yayu deserved his plight for provoking master in all sorts of ways. Now that he’s dead, there is no one to steal faith from master anymore. That is something worth congratulating.”

Zu An’s eyebrows shot up. Faith? Sounds like faith is important to celestials. 

His heartbeat hastened slightly, but that unfortunately caught Er Fu’s attention. The latter raised his massive head and looked over. “Who is it?”

Zu An was alarmed. He immediately tried to leave with the Saintess, but much to his astonishment, a tremendous burst of energy suddenly appeared before him. He hadn’t seen any traces of Er Fu making a move! It was as if the attack had just suddenly manifested around him. It was beyond comprehension!

Despite being on his guard, he couldn’t protect himself from the attack. The terrifying energy was already on the verge of striking him. All he could do in the spur of the moment was to drag the Saintess behind him and gather his ki to tank the attack, but he wasn’t sure if he could endure an attack from a deity. 

Just then, a sliver of light shone. A massive tortoise shell suddenly appeared in front of him, shielding him. It was the shell he had received from Wu Peng! 

Kacha!

The tortoise shell shattered into dust, but it also neutralized half of the terrifying attack. That bought enough time for Zu An to react. He grabbed the Saintess and performed instantaneous movements to flee outside.

The earlier clash couldn’t have made it clearer that Er Fu was far stronger than Zu An. Even the Lord of Slaughter and the Monster Lord at their peak wouldn’t have been a match for him!

Even though Zu An hadn't experienced a deity’s divinity, he had most definitely experienced a deity’s terrifying prowess. 

“The familiar yet hateful aura of the shamans.” Er Fu’s expression turned cold. “Take down the intruder.” It was beneath him, a celestial, to personally make a move.

“Yes!” The birdbrained woman bowed. She gently leaped off the ground and transformed into a delicate swallow. Despite her small size, she was as fast as lightning. 

Zu An used Grandgale consecutively to escape. Mo Xi’s Celestial Jade Palace skill reduced the cooldown time of his instantaneous movements, but it wasn't omnipotent. While his skill was on cooldown, he was shocked to see a swallow appear on his back. Having heard of Wei Yueyan’s name, he didn’t dare to underestimate her. 

The swallow swooped in front of the two of them and transformed back into that bird-headed, human-bodied woman. She sized up the two of them with surprised eyes. “Hm, two mortals? Are you here to seek death? Come with me to meet my master.”

Zu An smiled. “What if I say no?”

Wei Yueyan’s lips curled in disdain. “You must be confident in yourself to dare intrude on this place. I can tell you’re different from other weak mortals, but you are completely oblivious to the gap between a mortal and a celestial!”

She released a burst of intertwined black and white light.

Zu An was alarmed. He didn't know the fundamental difference between a mortal and a celestial, but his danger instinct was tingling. He'd intended to make a preemptive move, but he couldn’t possibly be faster than light. 

As the black and white light shone on him, he felt his body becoming heavy as if he was submerged into a viscous marsh. Not only was moving difficult, but even breathing became hard. He murmured, “Domain?”

Wei Yueyan scoffed, “Is that the limit of your comprehension? How can a divine halo be something as lowly as a domain?” She casually waved her wide sleeve, flicking it toward Zu An and the Saintess. She was planning to capture them alive and bring them to her master. 

However, in the next second, she froze up. To her astonishment, the other party had somehow regained his ability to move and dodged her sleeve. “How is that possible?”

Then, her face twisted. “The Taotie’s ability!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2518: Ritual
chapters.body:
Chapter 2518: Ritual

Zu An was taken aback. He had used the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra—it was inspired by his fight with the Shadow Monster King in the Sealed Land. The Shadow Monster King had also been shrouded in light, and had warped the space around himself. Back then, Zu An was able to unravel the warped space with the Heaven-Devouring Sutra. 

Wei Yueyan’s divine light reminded him of that experience, which was why he'd tried using the Heaven-Devouring Sutra, turning his body into a black hole to absorb the surrounding energy. Much to his surprise, he successfully overcame the divine light. 

It wasn't the first time someone recognized the Taotie’s power. Their words also suggested that the Taotie they spoke about was different from the little Taotie monsters he had previously met in the Yinxu secret dungeon. 

I wonder if I’ll meet the real Taotie one day. 

Wei Yueyan snapped out of her daze and said, “You have tricks, but you can’t escape death.”

Not daring to let her guard down anymore, she went all-out and morphed into a black bolt of lightning. Her sharp wings turned the surroundings into a lethal trap, performing thousands of cuts within the blink of an eye. She didn’t think that anyone could escape unscathed from her concentrated attacks. 

However, her smile soon stiffened. To her shock, Zu An and the Saintess had disappeared into thin air. 

“A teleportation formation?” Despite being Er Fu’s subordinate, she was still a deity; she could sense a disturbance in the surrounding spatial ki.

With a sneer, she slipped into a spatial crack and soon arrived in a room. However, Zu An and the Saintess were still nowhere to be seen. 

“They prepared multiple teleportation formations? You’re too naive if you think you can shake me off like that!”

She slipped into the cracks of space once more and performed ten consecutive teleportations, stopping by forests, cliffs, and quarries along the way. The final teleportation brought her back to the room she'd reached in her first teleportation. 

Her face finally darkened. It was now clear to her that the other party had tampered with the spatial ki disturbance to mislead her, leaving behind a fake trail of teleportations to render her effort useless. Chances were that those two had already fled in the meantime. 

A terrifying pressure suddenly bore down on Wei Yueyan.

Er Fu’s massive snake body appeared on the room’s ceiling. He lowered his human head and said, “You seem to have lost the targets.”

Wei Yueyan anxiously half-kneeled on the ground and said, “Forgive me, master. The intruders were too cunning. Their grasp of the spatial laws surpasses that of ordinary people.”

“You have disappointed me.” Er Fu’s eyes turned cold. 

Wei Yueyan shuddered in fear. Goosebumps rose all over her body as Er Fu slowly approached her with a widened mouth. She trembled in fear, but she didn’t dare to avoid her master. 

All of a sudden, Er Fu raised his massive head and looked back at the hall. “How dare you!”

His massive snake body abruptly vanished into thin air. Shortly after, furious roars came from the distance as terrifying ki pulsations rippled from the palace at the mountain peak. 

Zu An and the Saintess observed the commotion from a tree outside the palace.

“It’s a huge relief that little sister Ling’er reminded me to set up that trap in our teleportation formations, or else she would have caught up with us,” Zu An remarked in awe. The Saintess’ grasp of spatial formations seemed to be above his. 

The Saintess smiled shyly. “I just thought that deities could have the ability to slip through space, so it would be good to prepare countermeasures. I didn’t expect it to be of use either.” 

She didn’t want to dwell on this topic, so she naturally diverted the conversation toward the ongoing battle in the palace. “Big brother Zu, those shamans are stronger than I thought.”

“Indeed, they are stronger than I thought,” Zu An said with a frown. His brief encounter with Er Fu had left a deep impression on him, so he was surprised that the Kaiming Six Shamans could fight the latter on equal grounds. 

“It doesn’t seem as if there’s anything different about deities other than them being stronger than mortals,” the Saintess said. 

Zu An shook his head. “No, that’s not right. I didn’t see Er Fu move when he attacked us earlier. It’s as if he can control the surrounding ki with his will. I'm familiar with that feeling. I could do the same in the Cultivation World when I had the World Law Beacon.”

The Saintess was taken aback. “Does Er Fu have this world’s World’s Law Beacon?”

Looking at her nervous face, Zu An chuckled. “I don’t think so. If he had this world’s World Law Beacon, we would have been long dead, and he wouldn’t have had to murder his little brother out of envy either.

“I think deities have the ability to freely control ki within a set radius. It’s not as powerful as the World Law Beacon, which can control an entire world’s ki and will. However, this world is far stronger than the Cultivation World, so even utilizing a small portion of its ki can make one very powerful.” 

“No wonder he exerted such terrifying pressure,” the Saintess murmured contemplatively. “What else did you figure out?”

“Deities also have an ability known as divine halo,” Zu An deduced. “Now that I think about it, there were iridescent lights when the Celestial Court bestowed me with the Sun Slaying Bow the last time I was here. I thought it was just a special effect, but it might have been a divine halo.

“Wei Yueyan isn't a strong deity; that’s why her divine halo is only black and white. I’m not sure if I could have broken free if it had been a stronger deity with an iridescent divine halo.”

The Saintess lowered her gaze in contemplation.

Zu An continued, “Aside from those two factors, I think faith is also very important to deities. It may directly determine their fighting prowess.”

The Saintess looked at him with conflicted eyes. He’s formidable, to be able to deduce so much despite the brief encounter. 

“Let’s go. It looks like the Kaiming Six Shamans have succeeded,” Zu An said. The fighting in the palace had died down, leaving Er Fu to rage alone. 

He and the Saintess made their way to the meeting spot with the Kaiming Six Shamans and found them surrounding a stone platform. A human-faced, snake-bodied corpse had been placed on the platform. 

The six shamans murmured an obscure chant, and blue lights began glowing from the formation beneath their feet. The blue lights converged on the stone platform, causing the corpse to glow with a faint light. The surroundings were suffused with tremendous life force. 

Zu An wanted to walk over and ask about the Brilliant Pinflower, but the Saintess stopped him. “They are conducting an important resurrection ritual. You mustn’t interrupt them now, or else they might blame it on you.”

Zu An looked at the formation and sighed. “They won’t succeed.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2519: Searching For Her A Thousand Times In The Crowd
chapters.body:
Chapter 2519: Searching For Her A Thousand Times In The Crowd

“Why?” the Saintess anxiously asked.

Zu An glanced at her in surprise. Why is she so concerned? “I once used an immortal medicine to save Honglei. Aside from the immortal medicine, you have to find the person’s soul as well. Neither can be missing in the equation.”

“You seem to have deep ties with the underworld. Is there a way to find someone’s soul?” the Saintess asked.

Zu An shook his head. “I’ve checked. For some reason, the netherworld doesn’t exist in this world.”

The Saintess widened her eyes. She seemed to have thought of something and decided not to probe further. 

Zu An continued, “In the absence of the netherworld, deceased souls wander the world aimlessly without anywhere to go. Some dissipate swiftly, and some morph into vengeful spirits. It’s nigh impossible to find Yayu’s soul.”

While they were talking, the Kaiming Six Shamans had already concluded their ritual.

Wu Di exclaimed in excitement, “He’s moving, he’s moving! The immortal medicine works!”

Zu An regretted not asking to see what their immortal medicine was. It hadn’t been easy for him to find a Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine in the future. It would be great if he could find another immortal medicine in this world. 

The surrounding life force gushed into the corpse on the stone platform. The corpse’s hands and legs shook before it slowly opened its eyes.

“He has been revived!” The Kaiming Six Shamans cried tears of joy.

“Yayu, we have rescued you from Er Fu, and the Celestial Emperor was gracious enough to bestow an immortal medicine to you. That’s why we saved you.” The shortest shaman, Wu Fan, walked up to Yayu to explain the situation with a smile. 

Yayu initially looked confused, but a vicious glint soon flashed through his eyes. Without any hesitation, he gobbled Wu Fan up whole, shocking everyone present.

The remaining Kaiming FIve Shamans quickly retreated. Their leader, Wu Peng, was enraged. “Yayu, what are you doing?”

Yayu clutched his head and groaned in pain. He was unable to speak at all. 

In the meantime, Wu Peng and the others snapped out of their daze and rushed forward to rescue their comrade. They knew that Wu Fan was likely dead by now, but they had to at least give it a try. 

However, the charge of the Kaiming Five Shamans alarmed Yayu. With a roar, he fled outside and disappeared in the blink of an eye. 

Zu An and Saintess were dumbfounded. They quickly followed the Kaiming Five Shamans out.

It was a pity that Yayu was too fast, just like Er Fu. Much to the Kaiming Five Shamans' despair, they soon lost sight of their target. 

“How did this happen?” Wu Peng murmured in disbelief. 

The others were too shaken up to utter a word.

“Could the immortal medicine be fake?” the hot-tempered Wu Yang growled. 

They had succeeded in reviving Yayu, but the latter had devolved from a gentle, compassionate deity into a vicious monster. They felt indignant and enraged to have lost their comrade like that.

Wu Peng shook his head. “That can’t be. It was a bestowment from the Celestial Emperor, and we inspected it. There’s no way to fake it.”

“Could the Celestial Emperor have tampered with the pill in a way that eludes our…” Wu Yang muttered. 

Wu Peng interjected, “Watch your words!”

Wu Yang belatedly realized the outrageous words he had muttered.

Zu An took this opportunity to ask, “You've met the Celestial Emperor? May I ask what kind of deity he is?” 

He had too little information about the Celestial Court. This was a good opportunity to gather some intelligence.

“The Celestial Emperor is the Celestial Emperor, the ruler of all deities.” Wu Peng didn’t look interested in this topic. “We have only seen the Celestial Emperor from afar, and we didn’t dare to raise our heads to look at him. We don’t know much about him either.”

“Didn’t you receive the immortal medicine from the Celestial Emperor?” Zu An was surprised. He'd thought that both sides would be close. 

“How could we be qualified to meet the Celestial Emperor? We received the immortal medicine from Celestial Bao Jiang. He manages the Celestial Emperor’s immortal medicines.” Wu Peng replied with a shake of his head. He hadn’t recovered from his confusion, shock, and sorrow yet. 

Looking at how crestfallen he was, Zu An explained, “Yayu didn’t intend to eat your comrade. His soul hasn’t returned yet. What you revived with the immortal medicine is an undead monster.”

“Soul…” Light slowly returned to Wu Peng’s eyes. “Yes, yes! That’s where we went wrong!”

But his excitement quickly deflated into sorrow, as he realized that everything they'd done was meaningless. Not only had they failed to save Yayu, they'd even lost a comrade in the process too. 

The Saintess suddenly asked, “Do you know where we can find Bao Jiang?”

“Are you looking for immortal medicine? It’s useless. No one can obtain an immortal medicine without the Celestial Emperor’s permission.” Wu Peng shook his head. “However, the Brilliant Pinflower you asked about can be found on his Treasure Origin Mountain. We’ll uphold our promise even though we failed to save Yayu. We’re grateful for your help.” 

Zu An was overjoyed to receive news about the Brilliant Pinflower. 

“We should be bringing you there, but we’ll have to pursue Yayu. I’m afraid we can only point the location of Treasure Origin Mountain out for you…” Wu Peng took out a tortoiseshell and drew on it. “Here’s a map to Treasure Origin Mountain and a letter. Once he sees the letter, Bao Jiang will know you are my friend and will hand the Brilliant Pinflower to you.”

Zu An accepted the tortoiseshell. “Thank you, elder.”

Wu Peng waved his hand in disinterest. He wasn’t in the mood for pleasantries.

Zu An knew Wu Peng wouldn’t be in the mood to answer his questions, so he decided to head to Treasure Origin Mountain to obtain the Brilliant Pinflower first. The thought that he was going to obtain another material to save Ji Xiaoxi lifted his spirit.

“Big brother Zu, we should make haste,” the Saintess reminded him.

Zu An was taken aback. “Why?”

“Have you forgotten about the story you heard in the future? Bao Jiang, who manages the immortal medicine, was assassinated,” the Saintess replied grimly.

Zu An’s face darkened right away. He anxiously dragged the Saintess with him to Treasure Origin Mountain as fast as he could. It felt as if Wu Peng’s drawing was equipped with a navigation system; it persistently pointed in the direction they should head to reach their destination.

Zu An and the Saintess made no stops along the way. They continued to fly full speed ahead, occasionally popping a pill into their mouths to replenish their stamina and ki. Fortunately, Treasure Origin Mountain was only around 500,000 kilometers away. Had it been any further, they would have had to travel via teleportation formation. 

After days of travel, Zu An and the Saintess arrived at Treasure Origin Mountain. It was extremely near Spirit Mountain, which he had visited the last time he was here; but he was in no mood to check if his old friends, the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans, were around. 

He made his way to a cave and shouted, “I have come to seek an audience with Deity Bao Jiang under the guidance of the Kaiming Six Shamans.”

“The Kaiming Six Shamans?” a seductive voice echoed from the cave. 

Zu An and the Saintess were taken aback. Is Deity Bao Jiang a woman?

A woman slowly made her way out of the cave. Her sentimental peach blossom eyes, mesmerizing face, and curvaceous figure drew their eyes. 

Zu An was delighted to see her. “Manman!”

He had been extremely worried about Pei Mianman in recent days, and his failure to find any clues about her had been causing his unease to reach its peak. Seeing her safe and sound made his heart burst with excitement, and he leaped forward to embrace her. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2520: Goddess
chapters.body:
Chapter 2520: Goddess

When she saw the woman’s face, the Saintess’ face twisted in shock. She opened her mouth several times, but no words came out.

A terrifying aura suddenly burst forth from the woman. “How dare you!”

Zu An was jolted a great distance back. 

The woman’s hair and robes fluttered as her body emitted an iridescent light. With a reddened face, she glared furiously at Zu An.

Zu An was taken aback too. He hadn’t expected Pei Mianman to be so vehemently opposed to his embrace. What’s going on?

You have trolled Yaoji for +999… +999… +999…

Who is this Yaoji?

Zu An stared confusedly at the woman in front of him. With those portrait-like eyebrows, an enchanting face, and that distinctive talent, who else could she be if not Pei Mianman? 

The Saintess noticed that the woman was exploiting Zu An’s daze to channel a huge move, so she quickly raised her hands to stop them. “Big sister Pei, why are you so angry? Big brother Zu merely got too excited after not having seen you for such a long time.”

The woman was taken aback. “Big sister Pei?”

The Saintess quickly replied, “That’s right, big sister Pei. Big brother Zu has been searching all over for you, from the resistance army camp to the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb. He even risked his life to fight with the Lord of Slaughter and the Monster Lord for you!”

“The Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb? The Lord of Slaughter and the Monster Lord?” The woman frowned. The killing intent in her eyes faded a little.

“What’s wrong, Manman?” Zu An could fathom a guess about the situation, but he would rather believe that Pei Mianman was suffering from amnesia. 

The woman’s face turned cold, and her killing intent flared again.

The Saintess glared at Zu An as she quickly explained, “We came here under the Kaiming Six Shamans' guidance to seek an audience with Deity Bao Jiang to obtain a Brilliant Pinflower to treat little sister Xiaoxi. You went missing searching for this flower. I didn’t expect we would be reunited here. How did you get here?”

She spoke quickly but clearly, concisely revealing their relationship with Pei Mianman while highlighting their relationship with the Kaiming Six Shamans.

The beautiful woman could roughly figure out what was going on, and she coldly replied, “I’m not your big sister Pei or Manman or whatever. You got the wrong person.”

Zu An was stunned. His mind went blank when his suspicions were confirmed. How could two people in the world look so identical?

But he quickly recalled the Mermaid Queen and the Peacock Princess; they were identical to the future Shang Liuyu and Kong Nanwu. From that angle, it wasn’t impossible for someone to resemble Pei Mianman too. However, losing another lead to Pei Mianman’s whereabouts made Zu An uneasy.

The Saintess had thought as much, but she feigned astonishment and exclaimed, “You aren’t big sister Pei? How is that possible? There’s no way we would fail to recognize her!”

The woman lost her patience. “You have the wrong person. But what are these Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb, Lord of Slaughter, and Monster Lord? Why have I never heard about them before?”

Upon hearing that she didn’t know about those things, Zu An had no choice but to give up. “It seems we indeed had the wrong person. I apologize for my earlier discourtesy.”

His earnest attitude placated the beautiful woman’s anger. In truth, she was perplexed too. Given her temperament, she wouldn’t have hesitated to kill any men who dared to suddenly embrace her. But for some reason, she felt no killing intent toward him despite her anger. Otherwise, she wouldn’t have held back even with the Saintess’ intervention. It was weird how she felt intimate toward him despite having never met him before. 

“May I know if you are Deity Bao Jiang?” the Saintess asked.

The beautiful woman shook her head. “I am not Bao Jiang. I am his neighbor, and I came here because I have business with him.”

“Neighbor?” Zu An looked at the neighboring spirit mountains and wondered where she resided. 

“This is big brother Zu An, and I am Yumen Beiqing. May I ask what your name is?” Seeing that Zu An was in no state to socialize, the Saintess had to step up and gather more information. 

The beautiful woman was hesitant to reveal her identity, but the inexplicable intimacy she felt toward Zu An made her relent. “You can call me Yaoji.”

“Yaoji?” The Saintess was surprised. “Are you the Flame Emperor’s little daughter, the Goddess of Wu Mountain?” 

Zu An was taken aback. He finally remembered why that name sounded so familiar. The Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans had mentioned this neighbor of theirs the last time he was here, but he hadn’t expected the other party to bear such a striking resemblance to Pei Mianman. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain looked at the Saintess. “You know me?”

Zu An was perplexed as well. Even he took time to recall this piece of information; it was puzzling how quickly little sister Ling’er reacted here. 

“I have long heard about your legend. You are indeed as ethereal as others claim,” the Saintess threw a compliment over. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was unfazed, having heard many such compliments before. “You said the Kaiming Six Shamans sent you here. Do you have evidence of that?”

As soon as Zu An took out Wu Peng’s tortoiseshell, it abruptly flew into the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s hand. She said, “Yes, this is indeed Wu Peng’s aura. You must be trustworthy to have earned their recognition.”

The tortoiseshell floated back to Zu An. The light fragrance that lingered on it left him dazed. Is she really not Pei Mianman?

“You came at a bad time. He has already left,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain said. 

“He left? Toward where?!” the Saintess anxiously asked.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain calmly replied, “I was looking around and saw a note saying that he left. He must have reported back to the Celestial Court.”

“Am I still a step too late…” The Saintess fell into a daze. She looked as if someone had sapped her energy dry.

Zu An looked at her contemplatively before asking, “Goddess, is there a way to find him? There’s something I need his help with.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain impatiently replied, “Do you think you would be more important to him than the Celestial Emperor’s orders?”

Zu An detected disdain in her voice and explained, “He is in danger right now. He could die if we don’t get to him right away. Goddess, you must be his friend to freely wander around his cave. I’m sure you wouldn’t want anything to happen to him either.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was taken aback. “What makes you say that?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2521: Chance Encounter
chapters.body:
Chapter 2521: Chance Encounter

Instead of directly answering the question, Zu An asked, “Goddess, does anyone in the world know where to find immortal medicine as of late?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain shook her head. “Immortal medicines are supreme treasures. Many covet them but to no avail, as they are highly elusive.”

“Someone was fanning conflict between Er Fu and Yayu, prompting the former to kill the latter. Er Fu and Yayu are both deities, which makes that an extremely risky move where a misstep could result in eternal damnation. What could drive someone to take such a risk? Surely it couldn’t be because they have a personal vendetta against Yayu?” 

Zu An chose not to reveal the final outcome of the matter. There was no way he could explain why he knew so much if the other party asked about it. Revealing that he was from the future in a world filled with deities could lead to unforeseen complications, so he decided to guide the other party’s train of thoughts with questions instead. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was no fool. She understood what Zu An was driving at and asked, “You think the person behind this wishes to make use of this opportunity to figure out the whereabouts of an immortal medicine?”

“That is the most likely possibility, now that Bao Jiang has left his cave to report to the Celestial Court. If the mastermind plans to make a move, now is the best time,” Zu An said. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was startled. She quickly took out a cloud-shaped mirror with a wave of her hand, and its surface rippled.

“I can’t reach him.” She stowed away the mirror with a grim expression.

Zu An figured that the mirror was something similar to the Cultivation World's recording mirrors. 

“Do you know which path he took? Is there a chance we can still make it in time if we make haste?” he anxiously asked, thinking that this could be a good opportunity to gather some intelligence about the Celestial Court. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain paced around the room. Even the Saintess had to concede that she was a charming woman. “Deity Bao Jiang recently mentioned that he needed to harvest a special herb, which can be found on the way to the Celestial Court. It might be possible to catch up with him if you give chase now.”

Zu An was taken aback. “Goddess, aren’t you coming too?”

He instinctively felt close to her because of her striking resemblance to Pei Mianman, and he also wanted to see if she was related to Pei Mianman in any way. He was disappointed that she showed no inclination to travel with them. 

“I can’t stray too far away from Wu Mountain,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain calmly replied without explaining too much.

Zu An could tell that the other party was reluctant to share more information, so he turned the topic back to Bao Jiang. “Which road should we take to find Deity Bao Jiang?”

This world was so huge that blindly looking for Bao Jiang would be even tougher than finding a needle on the ocean floor.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain swirled her finger in midair. Clouds gathered at her fingertip and formed a simple map. “Follow this map. You should be able to find him in the vicinity of Kunlun Mountain.”

“Kunlun Mountain?” Zu An glanced at the Saintess. Back then, the latter had suggested heading to Kunlun Mountain. Who would have thought that after so many twists and turns, they would still be heading there in the end?

“All routes to the Celestial Court pass through Kunlun Mountain,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain explained. 

Zu An nodded in realization. No wonder Kunlun plays a significant role in so many folktales and legends. 

He took this opportunity to ask more about the Celestial Court, but the Goddess of Wu Mountain's expression turned cold. “I don’t like that place, and I don’t want to talk about it.”

She flung her sleeves and left, leaving behind a graceful silhouette. 

Zu An was stunned. Is she angry because of my earlier brash behavior?

The Saintess tugged his sleeves and said, “Stop looking. She’s already gone.”

Zu An replied with a sheepish smile, “Little sister Ling’er, is it possible for two people to look so identical in the world?”

“There are similar-looking people in the world, but for them to bear such great resemblance to each other…” The Saintess fell into deep thought. There’s only one possibility…

Worried that Zu An would continue probing, she changed the topic. “We should quickly head over. It’ll be terrible if we don’t make it in time.” She grabbed Zu An and headed out of the mountain. 

Her urgency moved Zu An. Little sister Ling’er truly cares about Xiaoxi. She’s worried that we won’t be able to find the Brilliant Pinflower. 

While they were leaving, a stone peak vaguely peeked out amid the fog. It slowly morphed into a woman with a fluttering skirt. If Zu An and the Saintess were here, they would be able to tell that she was none other than Yaoji.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain looked at their departing silhouettes with a slight frown. “That’s weird…” She couldn’t understand why that strange man evoked a sense of familiarity in her. At the same time, she also detected an aura she didn’t like from the woman next to him. 

As the fog drifted past, the woman’s silhouette slowly vanished before morphing back into a mountain peak. 

While they were traveling, Zu An noticed a hint of exhaustion on the Saintess’ face and apologized, “I've burdened you, little sister Ling’er. We've been traveling without rest for the last few days.”

It was a hilarious situation. They had rushed to Treasure Origin Mountain, only to have to rush to Kunlun Mountain right after. As long as they were careful, the aerial monsters no longer posed a threat to them, but this world was so vast that even Zu An was starting to feel tired despite his cultivation.

The Saintess chuckled. “Don’t worry, big brother Zu. I can still hold on. We can’t lose time now, or else we might lose Bao JIang.”

Zu An nodded. “Yes, we need to hurry.” But worry filled his face right after as he continued, “Even so, we might not be able to make it in time with our current speed.”

Kunlun Mountain was still far away. It would be ideal to travel via a teleportation formation, but Zu An had never been there and didn’t know its terrain and coordinates, so he couldn’t construct a teleportation formation even if he wanted to. 

“If only we had a mount…” The Saintess lowered her gaze. She looked conflicted.

Zu An took out the Crystal Boat he 'd gotten from the East Sea Dragon King. “This boat is slower than us, but we can recover our stamina while riding on it. Otherwise, we won’t have the strength to rescue Bao Jiang even if we arrive on time.”

The Saintess frowned. She found the Crystal Boat too slow, but she couldn’t find a pretext to refute him. In the end, she boarded the Crystal Boat with him and rested in a room. 

Zu An took out a couple of pills for them to consume. While recuperating via meditation, he said, “There’s another matter I’m worried about. Judging from our encounter with Er Fu, the deities are extremely powerful. Even if we make it in time, will we be able to save Bao Jiang?”

The Saintess chuckled. “Big brother Zu, you’re thinking too much. Bao Jiang is a deity himself. Back then, he probably only died to his assailants because his guard was down. He should be able to survive the ordeal as long as we make it in time to warn him. Furthermore, he has the Celestial Emperor’s backing. I reckon the conspirators wouldn’t dare to go too far.”

Zu An was awed. “Little sister Ling’er, your analysis is spot-on. You've grown a lot over the years.”

The Saintess blushed. “I took big brother Zu’s teachings to heart and put them into practice.”

Zu An’s heart twinged at her coy appearance. He was about to touch her face when they heard a masculine voice outside asking, “Friends on that crystal boat, may I hitch a ride?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2522: Celestial Heaven, Houtu
chapters.body:
Chapter 2522: Celestial Heaven, Houtu

Startled, Zu An and the Saintess made their way to the deck and saw a tall, muscular man flying not too far away. He wore a tigerskin cloth covering only half of his upper body, along with a pair of shorts. His thighs and arms bulged impressively. Despite his muscular stature, he didn’t seem heavy. Rather, his muscles seemed to be pulsating with explosive prowess. 

Such a physique would win the reverence of the gym rats in my previous world.

Zu An quickly pulled himself together and apologized, “I’m sorry, but we're rushing to Kunlun Mountain. I’m afraid we can’t take you along.”

Had it been any other occasion, he would likely have invited the other party onto the Crystal Boat. This was a rare opportunity to interact with a native and gather intelligence. However, they were in a rush to rescue Bao Jiang, so they dared not lose any time.

Unexpectedly, the man laughed. “What a coincidence! I’m heading to Kunlun Mountain too. We’re going in the same direction, so I won’t hinder you.”

Zu An was conflicted. 

The Saintess glanced at the man’s bow before sending a telepathic message to Zu An. “This man is strong and skilled at archery. If we offend him with our rejection, we might run the risk of him secretly sniping us with his arrow.”

Zu An nodded. “Since it’s convenient, please come aboard.”

As long as it didn’t hinder their journey, it didn’t matter whether they had an additional person or not. Anyway, this was a chance to interact and learn about this world. So, Zu An released the Crystal Boat’s defensive formation and allowed the man on. 

The man flew onto the deck. “Thank you!” He examined the boat and remarked in awe, “This flying boat is exquisite. I sense a dragon’s aura. Are you two descendants of the Dragon race?”

Zu An was amazed by his keen discernment. “We aren’t from the Dragon race, though this boat is a gift from a dragon friend.”

He dared not reveal it was from the East Sea Dragon King, as he didn’t know if this world had the concept of the Four Dragon Kings or not. 

The man nodded. “You must be an extraordinary person to receive such a valuable gift from the Ancient Dragon race.” 

Zu An was stunned. Ancient Dragon race? And he appears to harbor great respect for the Dragon race too. 

Such a remark would have been understandable if it happened in the future when the Dragon race was at the peak of its power. But it was hard to believe that the Dragon race still enjoyed such an exalted standing in this world filled with deities. 

“How should I address you?” the Saintess suddenly asked. 

The man clasped his fist and replied, “I am Yi from the Shaman races. How can I address the two of you?”

The Saintess and Zu An froze up. 

Yi?

In Zu An’s previous life, this man's legend was extremely famous. The last time he came to this world, Zu An himself had simulated the other party’s heroic journey. Could this be the legendary man who shot down the nine suns?

“You can call me Necropolis. She is my girlfriend, Yumen,” Zu An introduced themselves. 

“Girlfriend?” The man was initially confused, but he quickly picked up on the air of intimacy between them. “Ah, I get it. She’s your woman.”

The Saintess was speechless.

Seeing that the man had a candid personality, Zu An asked, “I see you're carrying a huge bow, so you must be proficient at archery, no?”

“I have learned it before,” the man replied with a smile in an unexpected show of humility. 

Just as Zu An was wondering how he could verify the other party’s identity, the Saintess suddenly asked, “If I could ask, what do you intend to do at Kunlun?”

The man’s face reddened. “I am heading there to propose to a woman.”

“Propose to a woman?” Zu An and the Saintess exchanged looks. They hadn’t expected such an answer. 

The man had no notable possession other than his bow. Even his clothes were tattered. It would be much more credible if he said he was heading to Kunlun to hunt instead. 

Seeing the doubt in their eyes, the man laughed awkwardly. “I encountered a bunch of detestable fiends along the way and fought them. My comrades lost their lives, and the betrothal gifts I brought were destroyed in battle. Of course, the fiends didn’t have it any better. We massacred them all despite being outnumbered.”

Killing intent flared from the man as he recalled the situation back then. 

“Fiends?” Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. This fellow seems stronger than I thought. 

The man snapped out of his recollection and quickly reined in his aura. “It’s the bunch you mortals refer to as celestials.”

“Celestials!” Zu An was shocked. He hadn’t expected the celestials to be fiends. Did this mean the Celestial Court was filled with fiends too? 

No wonder Mi Li mentioned the Celestial Court’s Fiend races! And this fellow just massacred a bunch of celestials?

He had no idea how strong those celestials were compared to Er Fu, but he had clearly underestimated the man’s strength. 

“What sparked the fight?” the Saintess asked. As she assessed the man before her, she stayed a step behind Zu An. 

“What other reason is there? We fought because we saw each other as an eyesore,” the man casually replied. 

The Saintess seemed to understand that, but Zu An was shocked. “Is that it?”

“Of course.” The man was surprised by Zu An’s reaction. “The Shaman races and the Fiend races have fought for so many years that there is a deep-seated grudge between both sides. The relationship has become so convoluted that no one can untangle it anymore. So, we just come to blows whenever we see each other.”

“The war between the shamans and the fiends…” Zu An murmured. “Does the Celestial Emperor not intervene in this battle?” 

“The Celestial Emperor is merely the Fiend races’ Celestial Emperor. He has no power over us.” The man harrumphed. “We shamans worship Lady Houtu. We formed a pact in the past, stating that one would govern the heavens, and the other would govern the earth. That reduced the conflict between our races, but if both sides encounter each other, a fight is still inevitable.”

Zu An was taken aback. He was starting to get a grip on this world’s power balance. It was fortunate that he'd met this fellow on the way. The latter had provided him with too much useful information, especially concerning Houtu. He'd had an idea of how powerful Houtu was, but he hadn't thought that it would be to the extent of opposing the Celestial Emperor.

I hope I haven’t offended her in any way.

“May I know which lady is fortunate enough to be your lover?” the Saintess suddenly asked.

The man’s face reddened in shyness. He raised his head toward the sky as his eyes gleamed expectantly. “She’s the Chang clan’s Saintess, as well as their most beautiful woman.”

Zu An gasped. “Is that Saintess Chang'e?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2523: Chang’E
chapters.body:
Chapter 2523: Chang’E

Surprised, the man stared at Zu An with widened eyes. “How do you know?”

The Saintess also looked at Zu An with questioning eyes, perplexed by how he knew that name. 

Zu An was stunned. It really is Chang’e. This man must be the legendary figure who shot down the suns. 

Zu An’s lack of response caused the man to narrow his eyes dangerously. “Brother, are you after Chang’e as well?” 

Sensing the other party’s growing hostility, Zu An snapped out of his thoughts and smiled. “You misunderstand! You mentioned that she’s the Chang clan’s most beautiful Saintess. Her name spreads far and wide. It’s only normal for me to have heard about her.”

“Is that so?” The man breathed a sigh of relief. He scratched his head and laughed. “I didn’t expect her to be this famous. To think you've heard of her despite not even knowing about the grudge between the shamans and the fiends.”

Zu An wiped away some cold sweat. “Haha, it’s a coincidence.” He took the opportunity to gather more details. “You two must be very close, for you to be proposing to her.” 

The man looked at Zu An as if he was talking nonsense. “I haven’t even met her yet.”

It was Zu An’s turn to be dumbstruck. “You’re proposing to her despite having never met her?”

“Of course! The most beautiful woman should be with the strongest man!” the man declared as he flexed his bulging biceps. He turned to the Saintess and awkwardly added, “Your woman is beautiful too, but to me, the most beautiful woman is my future wife.”

The Saintess rolled her eyes. You might as well just stay quiet. 

Zu An could relate to him, saying, “Brother, I can tell that you’re a sentimental person. I am in awe.”

That compliment brought a wide grin to the man’s face. Fueled by excitement, he began sharing his history with Chang’e. “Actually, it’s not as if we're completely unrelated. My clan elders have made an agreement with the Chang clan to betroth us to each other.”

“Ah, so it was an arranged marriage!” Zu An and the Saintess nodded in realization.

The man heaved a big sigh. “But shortly after we were betrothed, our clan encountered a disaster and suffered great casualties, resulting in our clan’s decline. I reached the age of marriage long ago, but I didn’t want my future wife to suffer any grievances, so I decided to make a name for myself before proposing to her. That way, her reputation wouldn’t be sullied.”

This reminded Zu An of a webnovel trope in his previous life—a destitute young man slowly rising through the ranks with his own capabilities, eventually reaching the top of the world.

The Saintess pointed out, “You must be famous then.”
