The man patted his bow and proudly replied, “I dare say that if I proclaim myself second in the world at archery, no one will dare take the first spot.”

A gleam flickered across the Saintess’ eyes. “What a coincidence. My big brother happens to be skilled in archery too.”

That piqued the man’s interest. “Oh? What a coincidence! Brother Necropolis, where’s your bow? Shall we have a duel?”

Zu An glanced at the Saintess, surprised by her remark. Is it because she doesn’t want me to be overshadowed? But he was in no mood to have a duel when he had more important matters at hand. “She’s pulling your leg. I’m merely an amateur.”

The man frowned. “Your archery must be outstanding for her to say so. A man shouldn’t disappoint his woman. You don’t have to feel stressed. It’s just a friendly competition. Let’s keep things light-hearted, all right?” He casually gestured toward a flock of birds in the distance. “Those vultures are extremely cruel. They are fond of feasting on infants’ brain matter. Let’s shoot an arrow each and see who can shoot down more birds.”

It would be impolite to continue rejecting him now, so Zu An took out the Sun Slaying Bow. However, he immediately regretted his decision. He belatedly recalled that the man had a deep tie with the Sun Slaying Bow in history, having used it to shoot down the nine suns.

Upon seeing the red bow, the man’s eyes lit up out of enthusiasm. “What a wonderful bow!”

Zu An was worried that the man would covet the Sun Slaying Bow, but the latter was simply appreciating its craftsmanship. He heaved a sigh of relief and suggested, “Brother, why don’t you go first?”

The man frowned. “You’d be at a disadvantage if I went first. Why don’t you go first?”

Zu An smiled in embarrassment. “You’d be at a disadvantage if I went first too…”

“Then let’s go together.” The man laughed heartily. He nocked his arrow and released it like a shooting star. 

The Saintess was dumbstruck. 

The arrow pierced through a vulture’s eye before striking another, and another. It was baffling how the released arrow could twist and turn, catching up with the flustered vultures no matter where they fled to. In the blink of an eye, half of the vultures were already shot down. 

It was then that Zu An’s arrow arrived. There was a brilliant flash of light, and all the remaining birds, be it near or far, were blasted to cinders. 

The man was dumbstruck before bursting into laughter. “You shot down one more vulture than me. It’s your victory.”

Zu An shook his head. “Your archery skills are clearly superior to mine. I merely took advantage of this bow’s superior prowess.”

The man wasn’t bothered by that. “It’s also a skill to be able to acquire a good bow. Since that’s the case, why don’t we call it a draw?”

Zu An respected Yi’s hearty and magnanimous personality. 

While the two men were bonding with each other, the Saintess lowered her gaze. Her face was pale, and her fists were clenched tightly in her sleeves. 

Amid chatter, they finally arrived in the Chang clan’s vicinity. The man clasped his fist at Zu An and said, “Thank you for the ride. I've reached my destination. May we meet again.”

Zu An was curious to meet the legendary Chang’e, but he had more important matters at hand. Besides, he already considered Yi his friend now, and it wouldn’t do for him to steal his friend’s wife. 

“Brother Yi, may you return home with your bride!” Zu An clasped his fist as he bade farewell. 

“Hahaha! You can be sure that I’ll invite you to our wedding!” While embracing Zu An, the man took the opportunity to send a telepathic message over. “Be careful of your woman. She carries a fiend’s aura.”

Zu An was stunned. Before he could process what he had just heard, the other party had already leaped off the Crystal Boat with a hearty laugh, leaving behind a waving silhouette. 

Zu An turned to the dazed Saintess. Yi has sharp eyes. He’s able to tell that little sister Ling’er is from the Fiend races. 

Needless to say, Yumen Beiqing, as the Fiend races’ Saintess, was a fiend. It was inevitable for Yi, due to the war in this era, to harbor prejudice toward fiends. 

“What did he say earlier?” the Saintess asked as she walked over.

“He wished us a blissful marriage,” Zu An replied with a smile. There was no need to trouble her with such trivialities. 

The Saintess laughed awkwardly. Her face was slightly flushed, but her eyes were tinged with confusion and fear. 

Meanwhile, the man made his way to the foot of the mountain, where a town lay. He was about to report his background when he noticed that the Chang clan was in chaos, searching for something.  He grabbed a random passerby and asked, “What happened?”

“You…” The passerby was about to flare up when he sensed the man’s intimidating aura, so he obediently answered, “The Saintess had gone missing.”

“What?” The man was stunned. “Why would she suddenly go missing?”

The passerby replied, “I heard that the Saintess’ childhood fiancé is going to propose to her today. There are rumors he is three meters tall, his waist is three meters wide, and he devours ten cows each meal. How can our delicate Saintess marry such a brute? She must have fled to escape him.”

The man widened his eyes in disbelief. “What bullshit are you spouting?!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2524: Someone Who Shouldn’T Be There
chapters.body:
Chapter 2524: Someone Who Shouldn’T Be There

“It’s not bullshit. Everyone is saying that!” the passerby exclaimed indignantly. However, he suddenly assessed Yi’s clothes and widened his eyes in fear. “Y-You can’t be…”

Yi had black streaks all over his face. “That’s right, I am your Saintess’ fiancé, the brute you speak of!”

The passerby felt a rush of awkwardness. “It’s all a misunderstanding, bro! That’s not what I meant.”

“Then what did you mean?” Yi replied with a livid face.

The passerby was speechless. Why are you asking when you know the truth?

Yi looked at the bustling town in the distance. “Did your Saintess really escape?”

The passerby replied with a strained smile, “Our Saintess is indeed missing, but it might not be because of you. There could be other reasons at play.”

Yi’s complexion looked terrible. He had come with a heart brimming with excitement, his head filled with the image of his gorgeous fiancée welcoming him with a shy smile. Who could have thought that she would be intimidated by him instead?

The commotion on their side caught other people’s attention. Many Chang clansmen thought he was a troublemaker and rushed over with weapons in hand.

Yi could only explain himself, but that only made the Chang clansmen’s eyes turn more hostile. Chang’e was adored by all the youths in the Chang clan, so they were already dissatisfied with her fiancé despite having never met him before.  Who could have thought that the Saintess would disappear because of him? That further stoked their anger. 

Fortunately, a Chang clan elder rushed over to defuse the situation and led him into the inner hall to explain the situation. 

“What? The Golden Crow Third Crown Prince!” Yi was infuriated. He hadn’t expected there to be such a twist. 

The elder took out a golden feather that was still crackling with flame. “That’s right. The Saintess’ sudden disappearance caught us off guard. Many nosey folks have been speculating about her disappearance, thus the rumors about her… escaping from her marriage. We were worried that the truth could lead to an irreconcilable conflict, so we didn’t clarify the rumors.”

Yi was both delighted and worried. He was glad that Chang’e hadn’t run away because she detested him, but at the same time, he was worried because her disappearance appeared to be related to the Golden Crow Third Crown Prince. 

“We found this golden feather near the Saintess’ residence. He must have been here. Who knows what the Golden Crow race is plotting,” an elder muttered worriedly. 

“What else could they be plotting? Those fiends are trying to undermine the bonds within our Shaman races!” Yi slammed his hand on the table and shot to his feet. “I shall rescue Chang’e from the clutches of the Golden Crow Third Crown Prince!”

Ignoring the elders’ shouts for them to think things through, Yi rushed outside. The thought of his fiancé being in the Fiend races’ clutches made him feel as if ants were gnawing at his heart. 

If she loses even a strand of hair, I’ll go to the Scalding Valley[1] to settle the score with those Golden Crows! 

… 

After sending Yi off, Zu An continued maneuvering the Crystal Boat toward Kunlun Mountain. At the moment, he was in a room with Yumen Beiqing, discussing their analysis and speculations about Yi. However, Yumen Beiqing appeared to be distracted.

When Zu An asked her what was wrong, they suddenly heard a surprised exclamation outside. “Big brother Zu, is that you?”

Zu An’s body shuddered. He instantly teleported to the deck outside. 

A beautiful woman floated in midair, looking as if she had emerged from the fog. Her appearance made the dim sky light up a fair bit. This woman could enchant anyone no matter how many times they saw her. Her curvaceous figure was wrapped in a sea-colored dress, and her fair skin glowed radiantly like the moonlight. 

Zu An was both surprised and delighted to see her. He hadn’t expected to meet her here.

It was the Mermaid Queen, Gong Suyin!

“Big brother Zu, it really is you!” Her voice sounded a little hoarse. Unable to hold back her excitement, she leaped right into his arms. 

Had it been in the past, Zu An would have avoided her. But sensing the intense feelings she harbored for him, he couldn’t bring himself to do that. He embraced her, and that made him realize that her body was trembling. “Yes, it’s me. You don’t have to be afraid.” 

Yumen Beiqing arrived on the deck slightly later than Zu An. The first thing she saw was a ridiculously beautiful woman leaping into her lover’s arms as if their love transcended life and death. That made her feel a rush of jealousy. Where did this hussy come from?

That thought alarmed her. Why would I feel jealousy? Do I truly have feelings for this fellow? How is that possible?!

She quickly consoled herself. It’s normal to be angry if someone takes my toy away without my permission. 

It was then that the Mermaid Queen noticed Yumen Beiqing and anxiously tore herself away from Zu An’s embrace. “Big brother Zu, she is…”

“She’s the Fiend races’ Saintess, whom you were full of praises for and have been wanting to meet, Yumen Beiqing.” Zu An figured that there was no point in saying too much, since she wouldn’t know Xie Daoyun. “Saintess, this is the Ocean races’ Mermaid Queen, Gong Suyin.”

“As expected of the Ocean races’ number one beauty. How gorgeous!” Yumen Beiqing remarked as she eyed the other party’s chest. She’s big. They're almost on par with mine. 

“So you’re the Fiend races’ Saintess! I have heard a lot about you.” The Mermaid Queen was surprised too. She had heard a lot about this legendary woman and had been wanting to meet her, but who could have thought that they would meet under such circumstances?

Their exchange of pleasantries was a little awkward. 

Yumen Beiqing could guess their relationship from their intimate embrace. This playboy! The last time I saw him, he had three gorgeous women with him. Now, he has yet another one here. And how can all of them be so beautiful?!

Despite only being in this world for a short time, he's already gotten together with even the famed Mermaid Queen. Is he an incubus?!

The Mermaid Queen was perplexed too. She could tell that something was going on between Zu An and the Fiend races’ Saintess—the intimacy between lovers was obvious. 

Isn’t big brother Zu from the future? Why is he together with the Saintess? Was everything he told me before a lie? Does he not like me at all?

Her face darkened. The clouds gathered, as if lamenting alongside her. 

“What brings you here?” That was the biggest question in Zu An’s mind. Given that they had entered this place via the Fiend races’ Imperial Tomb, it was baffling that the Mermaid Queen was here too. 

The Mermaid Queen was about to speak when countless figures charged out of the forest. “The Saintess is there! Capture her!” 

Zu An instinctively glanced at Yumen Beiqing. The latter looked bewildered too. Why is someone after me in this world?

“Chang’e, you aren’t getting away!”

Those words, along with the anxiously hiding Mermaid Queen, evoked an utterly ridiculous thought in Zu An’s mind. “You can’t be Chang’e, can you?”

1. In Chinese folklore, the Scalding Valley is where the sun rises from. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2525: The Victim Comes Knocking
chapters.body:
Chapter 2525: The Victim Comes Knocking

The Mermaid Queen was stunned. “Big brother Zu, how do you know that? I don’t know what’s going on either. Ever since I arrived here, everyone has been calling me Chang’e.”

Zu An and Yumen Beiqing were speechless.

Zu An slapped his forehead. How could something so cliche happen here? I was just wishing Brother Yi and Chang’e a blissful marriage, only to learn right after that Chang’e is the Mermaid Queen. How am I supposed to earnestly wish them happiness now?

While he had been keeping his distance from the Mermaid Queen, he couldn’t turn a blind eye when she was forced to marry a man she didn’t like. The thought of Yi angrily confronting him over this gave him a splitting headache.

However, this wasn’t the time to be thinking about such things. He turned to the soldiers and saw them encircling the Crystal Boat, their eyes focused on the Mermaid Queen. 

Zu An frowned. He sensed auras similar to Er Fu coming from those people. According to Yi, these people were likely from the Fiend races. The good thing was that these fiends were nowhere as powerful as Er Fu or Wei Yueyan. However, the bad news was that their auras weren’t weak; they were at least at the level of the strongest of the Eighteen Monster Generals.

“Why are they pursuing you?” Yumen Beiqing asked with a frown. 

The Mermaid Queen shook her head. “I don’t know either. I learned that Chang’e is betrothed to someone, and her fiancé is going to propose to her today. There was no way I could accept the marriage, so I escaped. However, these people suddenly appeared out of nowhere and began hunting me down. It took everything I had to flee here.

“I was in despair until I noticed the Crystal Boat. I thought I was dreaming, so I tried calling out to you. I didn’t expect it to truly be big brother Zu!”

As she looked at Zu An with deepening feelings, her eyes began to brim with tears. She felt that this was all decided by fate. If the world is already helping me, how can I back off like I did before?

Zu An and Yumen Beiqing felt sympathy for Yi. However, they turned to the crowd encircling the Crystal Boat. 

“Is there some kind of misunderstanding here? My friend doesn’t know you.”

Those people exchanged looks. The pig-headed leader standing in front said, “That's the Chang clan’s Saintess Chang’e; we wouldn’t mistake her for anyone else. Our Golden Crow Third Crown Prince wishes to meet her. You better not stick your head where it doesn’t belong.”

They noticed how extraordinary the Crystal Boat was, not to mention it was tinged with the Dragon race’s aura. They couldn’t figure out Zu An’s background on the spur of the moment, so they chose to bring out their backing to intimidate the other party. 

“The Golden Crow Third Crown Prince…” Zu An’s face turned cold. My eight characters[1] must clash with the Golden Crow race. Why do I get into a conflict with them at every turn?!

Yumen Beiqing harrumphed coldly. “Nonsense! How can you be the subordinates of the Golden Crow Third Crown Prince?”

Zu An was taken aback. Little sister Ling’er’s reaction is weird. Has she gotten on close terms with the Golden Crow Imperial Family in the years she spent as the Fiend races’ Saintess? 

Even though the Celestial World’s Golden Crow race was bound to be different from the Cultivation World’s Golden Crow race, there were still bloodline ties between them. 

The pig-headed chief eyed Yumen Beiqing with greedy eyes. “Beauty, there’s no need to prove to you that we are the Golden Crow Third Crown Prince’s subordinates. Who in the world would dare to use his name in vain?”

He was surprised to see that someone could rival Chang’e in beauty. 

Yumen Beiqing coldly sneered, “Chang’e is the fiancée of the Shaman races’ powerhouse, Yi. There have been many conflicts between the shamans and the fiends over the years, and their relationship has gotten so tense that a war can easily break out at a moment’s notice. If the Golden Crow Third Crown Prince kidnapped Chang’e at this juncture, he would not only offend Yi but the Chang clan as well. That’s bound to stir a massive war between the races.

“The Golden Crow Third Crown Prince is famous in the Celestial Court; he would have surely thought of that. Taking a thousand steps back, even if he was interested in Chang’e, he would capture her quietly instead of announcing his identity in public.”

The fiends exchanged awkward looks.

The Mermaid Queen was awed by Yumen Beiqing. As expected of the legendary Saintess. She is truly wise.

Even Zu An was surprised. Little sister Ling’er has changed a lot. 

The pig-headed chief was infuriated. “A woman like you can’t begin to fathom a celestial’s thoughts. Hand Chang’e over right now, or don’t blame us for getting violent!”

His comrades raised their weapons, ready to make a move at his command.

Just then, a crisp bell rang—it was the Horror Bell! The surroundings fiends felt a surge of fear. They were worried that their master’s plan had been unraveled. 

At the same time, a massive black flag emanating a ghostly aura appeared in midair. With a slight sway of the flag, the surrounding fiends suddenly felt their mental states being rattled. It was the Fallen Soul Banner!

These two artifacts were Zu An’s spoils of war from the Monster World’s top experts. As the trump cards of those top experts, their prowess wasn't to be scoffed at. 

But while the artifacts had shaken up the fiends, they weren’t enough to claim their lives. Zu An also hadn't been expecting the artifacts to do that much; it was good enough as long as the items at least sent them into a temporary daze. 

He was about to make a move when he suddenly frowned. Without any hesitation, he transported the Mermaid Queen into the cabin using a teleportation formation and instructed, “Don’t make a noise.”

In the next moment, a red light shot over from afar. Just as it had impaled the vultures in the sky, it easily claimed the heads of the fiends surrounding the Crystal Boat. It was so fast that the fiends didn’t have time to react before their heads burst apart. 

Only the pig-headed chief had time to raise his weapon, but he was still too late. The red light pierced through his forehead, and he plummeted from the sky. 

Soon, Yi flew over from afar. He lowered his head and saw the pig-headed chief’s body dissipating as a light wisp of smoke, and that made him frown. “That fellow still has some ability. To think that he escaped with his primordial spirit.”

This reunion left Zu An feeling conflicted. “Brother Yi, thank you for your help.”

At the same time, he was astounded. Even though the Horror Bell and the Fallen Soul Banner had delayed the fiends’ reaction, it was still shocking to see Yi massacring all of them from afar with a single arrow. It looked as if the latter hadn’t gone all out in their previous friendly duel.

“Brother Necropolis, you could have easily dealt with them even if I didn’t make a move. It’s just that I’m not in a good mood now, so I’m taking it out on them,” Yi huffed. 

Zu An smiled. “Why are you so angry, Brother Yi? Weren’t you in high spirits when we parted? Oh right, I thought you were going to propose to Chang’e?”

Yumen Beiqing rolled her eyes. This fellow is good at feigning ignorance. 

“Don’t mention it. Just thinking about that makes me angry,” Yi grumbled indignantly. “The Golden Crow Third Crown Prince kidnapped Chang’e. I’m chasing after him now. Did you see anyone suspicious along the way?”

Zu An pointed to the corpses on the floor. “Those people are plenty suspicious…”

Yi was confused.

Zu An quickly explained the situation. “They claim to be the Golden Crow Third Crown Prince’s subordinates, but they might not be trustworthy.”

He quickly shared the Saintess’ earlier analysis.

“I won’t refute that possibility, but the Golden Crow Third Crown Prince is still the likeliest suspect. We found his feather at Chang’e’s residence.” Yi clenched his fists.

Yumen Beiqing’s face stiffened.

1. The Eight Characters indicate the year, month, day and hour of a person's birth, which are often used in fortune-telling in China. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2526: I’M Willing To Pay Any Price
chapters.body:
Chapter 2526: I’M Willing To Pay Any Price

“Could someone be framing him?” Yumen Beiqing asked. 

Yi waved his hand. “That’s unlikely. Given the lofty stature of the Golden Crow Third Crown Prince, how can he not know if someone steals his feather?”

Yumen Beiqing fell silent. His words made sense.

Yi clasped his fist and said, “Thank you for your intelligence. I’ll pursue that pig head’s primordial spirit now. If you find Chang’e, please inform me or the Chang clan.” He passed a piece of jade to Zu An. “You can contact me through this jade if you find anything.”

Zu An stowed away the jade and replied with an awkward smile, “Alright.”

Before leaving in a hurry, Yi thanked the two of them again. He was anxious to find his missing fiancée.

“You seem to be very concerned about the Golden Crow Third Crown Prince?” Zu An asked Yumen Beiqing with an ambiguous smile.

Yumen Beiqing chuckled naturally. “I have fought alongside the Golden Crow race for many years. The thought that someone might be framing them bothers me.” She quickly added, “Do you not plan to tell Yi about Chang’e’s whereabouts? If he learns that you have been lying to him, he might turn against you.”

Zu An was in a dilemma. “I can’t hand the Mermaid Queen to him. I can only take things one step at a time.”

He dared not call the Mermaid Queen to the deck because of her prominent identity in this world, so he headed down into the cabin to look for her.

The Mermaid Queen was sitting nervously on a chair. It was only upon seeing him that she revealed a faint smile. “So that’s Yi. You are acquainted with him?”

“It was a coincidence. We bumped into him along the way and took him with us.” Zu An still found this situation ridiculous. “So, how did you come here?”

The Mermaid Queen’s face reddened as she stuttered, “I don’t know either. One day, I suddenly heard a woman’s voice, saying that she could fulfill my wish, but she asked if I was willing to pay the price for it…”

Zu An got agitated. “What price did you pay?”

The Mermaid Queen pursed her lips. “I didn’t pay anything. Perhaps it was because she pitied me. When I opened my eyes, I was already in this world. Then… I met you.”

After parting ways with Zu An back then, she'd returned to the Ocean races to distribute the antidote to her people. The Ocean races had returned to their former strength, but she felt lonelier than ever.

One day, she'd gone to a sandbank on the surface of the sea and gazed at the moon while silently murmuring her feelings for Zu An. However, as they weren’t from the same spacetime, she knew that they didn’t have a future together. The more she thought about it, the more depressed she became. In the end, she couldn’t hold back her tears.

After crying for a long time, she'd suddenly heard a faint woman’s voice asking her if she truly wanted to be together with her big brother Zu. The Mermaid Queen had naturally replied with a nod.

The woman had then asked her, “Will you pay the price if I realize this wish for you?”

“I will!” It sounded reckless in hindsight, but back then, she'd answered that question without hesitation. 

The woman had been surprised. “You answered despite not knowing the price you have to pay?”

“I’m willing to pay any price as long as I can be with big brother Zu.” The Mermaid Queen knew that there was no way they could be together, so she'd wanted to hear the woman out even if she was lying to her. 

“What a fool…” The woman sighed. “You’ll suffer long years of loneliness just for a brief reunion with him. Are you still willing to do so?”

The Mermaid Queen had been stunned. She wasn't hesitant because of the price, but because the other party’s offer was starting to sound credible. Perplexed, she asked, “Who are you?”

“You need not care who I am. You just have to answer my question,” the woman had replied.

The Mermaid Queen had hardened her determination. “I’m willing to pay the price!”

She'd thought that the woman was lying, but even if she wasn't, she was willing to pay the price to be with big brother Zu.

Shortly after she answered the question, she suddenly fell into a daze. By the time she snapped out of it, she had already become the Chang clan’s Saintess, and she had a fiancé who was waiting to propose to her. This had shocked her. She had no idea why she had suddenly been transported into this foreign world. 

However, she had no time to think too much about it. The imminent arrival of her fiancé had stoked her sense of urgency. It had crossed her mind to wonder whether that fiancé named Yi was big brother Zu, but she hadn’t dared to bet on it. If her bet was wrong, it would all be over. So, she'd chosen to escape.

Little had she expected to encounter a group of assassins shortly after she fled. She'd had to exhaust all her methods before she managed to temporarily shake them off her trail. 

She'd thought she wouldn’t have a chance to see big brother Zu again, but the familiar Crystal Boat suddenly appeared in front of her. She'd called out to him with some skepticism, but the big brother Zu whom she'd been thinking about day and night really had appeared in front of her. At that moment, she'd felt so blissful that she could faint.  

She shared everything that had happened with Zu An, though she did leave out some of the more embarrassing details, including the exact price she had to pay. 

I wouldn’t mind telling everything to big brother Zu if there were no one else around, but the Fiend races’ Saintess is right there. They seem to be lovers. It wouldn’t be good for me to say those words here. 

Even so, Zu An and Yumen Beiqing were still stunned. 

“Who was that woman? To think that she has the power to directly transport you to this world!” Yumen Beiqing was far more shocked than she let on. Even she at her peak didn’t possess such powers.

The Mermaid Queen shook her head. “I don’t know who that woman is. I tried contacting her after arriving in this world, but I never heard that voice ever again.”

“A woman’s voice…” Zu An could already fathom a guess. “Can you imitate that voice?”

“I should be able to.” The Mermaid Queen was a born musician. She had extreme sensitivity toward melodies and voices. She closed her eyes to recall the situation back then before slowly imitating the woman’s words. 

Others could have struggled with the details, but the Mermaid Queen, with her impeccable skills, was able to fully recreate the woman’s voice. 

Yumen Beiqing widened her eyes. To think it was her!

Zu An also looked conflicted. Seconds later, he let out a sigh. “I have an idea of who it is.”

Yumen Beiqing was surprised. She looked at Zu An with questioning eyes, not expecting him to know who that woman was. 

“Who is it?” the Mermaid Queen anxiously asked. That was the thing she was the most curious about. Who could have the power to fulfill her wish?

Zu An took a deep breath before slowly revealing a name. “Lady Houtu.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2527: Everything Is Fated
chapters.body:
Chapter 2527: Everything Is Fated

Upon hearing the name, Yumen Beiqing looked grim.

“Lady Houtu…” The Mermaid Queen was initially confused before she was overtaken by shock. “Is that the person you met in the underworld?”

Zu An nodded. He would have wondered if Houtu was capable of such a feat in the past, but after learning that Houtu was a being on par with the Celestial Emperor, he realized that she was far stronger than he had expected. Not to mention she governed the Six Paths of Reincarnation. It shouldn’t be an impossible feat for her to transport someone across worlds. 

The true question was why she'd done that. Furthermore, she had chosen not to reveal her identity. If not for the Mermaid Queen perfectly emulating her voice, they wouldn’t have known she was behind this. 

She didn’t send me any messages even though I promised to find and deliver the six power insignias to her. Instead, she suddenly sent the Mermaid Queen here. What is she planning?

And what could the price the Mermaid Queen mentioned be?

Many doubts popped into his mind. He felt as if he'd been dragged into a massive conspiracy, and that there was a pair, or even many pairs, of eyes staring at him. The thought of it made him shudder.

“Why did Lady Houtu do that?” The Mermaid Queen was perplexed.

Yumen Beiqing’s eyes darted around as if she had a thought in mind, but she couldn’t confirm it.

Zu An asked out of curiosity, “Little sister Ling’er, what are your thoughts?”

“There’s too little information to work with.” Yumen Beiqing shook her head. She turned to the Mermaid Queen and asked, “What do you plan to do next?”

The Mermaid Queen had no answer. She knew too little about this world, and she vaguely sensed that the Fiend races’ Saintess was hostile to her. Is it because of big brother Zu…

Fortunately, Zu An interjected, “You should come with us. I’ll keep you safe.”

The Mermaid Queen nodded in relief. Now that she had his promise, nothing else was important.

Yumen Beiqing hesitantly spoke up. “The earlier clash showed that Yi is extremely strong. If he learns that his fiancée is with you, things will not end well.”

Zu An sighed. “I’ll explain it to him. If he can’t accept it, we’ll have no choice but to come to blows. I can’t turn a blind eye to Suyin.” He couldn’t bring himself to abandon the Mermaid Queen, whether it was due to his sense of responsibility, or the feelings he had for her. 

When she heard Zu An calling her name, the Mermaid Queen’s eyes narrowed into tender crescent smiles.

Yumen Beiqing wanted to say more, but she suddenly thought of another possibility, and her eyes lit up. This development isn’t that bad either… 

So, she grabbed the Mermaid Queen’s hand and said, “My queen, please forgive me for my harsh words. I was just worried about our circumstances; I don’t mean to leave you in a difficult situation. Pardon me for that.”

The Mermaid Queen was already in a good mood from her reunion with Zu An, and now, the Saintess was expressing her goodwill as well. There was no reason for her to mind it. “Don’t worry, Saintess. We should be standing on the same front and working together.”

Yumen Beiqing wasn’t used to this heartfelt sisterly interaction, but she knew it wouldn’t be apt for her to retract her hand now. 

Fortunately, the Mermaid Queen changed the topic, asking, “How did the two of you get here?” 

Yumen Beiqing took this chance to pull her hand away. “We’re heading to Kunlun Mountain to rescue Celestial Bao Jiang…” She quickly summarized the situation for the Mermaid Queen.

“Bao Jiang?” The Mermaid Queen frowned. “I have heard that name.”

“Where did you hear it?” Zu An was overjoyed. Kunlun Mountain was so massive that finding Bao Jiang there was as good as finding a needle in the ocean. Having some clues could greatly narrow their search radius. 

“I remember it now!” The Mermaid Queen’s eyes lit up. “He stopped by and stayed at the Chang clan's residence for a few days. He was looking for a herb, and the clan elder guided him. Back then, I was too shaken up by this foreign world to pay much attention, so I only remembered it just now.”

“When did he arrive at the Chang clan?” Yumen Beiqing asked.

The Mermaid Queen spent a moment in thought before replying, “Around half a month ago.”

“What?” Zu An was astounded.

He'd been hoping to catch up with Bao Jiang to save his life, but even if that didn’t work out, it would be wonderful if he could get some clues about the Brilliant Pinflower’s whereabouts. But Bao Jiang had arrived at the Chang clan half a month ahead of them. There was just no way they could catch up with him anymore. 

The schemers could have already killed Bao Jiang and obtained clues about the immortal medicine. Learning that their hard work was all in vain made Zu An slump in disappointment. 

“There’s no need to be too disheartened. There’s a saying that a day in heaven is equivalent to a year on earth. Who knows? Bao Jiang might have just reported to the Celestial Court and started on his way back now,” Yumen Beiqing said. “When do you think would be the best time for the culprit to make a move? If Bao Jiang were killed on his way to report to the Celestial Emperor, the Celestial Court would swiftly notice something was amiss. But if Bao Jiang met the Celestial Emperor and received his orders, the Celestial Court would be unlikely to contact him again in the short term. If he died on his way back, the Celestial Court could fail to notice it for decades.”

Zu An’s eyes lit up upon hearing that analysis. “You’re sharp to have thought about that, little sister Ling’er!” If I were the culprit, I would have chosen to strike on Bao Jiang’s way back too.

Furthermore, with his current grasp of spatial transformations, he knew that the adage that a day in heaven was equivalent to a year on earth wasn't just hearsay.

He anxiously asked the Mermaid Queen, “Do you know where Bao Jiang headed when he left?” He figured that Bao Jiang could return via the same path. 

The Mermaid Queen gave it some thought. A blue light glowed on her fingertip, and she drew a map on the table. “While planning my escape, I paid close attention to the terrain around the Chang clan's residence. I think he left from here.”

Zu An compared the Mermaid Queen’s map to the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s map. The maps were broadly similar, with the former having more details. Having confirmed the direction, he quickly steered the Crystal Boat over.

Just like that, they flew for days but didn’t find anything. The trio began to worry that they had gotten the direction wrong.

Out of the blue, however, Yumen Beiqing pointed in a direction and exclaimed, “I see movement there!”

Zu An looked over and saw a forest swaying. He didn’t sense anything unnatural about the movement, but he still decided to take a look. While they were heading there, he noticed a highly profound formation in the forest. He exchanged looks with Yumen Beiqing, and the two of them began hurling formation flags around. Soon, the surrounding space warped to reveal a white-bearded elder leaning against a big tree. 

The white-bearded elder’s snowy-white robe was dyed red with blood. He looked to have suffered a severe blow to his ribcage. He had no doubt constructed the formation to conceal himself, yet he didn’t react to the breaching of his formation. 

“Bao Jiang!” Recalling the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s description, Zu An could tell that the other party was the person they had been looking for at a glance. 

When she saw Bao Jiang’s condition, Yumen Beiqing trembled. Her face turned pale, and her eyes became dazed. Has it all been decided? Is it impossible to change fate… 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2528: Fate
chapters.body:
Chapter 2528: Fate

Bao Jiang was said to have white hair but a youthful face, but the severe injuries he had sustained made him look no different from an unkempt old man. 

Seeing Bao Jiang’s shallow breathing, Zu An quickly took out a spirit medicine and fed it to him. It was a pity he had depleted his Feather Mountain Spring Water, and he didn’t know if the pills he had concocted in the future would work on a deity. 

After swallowing the spirit medicine, Bao Jiang’s face regained some redness. His eyelids fluttered, and he weakly opened his eyes. “Did I just eat an immortal…”

Upon seeing Zu An’s group, he quickly stopped mid-sentence and asked, “You are?”

“Elder, we visited Treasure Origin Mountain under the Kaiming Six Shamans’ guidance to request a Brilliant Pinflower from you. However, we learned that someone was after your life, so we rushed over to aid you with the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s directions.” Zu An didn’t want to look so eager to claim credit, but Bao Jiang looked as if he would drop dead at any moment. They had worked so hard to save Xiaoxi, not hesitating to even travel across spacetime to gather the required materials. He didn’t even want to imagine what would happen if everything was in vain. 

Those names brought some spirit back into Bao Jiang’s turbid eyes. He reached into his robe and took out a flower. “I harvested this flower a few days back. You may have it.”

Zu An looked at the flower; it had a glass-like transparency and resembled a pinflower. He didn’t need an introduction to know that this was what he was looking for.

After Zu An accepted the Brilliant Pinflower, Bao Jiang continued, “I have something I would like to ask of you, little brother…” He took out a gleaming golden pearl and passed it to Zu An. “Please hand this Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine to the Goddess of Wu Mountain. Have her entrust it to a fated someone to nurture it…” 

Immortal medicine? 

Yumen Beiqing and the Mermaid Queen looked at the golden pearl. Is this the legendary immortal medicine?

“The Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine!” Zu An shuddered. 

No one was more familiar with the Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine than him. He had found one in the Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons in the future and used it to save Qiu Honglei. Mi Li had told him that the immortal medicines in this world were unique. This Divine Dragon Immortal medicine had to be the seed of the one he encountered in the Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons in the future.

Zu An suddenly felt as if he was trapped in the constructs of fate, and it made him feel helpless and fearful. 

“Who’s behind your injuries?” Yumen Beiqing asked. 

Bao Jiang shook his head with a smile. “Knowing too much will only put you in danger. I’ll be grateful to you if you can fulfill my final wish…”

In the end, his voice faded away.

Alarmed, Zu An quickly checked on Bao Jiang. Seconds later, he shook his head at the two women and said, “He has passed on.”

“We’re still too late. Gu got to him before us,” the Mermaid Queen remarked with a sigh. Zu An had already filled her in on the details along the way. Even though she wasn’t acquainted with Bao Jiang, she could tell that he was a kind person from how he didn’t want to put them in danger despite already being at death’s door. 

“Who are you, woman? Why do you know my name?” an eerie voice echoed from afar.

Zu An and the others were startled.

They looked over and saw a giant snake coiled around a colossal tree. The upper half of his body was that of a young man with red hair that fluttered with the wind, resembling little serpents. His irises were yellow slits, and his forked tongue flicked out now and then. He looked terrifying. 

Zu An’s complexion turned awful. Yu Yanluo must have monopolized the beautiful genes of the Snake race. Why do all other snakes look so amazingly hideous?

A massive, bizarre bird resembling an eagle-owl hybrid landed on a nearby branch. It had a white head, a black spotted body, a red beak, and tiger-like claws. No one would doubt that a single swipe of its claws could tear through even a flood dragon.

It overheard Gu’s question and answered, “Milord, this woman appears to be the Chang clan’s Saintess.” 

“The most beautiful woman in the world, Chang’e? She is indeed beautiful, and the woman beside her doesn’t pale much in comparison either. Looks like today is my day!” Gu eyed Zu An and the others before bursting into laughter.

Yumen Beiqing’s expression turned cold. The Mermaid Queen hid behind Zu An in fear. 

“You must be here for the immortal medicine. Here, you may have it.” Zu An tossed the golden pearl toward the distant mountain valley.

At the same time, he grabbed Yumen Beiqing and the Mermaid Queen’s hands and fled in the opposite direction using Grandgale, not hesitating in the slightest. He didn’t bother to talk things out with Gu, knowing that the latter would choose to silence them regardless of the situation. Not to mention, Gu had expressed interest in Yumen Beiqing and the Mermaid Queen. That made it even less likely for him to spare them. 

The words ‘immortal medicine’ caught Gu’s attention. He lunged at the golden pearl at a lightning-fast speed that seemed impossible for his massive body. As soon as he caught the golden item, however, his smile suddenly stiffened.

It was an ordinary piece of gold.

“How dare they fool me?” Gu gritted his teeth and crushed the piece of gold into dust. “Qin, capture them!”

Zu An executed a series of instantaneous movements, causing the scenery to swiftly retreat behind him. However, he didn’t look relieved in the slightest. The bizarre bird, Qin, was heading in his direction, and its movements were even faster than his. The other party was likely a subordinate deity similar to Wei Yueyan; it would be foolish to think of him as a simple beast. 

Grandgale had a cooldown time. Knowing none of them would get away at this rate, Zu An pushed the two ladies ahead and shouted, “You two leave first! I’ll find you later!”

He drew his Tai’e Sword, turned around, and unleashed a burst of sword ki at Qin.

Qin’s eyes narrowed at the sight of the massive outburst of sword ki. A divine halo consisting of red, white, and black light enveloped its claws, and it swiped at the incoming sword ki. The energy was torn to shreds. 

Zu An was stunned. He had expected Qin would be a formidable opponent, but he hadn't thought the other party would be this powerful. In particular, the celestials’ divine halo appeared to have a suppression effect on all other living beings. 

In the blink of an eye, the other party’s claws were already right in front of him. Space seemed to freeze before it—the other party was guarding against his instantaneous movement. No matter where Zu An dodged, he couldn’t escape the fate of having his head split apart. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2529: The Meeting Of The Wind And Clouds
chapters.body:
Chapter 2529: The Meeting Of The Wind And Clouds

Yumen Beiqing and the Mermaid Queen rode on the momentum of a powerful force for several kilometers before finally drifting to a halt. 

The Mermaid Queen wanted to turn back, but Yumen Beiqing grabbed her. “Where are you going?”

“I can’t let big brother Zu brave danger alone!” the Mermaid Queen exclaimed. 

Yumen Beiqing snorted in anger. “Can’t you tell that they are both celestials? They are many times stronger than you and me. You won’t be of any help to him; you’ll merely be marching to your death.”

The Mermaid Queen replied, “I am aware of my weakness, but my buffs should be able to help big brother Zu. Taking a step back, even if we lose the battle, I am content to die together with big brother Zu.” Ignoring Yumen Beiqing, she ran back. 

Yumen Beiqing was left looking conflicted. Why are so many women so devoted to that fellow? She stomped her feet and followed the Mermaid Queen. She'd been planning to send the Mermaid Queen away before sneaking back to save Zu An, but given the current circumstances, she could only play along and see what she could do. 

On the other hand, Zu An realized that he couldn’t dodge Qin’s claws, so he brandished his Tai’e Sword to parry the attack instead. It was a flesh body clashing against a divine weapon, yet sparks flew. With a loud clang, the two of them were knocked backward over a hundred meters away. 

Qin circled the sky while eyeing the Tai’e Sword. “That’s a good sword.”

Zu An frowned. Even though the clash was a draw, he had the weapon advantage, and he had employed the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra to absorb the other party’s striking force to achieve this effect.

Qin was even stronger than Wei Yueyan. I wonder how the power levels of these celestials are determined.

However, Zu An didn’t panic. Over the years, he had accrued plenty of experience clashing with stronger opponents. It was only in recent days, after obtaining the Cultivation World’s World Law Beacon, that his battles had become much easier. He'd nearly forgotten how intense a battle could be.

Just then, two silhouettes rushed over from afar. “Big brother Zu, I’m here to help you!”

Hearing the Mermaid Queen’s voice and seeing Yumen Beiqing trailing behind, Zu An felt a headache settling in. It would have been much easier for him to slip away if they had left first. Now that they had returned, his only option was a fight to the death. 

However, he didn’t blame them. How many men dreamed of a woman putting her life on the line to brave danger with them? That made him puff his chest outward, and he said, “Very well, let’s fight together!”

Even though the enemy was stronger than them, there was still a shot at victory.

“What arrogant words from a mere ant!” a cold voice echoed. A snake body slithered across the tree branches. 

Zu An and the others were taken aback. By the time they looked up, the celestial named Gu had already enveloped the entire forest with his body, with his head loftily assessing them from above. 

Inexplicable pressure bore down on Zu An and the others, causing their faces to turn pale. This suppression seemed to be caused by more than the disparity in their cultivation; it also felt as if a deity was exerting his dominion on mortals. 

Zu An’s heart sank. He'd been confident he could dea with Qin even though the latter was a deity, but if the stronger Gu were to join the battle too, the chances of victory were slim even if he brought out all his trump cards. 

The best I can do here is mutual destruction with the two of them. Hopefully I can save little sister Ling’er and the Mermaid Queen. 

The Mermaid Queen racked her brain. We’re probably cornered here. I should find a chance to activate my founding ancestor’s forbidden art so big brother Zu has a chance of survival. 

Yumen Beiqing was deep in thought. How can I save Zu An while hiding my identity? What a headache!

“A twat who struts around as if he’s a big shot just because he has a good bloodline thinks that he’s a deity?” A hearty laugh echoed.

A figure flashed over from afar. Gu tried to stop the figure with his snake body enveloping the forest, but the latter easily blasted through him with a punch. 

Zu An was overjoyed. “Brother Yi, what brings you here?”

Yi clasped his fist at Zu An, only to be momentarily startled when he saw the Mermaid Queen. However, he quickly snapped out of it and replied, “I chased the pig head’s primordial spirit here when I sensed the jade I gave you shattering in the vicinity, so I came over to check things out.” 

Zu An reached into his robe and retrieved a shattered piece of jade. It turned out that he had failed to properly neutralize Qin’s attack earlier. The shockwave wasn’t strong enough to hurt his resilient body, but the same couldn’t be said about Yi’s brittle jade. 

“Yi?” Gu’s face darkened. He recognized the other party. This makes things troublesome. 

“Gu, you’re getting from bad to worse. You’re a celestial, for heaven’s sake. Don’t you find it humiliating to be picking on a couple of mortals?” Yi sneered. 

“You should worry about yourself,” Gu scoffed. “I heard you were betrothed to the Chang clan’s Saintess, so why is your fiancée with another man?”

Yi was taken aback. He turned to the Mermaid Queen and asked, “You’re Chang’e?” No wonder I felt something when I saw her earlier. 

The Mermaid Queen was intimidated, but it was beneath her to hide behind a man and draw hatred for him. She took a deep breath and said, “It’s a misunderstanding. I’ll explain things to you when it’s all over.”

“All right.” Yi didn’t pursue the matter. It was enough for him that she was Chang’e.

Gu continued to provoke Yi, saying, “Hoh, I didn’t expect the renowned Yi to be a tortoise. I just saw Chang’e getting all chummy with that man, looking as if they would do a lover’s suicide.”

Yi’s cheeks twitched. “No one will think you’re a mute just because you stay silent.” He hurled a punch at Gu.

You have successfully trolled Yi for +119… +119… +119…

Small beads of sweat formed on Zu An’s forehead. Yi must be bothered by it. I’m glad that his rage is still within a manageable level.

Yi’s fist manifested into a massive avatar that descended upon Gu. 

As he raised his hand to neutralize the attack, Gu unleashed a five-colored divine halo, but his body still shook under the blow. Yi’s punch must have been difficult for him to receive. 

“Brother Necropolis, you escape with them first. I’ll look for you afterward!” Yi said grimly. He knew better than to underestimate a celestial’s strength, and he was worried that Chang’e would be injured by the shockwaves of the battle.

“Take care, Brother Yi!” Zu An bowed to Yi before fleeing with the two women.

Gu sneered, “Qin, I’ll leave them to you.”

Qin snickered, “Yes, milord!” It unfurled its wings and chased after Zu An and the others. 

Yi wanted to stop Qin, but Gu quickly blocked him with his body. “I’m your opponent.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2530: Breakthrough
chapters.body:
Chapter 2530: Breakthrough

Yi eyed him coldly. “Your father is one of the Shaman races’ ancestors, so why do you lower yourself to be a celestial?”

Gu licked his lips as his face twisted in contempt. “What do you mean by lowering myself? With my noble bloodline, I don’t have to do anything myself for the Celestial Court to confer me as a celestial. All I hear are a loser’s words of envy.”

Then, he chuckled. “Ah, I heard you have done many things for the Shaman races in recent years, but the Celestial Court didn’t confer you any post. I guess the difference between us is a good father.”

Gu thumped his chest and ‘apologetically’ said, “Ah, I’m so sorry. I nearly forgot that you don’t have any parents. You’re an orphan!” His words were dripping with disdain.

Yi flew into a rage. “You’re courting death!”

He once again hurled a punch at Gu, but a thick snake tail coiled around his body and lifted him. 

Gu stared coldly at the struggling Yi and sneered, “Little shaman upstarts like you wouldn’t know how noble our bloodlines are. How dare you challenge me? Today, I shall show you death!” He began to tighten his grip around Yi, squeezing the latter’s body so hard that veins began popping out. 

Yi let out a howl, and his body swiftly expanded until he was a three-hundred-meter tall giant. 

Gu was forced to release him. “Sss!”

Feeling humiliated, Gu’s body began to expand too. Soon, his body grew even larger than the massive dragon Zu An had seen in the future, looking almost like a small mountain. 

Yi didn’t stop there either. His body continued to grow, soon crossing three thousand meters. 

Both sides glared at each other. It was hard to tell who made the first move, but both sides released a furious roar and charged into each other. 

The intense quake rattled Zu An and the others’ nerves. They turned around and saw a massive Yi brawling with a humongous snake. Wherever they passed, forests and mountains collapsed like tofu. 

The Mermaid Queen’s face turned pale. “These ancient deities are shockingly powerful.”

The monsters who had invaded the Cultivation World were already strong enough to drive them to despair, but compared to the deities in this world, even they looked like children at play.

“I wonder if Brother Yi can escape.” Seeing the snake slowly coiling itself around the giant’s body, Zu An couldn’t help but be worried. Yi had only gotten involved to help him, and it would weigh on his conscience if something happened to him as a result. 

“Rest assured, he’s strong. He withheld his strength for the earlier friendly duel,” Yumen Beiqing consoled as she looked at the choking giant with an unreadable face.

“Let’s hope so,” Zu An said.

A shrill laugh suddenly echoed from ahead. “You shouldn’t be worrying about others now.” A massive eagle-owl blocked their path.

Zu An released the hands of the two women and calmly looked at Qin. “Do you think we’re afraid of you?”

The only one he was worried about was Gu. Qin was formidable, but he had ways to deal with it. 

Zu An’s impassive eyes made Qin’s heart skip a beat. This fellow is too composed. Does he truly possess the means to defeat me?

He quickly shrugged off that ridiculous thought and said, “Empty words. I just received orders from milord. The only fate awaiting you is death, though milord can consider sparing those two beauties beside you.”

Sensing Qin’s lustful eyes, Yumen Beiqing’s expression turned cold. She threw out the Emperor’s Suppression Talisman, and a golden aura billowed in the sky. Two golden-armored warriors emerged from the clouds. 

Qin was taken aback. “You can summon celestial soldiers? How is that possible?!”

He frantically flapped his wings to flee, but it was too late. The golden-armored warriors stretched their hands down from the clouds, their movements clumsy but astonishingly fast. Before he knew it, they already had their grasp on his wings. Qin struggled with all his might, but he was unable to break free of the two golden-armored warriors.

Both the Mermaid Queen and Zu An were stunned. What’s going on? When did little sister Ling’er become this powerful? 

More importantly, the pressure emanated by those two golden-armored warriors was so great that even Zu An felt insignificant. It wasn't his first time seeing the Emperor’s Suppression Talisman, but he had never seen it exert such incredible prowess before. 

Yumen Beiqing noticed their shock and hurriedly feigned exhaustion. “Quick, I’m reaching my limit!”

The two golden-armored warriors began to dissipate, and it looked as if Qin would break free at any moment. 

The Mermaid Queen quickly played a melody on her harp to stack buffs on Zu An.

Without any hesitation, Zu An unleashed the Star Shattering Imprint with its tenfold power! At the same time, he unleashed a barrage of sword ki with his Tai’e Sword.

Qin was unfazed. He scoffed at Zu An, “A mere mortal hopes to hurt me?”

The three-colored divine halo enveloped Qin once more. As long as he had his divine halo, Zu An wouldn’t be able to hurt his main body no matter how formidable his sword ki was. 

Even so, Zu An had already expected that and activated the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra in advance. An invisible black hole appeared around Qin and devoured the three-colored divine halo.

“H-How is this possible?!” Qin finally panicked and screamed in shock.

But it was too late. The sword ki blasted through his celestial body with ease, slicing him in two. His two severed halves tried to reconnect, but the sword ki harnessed destructive energy that continuously tore through his body until it was reduced to dust. 

Zu An was stunned. He felt an immense surge of ki rushing toward him. 

His experience bar had been stuck at level 79 for the longest time, but he couldn’t make a breakthrough no matter how his ki grew. It was as if an invisible ceiling had been capping his growth. However, this rush of ki seemed to contain something that he couldn’t put his finger on, and it rattled the ceiling that had limited him thus far.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2531: Undergoing Tribulation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2531: Undergoing Tribulation

Clouds began covering the previously clear sky, turning it dark. Lightning crackled amid the clouds. An inexplicable air of heaviness and solemnity lingered in the surroundings. Countless birds and ferocious beasts in the nearby mountain forests fled in a fluster. Before this, no one had known that there were so many living beings residing in the vicinity. 

Even the Mermaid Queen felt difficulty breathing. Her face paled in shock. “What’s going on?”

Yumen Beiqing grimly replied, “He’s about to undergo a heavenly tribulation.”

“Heavenly tribulation?” The Mermaid Queen was taken aback.

“It is a trial mortals have to overcome to become immortals.” Yumen Beiqing looked at Zu An’s silhouette with a deep gaze. 

“I heard that most people die amid their heavenly tribulation. Will big brother Zu be fine?”  the Mermaid Queen asked.

She came from the Cultivation World of thousands of years ago, when its ki was much stronger. She had heard legends about heavenly tribulation. 

“It will be dangerous, but you don’t have to worry about him. He should be able to deal with it. Let’s leave this place first, lest we be implicated by his tribulation lightning,” Yumen Beiqing said as she grabbed the Mermaid Queen’s arm and flew away.

“Are you sure he can deal with it?” The Mermaid Queen was so worried that she wanted to find assurance from Yumen Beiqing. 

“Let’s watch and see.” As she watched Zu An’s silhouette, Yumen Beiqing’s face flickered under the flashes of lightning. 

She didn’t tell the Mermaid Queen the truth. Most people lost their lives while undergoing the heavenly tribulation, and that was considering how most challengers would have prepared pills, artifacts, and even had elders or daoist companions protecting them nearby. 

But Zu An had abruptly encountered heavenly tribulation without any preparations. That would spell certain doom for anyone else. 

With Zu An’s strength and his array of formidable methods, there was a good chance he could have overcome the heavenly tribulation had he encountered it in the real world. However, in this Celestial World, tribulation lightning would be at least two to three times stronger.  

By now, Zu An had also sensed the anomaly too. The ki in his body had built up to a breaking point, and it felt as if it would shatter the space around him at any moment. He looked up at the tribulation lightning. There was an inexplicable connection between it and his body, and he could sense that it would descend on him at any moment now. 

“A tribulation, huh…” He had previously read something similar in novels. He had also previously witnessed Zhao Han facing a tribulation, so it wasn’t hard for him to figure out his current plight. 

But why does my tribulation feel much more difficult than Zhao Han’s?

The sky was completely covered in dark clouds, making it impossible to see even the towering Kunlun Mountain anymore. 

Is it because of my strength, or just that this world is too powerful?

Zu An had no time to ponder that question, as the first bolt of tribulation lightning fell just then. A white streak that was thicker than a tree trunk fell from the clouds and instantly struck toward his head. 

Zu An was already prepared for it, however. He sent his fist forth to crush the tribulation lightning. He didn’t dare to use the Tai’e Sword because Mi Li was inside. Heavenly lightning was the nemesis of soul entities, so he was worried that it could hurt her. 

As soon as the lightning dissipated, Zu An felt traces of incredibly pure ki flowing into his body. This was the energy that had been contained within the lightning bolt.

It made him realize that even though tribulations were dangerous, they were also a fortuitous encounter. His clash against the tribulation lightning was also a chance for him to steal the lightning’s energy to temper his body. 

Before this, he had considered using a Faraday Cage to avoid the heavenly tribulation. That method could work, but it would cost him an invaluable fortuitous encounter. 

While he was deep in thought, another round of tribulation lightning descended. This time, nine bolts of brilliant lightning struck him consecutively. 

The Mermaid Queen was horrified. The lightning bolts were so bright that they instantaneously enveloped Zu An’s entire body.

In contrast, Yumen Beiqing was much calmer. She watched the spectacle from afar. 

A massive crater appeared in the region. Nearby trees were blasted into bits. Under the immense heat sparked by the tribulation lightning, even rocks melted into lava.

Even so, an upright figure stood at the center of the crater. 

Yumen Beiqing’s face reddened. I have to admit that he has a good figure… 

The nine consecutive lightning bolts had incinerated Zu An’s clothes into ashes, exposing his bare skin. 

The Mermaid Queen also blushed furiously, but she was more concerned about another matter. “How many waves of tribulation lightning are there?” She had no idea why she was posing this question to the Fiend races’ Saintess, but somehow, she subconsciously thought that the latter would know these details. 

“It’s hard to say. Some only undergo one wave, but some undergo nine waves or even more. Usually, the more waves of tribulation lightning one faces, the higher the limit is for their cultivation. Of course, more waves of tribulation lightning may fall on vile individuals who pique heaven’s scorn to decimate them too,” Yumen Beiqing answered smoothly, as if she already knew these things ahead of time.

The Mermaid Queen breathed a sigh of relief. “That’s good to hear. Big brother Zu has been doing good his entire life. Surely the world will not seek to destroy him.”

However, looking at his tattered clothes, she couldn’t help but worry. “His clothes are already destroyed even though it’s only the second wave. Things don’t look good.”

She was strong enough to be considered an expert in the Cultivation World too. She understood that Zu An’s clothes had only been destroyed because he was unable to fully neutralize the lightning. That meant he was already struggling a little on the second wave. 

Yumen Beiqing could tell as much too. She calmly said, “Don’t worry, he's still enduring the lightning with his body alone. He hasn’t used his weapon yet.”

The Mermaid Queen breathed a sigh of relief.

Just then, another wave of tribulation lightning manifested in the sky, forming crimson lightning spheres.

Yumen Beiqing frowned. 

“What’s wrong?” the Mermaid Queen asked nervously. 

“The Planetary Tribulation Lightning should be far stronger than the earlier Barrage Tribulation Lightning, but I have no idea why it’s crimson.” Yumen Beiqing was confused.

“Is red lightning the most dangerous one?” The Mermaid Queen was horrified. 

Yumen Beiqing shook her head. “It’s the opposite. Of the seven rainbow colors, red lightning is the weakest of all. It is a fearsome force to ordinary cultivators, but it is too weak for a cultivator of his caliber.”

The Mermaid Queen breathed a sigh of relief. “Isn’t that good news? It means that the odds of big brother Zu An surviving the heavenly tribulation are much higher.”

“It’s not necessarily a good thing,” Yumen Beiqing coldly pointed out. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2532: Death
chapters.body:
Chapter 2532: Death

“Ah? Why is that?” The Mermaid Queen was taken aback. 

“The strength of the tribulation lightning is linked to the challenger’s future cultivation limit. The weaker the tribulation lightning, the lower their limit will be,” Yumen Beiqing replied. 

Objectively speaking, the heavenly tribulation Zu An faced wasn't weak, but she still felt a little disappointed.

The Mermaid Queen smiled. “That’s nothing. I just need big brother Zu to be safe and sound.”

Yumen Beiqing glanced at her. This woman is a famous figure in that world, so why does it seem as if her mind turned to mush after meeting that fellow? What charm does he have, to leave so many women falling head over heels for him… 

A crimson lightning sphere descended toward Zu An. It floated down slowly, but no matter how Zu An dodged, the sphere followed his movements. 

He spent some time dodging the spheres to first calm the ki inside his body before hurling a series of punches at them. He was glad to have once possessed the Cultivation World’s World’s Law Beacon, which deepened his understanding of ki to a high level. 

This confrontation with the lightning spheres could seem reckless, but he was capable of using the smallest force to unravel the latter’s weakest spot. Perhaps he'd look disorganized in others’ eyes, but in truth, he had minimized his ki depletion.

After Zu An shattered the nine crimson lightning spheres, the Mermaid Queen was about to cheer when her smile froze in place. She noticed that the tribulation clouds were still refusing to scatter, and orange tribulation lightning was starting to manifest. 

Yumen Beiqing widened her eyes. “Ah, it’s the legendary Seven-Colored Divine Tribulation!”

“Is it dangerous?” The Mermaid Queen felt her heart clenching. 

Yumen Beiqing nodded. “It’s very dangerous. Only two or three people have been known to have cleared this tribulation since ancient times. Others who encountered this tribulation were all decimated.”

She'd initially thought the tribulation lightning was too weak, but that wasn’t the case at all. If anything, it was too strong!

“Are there no solutions?” At this moment, the Mermaid Queen couldn’t care less why Yumen Beiqing knew so much; her attention was devoted to overcoming the current crisis.

Yumen Beiqing didn’t answer, but her gaze looked grim. Even those who have prepared for the Seven-Colored Divine Tribulation are likely to die, let alone the unprepared Zu An. Everything I have prepared and worked for has been futile anyway, so this isn’t that bad…

The orange tribulation lightning in the sky merged to form a terrifying whip. It lashed straight toward Zu An, slicing through space wherever it passed. 

Yi was stunned to witness that. “The Seven-Colored Divine Tribulation? Brother Necropolis is indeed an extraordinary man!”

Angry rumbles echoed beneath him. Gu’s massive snake body was squirming under him, trying to get back up. 

“Stay still!” While observing the heavenly tribulation, Yi hurled another series of punches at Gu’s head. 

“How dare you humiliate me!” Gu’s eyes were spinning from the consecutive punches. Blood flowed from his mouth and nose. When had he, a celestial, suffered such grievance?

“Are you resorting to your mouth because your punches aren’t strong enough?” Yi sneered. 

“My father is the Torch Dragon! You’ll have hell to pay for if you dare touch me!” Gu roared. 

Given his lofty bloodline and his high standing as a celestial, he'd thought he could easily subdue an unaffiliated cultivator. Initially, everything went smoothly; he'd easily wrapped his body around his opponent’s neck.

Little had he expected his opponent to bite him, leaving a gaping hole in his steel-like body! 

How the hell did he train his teeth? Are his teeth his soulbound artifact?

Yi’s face darkened. “I look down on those who rely on nepotism!”

He continued smashing his fist into Gu until the latter’s head swelled like a pig head. He had grown up as an orphan and gone through a lot from a young age, which was why he scorned those who only relied on their parents. 

“I don’t believe that you would dare kill me!” Gu scoffed in anger despite being completely pinned down. 

His father was one of the strongest experts in the world, and his mother was of exalted standing too. Given that this bastard was also from the Shaman races, he didn't think that the other party would truly dare take his life. 

Yi’s gaze turned cold. “I shall purge you on behalf of our Shaman races!”

The suffering he'd gone through from a young age had nurtured a reckless, extreme personality. Faced with Gu’s provocation, he couldn’t withhold his killing intent anymore. He gathered immense destructive energy in his fist and hurled it at Gu’s head.

However, a beam of golden light suddenly shot down. Yi quickly changed the direction of the punch to strike the light. 

Boom!

A loud explosion ensued, and Yi was jolted a hundred meters back. He looked in a certain direction with a grim expression.

Golden lights suddenly lit up the sky, and a dashing man emerged. He had a sharp hook nose, thin lips, and long eyes that gave off an air of cold authority. 

Yumen Beiqing glanced at the golden lights in astonishment. “What is he doing here?”

The dashing man noticed the heavenly tribulation and remarked, “The Seven-Colored Divine Tribulation? To think someone in this world is capable of evoking such a heavenly tribulation!”

He was interested in taking a look, but he needed to settle the issue before him first. 

Yi looked at the dashing man with a frown. “Emperor Jun?”

The other party was of exalted status. Why would he suddenly appear here?

The dashing man shook his head at Yi. “What a pity.”

Despite knowing that the other party was stringing him along, Yi still asked, “What’s a pity?”

“You easily defeated Gu, a celestial. It was only a matter of time before you became a top-notch expert in the world. It’s a pity you won’t have the chance to do so,” the dashing man said with a sigh.

Yi narrowed his eyes in confusion. “You’re planning to kill me?”

Emperor Jun was already the regent Celestial Emperor, and Zhuanxu was soon to officially step down from his position. Given that there was no grudge between them, why would Emperor Jun want to take his life?

“You shouldn’t have come here.” Emperor Jun slowly raised his finger.

Yi was taken aback. There was no time for him to ponder the reason; at this moment, he felt unprecedented danger. Without hesitation, he drew the bow he'd never touched during his battle with Gu. He pulled his bowstring and unleashed a brilliant arrow reminiscent of a shooting star at Emperor Jun.

Emperor Jun also released a golden light beam from his hand that collided with Yi’s arrow. The beam tore through the arrow, and Yi watched in bewilderment as it shot into his forehead. 

At the same time, Emperor Jun looked down and saw the two halves of the arrow lodged in his body. Two droplets of golden blood dripped from his wounds, causing the earth beneath to blaze into flames. 

A divine light shone from his body, and the two arrow halves were reduced to ashes. 

His voice was filled with commendation. “What exquisite archery. If you had a good bow, you would have been able to put up a fight against me. A pity indeed.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2533: True Identity
chapters.body:
Chapter 2533: True Identity

Yi stared at Emperor Jun in disbelief. He opened his mouth as if to say something, but nothing came out before he collapsed on the ground. 

Meanwhile, Gu was finally able to get back up. He felt exuberant at Yi’s death. He spat a mouthful of blood and sneered, “Pui! You deserve to die.” He turned to the dashing man and said, “Thank you for your aid, Milord. This brute would have killed me if it weren’t for your timely arrival.”

The dashing man eyed him coldly. “You have disappointed me.”

Gu smiled sheepishly. He was embarrassed that he, a celestial, had been pinned onto the ground and helplessly beaten up by a shaman.

“You didn’t tell anyone else what happened, right?” the dashing man asked. 

“Rest assured, Milord. I wouldn’t talk about a matter of such great importance with others. Even my subordinate, Qin, doesn’t know about it.” Gu thumped his chest and gave his assurance. 

“That’s good.” The dashing man slowly nodded before raising his finger. 

Gu was horrified to see the other party’s expression and movements. “Milord, are you planning to silence me? Please be assured that I won’t share this matter with anyone!”

The dashing man had an utterly cold look on his face. “Only the dead tell no tales, but you don’t have to worry. I won’t kill you. You are still useful to me.”

His finger lightly tapped on Gu’s forehead. There was a flash of golden light, and the latter’s expression turned dull before he passed out. The dashing man then casually waved his hand, and a golden flame enveloped Yi’s corpse. Soon, his body was burned to ashes. 

“No one should know now. All that’s left is the situation over there,” the dashing man murmured as he turned his sights toward the heavenly tribulation.

… 

Seeing the imminent arrival of the orange lightning whip, Zu An quickly manifested the Tranquility Bell to shield himself. However, it only held for a second before shattering into pieces. 

Tsk, that’s why pirated things can't be trusted. I don’t think it's ever succeeded in stopping the enemy’s attack before. Oh well, at least it can greatly weaken my enemy’s attacks.

At the very least, the whip did dim significantly after tearing through the Tranquility Bell. 

Zu An channeled the White Lotus Flame into his Flame Blade before swinging it down on the whip, severing it into two. The massive lightning whip dissipated into sparks that flowed into Zu An’s body and tempered it.

The clouds in the sky bellowed furiously. Golden lights peeked through the clouds, dyeing the pitch-black sky yellow. 

Sensing the increasing pressure from the tribulation clouds, Zu An didn’t dare to let his guard down. He channeled the Primordial Origin Sutra to heal his wounds. Despite his not preparing pills beforehand, the Primordial Origin Sutra’s recovery prowess was more useful than most pills. 

The Mermaid Queen tried to rush over to Zu An’s side, only for Yumen Beiqing to stop her. “What are you doing?”

“I want to deliver some pills and offer him some buffs to help him tide through the heavenly tribulation,” the Mermaid Queen said anxiously.

“Don’t even think about going there. You will only put him in greater danger,” Yumen Beiqing warned. “If you step within a certain radius of it, the heavenly tribulation will grow even stronger. You will only be making things more difficult for him.”

The Mermaid Queen was horrified. “I’m glad that you’re here, Saintess. Otherwise, I would have made a grievous mistake.”

At the same time, she was curious to know why the Saintess was so familiar with heavenly tribulation. She was about to ask that when a golden light suddenly shone beside her. 

A figure stepped out from the light. He extended a single finger toward her, looking as if he was going to crush her like a mere ant. 

The Mermaid Queen tried to raise her hand and activate the Mermaid race's forbidden arts, only to realize that she couldn’t move. Time seemed to stop around her, causing even her thoughts to stall to a halt. 

Yumen Beiqing was startled. 

As she thrust her palm toward the figure, a blinding light shone around her too. The clash between the finger and the palm sent her flying a hundred meters back, whereas the golden figure merely swayed on the spot. 

“Hm?” The dashing man didn’t continue his attack. Instead, he looked at Yumen Beiqing with questioning eyes. “It’s you. What are you doing here?”

Yumen Beiqing’s face darkened. She glanced worriedly at Zu An and the Mermaid Queen. 

“Don’t worry. They can’t see what’s happening here,” the dashing man replied. 

Yumen Beiqing still refused to speak a word. She stared intently at the dashing man. 

The dashing man chuckled. “Why are you looking at me with such eyes? Is it because you haven’t seen me in a long while, my wife, Sun Goddess Xihe? What are you doing in this mortal body?”

Yumen Beiqing’s eyes turned cold. She didn’t display the slightest joy at this reunion. “Don’t disgust me by calling me that! What are you doing here?”

The dashing man smiled. “I sensed a phenomenon, and it was the legendary Seven-Colored Divine Tribulation at that, so I came here to have a look out of curiosity.”

“Do you think I’d believe that?” Yumen Beiqing sneered. “You, the Celestial Emperor, descending to the mortal world over such a triviality? I reckon you came to deal with Gu.”

The dashing man’s expression turned cold. “I don’t need to explain myself to you.”

Yumen Beiqing scoffed in contempt, “Of course. Who doesn’t know that the great Emperor Jun has lovers everywhere? There’s no one you are accountable to.”

“Are you jealous?” The dashing man smirked. “This human body doesn’t look too bad, and that mermaid over there is quite a charmer too. Did you find them especially for me?”

“Your narcissism is as repulsive as ever.” Yumen Beiqing’s eyes were frigid. “If you can fool around, I can do the same too. I can now tell you that this woman and I are that man’s lovers.”

“That man…” The dashing man looked at Zu An with a frown. “So he’s the one who invoked the legendary Seven-Colored Divine Tribulation. I see potential in him, but his strength is lacking…”

Just then, his smile suddenly stiffened. He turned his gaze back to Yumen Beiqing and huffed. “Xihe, I know you're displeased by my womanizing ways, and that you’re saying those words to irk me. You wouldn’t fancy an uncouth man like that. What are you scheming?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2534: What Should I Do Now?
chapters.body:
Chapter 2534: What Should I Do Now?

“I’m trying to irk you?” Yumen Beiqing laughed. “I shan’t question your affairs, so don’t bother questioning my affairs either.”

The dashing man frowned, but he chose not to argue with her. “Fine, I shan’t question you, but I’ll be killing that man. As for this woman…” Looking at the Mermaid Queen’s beautiful face, he murmured, “It would be a pity to kill her. I’ll bring her with me.”

Yumen Beiqing sneered, “You’re true to your character. You won’t be touching either of them, though. They are my people.”

The dashing man frowned. He eyed the man standing amid the tribulation clouds, and saw that the latter was more handsome than him and had a physique that would make women go crazy. He suddenly had an ominous feeling. “You couldn’t have truly fallen for that man, did you?”

His killing intent deepened.

“It has nothing to do with you. Don’t foil my plan.” Yumen Beiqing glared at him with cold eyes. 

The dashing man breathed a sigh of relief. She’s scheming something after all.

“Forget it. Since you insist, I shall spare them on your account," he said. He walked closer to Yumen Beiqing to better admire her beauty. “Your current body looks so beautiful. Shall we get intimate like that? Perhaps we can find the passion we used to have.”

Yumen Beiqing stepped back to avoid his touch. With eyes as sharp as daggers, she bellowed, “Scram!”

The dashing man’s face twisted in anger. “I am the next Celestial Emperor, which makes you the next Celestial Empress. Are you going to snub me?”

Yumen Beiqing continued staring at him with cold eyes, not saying a word.

The dashing man harrumphed coldly. “Forget it!”

Feeling displeased, he flung his sleeves and morphed into a golden light. Before leaving, he took one last glance at the Mermaid Queen. She’s gorgeous. Why have I never heard about her before? I should look into her and approach her once Xihe is gone… 

Following his departure, the Mermaid Queen’s perception of time reverted to normal. She anxiously scanned her surroundings while asking, “Where’s that golden man?”

Yumen Beiqing calmly replied, “What golden man? You must have been hallucinating because of the tribulation clouds.”

“Did I?” The Mermaid Queen rubbed her head in confusion. Was it a hallucination?

Yumen Beiqing wasn’t in the mood to deal with her. She fell into deep thought. Why was Emperor Jun here? Could it be that he’s involved in everything too? But that doesn’t make sense! Why would he… 

“Saintess, the tribulation lightning resembles the celestial warriors you summoned earlier.” The Mermaid Queen tugged at Yumen Beiqing, jolting her from her thoughts. 

Yumen Beiqing looked over and saw that the tribulation lightning had morphed into an army of celestial warriors that charged straight at Zu An.
