The Mermaid Queen was horrified. “There are so many of them! Do you have a way to control those celestial warriors?”

Yumen Beiqing looked at the golden-armored warriors in the heavenly tribulation with cold eyes. Even if I could control them, why would I do so?

Even Zu An was intimidated by the sight of so many massive warriors charging at him. 

“Don’t worry. The Tai’e Sword is a deity-grade weapon, after all. I won’t be affected by the heavenly lightning in here,” Mi Li suddenly said. 

“Master empress!” Zu An was delighted to hear her. 

“You’re at the heart of the heavenly lightning, so I’ll be going into hibernation soon. Be careful, especially for the next few waves of heavenly tribulation. Make sure to remain steadfast to your true nature. If need be, ask that Saintess for help,” Mi Li quickly said before falling silent. 

Zu An was confused. What does Mi Li mean by those words? Why did she tell me to ask little sister Ling’er for help? Her cultivation is beneath mine, and she knows less about the heavenly tribulation than me. How would she be able to help me?

But faced with the onslaught of the celestial warriors, he had no time to ponder these questions.

He brought out his Rune Weapon Chart. Golden ripples appeared behind him, and several weapons, including the Tai’e Sword and the Human Emperor Seal, drifted out from within. He sent those weapons hurtling toward the celestial warriors.

The celestial warriors brandished their blades to deflect the incoming weapons, but unfortunately, the Rune Weapon Chart could conjure unlimited weapons. By the time the celestial warriors deflected the first wave, the second wave had already arrived.

The weapons conjured by the Rune Weapon Chart included Gatling guns, bombs, attack helicopters, bombers, and fighter jets; things that no one here had ever seen before. 

Even Yumen Beiqing was stunned. What are these things that man has brought out?

In contrast, the Mermaid Queen was overjoyed. Her eyes gleamed with excitement. 

Zu An opened his hands and enjoyed the boundless ki around him. This world was even richer in ki than the time he entered this place in the future. Only such a colossal amount of ki could sustain his continuous generation of weapons.

Most cultivators feared going up against an army, let alone one of these celestial warriors' caliber. But Zu An’s Rune Weapon Chart could unleash unlimited attacks, making it the ideal weapon against armies.

The only downside to his Rune Weapon Chart was its terrifying ki consumption. Even with a colossal amount of ki to tap, he gradually approached his limit, resulting in a steadily decreasing number of conjured weapons. 

It was a relief that the previous bombardments had destroyed most of the celestial warriors, and the few survivors’ golden lights had become dim. 

So, Zu An decided to stow away his Rune Weapon Chart and instead charge at the celestial warriors with his Tai’e Sword. Sword ki rampaged amid the tribulation clouds. The remaining celestial warriors collapsed in the blink of an eye, turning into specks of golden light that lingered in the air; Zu An unleashed the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra to absorb the specks.

A formation that aided in the recovery of Zu An's life force and ki suddenly appeared beneath him. He glanced at the Mermaid Queen, who was playing her harp from afar, and acknowledged her with a smile before focusing on his recovery. Soon, his almost depleted ki became plentiful once more. 

Yumen Beiqing watched their interaction with impassive eyes. A frown slowly formed on her face. What should I do now?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2535: The Four Great Beauties Of History
chapters.body:
Chapter 2535: The Four Great Beauties Of History

By this point, most of Yumen Beiqing's plans had already failed, and she was only holding on because of her reluctance to give up. She knew that the best course of action right now was to kill this fellow. Even if she didn’t do that, it would be best to reenter this point in spacetime and do things anew in hopes that something could be different. 

And yet, she felt lost. She had planned everything out, and she'd been confident she could pull it off. However, she'd been a step late for everything. She couldn’t tell whether it was because she wasn’t doing well enough, or if everything had been decided by fate. 

No matter how she struggled, the world was still able to pull everything back to its original trajectory. 

She looked at the man amid the tribulation clouds with a flickering expression. I've made such a huge sacrifice. Am I supposed to give up just like that? Even if I have to give up, I should at least kill him to vent the humiliation I suffered. 

She slowly raised her hand, but right as she was about to make her move, she suddenly hesitated. No matter what, this is the one time that I came closest to success. Should I give it one more try? But the Nature Jade Plate only has one last use. Should I still use it on him… 

While she was hesitating, the Mermaid Queen exclaimed, “Hm? Why did big brother Zu suddenly turn green?”

Yumen Beiqing snapped out of her thoughts to look at Zu An. Indeed, his body had turned completely green. It started with just his thighs, but it quickly spread throughout his entire body. Even the light coming from his body became a little warped. It seemed to be the effect of the surrounding temperature.

“Big brother Zu is on fire!” The Mermaid Queen finally realized what was happening. She formed a hand seal and summoned a deluge of water onto Zu An. 

But not only did the water fail to put out the fire, it even made it blaze with greater fervor. 

“Why isn’t the fire going out?!” The flustered Mermaid Queen wanted to rush over to help.

Yumen Beiqing held her back. “Don’t go over. That’s not ordinary fire.”

“What do you mean?” The Mermaid Queen grabbed Yumen Beiqing’s hand so tightly that it hurt the latter a little.

But Yumen Beiqing didn’t blame her for it and even explained, “This should be another wave of the heavenly tribulation. That flame is neither heavenly flame nor mortal flame; it’s called the yin flame. It begins surging up from the leg’s Yongquan Acupoint up to the head. If he fails to overcome this ordeal, his organs will be burned to ashes, and his limbs will wither. If that happens, his years of cultivation will have been in vain.”

Yumen Beiqing was shocked as well. This was one of the Three Disasters that deities encountered once every five hundred years. Why would Zu An encounter that when he wasn’t even a deity yet?

Even deities had died to this tribulation before. A mortal encountering it was bound to die.

The Mermaid Queen was horrified. She anxiously asked, “Is there a way to deal with this yin flame?”

Yumen Beiqing looked at Zu An’s green figure and calmly replied, “That flame is fueled by desires. He can survive this ordeal if he holds to his heart. Otherwise, he will be reduced to ashes. Outsiders won’t be able to help him at all.” 

The Mermaid Queen breathed a sigh of relief. “That’s good to hear. Big brother Zu has a strong heart. He’ll weather through this ordeal.”

Yumen Beiqing sneered. This woman doesn’t know how terrifying the yin flame is. Even deities have fallen prey to it, let alone that lustful fellow!

In the time they had spent together, she had witnessed how great his desires were. It was unknown what surfaced in her head, but her cheeks reddened, and her eyes narrowed in rage. Hmph, this works fine. It’s heaven’s will if he dies here. I won’t have to make a move. 

Zu An was unaware of the situation outside. He thought groggily, What was I doing earlier? 

He noticed thin veils hanging around him as he sat on a massive, soft bed. A subtle sweet fragrance lingered in the air, mesmerizing him. 

While he was trying to make sense of the situation, he heard a melancholic pipa melody that sounded pleasing to the ears. 

He looked over and saw a gorgeous woman wearing a red cloak. She was dressed like a nomad, giving her an air of valiance. And yet, she also carried the coyness of a woman from the Central Plains. Her hands lightly stroked the pipa, producing beautiful melodies that seemed to tug directly at Zu An’s heartstrings. 

Moonlight shone through the window and bathed her in its cold radiance.

Zu An was suddenly reminded of a poem: In the Qin’s Han residence, the shadows illuminate Consort Ming.

He was amazed that such a ravishing woman could exist in the world. He was about to ask her what was going on when he suddenly heard a crisp, bell-like chuckle. 

It turned out that two women were dancing gracefully in the room. One of them wore a graceful and dignified dress from the Qin-Han era. The other wore a fluttery red dress that looked as vibrant as a blossoming flower. They held hands while dancing lithely. It looked as if they were competing to be the most beautiful flower in the room, yet their movements harmonized into an enchanting performance. Their faces were reddened with embarrassment, and amid their dance, they looked at Zu An with amorous eyes. It was as if they were doing all these things to pleasure him, to receive his affirmation and love. 

Zu An had to concede that he had been presumptuous. Only a moment earlier he'd wondered how there could be someone as beautiful as the red-cloaked woman playing the pipa, but two other equally gorgeous women appeared right after. 

“What’s going on?” Zu An wondered, feeling disoriented. His normally quick mind felt bogged down as if it was trapped in a swamp, preventing him from thinking normally. All he could do was obey his instincts and admire the beauties before him. 

“Hubby~” A seductive voice echoed from behind, accompanied by the sound of splashing water. 

It was only then that Zu An realized there was a huge spring in the room, and a gorgeous woman was bathing in it. Her arms rested against the border, and she looked at him with an inviting smile. Glistening water droplets flowed down her neck, drawing one’s eyes to her cleavage. 

Zu An gulped. To think that there are four beauties of such caliber here!

“Hubby, why are you still in a daze? Come here and bathe with me. Xi Shi, Diaochan, and Zhaojun, you must be exhausted too. Come and join us!” The beauty in the spring beckoned them over. 

It took tremendous willpower for Zu An to keep his nose from spurting blood. He was shocked by the names of the women. Aren’t they the Four Great Beauties[1] of history? The woman inside the spring must be Imperial Consort Yang.

Who am I? Where am I? What am I going to do?

He felt as if insufficient blood was flowing into his head, possibly because it was charging into his other head. 

Xi Shi chuckled. “That won’t do. He hasn’t chosen yet.” She looked at Zu An with amorous eyes. “Hubby, who do you want to serve you tonight?”

There was an inkling that something was off at the back of Zu An’s mind, but in his dreamlike state, he was unable to connect with his rationality. He instinctively laughed and said, “Only a child chooses. I want it all!”

1. The Four Great Beauties are four Chinese women who were renowned for their beauty. The four are usually identified as Xi Shi, Wang Zhaojun, Diaochan, and Yang Yuhuan. Diaochan is a fictional character, while the rest have been greatly embellished by legends. They gained their reputation from the influence they exercised over powerful men and, consequently, their impact on Chinese history. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2536: Slave And Master
chapters.body:
Chapter 2536: Slave And Master

Diaochan and Wang Zhaojun exchanged surprised looks before chuckling behind their hands. “Hubby, you shouldn’t be so greedy. Choose one of us. This is for your own good.”

“One is hardly enough. Who are you underestimating? Since it’s a dream, why should I have just one beauty?” Zu An thumped his chest as if to say that he could take it. 

This was a weird situation, but his rational mind was no longer functioning. Everything was going according to his true heart. He couldn’t explain the current situation, so he instinctively assumed that it was just a dream. As for why he was having such a dream and what he'd been doing before it, he had no idea at all, and he didn’t think of asking those questions either. 

At Zu An’s insistence, the four beauties exchanged looks. 

“Since hubby insists on it, we will serve you well.” 

The four women chuckled coyly. As they gathered around him, their dresses fluttered. 

All Zu An could smell were their mesmerizing fragrances. How he wished that he had more hands and feet! He finally understood why there were so many tentacle comics in his previous world. It turned out that they were fueled by the experience of realistic limitations.

Before he knew it, they were all soaking in the spring. The women's thin dresses floated on the water like vibrant flowers competing for attention. Hearing their coy laughter and feeling their smooth skin, Zu An thought that there was no paradise greater than this. 

The world scorns King Zhou, yet the world desires to be King Zhou[1]. 

Imperial Consort Yang suddenly chuckled seductively before diving into the water like a mermaid. Zu An was confused as to what she was planning to do when he suddenly gasped. 

He immediately understood what was going on as his restless body was comforted with tenderness. 

… 

Meanwhile, the Mermaid Queen nervously grabbed Yumen Beiqing’s arm as she asked, “Oh no, why is the green flame burning stronger and stronger?”

Looking at Zu An’s dogged expression, Yumen Beiqing’s face turned red. She spat, “He can only blame himself for his weak willpower. He became enslaved to his desires so easily. Not even the gods can save him.”

Her tone revealed her anger at his incompetence. He’s at least a hero. I thought he would be better than this, but how could he succumb so easily? I have misjudged him!

The Mermaid Queen found her tone peculiar. It doesn’t sound like she’s worried about big brother Zu’s life and death. However, she didn’t push the issue and instead asked, “Is there any way to save him?” 

“None at all. We’re just waiting for his death,” Yumen Beiqing coldly scoffed. “I’ve already said it. He only has himself to count on for this tribulation.”

The Mermaid Queen bit her lip. Unable to remain still, she took out her Deep Sea Harp and gently stroked it. A cleansing song manifested as tangible music notes that drifted toward Zu An. She had no idea whether this would be helpful or not, but she could only do whatever she could.

What’s going on? How humiliating it would be if I can’t even withstand something of this extent! Taking a deep breath, Zu An channeled his ki to calm his emotions.

But Diaochan and Wang Zhaojun took this chance to kiss his chest, while Xi Shi sealed his lips. He immediately stiffened up. Even steel tempered a hundred times would be reduced to jelly under such tenderness!

His face reddened. No, I can’t admit defeat at a time like this. It might be unfair, but now that I’m outnumbered here, I have no choice.

He channeled his Primordial Origin Sutra and unleashed his Golden Phoenix ability. 

Perhaps they were famed beauties in history, but they were still women. How could they withstand the combined prowess of the primordial ki and the Golden Phoenix’s skill? The tables were quickly turned.  

These women were too formidable; they were the cream of the crop. Ordinary men wouldn’t hesitate to toss aside their armor for any one of them. While Zu An was extraordinarily talented, he was at a disadvantage going up against four of them at once. The situation was too precarious. If he let the four women claim the initiative, it would only be a matter of time before he succumbed.

So, he found an opportunity and went on the offense. Not only was he talented, but he also had plenty of experience with his lovers. He usually held back out of concern for his lovers, but there was no reason for him to do so here. 

All of a sudden, the hunters had become the prey. 

… 

Looking at the four women lying weakly by the spring, showing expressions very similar to those from erotic doujinshis, Zu An suddenly felt a massive sense of triumph. He cried out, “Come, let’s go at it for another three hundred rounds!”

Seeing that Zu An was getting more and more excited, the four women turned pale and hurriedly fled. Zu An tried to give chase, but the four women were so fast that they quickly disappeared from his line of sight. 

“What’s going on?” Zu An stood naked on the spot, feeling a little confused by the situation. 

Just then, he heard a calming melody that gradually calmed his restless heart.

“Ah, I’m still undergoing my tribulation…” He finally remembered the plight he was in, which also explained the inexplicable situation he had just been through. 

Slowly opening his eyes, he first looked at the tribulation clouds above, before looking at the green flames slowly receding from his body. He was a little confused. So, did I succeed or not?

Countless planets suddenly appeared out of a void in the universe. They were interwoven together in a way that resembled the opening of a massive eye. Those who approached these planets would feel a surge of desire and depravity, and if they were lacking in willpower, they could succumb right away. Only a few experts with steadfast minds could keep themselves together. 

“Instead of being enslaved to your desires, you’re seeking to become the master of your desires? Interesting…”

The eye closed, and everything reverted to normal. But a commotion broke out amid the lifeforms dwelling in the region. They speculated fervently about why that being had suddenly opened its eyes. 

Meanwhile, Yumen Beiqing was extremely shocked that Zu An had surpassed the Fire Tribulation. She'd thought that lustful man would struggle the most here, and the outcome was beyond her expectation.

The tribulation cloud swirled once more, and a tinge of blue surfaced. A wind began to brew.

Yumen Beiqing’s face twisted in shock. She instinctively shouted to Zu An, “Be careful! The Wind Tribulation is up next! It’s not directional winds, seasonal winds, nor nature winds, but Crow Wind! It can blow into your body through your forehead and into your innards, dantian, acupoints, bones, and flesh, disintegrating everything along its path…”

Before she could finish her words, a terrifying wind had already entered Zu An’s body through his head. Despite his body being more resilient than a dragon’s, he couldn’t withstand it at all. His brawny body began to melt, turning into a withered skeleton in almost an instant. 

1. The story of King Zhou became a cautionary tale on what could befall a kingdom if its ruler gave into corruption and moral depravity ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2537: Aiding In Secret
chapters.body:
Chapter 2537: Aiding In Secret

“Ah?” The Mermaid Queen’s blood turned cold. Her breathing suddenly became difficult, and the world seemed to lose its color. 

However, she wasn’t so weak as to cry helplessly in a predicament. She quickly recovered and cast a healing blessing at Zu An. Unfortunately, her musical notes instantly popped like bubbles upon falling on Zu An; they were ineffective. 

“How can this be…” The Mermaid Queen’s heart sank. She wanted to rush to Zu An’s side, but Yumen Beiqing held her in place.

“You’ll only be struck to death if you head over right now,” Yumen Beiqing uttered coldly. 

“If big brother Zu is dead, nothing else matters. I just want to send him off.” For the first time, the Mermaid Queen’s usually euphonious voice sounded hoarse. 

“Who says he’s dead?” Yumen Beiqing huffed.

“He’s still alive?” Light returned to the Mermaid Queen’s dull eyes. 

“This is the Wind Tribulation of the Three Disasters that deities encounter once every five hundred years. The Fire Tribulation tests one’s heart, whereas the Wind Tribulation tests one’s willpower and cultivation. Those who survive this ordeal will undergo metamorphosis, whereas those who fail will be reduced to shreds.” Yumen Beiqing’s eyes remained fixed on the man in the middle of the dark clouds. She harbored a tinge of hope that he would pull it through. 

“What can we do to help him?” the Mermaid Queen asked anxiously.

Yumen Beiqing replied, “There’s nothing you can do to help him. This is a tribulation he needs to overcome by himself. The only thing you can do is to pray for his safety.”

The Mermaid Queen took a deep breath. She clasped her hands in front of her chest and earnestly prayed, “Esteemed Ocean Deity, please bless big brother Zu for this ordeal. I’m willing to earnestly pray to you every night under the emerald sea…”

Yumen Beiqing secretly snickered. This woman is a fool. She isn’t as close to that fellow as me, yet she’s willing to go this far for him. 

But as she laughed, she soon fell silent. Looking at the furious swirling wind, she couldn’t help but wonder if her cynicism was because she had never experienced true love before.

Emperor Jun had many women, but those women only loved his strength and high standing. None of them were like the Mermaid Queen. She was devoted to Zu An, and so were Xie Daoyun and Pei Mianman. Just what kind of sorcery had that lustful man cast on them?

Meanwhile, Zu An felt as if he was on the verge of death. A wind was blowing down from his forehead, and it scoured every single part of his body that it passed by. His body was supposed to be resilient enough to withstand any physical scouring, but this wind was intangible and attacked him from the inside, rendering his defenses ineffective. 

He tried to channel the Primordial Origin Sutra to heal his injuries, but the wind hindered the flow of his ki, making it impossible for him to circulate it normally. The experience gave him a glimpse into the suffering of those who were sentenced to death by a thousand cuts. For a moment there, he even considered giving up, knowing it could save him from the pain. 

However, there were too many people and matters that he couldn’t let go of, so he continued to hold on. Even so, under the excruciating pain, his mind was swiftly reaching its limit. 

It was then that he heard a woman’s voice saying, “This is both a trial and an opportunity to undergo metamorphosis. It only depends on whether you can pull it through or not.”

Zu An vaguely thought that the voice sounded familiar, but he couldn’t put his finger on it, and he didn’t have the spare energy to think about it either. He was like a man who had been wandering the desert for a long time, but then suddenly stumbled upon a clear spring and rushed to greedily slurp it up. 

In truth, most people weren’t afraid of suffering or exhaustion; what they feared the most was hopelessness. They didn’t want to persist in something that would bring them endless suffering and give them nothing in the end.

Those words gave Zu An a ray of hope. There was meaning to his persistence. The darkness was only temporary, and it would soon give way to light. Many of his hesitations disappeared. It’s just bearing the pain. You don’t know how much I've suffered since I transmigrated here and started with the worst possible hand! I am no stranger to pain!

Now that he had tossed everything aside to focus on the present, he began thinking of ways to alleviate or neutralize the pain. His Primordial Origin Sutra granted him formidable regenerative prowess, but the wind rampaging in his body prevented him from channeling it. The same went for his other skills too. 

Since it’s an intangible wind, I should use something intangible to counteract it. 

Why am I so fixated on circulation pathways? Take the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra for instance. I'vee previously succeeded in conjuring black holes around me. Black holes devour not just the tangible but also the intangible… 

As such thoughts surfaced in his mind, his body slowly turned intangible like a black hole. These winds were formidable, but they weren't fast enough to escape the grasp of a black hole. 

… 

“Hm?” Yumen Beiqing narrowed her eyes in shock.

She was just wondering why she had secretly offered him some advice when the situation began to change. Sensing the black hole forming around Zu An, she couldn’t help but bite her lips. Even she felt a tinge of fear toward him. She was the Sun Goddess, but the sun feared a black hole too. Her fear was only alleviated by the knowledge that the other party was Zu An. As she looked at him, her expression slowly became fervent. 

She hurriedly averted her gaze. Pui pui pui, I can’t be truly interested in him. It’s just that the black hole holds a tremendous attractive force toward everything in the world.

“Saintess, what’s happening to big brother Zu?” The Mermaid Queen also sensed the change in Zu An.

“Don’t worry, your big brother Zu will be fine very soon.” Yumen Beiqing harrumphed.

“Isn’t he your big brother Zu too?” the Mermaid Queen asked while heaving a sigh of relief. She was perplexed by the twist in their dynamics. Despite being younger than her, the Fiend races’ Saintess seemed to have somehow taken the role of the ‘big sister’ here. 

“My big brother Zu…” Yumen Beiqing was dazed for an instant before quickly calming down. 

At some point, Zu An noticed that the scraping pain had vanished, replaced by a gentle breeze. It nourished every cell in his body, and his blood and flesh regrew at a visible pace. His body looked as impeccable as the finest jade.

He felt as if he had become one with his surrounding ki; he could now tap into it freely. This sensation wasn't new to him, but he had previously only been able to achieve that level with the aid of the World Law Beacon. Yet now, he could achieve the same effect with just his body. His area of control over the surrounding ki was nowhere near the scale of an entire world, but he was no longer an ordinary mortal. 

Yumen Beiqing looked at Zu An with an unnatural expression. He has good skin. 

Upon seeing him safe and sound, the Mermaid Queen cried out in delight.

Zu An belatedly noticed his two spectators. His face reddened, and he quickly took out a shirt and clothed himself. But before he could say a word, the clouds in the sky suddenly turned indigo. 

He looked up and was shocked to see the tribulation clouds parting open. A giant indigo planet was falling from the sky toward him. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2538: A Breakthrough
chapters.body:
Chapter 2538: A Breakthrough

The indigo planet resembled his previous life’s Earth. It was deep blue and looked beautiful.

Had he seen it under any other circumstances, Zu An would have been impressed, but to see such a huge planet crashing down on him amid his heavenly tribulation caused immense fear that couldn’t be explained with words. 

He had heard the term ‘megalophobia’ in his previous life, but since he had already gotten used to seeing something huge whenever he went to relieve himself, he'd never thought that he could suffer from it. However, he now felt as if he was a megalophobia patient. 

How insignificant was an individual before an entire planet?

He didn’t have the time to think about why the planet was falling on him, or why the planet he was on wasn’t affected by this celestial body. Rather, he was more anxious to figure how he could overcome this ordeal. 

The Mermaid Queen stared at the massive indigo planet with a pale face. While she wasn’t the main target of it, when it came to something as huge as that, it didn’t make a difference. 

“Do others face such trials in their heavenly tribulation too?” she asked, stunned.

Yumen Beiqing stared at the falling planet in utter shock as well. “I’ve never heard of anyone undergoing this in their heavenly tribulation. This is bizarre.”

However, she quickly snapped out of it and said, “No, that’s not a real planet. It’s just an illusion conjured by the heavenly tribulation. It’s probably targeted at the challenger and not us.” She was the Sun Goddess, after all. She could tell whether the planet was real or not.

“But what about big brother Zu?” The Mermaid Queen had many questions on her mind. “Why are big brother Zu’s tribulations so much tougher than those of others? How is he supposed to survive this?”

Yumen Beiqing fell silent. I have already helped him once. Should I help him again? Why am I even here at all? It’d be for the best if he died here, but why am I not as happy as I thought?

Zu An stared grimly at the descending planet. With a wave of his hand, the red Sun Slaying Bow appeared in his grasp. He nocked an arrow onto the bow and drew the bowstring. It was as if he had turned into the bow himself, his bulging muscles pulsating with strength.

The Mermaid Queen squealed in glee. “Big brother Zu’s bow is formidable! I can sense a destructive energy building up on the tip of his arrow.”

There was no response from Yumen Beiqing, so she turned around and was shocked to see the latter with a livid expression. Yumen Beiqing was trembling, and the hands hidden in her sleeves were clenched into fists. 

The Mermaid Queen was startled. “Is big brother Zu in danger?”

“It would be best if that was the case!” Yumen Beiqing clenched her jaw. She had plotted all these events to change the future of Yi shooting down the suns. That was the very bow that killed her nine sons in history! How could she not be furious?

The days she'd spent with Zu An weren't enough for her to fall in love with him, but they had changed her impression of him. Yet the bow reminded her of the last time she'd seen him shooting down the suns. Back then, she'd sworn to kill him, but she'd almost forgotten about the vow she made back then.

The Mermaid Queen was stunned. She had no idea why Yumen Beiqing was reacting in such a manner. She seems to have a deep grudge against big brother Zu. Aren’t they lovers? 

She thought that it could be a misunderstanding, so she didn’t dwell on it. At the same time, the massive congregation of white light on Zu An’s side drew her attention. 

A white arrow was released, and it flew across the horizon like a shooting star. In the blink of an eye, it struck the indigo planet. 

Pah!

The planet instantly unraveled, shattering into starlight that scattered all over the sky. 

“He overcame the tribulation so easily?” The Mermaid Queen rubbed her eyes. She couldn’t believe that the other tribulations were so difficult, but the scariest one thus far was overcome with such ease. 

Yumen Beiqing sneered, “It’s all because he has a good bow. Otherwise, he wouldn’t have stood a chance in this trial.”

“Saintess, you don’t seem happy about his victory,” the Mermaid Queen said carefully.

Yumen Beiqing simply looked at the tribulation clouds coldly without answering. 

With the shattering of the indigo planet, the tribulation clouds billowed once more and slowly turned violet. 

“Why does his heavenly tribulation have so many phenomena?” Yumen Beiqing fell into deep thought. The Seven-Colored Divine Tribulation had happened before, but it was never to this ridiculous extent. 

Every tribulation Zu An had encountered thus far could easily be the most dangerous one for other people’s heavenly tribulation, yet he faced them one after another in succession.

All of a sudden, Yumen Beiqing froze up before sneering. I don’t care whether he lives or not! It would be best if he died here. 

The violet tribulation clouds slowly parted to reveal a brilliant universe in the sky. The Mermaid Queen and Yumen Beiqing had seen the starry sky before, but they had never seen one as beautiful as this. 

The sky suddenly tilted, and the stars began pouring down. However, each of these stars was actually a planet. It was utterly insane to have all these planets simultaneously crashing down on one person. 

The Mermaid Queen was flabbergasted. She didn’t even ask Yumen Beiqing about it, as she thought that it wouldn’t make a difference no matter who she asked. Even a single planet was already a terrifying force to behold, but here, Zu An was up against the countless planets of the universe. 

How could Zu An shoot down so many planets? Even a deity would be helpless before such a force, let alone a cultivator. 

Yumen Beiqing looked grim too. She had never heard of such a heavenly tribulation. Even Emperor Jun would have died for sure if he had encountered this heavenly tribulation back then, let alone her.

Meanwhile, Zu An was exceptionally calm. After overcoming six tribulations, his body and mind were now sharpened to the fullest. In the face of the falling planets, he emptied his mind, and his body slowly became ethereal before finally morphing into a black hole. 

He wasn’t planning to absorb all these planets, however. Even a real black hole would explode if it were to absorb all the planets in the universe! Rather, he was hoping to become a part of the universe as well as a black hole. 

Even though Yumen Beiqing wished for his death after seeing the Sun Slaying Bow, she couldn’t help but admire him for the solution he had come up with. 

If only this man weren’t an enemy… 

Zu An had already emptied his mind and become a part of the universe. Witnessing the birth and destruction of the universe and countless planets, he gained a profound understanding of the world. 

With that, he finally overcame his bottleneck!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2539: Seven-Colored Divine Halo
chapters.body:
Chapter 2539: Seven-Colored Divine Halo

The dangerous tribulation clouds morphed into colorful auspicious clouds, and the sky rained flower petals. These weren’t real petals, however, but immense aggregations of ki manifested in the shape of petals. 

Bathing in them, Zu An felt his cells cheering in exuberance. He had never been immersed in such rich ki before. It was unlike the usual surrounding ki. It was much purer and more soothing. Every breath he took reminded him of the sensation he'd felt when he first drank the Feather Mountain Spring Water. 

“Is this the legendary immortal ki?” Zu An wondered. 

His body was undergoing a massive change. It had already surpassed those of ordinary cultivators, perhaps even exceeding those of dragons, due to him previously tempering his body with the Primordial Origin Sutra. But now, it was still growing stronger. It wasn't just a simple stats enhancement, but a metamorphosis. His skin shone with a faint luster reminiscent of the moonlight, and he felt as if every part of his body was resonating with the surrounding ki without the slightest impediment.

It was as if he had become a part of the world. 

Zu An realized that he might have achieved the legendary ‘Indestructible Body’. Not only did he now boast incredible defensive prowess, his body could also regrow without limits as long as his soul remained intact.

He had no way of knowing for sure, but he didn’t think most deities had reached this level. At the very least, Er Fu, Wei Yueyan, and Gu hadn't. 

Just then, Zu An heard a divine symphony. A golden celestial palace appeared in midair. Its doors slowly opened, and a golden light shone down on him.

He thought that it was yet another tribulation and was about to put up his guard when he sensed no danger from the golden light. On the contrary, it felt comfortable to be basked in it. 

The distant Mermaid Queen asked Yumen Beiqing, “What’s that ray of golden light?”

Somehow, she trusted the judgment of this woman even though the latter was younger than her. 

“Those who achieve immortality manifest divine halos. The more colors or the purer their divine halos are, the stronger they will be, and the greater their potential as well,” Yumen Beiqing replied while keeping a close eye on Zu An. She was curious how many colors the latter’s divine halo would have.

The Mermaid Queen thought about how the celestial named Qin had a tricolored divine halo and nodded in partial understanding. 

Meanwhile, Zu An’s attention was on a phenomenon inside his body. He sensed a formation inside his mind slowly lighting up, forming a peacock imprint. He was overjoyed. He had cultivated the Phoenix Nirvana Sutra for many years, and he would usually unlock a divine bird ability with each major breakthrough. However, despite having made several breakthroughs, he hadn’t unlocked a new divine bird ability since the Kun Peng.

That had made him wonder if his skill was incomplete, and if it was impossible for him to reach the legendary ninth level. It was only now that he realized that the Phoenix Nirvana Sutra’s scaling was different from ordinary cultivator skills. 

It was rumored that it was possible to achieve immortality through the Phoenix Nirvana Sutra, but who could have thought that it would be true?

He sensed circles of different colors forming around him. He was curious to know what ability he would receive from the peacock. 

“Three, four, five… six, seven?!?!” Yumen Beiqing counted his divine halo colors in shock. 

They had spent enough time together for her to know that Zu An was anything but ordinary. There was no way he would manifest just one or two colors for his divine halo when he became a deity. However, she hadn’t expected his divine halo to manifest that many colors!

Isn’t the limit for divine halo five colors? What the heck is a seven-colored divine halo?

It was also at this time that the Keyboard System’s voice echoed in Zu An’s head. “Congratulations on unlocking the Peacock’s ability. The five-colored divine halo allows you to neutralize anything within the Five Elements.”

Zu An’s breathing quickened. Could this be Master Kong Xuan’s Five-Colored Divine Halo from Investiture of the Gods? He could sweep through anyone except for the saints! Thanks to that ability, fans deem him the strongest character beneath the saints!

With this ability, I will be undefeatable!

Just then, the Keyboard System continued, “During the awakening of your ability, you underwent the legendary Seven-Colored Divine Tribulation, causing your Five-Colored Divine Halo to mutate with this world’s origin dao and become a Seven-Colored Divine Halo. You will have to uncover its ability by yourself.”

Zu An was stunned.

Huh? What’s going on? Where’s my Five-Colored Divine Halo? Give me back my Five-Colored Divine Halo!

Even though the Seven-Colored Divine Halo sounded more formidable, given that it had two more colors, there was no way to say whether it followed that logic for sure. What if his ability had become even weaker after the mutation?

There was no way he could be happy when a guaranteed god-level skill pick suddenly turned into another round of gacha draw, where he could end up getting something trashy instead. 

However, there was nothing he could do. The only silver lining was that he had finally leveled up. He didn’t stop at level 80—the experience points he had accumulated all this time pushed his level all the way up to level 83! The experience points he needed for each level up had reached a terrifying amount, but fortunately, he had devoured many experts’ energy and had received an immense amount of ki from the heavenly tribulation.

“So this is what power feels like…” Zu An quivered in excitement as he felt the changes in his body. Not only was his ki many times stronger than before, even his grasp of energy had reached a completely new height. If he acquired the World Law Beacon once more, he would be able to exert far greater power than before.  

“Big brother Zu, you have reached immortality?” the Mermaid Queen exclaimed in delight. She flew over, looking like she wanted to leap into his embrace, but was hesitant to do so.

Beside her was a conflicted-looking Yumen Beiqing. 

“I think so.” Zu An looked at his hands. He still felt like he was in a dream.

“You smell really good now.” The Mermaid Queen’s face reddened. 

“An immortal’s body is pure and impeccable. The heavenly tribulation has cleansed all filth; that’s why I smell good now. Do you want to come closer to smell it better?” Zu An regretted those words right away. Under normal circumstances, he would have avoided saying such suggestive words to the Mermaid Queen, but his breakthrough had made him overexcited.

The Mermaid Queen was moved, but with Yumen Beiqing around, she didn’t dare to go too far. Instead, she quickly changed the topic and asked, “Is there any difference now that you have achieved immortality?”

“Other than my deep grasp of ki, the way I see this world is also different now," Zu An replied.

Somehow, the world now looked much more vibrant to him. He could now clearly see many things that he 'd overlooked before, such as the wind trails and the flow of ki. He had gained a deeper insight into the fundamentals of the world. 

The Mermaid Queen wanted to ask more questions, but Yumen Beiqing interjected, “How is your divine halo seven-colored? Does it have a special ability?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2540: Vengeance
chapters.body:
Chapter 2540: Vengeance

Zu An was perplexed too. “I also don’t know what’s going on…”

Yumen Beiqing quickly added, “You should try releasing it to see what it’s capable of. The divine halo is extremely important for those who have reached immortality. You can think of it as a special domain that ensures complete domination against those without it. Even among deities, those with a stronger divine halo can suppress those with a weaker one.”

Zu An took her advice and unleashed his divine halo. Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet—his body was completely covered by seven-colored lights, so much so that it was impossible to make out his appearance. 

Yumen Beiqing wanted to take a closer look when she and the Mermaid Queen suddenly disappeared in a flash.

Zu An frowned. He willed them to return. There was a flash of seven-colored light, and the two women returned to where they were. 

The Mermaid Queen’s face gleamed with excitement. “I think I entered a seven-colored space earlier. I felt groggy, and I couldn’t move at all. Big brother Zu, how did you do it?”

In contrast, Yumen Beiqing looked horrified. Even though her guard was down, the thought that she could be so easily transported into another space chilled her heart. 

“That could be the effect of my divine halo.” Zu An thought that this was similar to the effect of Master Kong Xuan’s Five-Colored Divine Halo in Investiture of the Gods[1]. So what’s the point of me having two more colors?

“You have a unique divine halo.” Yumen Beiqing seemed to have a lot on her mind.

“Little sister Ling’er, how do you know so much about deities?” Zu An was intrigued. Despite having become a deity himself, his understanding of deities was incomplete, yet the other party seemed to know far more than him. 

Yumen Beiqing replied with a strained smile, “I learned about all these things through the Fiend races’ records, though I didn’t know whether they were true then.”

“I see.” Zu An thought that the records had to have been lost in time. He had never heard the future Fiend Empress mentioning them. 

The Mermaid Queen widened her eyes in realization. No wonder the Saintess knows so much. 

Zu An suddenly recalled something and exclaimed, “Oh no, Brother Yi was fighting with Gu earlier. I wonder what the situation on his side is.”

Yumen Beiqing looked conflicted. Zu An grabbed the two ladies and flew over to where Yi had been previously. A rainbow formed in his wake. 

In the blink of an eye, they arrived where Yi and Gu had previously fought. 

Zu An was stunned. How am I so fast? This distance should be beyond the limits of my instantaneous movement. 

Yumen Beiqing was shocked. “Rainbow Morph!”

“What’s Rainbow Morph?” the Mermaid Queen asked in confusion. 

“As its name suggests, it allows one to surge across the sky like a rainbow. This is a movement technique that only top powerhouses can pull off. It is exceptionally useful for escape.” Yumen Beiqing paused for a moment to stare at Zu An before adding, “But your earlier execution looks rough. If you master it fully, practically no one will be able to catch you.”

Zu An’s eyes lit up in delight. “I like this skill. I guess I’ll have to practice it more in the future.”

He eyed their surroundings before exclaiming, “Hm? Why don’t I see Brother Yi? That big snake is gone too.”

His tone was initially relaxed, but his expression slowly turned grim. Marks of Yi and Gu’s fight could be seen all around, from the collapsed trees to the blood splattered all over the mountain. Judging from the scales mixed into the blood, Zu An could deduce that Yi had claimed the upper hand in the battle.

But why did the battle suddenly halt?

Zu An stopped before a giant pit. The nearby trees were bent, and the nearby stones had been reduced to rubble. Marks of struggle could be seen on the ground. Judging from these clues, he could form a picture of what had happened then. Yi had to have been riding on Gu’s body, beating him up, but something suddenly happened that caused Yi to stop.

He soon moved to another spot in the distance. Heat lingered in the surroundings, and the nearby stones and dirt on the ground had crystallized. There had to have been an intense blast of heat earlier. 

“Big brother Zu, did you notice something?” the Mermaid Queen walked up to Zu An and asked. 

“Brother Yi might be dead,” Zu An said grimly. As the lord of the underworld, he was extremely sensitive to death, and he could feel an aura of death looming. The vibrant fire ki lingering in the vicinity didn’t belong to Yi, so there had to have been a third party here. 

Yumen Beiqing’s eyes widened. It went without saying that she knew what had happened here, but she hadn’t expected Zu An to be so sharp as to figure out the situation despite Emperor Jun erasing his trail and destroying the corpse.

“Did you see anything earlier?” Zu An asked the two ladies. Earlier, he'd been focused on dealing with the heavenly tribulation, so he hadn’t had the energy to observe his surroundings. 

“There was a ray of golden light here earlier, then a terrifying aura descended. However, it quickly disappeared afterward.” The Mermaid Queen felt apologetic that she wasn’t of much use. “I was too focused on you, so I didn’t pay attention to this place.”

Yumen Beiqing noticed Zu An’s gaze falling on her and replied, “It’s the same for me as well. I was too focused on you. We were also too far away to keep an eye on Yi.”

Had it been any other circumstance, she wouldn’t have minded exposing Emperor Jun. Despite being a couple, the two of them were already estranged anyway. The problem was that she would risk exposing her true identity, and she was worried that Zu An’s wrath would implicate her sons along with Emperor Jun… 

All of a sudden, her face paled. Could that be the reason he shot the suns in history?

She tried to console herself. No no, that’s not right. Yi is already dead. History might have changed. 

Zu An stared at the crystallized ground. A golden light. Strong fire ki. Capable of overpowering Yi. Could it be… 

Seeing his contemplative look, Yumen Beiqing quickly interjected, “This matter should have nothing to do with us, and it might be good news too. The Mermaid Queen has taken the identity of the Chang clan’s Saintess, Chang’e, which means that she is betrothed to Yi. Now that things have unfolded the way they did, we don’t have to worry about that anymore.”

The Mermaid Queen frowned. “I don’t want to marry him, but I didn’t wish for his death either.”

“Indeed.” Zu An nodded. “Brother Yi intervened to save us. We're indebted to him. If not for him, I could have been the one to die here. I have to avenge him!”

“But how can we avenge him when we don’t even know who our enemy is?” the Mermaid Queen asked worriedly. 

Zu An fell into deep thought. Behind him, Yumen Beiqing narrowed her eyes as the fingers hidden beneath her sleeves gradually turned sharp. 

1. Kong Xuan had five different rays of light that would sweep his enemy into a void. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2541: Marriage
chapters.body:
Chapter 2541: Marriage

While pondering about this issue, Zu An told the Mermaid Queen, “I intend to first return to Wu Mountain to inform the Kaiming Six Shamans and the Goddess of Wu Mountain about Bao Jiang’s death. They're Bao Jiang’s friends, so I reckon they should have a way to redress his grievance. The Celestial Court will have to investigate this matter, and they should figure out who Yi’s murderer is in the process. If the Celestial Court refuses to punish Yi’s murderer, I’ll do it on their behalf.”

By now, Yumen Beiqing was ready to make a move. Looking at Zu An from behind, she could tell that he wasn’t on his guard. He wouldn’t have time to unleash his bizarre Seven-Colored Divine Halo. If she went all out, there was a chance that she could kill him in a single strike. 

But looking at him from the side, she was reminded of the time they had spent together, and that made her hesitate. The real Yi is dead. Do I have to kill him over a mere possibility? But why did I see him shoot the suns back then… Something must have gone wrong in history!

As she thought about her sons, her expression stiffened. She discreetly positioned her finger behind Zu An’s head, and she was about to make a move when footsteps suddenly became audible.

As Zu An turned around, Yumen Beiqing anxiously retracted her hand and pretended to turn around as well to look in the direction of the footsteps. 

A group of people resembling the barbarians from TV dramas emerged from the nearby forest. Their bodies were mostly exposed, revealing their bulging muscles. They were far more muscular than most humans. 

Unlike barbarians, however, their clothes were exquisite. Primitive tribes wouldn’t be capable of weaving such beautiful textiles. 

They first assessed the three of them before their gaze finally fell on the Mermaid Queen. “Saintess!” They quickly rushed over and danced in delight. “We have finally found you, Saintess!”

When he saw them rushing over, Zu An was initially wary of them, but their words surprised him. He instinctively turned to the Mermaid Queen. 

The Mermaid Queen looked back at him with an awkward smile as she softly explained, “They are from the Chang clan.”

Zu An nodded in realization. So they think that the Mermaid Queen is Chang’e.

“Chieftain, you’re mistaken! I am not Chang’e,” the Mermaid Queen explained.

The old man leading the group looked at the Mermaid Queen sternly. “Saintess, what nonsense are you spouting? How can so many of us mistake you for someone else? We took turns carrying you while you were younger. I know that you are reluctant to marry a fiancé you have never seen before, but this was a promise our clans made a long time ago. The Youqiong clan might have fallen from grace, but we can’t turn our backs on them!”

The Mermaid Queen was usually eloquent, but she had no idea how to explain this situation. Was she to tell them that she'd transmigrated here? 

Putting aside whether she could convince them of it or not, she also had to take into consideration that this world had deities and the Celestial Court. If they were to learn that it was possible to travel across spacetime, they could decide to capture the group for research. It wasn't a risk worth taking. 

Seeing her panic, Zu An took a step forward and said, “Elder, I’m afraid that there’s a little hiccup here. It might not be possible for your Saintess to marry Yi anymore.”

The old chieftain was stunned. “What do you mean?”

Zu An told him what had happened here together with his deductions. 

The old chieftain’s orange-skinned face showed many interesting expressions. “Bao Jiang and Gu… Should we be hearing these secrets?! How did Yi get roped into this matter? We’re done for… It’s all over!”

Zu An was confused. Why is he reacting so dramatically? Is Bao Jiang so powerful that it frightens even a chieftain like him?

All of a sudden, the old chieftain kneeled on the ground Yi had died on and pinched some of the crystallized stones. His expression slowly turned grim. “This is…”

“Chieftain!” The other Chang clansmen quickly stopped him from speaking further. 

The old chieftain raised his head and alternated his gaze between the Mermaid Queen and Zu An. He couldn’t help but notice how unnecessarily close they were standing to each other, and he narrowed his eyes. “Saintess, is he the reason you don’t want to marry your fiancé?”

“Ah?” The Mermaid Queen’s face reddened. There was nothing wrong with those words, but there was just something about the phrasing that felt off. 

Zu An helped her answer the question, “Elder, we're good friends. I won’t let her marry someone she doesn’t want to. Besides, that’s not the point here. Brother Yi has already…”

Before he could finish his sentence, the old chieftain raised his hand to stop him. “Young man, do you wish to marry Chang’e?”

“Chieftain!” The Mermaid Queen stomped her feet shyly. “It’s not like that between us!” She was worried that big brother Zu would misunderstand that she was exploiting this situation to coerce him. That’s not my intention at all!

The old chieftain harrumphed as he closely assessed Zu An. “You eloped for him. Shouldn’t he do something for you too?”

“But…” The Mermaid Queen wanted to explain the matter, but Zu An stopped her and gently smiled. 

“Of course I’m willing to marry her," he said, thinking this was a good opportunity to resolve the Mermaid Queen’s marriage issue once and for all.

A mini explosion broke out in the Mermaid Queen’s head, and she fell completely still. It was hard to tell whether it was out of excitement or shock. 

Yumen Beiqing looked at Zu An in scorn. This fellow is so lecherous.

As she looked at the man she loved with adoring eyes, the Mermaid Queen blushed furiously. Regardless of his intent, those words were more than enough for her. 

“Good, good!” The chieftain asked, “Which major clan are you from?”

“I’m not from any major clans.” Zu An figured it wouldn’t be easy to explain his identity in this era. 

“Do you have any masters?” the chieftain further probed.

“I’m afraid not.” Zu An figured he couldn’t tell the chieftain about Mi Li when he didn’t even know her true identity.

“That’s wonderful!” The chieftain clapped his hand in delight.

Zu An was speechless.

The chieftain loudly declared, “What a coincidence! On top of finding the Saintess, we have also found Yi! And it turns out they have feelings for each other! What joyous news!”

Everyone was speechless.

Someone from the Chang clan whispered doubtfully, “Chieftain, are you sure he’s Yi? They don’t look alike.”

Not many people had seen Yi when he came to ask for Chang’e’s hand in marriage, but there were still a number of people who had seen him. 

The chieftain nodded affirmatively. “Of course! I personally received him back then. There can be no mistake about this!”

Some remained doubtful, but they swallowed down their words of skepticism. The majority, who had never met the real Yi, quickly offered words of congratulations.

Zu An stared at the chieftain in absolute shock. “Chieftain, I’m not…”

Before he could finish speaking, the chieftain pulled him to the side and whispered, “This is the only way you can marry Chang’e. There’s an old prophecy in the Chang clan stating that the Saintess Chang’e can only marry Yi!”

Zu An was so shocked that he didn’t notice Yumen Beiqing’s trembling body. Her hands and feet had turned cold, and her eyes blazed with killing intent. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2542: Condition
chapters.body:
Chapter 2542: Condition

Yumen Beiqing had never thought that there would be such a twist in the situation. She had gone back in time to change some important events in history in hopes of changing the fate of the ten suns being shot down. And yet, she realized that no matter what she did, every major event along the way had still happened. 

She had already fallen into despair when she stumbled upon the legendary Yi, but then the latter actually died here. For a moment, she'd felt hope. Even when Zu An vowed to avenge Yi and she began feeling a tinge of unease, she'd still remained optimistic on the whole. 

Yet this Chang clan’s chieftain had just proposed for Zu An to become Yi, making her feel goosebumps. In truth, she had been confused as to why she had seen Zu An as Yi in history when he clearly wasn’t Yi, but now, the answer was finally apparent to her.

It turned out that she hadn’t changed anything at all. Remembering everything she had done along the way gave her a profound sense of helplessness. 

Zu An was flabbergasted too. “This won’t do…”

The old chieftain gestured for his clansmen to wait on the spot before leading Zu An and the others elsewhere. As soon as they were out of the sight of the Chang clansmen, the chieftain dropped to his knees in front of Zu An.

“What are you doing, chieftain?! Please, get up!” Zu An was bewildered.

The Mermaid Queen also rushed forward to pull the old chieftain to his feet. The latter had treated her well while she was in the Chang clan.

“I’ll kneel here forever if you refuse to accept my request!” The old chieftain looked at Zu An with a stubborn expression.

Zu An was silent. He didn’t like how the latter was threatening him here, so he waved his hand and supported the chieftain back up with gentle but firm force. 

The chieftain was shocked to realize that Zu An was so unfathomably powerful. He wasn't the Chang clan’s chieftain for no reason. If he refused to get up, most people wouldn’t be able to move him. “Do you find my actions laughable?” he asked. Seeing through Zu An’s thoughts, he sighed. “I didn’t want to do this either, but I guess I have no choice but to be honest with you now. It’s not that we old fogeys obstinately want Chang’e to marry someone she doesn’t love, but this matter concerns an old prophecy. Our Chang clan’s greatest priest once prophesied that our clan would one day encounter a crisis that might spell the end of our clan. Only the matrimony of a saintess named Chang’e and a Youqiong clan hero named Yi could resolve this disaster.”

Zu An frowned, replying, “That’s pure superstition. How can a clan’s rise and fall be tied to a single individual? And for the man and the woman to be so specifically named at that!”

“You can’t say that!” The chieftain anxiously waved his hand. “He’s our Chang clan’s greatest priest. His prophecies have always been accurate. We were initially skeptical too. How can someone from a long time ago know that we'd have a clanswoman named Chang’e, and the Youqiong clan would coincidentally have a clansman named Yi in the same generation?”

A hint of fear appeared on the chieftain’s face as he continued, “But fifteen years ago, a clanswoman of ours returned severely injured. Before she died, she gave birth to a child, and not knowing about the prophecy, she named her child Chang’e.”

He sighed. “Do you know how shocked we elders were when we first heard the name? We initially shrugged it off as a coincidence, though we sent someone to the Youqiong clan to check on things. Little did we expect there to truly be a young man named Yi on their side too. Furthermore, he carried a token similar to Chang’e’s. A Youqiong clan elder said that his father had betrothed him to another woman, and the other token was with his future fiancée.”

Noticing the old chieftain’s gaze, the Mermaid Queen reached for her waist token, which resembled the Deep Sea Harp. “This…”

The old chieftain scoured the crystallized grounds where Yi had died and found a token in it. This token was identical to the one the Mermaid Queen carried; the two complemented each other! The Mermaid Queen instinctively grabbed it and put them together. 

They were a perfect fit!

She suddenly fell into a daze. It was said that she'd had this token in her mouth when she was born, and it was later discovered to be a special artifact—it could transform into the Deep Sea Harp. Thanks to that, she was viewed as the unquestionable successor of the Mermaid Queen. 

When they called her their Saintess, she'd thought that the Chang clan had gotten the wrong person, but it appeared now that she had some ties with Chang’e after all. 

Zu An took the two tokens. Individually, they were Deep Sea Harps, but when put together, they formed a complete round moon. It reminded him of the legends surrounding Chang’e and the moon, and his eyes widened.

The chieftain continued, “When I heard about Yi’s death, I fell into despair. I couldn’t watch the Chang clan being destroyed like that, which is why I came up with this ploy.”  Noticing Zu An’s silence, he turned to the Mermaid Queen and asked, “Chang’e, has our clan treated you well over the years?”

“Yes, the clan has treated me well.” The Mermaid Queen pursed her lips. From her residence and environment, which had been arranged down to the fine details, she could tell that Chang’e had to have been pampered like a princess. 

“Since that’s the case, I humbly ask you to save our clan in view of that!” The chieftain began to kneel to her and kowtow. 

The horrified Mermaid Queen quickly supported him back up. She then walked over to Zu An’s side and whispered, “Big brother Zu, what do we do now?”

Zu An spent a moment in thought before replying, “Let me ask a few more questions.” He turned to the old chieftain and said, “It’s not that easy to impersonate someone. A few people in your Chang clan have already met Brother Yi, and his own clan can’t possibly mistake him for me.”

He could use his Myriad Transformations to assume Yi’s appearance, but he didn’t want to marry the Mermaid Queen in the form of another man. 

The old chieftain was overjoyed to hear Zu An’s tone softening. “Don’t worry. Not many in the Chang clan know his true identity, and I have my ways to curb them. As for the Youqiong clan, Yi’s remaining kin died while he was on his way to our Chang clan. He was the only one left in the Youqiong clan. There’s no one to expose you.

“But I heard that in order to become qualified for Chang’e. Yi has made a resounding name for himself over the years. There are many in the world who recognize him,” Zu An said. Considering how Yi had even triumphed over Celestial Gu, the chances were that his reputation had spread far and wide. 

The old chieftain replied, “I have been keeping an eye on Yi over the years. Most of his battles were just small squabbles; the more reputable powers don’t know him. Besides, your physique and disposition resemble his. With a bit of disguise, you should be able to fool most people.” Once again, he bowed to Zu An and said, “I implore you to save our clan in view of your ties with Chang’e and Yi.”

Zu An found those words weird. Does marrying Yi’s fiancée count as helping him?

He quickly expelled that thought from his mind. This is the Mermaid Queen, not Yi’s fiancée Chang’e.

Sensing Zu An’s hesitation, the old chieftain added, “Don’t you want to know who Yi’s murderer is? I found a clue about that.”

“Oh?” Zu An quickly asked about it, only to be met with the old chieftain’s enigmatic smile. Clearly, the latter would only talk if he accepted his conditions. In the end, he nodded and said, “I’ll accept your terms.”

The old chieftain was overjoyed. He quickly called his clansmen over. “Come, escort the Saintess and our son-in-law back!”

The Chang clansmen danced in joy, delivering their own earnest blessings. Many of their younger clansmen looked at Zu An with envy. All of them were fond of the beautiful Chang’e. 

Zu An took this opportunity to ask the old chieftain about the murderer. The old chieftain slyly blinked and said, “I’ll tell you after you get married.”

Zu An was speechless.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2543: I’M Willing
chapters.body:
Chapter 2543: I’M Willing

This chieftain is such a sly dog! As if I’d renege on my promise if he told me in advance. I can’t possibly torture the chieftain just because he’s unwilling to speak, right?

While Zu An was dumbstruck, the old chieftain had already called his clansmen over and tossed an artifact on the ground.

To Zu An’s bewilderment, the artifact was an orange, club-like object. No matter how I look at it… In’t this a carrot?

The carrot instantly grew many times until it became enormous. The Chang clansmen ushered Zu An and the Mermaid Queen onto it. 

Judging from the formation pulsating from the carrot, Zu An could tell that it was a flying artifact. He suddenly recalled Yumen Beiqing and beckoned her over. “Little sister Ling’er, come here.”

But Yumen Beiqing had an awful expression. She stared at him coldly.

Zu An thought to himself that no woman wouldn’t be upset to see her lover marrying another woman. While they were in another world, it wouldn’t be good for there to be a conflict between them now. If they lost each other here, he would regret it for life. 

So, he walked over and grabbed her hand. He was shocked by how cold it was. He quickly sent her a telepathic message, “Little sister Ling’er, this is just a temporary measure to resolve the current issue. Don’t pay it any mind.”

Yumen Beiqing scoffed, “Do you really have to be Yi?”

Zu An was taken aback. He'd thought that she was angry because he was marrying Chang’e, but it turned out that she was angry because he was becoming Yi?

He thought about it and figured that she could just be finding another excuse to grumble about this issue, so as to not directly offend the Mermaid Queen. After all, he wouldn’t be able to marry the Mermaid Queen if he wasn’t Yi.

“Brother Yi died while helping me. I have to get to the bottom of Brother Yi’s murder, so I have no choice but to accept the chieftain’s request first,” he explained. At the same time, he was intrigued by Yumen Beiqing’s temperament. It’s unusual for the gentle little sister Ling’er to react in such a way. 

Upon hearing his answer, Yumen Beiqing’s expression turned even colder, but she didn’t shake free of his grip and instead followed him onto the carrot. 

The enlarged carrot had plenty of space for dozens of people. It was unknown how the chieftain controlled it, but it rose into the air and shot forth like a rocket. The people on the carrot didn’t even feel the wind blowing against them; it was extremely stable.

Zu An was amazed by the artifact-forging skills of the seemingly primitive Chang clan. 

The old chieftain walked over and assessed Yumen Beiqing. “Our son-in-law, who is this lady?”

Quite a few youths also looked at Zu An with hostile gazes.

“She’s my little sister,” Zu An replied. A second later, he added, “As well as my lover.”

The youths initially sneered. What little sister? See how tightly you’re holding each other’s hands! There must be something more between the two of you.

Who could have thought that Zu An would admit their relationship outright? That further stoked their anger, and they complained, “Audacious! Why are you flirting with our Saintess when you already have other women?”

“Aren’t you being too greedy right now?!”

… 

Looking at the surge of Rage points in his system, Zu An also felt distressed. It showed just how well-respected Chang’e was as the Chang clan’s Saintess. 

The chieftain quickly raised his hand to stop the crowd. “Son-in-law, you are betrothed to our Saintess. I’m afraid you can’t get together with other women…”

Before the chieftain could finish speaking Zu An interjected, “Little sister Ling’er and I have mutual feelings for each other. I can’t do anything as heartless as abandoning her!”

His words further angered the Chang clan’s youths. Many roared for the engagement to be annulled, and in the blink of an eye, Zu An received over 100,000 Rage points. 

Yumen Beiqing looked at the man standing in front of her calmly. Had it been in the past, she would probably be moved by his attitude, but now…

The Mermaid Queen stepped forward to stop the crowd. “Don’t put him in a tough spot! He knew big sister Ling’er first…”

She directed an apologetic glance at Yumen Beiqing. Even though she was very fond of big brother Zu, she knew she was the third wheel here. Ever since she first found out, she'd thought that there was no chance between her and big brother Zu anymore. She would already be very satisfied even if this was just a marriage in name only. She didn’t want to harm anyone else, and she didn’t want big brother Zu to misunderstand her too. 

Seeing their beautiful Saintess protect Zu An made the Chang clan’s youths go mad with envy. Once again, they contributed another huge wave of Rage points. 

The chieftain stepped forward to resolve the situation. “Hahaha, our son-in-law is indeed a sentimental person. I can only marry Chang’e off to someone as reliable as you!”

The other clansmen were flabbergasted. “But he already has another woman! How can he marry our Saintess on top of that?”

“It’s normal for a man to have multiple wives.” The chieftain was unfazed. “Don’t I have multiple wives too?”

The other clansmen couldn’t refute that, but some indignant ones argued, “Even so, our Saintess has to be the main wife! The other woman can only be the concubine!” They even directed a taunting look at Yumen Beiqing right after.

Yumen Beiqing sneered. Her killing intent started to flare.

The chieftain stepped forward to mediate. “What do you mean by main wife and concubine? It’s only natural to treat all wives the same!”

Seeing that some of the clansmen still wanted to stir up trouble, the old chieftain angrily hollered, “That’s a matter between them! They don’t need outsiders like you to worry about their affairs. Stop running your mouth here and focus on the upcoming wedding!”

The Chang clansmen were dumbfounded. 

Zu An understood where the chieftain was coming from. His impersonation of Yi was a desperate attempt to save the Chang clan from doom. If this marriage was ruined, the chieftain wouldn’t be able to face his ancestors. 

The ‘carrot’ flew extremely fast and soon arrived at the Chang clan manor.

Zu An looked at the Chang manor and was shocked by the fanfare beneath. There were red lanterns and congratulatory red papers plastered everywhere. He asked, “Has there been a joyous event in your clan recently?”

“What are you saying? It’s your wedding with Chang’e! Today is an auspicious day. There is no better time for you two to get married!” the chieftain said as he gleefully stroked his goatee.

Zu An was dumbfounded.

Yumen Beiqing was dumbstruck too. Why are they in such a hurry?

The Mermaid Queen’s face reddened. She secretly glanced at Zu An with a mix of embarrassment and nervousness. 

Many youths who had a crush on her felt their hearts shattering into pieces. 

The ‘carrot’ landed in the Chang manor. A large group of Chang clansmen rushed up to Zu An and the Mermaid Queen to escort them to private rooms and doll them up. Before they knew it, they were already wearing wedding outfits.

A pleasant harp melody drifted to Zu An’s room before turning into the Mermaid Queen’s voice. “Big brother Zu, please don’t feel burdened. We’re doing this to uncover the culprit. This isn’t a true marriage.”

The Mermaid Queen would have preferred to personally explain things to Zu An, but due to her history of escaping from the marriage, the Chang clan was more uptight than usual. Nearly all the Chang clan’s ladies were gathered in her room to doll her up; there was no chance for her to slip out at all. She could only play the harp to relay her words. 

She was happy enough to have this wedding, even if it was all fake…

Zu An was initially hesitant, but after hearing the melancholic tinge and overwhelming feelings contained in the harp melody, he made up his mind. “Why should it be a mere show? If we’re going to marry, let’s make it the real thing. Or are you unwilling to marry me?”

“Ah?” Happiness came so fast that the Mermaid Queen’s mind was hurled into a tailspin. She wasn't a young, demure girl anymore, and she knew that she had to grasp the perhaps fleeting happiness presented to her. Thus, she replied, “I’m willing!”

They had kept a distance from each other because they were from different periods in spacetime and didn’t have a future together. But since they were both strangers to this world, she figured that they might as well have a passionate romance in this foreign world. 

While their feelings were ripening, Yumen Beiqing stood on the rooftop, looking at the joyous atmosphere that had suffused the Chang clan. Her face darkened. 

These damned people deserve to die!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2544: Something Happened
chapters.body:
Chapter 2544: Something Happened

Zu An was dressed up by the people around him like a puppet. He had never thought that marriage could be this complicated. In particular, the bridegroom's robe was astoundingly complicated. All kinds of gold accessories were put on him. If not for him being stronger than ordinary humans, he would have been crushed under their sheer weight. 

He had heard from the Chang clansmen that the more accessories the bridegroom wore, the more earnest he was in marrying the bride. Since this symbolized the Chang clan’s sincerity, he respected their tradition and went along with whatever they wanted. 

Soon, it was evening. He was dragged out for the marriage rites.

Much to his surprise, the marriage rites weren't conducted inside a room, but rather at a square. Chang clansmen, from the old to the young, were gathered in the square, each dressed in celebratory clothes. The women had beaming smiles as they eyed Zu An with curiosity.

In contrast, the men looked depressed. They glared at Zu An with envious and hateful eyes. 

Looking at the Rage points surging into his system, Zu An was awed by the Mermaid Queen, or rather Chang’e’s, popularity once more. Do they like her that much? We’re getting married. Shouldn’t they let it go? 

A few of them tried to screw with him while dressing up, but Zu An was no longer the type of person to lose his temper at a couple of youths. 

Just then, he heard exclamations. A commotion broke out among the youths as they turned to look in the same direction.

The crowd opened up, and a beautiful figure slowly emerged under the support of the Chang clan’s ladies. Her figure looked like a sculpted masterpiece. Not even the majestic bridal clothes could conceal her beautiful curves. Her waist lightly swayed as she walked, bringing a spring breeze to the hearts of the youths and elders present. The crowd was mesmerized. 

In the Chang clan’s traditions, the bride wore a tiara with bead curtains obscuring her face instead of a red veil. This made her look even more alluring. Her fair skin, contrasted against her red bridal gown, looked so dewy that it could leak water. She shyly eyed Zu An with eyes overflowing with bliss. 

When Zu An met her gaze, he felt as if flowers had blossomed all over the world. Even the moon wanes in shame when compared against such beauty. Idioms to describe beauty surfaced in his head, but he lamented his lacking literary ability to relay just how enchanted he was. 

Before they knew it, they were already standing before each other. The thought of being a couple with her loved one filled the Mermaid Queen with so much bliss that she didn’t think there was a need for her to return to her previous world anymore. This is fine too.

Zu An looked at the blush on her fair skin, which looked as if someone had applied a layer of rouge on smooth jade. What beauty.

The old chieftain hosted the ceremony. Marriage ceremonies in this era were much simpler. There was no need to bow to the heaven and the earth; the only one they had to pay respect to was Lady Houtu. 

It could be her overthinking it, but when she was paying respect to the latter, the Mermaid Queen seemed to see a smile on Lady Houtu’s face. 

Zu An couldn’t help but think that Lady Houtu’s standing in the Shaman races was much higher than that of the other Ancestral Shamans.

Next, they paid respect to their respective clans’ totems. 

The Chang clan’s totem was a full moon. Zu An thought that explained the pictures of full moons throughout the Chang clan. He was reminded of the legend regarding Chang’e and the moon and frowned. I wonder if Chang’e will elope to the moon in this world.

The Youqiong clan’s totem was a giant bow. Known for their archery prowess, they naturally worshiped the bow and arrow. That wasn't too surprising. 

Zu An thought the next step was for the couple to bow to each other, but that wasn't part of the rites. Instead, the two of them were told to link their hands together and dance for the crowd. 

Being asked to dance before a crowd all of a sudden was extremely awkward, but fortunately, the Mermaid Queen was highly skilled at dancing. She naturally guided him to the campfire at the center of the square and began dancing.  Cheers erupted from the crowd.

Feeling the Mermaid Queen’s tender eyes and the vibrant atmosphere, Zu An gradually immersed himself in the dance and matched her movements impeccably.

That drove the atmosphere to a new high. Young men began approaching the ladies they were interested in to ask them for a dance. Joy radiated from the entire Chang clan.

After the dance, the Mermaid Queen was escorted into the consummation room. Zu An wanted to follow her there, as there were too many things he wanted to say to her. 

However, the Chang clan’s youths didn’t let him off the hook and swarmed him with toasts instead.

Zu An was at a loss for words. It was one thing for the men to hinder him out of jealousy, but why were the women joining the commotion too? Some of the bolder ones even used intoxication as an excuse to throw themselves at him and touch him. He would have exploded in rage had he not known that the Chang clan meant no malice. 

One bowl after another, he drank a lot of wine. The Chang clan’s wine was much stronger than anything he had ever drunk before. He secretly used his ki to expel the alcohol from his system, but even so, he still remained mildly intoxicated. These wines were made for cultivators; if not for him having undergone the heavenly tribulation, he would have been knocked out here. 

One after another, the Chang clan’s youths collapsed after having drunk too much.

Zu An was finally free. He first looked for the mildly intoxicated chieftain to ask about Yi’s murderer, but the latter replied with a chuckle, “Today is a joyous occasion. Let’s not talk about inauspicious things. Go to the consummation room. I’ll talk to you tomorrow.”

The chieftain then continued chatting up a group of older women with thick makeup, making them burst into laughter. 

Black streaks appeared on Zu An’s face. This old fellow is so wild… Looks like he’s still worried about me turning my back on the Chang clan after learning about the culprit. He wants to make sure that the deal is sealed first. Knowing that the Mermaid Queen was waiting for him in the consummation room, his mind heated up. He decided to stop holding back and make his way there. 

When he opened the door, the Mermaid Queen was sitting upright on the bed. His staggering movements prompted her to quickly rush over to support him. “Why did you drink so much?”

“Those men went all out to make me drunk because they don’t want me to share the night with their dream lover. Too bad they underestimated me,” Zu An said gleefully. But as he leaned on the Mermaid Queen’s soft body, he suddenly felt drunk once more.

“Suyin, you’re so beautiful,” he said as he gently pried open her bead curtains.

The Mermaid Queen averted her gaze out of shyness. “Are you still going to call me Suyin now…”

Zu An understood what she was getting at. “My wife!”

“My husband~” The Mermaid Queen’s voice was coy and sugary sweet.
