It numbed Zu An’s mind. Unable to take it anymore, he leaned in for a kiss.

As she lay weakly in his arms, the Mermaid Queen moaned. 

All their previous hesitations about them being from different worlds and not having a future together were tossed to the back of their minds. Right now, they just wanted to vent the passion in their hearts and become one. 

After kissing for a long time, Zu An’s hands began scaling the snowy mountain to explore the scenery that countless experts only dreamed about. As his hands slid down to her belt, however, he suddenly thought of something and stopped. It looked as if he was going to retract his hand.

The Mermaid Queen grabbed his hand and quietly looked at him with a reddened face.

She didn’t have to say a word; her gaze said it all. Zu An cast aside his final sliver of hesitation and lightly tugged on the belt, causing her red bridal gown to unravel like a blossoming rose, revealing the most beautiful sight in the world. 

Zu An’s breathing quickened. The two of them naturally embraced… 

A dull harrumph suddenly echoed. Zu An immediately straightened up and vigilantly looked outside the window.

“What’s wrong?” The Mermaid Queen shyly tugged his arm, not knowing why he had suddenly stopped at this juncture. For a moment there, she even doubted her own charm. How could he stop now? Am I that unattractive? Do I have to take the initiative each time? But it’s so embarrassing!

She consoled herself by thinking about how intimate they already were. She could already feel the manifestation of his lust. It can’t be because of my lack of charm. Could he be concerned about something else? 

Ah, now that I think about it, I haven’t seen the Fiend races’ Saintess. Is it because of her… 

While she was feeling disheartened, Zu An took a deep breath and said, “Something happened!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2545: Misunderstanding
chapters.body:
Chapter 2545: Misunderstanding

“What happened?” The Mermaid Queen’s mind turned blank. 

Zu An quickly draped her bridal gown over her body. “Quick, put on your clothes. Something seems to have happened to the Chang clan.”

The situation outside unnerved him, but he didn’t dare to leave the Mermaid Queen alone in here, fearing that something could happen to her.  His grim expression relayed the severity of the situation to the Mermaid Queen. Her lust faded, and she quickly put on her clothes. Only then did Zu An lead her out of the room.

The Mermaid Queen took a sniff, and her expression warped in shock. “Blood stench!”

The Mermaid race revered nature, so they were exceptionally sensitive toward such smells. 

Zu An didn’t say a word. He grabbed her hand and led her outside to further investigate the situation. Several ladies were sprawled in the courtyard; these were the people who had escorted the Mermaid Queen into the consummation room earlier. They were beautiful, but all of them had stopped breathing. 

“They're dead?!” The Mermaid Queen was appalled. It was not long ago that these people were cheerfully dolling her up, and some even teased her about Zu An. It was shocking to see them as corpses just hours later. Tears brimmed in her eyes. 

Zu An nodded grimly. He continued heading out to investigate the situation. Most of the Chang clansmen had been gathered in the square. It was still bustling there when he slipped off to the consummation room, and youths had still been dancing merrily together. 

But by the time he arrived there, the whole place had fallen silent. All that could be heard was the crackling of burning firewood. The Chang clansmen were sprawled out on chairs, tables, and the ground. There was a strong alcohol smell in the air. Were it not for what they'd seen earlier, perhaps they'd have just shrugged it off as the Chang clan being drunk. But now, this silence just felt eerie.

Zu An inspected some of their bodies; they had no pulse.

Soon, he found the old chieftain. He was lying amid a couple of older women, and they were embracing one another comfortably. But there wasn't the slightest smile on his face. His face was twisted in fear as if he had seen something utterly horrifying.

The Mermaid Queen squatted down to check their pulse. Soon, her face twisted in shock. “Why are they all dead? What happened?”

“I don’t know either.” Zu An’s face clouded over. He had already spread his divine sense, and there didn’t seem to be a single survivor in the Chang clan. 

Even though he'd been distracted by the consummation, given his current cultivation, it should have been impossible for someone to kill all the Chang clansmen without alerting him… unless they all died at the same time, leaving him no time to react. 

But who could possibly do that?

His expression suddenly warped in shock. “This is bad!”

He began scouring the Chang clan as he got increasingly anxious.

“What’s wrong, hubby?” The Mermaid Queen followed him. 

“Little sister Ling’er is missing!” Zu An’s heart sank. Given that the Chang clansmen were all dead, it was unlikely that Yumen Beiqing was still alive. He didn’t dare to entertain that thought, however. 

The Mermaid Queen was stunned as well. If something happened to the Fiend races’ Saintess here, she would be a sinner. However, she didn’t lose her bearing and quickly took charge of the situation. “It’s also good news in a way that we haven’t found the Saintess’. Let’s continue searching together.”

Zu An understood what she was saying. As long as they hadn’t found Yumen Beiqing’s corpse, there was hope. He continued searching the premises while expanding his divine sense.

All of a sudden, he grabbed the Mermaid Queen and flew eastward. They soon arrived at the entrance of town, where they saw Yumen Beiqing slowly returning from outside. 

Relieved, Zu An rushed forward to embrace her. “Where did you go earlier?”

Yumen Beiqing didn’t expect such a forceful embrace. She instinctively tried to push him aside, but it was to no avail. In the end, she coldly replied, “I don’t need to report my whereabouts to you.”

“Of course you don’t have to. I’m just glad that you’re safe and sound.” The feeling of having found someone he thought he had lost filled Zu An with relief. He had been seriously worried about the worst-case scenario earlier.

“Saintess, something huge happened on our side. The Chang clansmen are all dead,” the Mermaid Queen helped to explain. She wasn't angry to see the two of them hugging each other; she was only glad to see Yumen Beiqing safe. 

“Oh?” Yumen Beiqing’s response was surprisingly calm. 

The Mermaid Queen frowned. She was extremely sensitive to emotions, so she keenly noticed how cold the other party’s response was. Even though she didn’t consider herself a true member of the Chang clan, the care and concern they had showered her with over the days had left her with some feelings for them. It hurt her to see something so tragic happening to them. 

However, the Chang clan had also warmly welcomed the Fiend races’ Saintess. The latter’s attitude was overly cold in comparison. 

“What were you doing outside?” Zu An asked.

“Was I to cheer you on while the two of you were having fun consummating your marriage?” Yumen Beiqing coldly retorted.

The Mermaid Queen felt awkward. Zu An was also rendered speechless. Little sister Ling’er feels different from before. 

While silence loomed among the trio, powerful auras suddenly surged from afar. Several towering figures rushed across the ground outside, heading in their direction. 

A deafening voice rumbled, “Why don’t we compete in terms of leg strength?”

“Sure!”

The two tallest figures suddenly accelerated. Despite only taking a few strides, they instantly arrived at the town’s entrance. 

The man carrying a giant axe and shield was slightly slower, and he muttered in frustration, “Kuafu, your speed is truly incredible.”

The other man held a green snake in his right hand and a yellow snake in his left. He burst into laughter. “Xingtian, that’s only because your axe and shield weigh you down. That gave me an advantage.”

The man named Xingtian brandished his axe and shield, causing a powerful wind to blow. “A loss is a loss. There’s no need for so many excuses! These weapons have long become a part of me; they can’t possibly slow me down!”

Zu An was stunned. Both Xingtian and Kuafu were renowned mythological figures in his previous life. Could these truly be them?

It was then that the two men noticed Zu An’s group. They were initially mesmerized by the Mermaid Queen and Yumen Beiqing’s beauty, but they quickly recovered and asked, “This is the Chang manor, right? Where’s your chieftain? Call him out to meet us.”

Zu An frowned. “I’m afraid he can’t meet you now. He…”

Before he could finish his sentence, the two men frowned.

“I smell an overwhelming stench of death.”

“There’s no one alive in there.”

The two of them widened their strides and instantly arrived at the square. They gasped upon seeing the corpses lying all around. They exchanged looks before returning to Zu An and the others. With hostile eyes, they asked, “Were you the ones who massacred the Chang clan?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2546: Suspect
chapters.body:
Chapter 2546: Suspect

“Of course not!” Zu An was rendered speechless. “We're also investigating their abrupt deaths.”

“Abrupt deaths?” The man holding snakes frowned. “What do you mean?”

“They were still fine moments ago, but all of a sudden, all of them died,” Zu An explained.

“The Chang clan is filled with experts. Who has the strength to discreetly kill all of them at the same time?” the other man wielding a shield and an axe pointed out in a deep voice. He eyed the three of them and asked, “Who are you? What are you doing here?”

The Mermaid Queen knew it would be hard to clarify their true identities, so she said, “I am the Chang clan’s Saintess Chang’e. Today was the day I would marry my fiancé, so the Chang clan was more bustling than usual. But moments after we entered our consummation room, we sensed something amiss and rushed out to investigate, only to find that my clansmen had lost their lives.”

“Saintess Chang’e?” the man holding two snakes murmured contemplatively. “I have heard about you. Your fiancé is the Youqiong clan’s Yi…” He turned to Zu An and asked, “You are Yi?”

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. He knows about Chang’e and Yi’s engagement. This will be tricky. 

It would be tough to explain the underlying reasons, so he said, “The old chieftain has indeed officiated my marriage with Chang’e.”

The man wielding an axe and a shield scoffed, “That’s impossible. I have heard of Yi. He’s a rising star in the Shaman races, but you don’t have the slightest shaman blood in you.”

The Mermaid Queen anxiously clarified, “He’s indeed the Yi acknowledged by the old chieftain and the entire Chang clan. The reasons are complicated…”

The man holding the two snakes interjected, “Given how lovey-dovey you are, you two are obviously together. Speak! Did you plan to impersonate Yi to marry the Saintess, only for the Chang clan to find out about your ploy and for you to massacre them out of anger?”

By now, their other companions had also arrived at the town. They were nowhere as muscular as Kuafu and Xingtian, but their massive statures made it clear that they were from the Shaman races. They surrounded Zu An and the others and focused their auras on them. 

Zu An sneered, “The first thing you did upon arriving here is to accuse me without evidence. Now that I think about it, you suddenly appeared here while we were investigating the Chang clan’s massacre. That makes you people suspicious too!”

The snake-holding Kuafu harrumphed. “The Chang clan’s chieftain invited us to witness the newlyweds’ marriage ceremony, but we faced some issues on our way here and were delayed by several days. That’s why we only arrived now.” 

The axe and shield-wielding Xingtian nodded.

Zu An asked, “Where’s your evidence?”

The two giants were at a loss for words. Kuafu raged, “What evidence can I offer for that?!”

You have successfully trolled Kuafu for +114… +114… +114…

Zu An nodded. I didn’t hear wrong. This is Kuafu, and the other one is Xingtian. Knowing that they were dealing with two mythological powerhouses, he felt troubled. 

Xingtian said, “We’re on close terms with the Chang clan’s chieftain; why would we bother leaving any evidence? Besides, the chieftain relayed the message to us using his skill... Hold on! We're the ones asking questions here, so why are you questioning us now?” 

Sensing the hostile gazes from all around, Zu An asked, “Can you ask your companions to back down first? There’s something confidential I need to discuss with you.”

Xingtian and Kuafu exchanged looks before gesturing for their companions to step away. Then, they turned to Zu An and said, “Hurry up and say your piece. I advise you not to try anything. Even if it’s just us, you won’t be able to escape.” As powerhouses of the Shaman races, they weren’t worried about anything going awry.

Zu An spent a moment in thought. Since the Chang clan’s chieftain invited them, they're likely friends with the Chang clan. As powerful as they are, given their huge physiques, I doubt they could massacre the Chang clan without me noticing. 

So, Zu An told them about the Chang clan’s chieftain’s plan to have him impersonate Yi and marry Chang’e to prevent the ancient prophecy from being made impossible. He spoke with a controlled cadence with clear logic, so it didn’t take long before the duo understood what he was saying.

“What? The real Yi is dead?”

“The Chang clan has such a prophecy?”

Kuafu and Xingtian were astounded. 

“Who killed Yi?” Xingtian asked.

Zu An shook his head. “I wanted to ask the old chieftain, but he insisted on only telling me after the ceremony. I didn’t expect such a mishap to happen in the meantime.” He had a guess, but he had no evidence, so he didn’t dare to run his mouth.

“You’re saying that the culprit might have killed them to silence them?” Kuafu stroked his lower jaw, allowing the snake he was holding to coil intimately around his neck. 

Looking at that, the Mermaid Queen had goosebumps. 

“That could be possible.” Zu An reserved his skepticism about that, as he hadn’t sensed the same unique energy present where Yi was killed. 

“Why did the culprit spare you?” Kuafu’s suspicion still wasn’t dispelled. 

“I don’t know.” Zu An frowned. “At this rate, if we continue dawdling here, the true culprit might escape.” 

“You don’t have to worry about that.” Xingtian harrumphed. “I scanned the vicinity, and there are no living beings in the area except for us.”

“So, you still suspect that we’re the culprits?” The Mermaid Queen frowned. 

“The two of you were in the consummation room. What about her? Where was she?” Kuafu pointed to the quiet Yumen Beiqing.

Yumen Beiqing eyed him coldly, not bothering to answer the question.

The Mermaid Queen anxiously spoke up. “She was with us!”

“With you?” Kuafu and Xingtian were stunned. Their gazes went back and forth among the three of them. He brought another woman into the consummation room? Both the bride and this woman are ravishing beauties, and yet they agreed to such an arrangement?

They looked at Zu An in awe. 

Yumen Beiqing harrumphed. “Drop those filthy thoughts! I was strolling outside the town!”

“Strolling? Alone?” Kuafu and Xingtian’s expression turned vigilant once more.

“Of course I was alone!” Yumen Beiqing stood upright as if she wasn't afraid of causing any misunderstandings.

Kuafu and Xingtian exchanged looks. Looking at each other, they verified their conjecture. This woman isn’t as simple as she looks. She has a power that makes us uncomfortable.

“You are the biggest suspect! Come with us!”

The green snake on Kuafu’s right hand shot toward Yumen Beiqing and coiled around her body. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2547: Meeting Houtu Once More
chapters.body:
Chapter 2547: Meeting Houtu Once More

Zu An’s eyebrows twitched, but he didn’t move right away, as if he was awaiting something. 

Yumen Beiqing looked as if she was stunned. Before she could react, the green snake wrapped itself around her and dragged her toward Kuafu. 

Zu An couldn’t wait anymore. He dashed over and slashed his blade down on the green snake. 

The green snake winced in pain as it anxiously released Yumen Beiqing. Zu An pulled her into his embrace as he punched the other yellow snake, which had darted in to attack. Then, he took advantage of the opening to bring Yumen Beiqing back to the Mermaid Queen’s side. 

“Are you fine?” Zu An quickly inspected her body.

Yumen Beiqing shook her head. Her icy eyes looked conflicted. 

“How dare you hurt my beloved snakes!” Kuafu roared.

He took out a staff to strike Zu An. A brilliant light and the sound of crashing waves emanated from the staff as it was swung with terrifying might that could crush a small mountain.

Zu An was about to fight back when he suddenly remembered the mysterious Seven-Colored Divine Halo. He released a seven-colored light ahead of him, and the charging Kuafu vanished into thin air. 

Everyone present was stunned. Kuafu should have had the upper hand there, so why did he suddenly disappear?

Some of Kuafu’s brethren brandished their weapons to attack Zu An, hoping to rescue Kuafu. However, they also vanished in a flash of seven-colored light.

“What sorcery did you use?!” Xingtian didn’t dare to let his guard down. He placed his shield in front of him while preparing his axe; he was ready to launch a fatal blow at any moment. 

His shield was imbued with tremendous defensive prowess. It could withstand most spells, forcing his enemy to engage him in close-quarters combat, which happened to be the strength of the Shaman races. 

It was a pity that such an ability was useless. With a flash of a seven-colored light, he also vanished. 

Seeing the previously bustling surroundings suddenly become empty bewildered the Mermaid Queen and Yumen Beiqing. 

“Hubb… Big brother Zu, where did they go?” The Mermaid Queen didn’t think it was appropriate to call Zu An 'hubby' in Yumen Beiqing’s presence. 

Yumen Beiqing reacted faster. “Is that the ability of your Seven-Colored Divine Halo?”

Zu An nodded. He sensed that Kuafu and the others had been put inside a peculiar seven-colored space. The weaker shamans had fainted on the spot, and only the strongest Kuafu and Xingtian were still holding on.

Kuafu’s two snakes grew until they were over a thousand meters long, and as they attempted to break free from the peculiar seven-colored space, they rampaged like massive dragons. Even so, no matter where they went, the two snakes couldn’t find a way out.

Xingtian roared as his body became incomparably huge. He furiously chopped at the seven-colored space like the great Pangu who had severed heaven from earth. Terrifying force rippled through the space, causing it to shake. 

Zu An thought, The Seven-Colored Divine Halo is similar to Kong Xuan’s five-colored divine halo in Investiture of the Gods. I can stow anything I want inside it, but there’s a limit. If the enemy is too strong, it might be possible for them to break free.

Even though Xingtian was causing a huge commotion, most of the force he used was absorbed by the special laws of the seven-colored space to reinforce it. In other words, he couldn't break free. 

But Zu An didn’t dare to assume the results would be the same against an enemy far stronger than Xingtian.

In Investiture of the Gods, Kong Xuan had dragged one of the strongest characters in the series, Zhunti of the Two Western Saints, into his five-colored divine halo; but Zhunti was able to break free from inside. 

I wonder if my Seven-Colored Divine Halo is any stronger than his five-colored divine halo… 

Zu An sensed that Xingtian and Kuafu were getting desperate inside the seven-colored world, and were preparing to use secret arts that demanded a hefty price to activate. He didn’t want to become the Shaman races’ mortal enemy in the first place, so this battle had no meaning.

The seven-colored light flashed once more, and the shamans appeared again. The weaker shamans collapsed onto the ground, while Kuafu landed on his butt. Only Xingtian maintained a firm footing, looking fierce like a war god. 

“I could have taken your lives, but it’s not my wish to become your enemy. All I wish to say is that I’m not the culprit behind the Chang clan’s massacre. Someone else is behind it,” Zu An said, although he was bluffing to an extent. It was easy for him to control them, but it wouldn’t be as easy for him to kill them. 

Even so, Xingtian and Kuafu were intimidated. They thought that Zu An truly had the power to kill them, but he'd chosen to patiently talk things out. There had to be a misunderstanding here. 

Xingtian undid his transformation and apologized. “Forgive us for our earlier offense.”

Kuafu apologized as well. 

Yumen Beiqing scoffed. These shamans oppress the weak, but fear the strong. 

“I don’t know what tricks the culprit used to massacre the Chang clan within such a short time, and I don’t know why they spared us either.” Zu An fell into deep thought. Xingtian and Kuafu’s doubts made perfect sense; he couldn’t make sense of the situation either.

Xingtian chimed in. “Someone capable of such a feat can’t be nameless.”

“Do you have any suspects?”

“Not at the moment, but we can consult the Ancestral Shamans,” Xingtian replied.

Zu An was overjoyed. “You’re able to contact the Ancestral Shamans? May I know which one?”

Some said there were ten Ancestral Shamans, whereas others said there were twelve. Regardless, there had to be quite a few of them around. But those were legendary figures. Zu An didn’t think that Xingtian could contact them directly. 

“Lady Houtu!” Xingtian replied with a look of reverence. 

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. Houtu again! What role does she play in this matter?

Xingtian made an incision on his finger, and fresh blood oozed out. He flicked the blood droplets toward the sky and quickly drew a series of mysterious runes. Each finger stroke he made contained a peculiar, arcane power. 

The blood-colored runes slowly diffused to form a thin portrait. A woman's impassive voice echoed from it. “Who calls me…”

Everyone present suddenly felt insignificant, as if a lofty god was judging them. 

Xingtian and Kuafu quickly kneeled and bowed. “Junior Xingtian (Kuafu) pays respect to Lady Houtu!”

Zu An tried to widen his eyes to take a better look at Houtu’s appearance, but the only thing he could see in the portrait was a blurry figure. He could vaguely tell that the other party was a woman, but he was unable to make out her facial features. Even though the latter was just an avatar, he felt tremendous pressure. She seems stronger than the Houtu I met in the future. 

“What’s wrong?” The woman’s voice remained placid. 

Xingtian hurriedly explained the situation.

“Oh?” Houtu’s avatar turned her eyes toward Zu An. “The Chang clan’s chieftain told you to become Yi?”

Xingtian and Kuafu were perplexed. They were here to request information about the culprit, but why was Lady Houtu more interested in this matter?

Zu An instinctively nodded. He was about to probe further into it when an aura flew out from the portrait and fell on him, infusing a wild power into his bloodline. This aura wasn't foreign to him. It was that of the Shaman races.

“From now on, he’s Yi. You can testify on his behalf,” Houtu’s voice echoed.

Xingtian and Kuafu trembled. They could feel the purest shaman bloodline coming from Zu An. Other shamans wouldn’t be able to tell that anything was wrong with him. They had no idea why Lady Houtu was doing this, but they didn’t dare to defy her orders. 

Zu An was dumbstruck. Yumen Beiqing’s hair fell over her lowered head, making it impossible to make out her expression. 

Translator's Thoughts
